summary: Dashboard Graph | Dashboard Graph Missing Details
description:
Dear Product Team,

I am submitting this product request to address a UI functionality gap within the Analytics Dashboard Reports, specifically impacting data visibility when multiple entities or extended date ranges are selected.

*FULL Product Request Description:*

The core issue is that the Analytics Dashboard UI is currently *missing important information, specifically data labels (the individual rating numbers on the bars) and entity names (on the X-Axis), which are expected to be displayed for quick and easy data interpretation*. (See Screenshots Attached

This problem consistently appears under two primary conditions:

* When approximately *15 or more locations (entities) are added* to the report.

* When a *date range longer than 30 days is included* in the metrics.

For example, when a user selects a date range such as "January 1 to July 31st," both the data labels and the entity names disappear from the graph. Furthermore, the individual rating numbers that are "supposed to say the individual ratings for each one" are absent from the bars themselves. While hovering over individual bars can reveal some information like the property name (e.g., "Apartment Central Centurion Property" or "Knox property") and associated individual (e.g., "Dej"), this is not a sustainable or efficient way to view a full report.

The client has explicitly requested that we update the functionality of the UI to *consistently include the names of the individual entities on the X-axis of the report, as well as the data labels (individual rating numbers) on the individual bars*, regardless of the number of locations or the length of the date range selected. It's important to note that this UI functionality *does work correctly when there are fewer than approximately 12 locations selected or for date ranges of 30 days or less*, but it fails to display this critical information when these limits are exceeded.

*Why the Customer Wants This and Why We Should Build It:*

The customer, Centerion Asset Management (Business ID: 4225755), relies heavily on these dashboard reports for operational insights into their properties (buildings) and the performance of their property managers. The absence of visible data labels and entity names hinders their ability to *interpret their performance at a glance and make informed decisions*. Without these crucial details directly on the graph, they cannot quickly identify individual property ratings or discern which bar corresponds to which property, especially when analyzing a large portfolio over a long period. This forces them into inefficient workarounds.

Building this functionality is important because it addresses a impractical flaw in the product's core data visualization capabilities. Providing clear, accessible data is paramount for a robust analytics platform. This enhancement will *improve user experience, increase data interpretation efficiency, and enhance overall product satisfaction* for our clients. It ensures the product delivers on its promise of insightful and actionable reporting without requiring burdensome manual interaction for basic data retrieval. This is a foundational expectation for any effective data analytics tool.



*Impact on Upcoming Renewal:*

I do not have direct information at this time to confirm if this specific issue will directly affect an upcoming renewal for Business ID: 4225755. However, the inability to effectively utilize a core analytics feature due to missing data is a significant usability concern that could contribute to overall dissatisfaction.

*Publisher/App Directory Integration Details:*

This request *does not pertain to a publisher or app directory integration*. It is a core UI functionality enhancement within our existing *Analytics - Dashboard Reports* product area.

I appreciate your attention to this critical product request. Please let us know if you require any further information or clarification.

Thank you,



Ticket for Reference - [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2272543|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2272543]
workaround:
Currently, the primary "workaround" for the user is to manually hover over each individual bar in the graph to reveal the entity name and individual rating. This is highly inefficient and impractical, particularly when dealing with 15 or more entities or extended date ranges, making the dashboard much less useful for quick insights.

We have not previously engaged a specific Product or SME team member on this precise issue, but this email serves as the official communication to address it.

summary: Improve/Disable Address Field Autocomplete Behavior
description:

I’m not really sure what can be done about this other than spacing out the fields more and/or adding an option to disable autocomplete, but I wanted to capture this feedback since USB is a new client with lots of growth potential.

{quote}I’ve gotten some feedback from my teammates about the predictive text functionality when entering an address for an established entity. I’m being told that there's a dropdown that is sometimes glitching and when an analyst goes to click the city to make an update after putting in the address, it selects a suggested address from the dropdown and changes all the fields. There have been occasions when the analyst clicks off the field to make the dropdown disappear, but the field to input the City is right where the bottom of the dropdown had been, and even though the dropdown is no longer visible, it is still selecting the bottom option and overwriting all previously completed address fields, prompting the analyst to double back and change the address again.{quote}
workaround: User should be able to hit ESC to close the drop-down menu without clicking/selecting any options. USB could update addresses via file upload to avoid this, but they should be able to make manual edits via the KG as well.

summary: Issue with Distance Calculation and Sort Results for FAD
description:
Hi Team, 

*Context:* The CUIMC FaD recently was rebuilt on React with Search ([doctors.columbia.edu|http://doctors.columbia.edu/]). Although the new site mimics the classic site, they are submitting a product feature request on how we calculate distance and sort results.

*Current Behavior:* The client understands that Yext mimics other platforms like Google and Bing to calculate distance by converting a user's input into a latitude and longitude to then calculate the distance between each result and that coordinate.

*Example/Issue:* However, when a certain location happens to be in the outskirts/farthest point of a certain area, other zipcodes may appear first. For example, a search for [10604|https://doctors.columbia.edu/?qp=West+Harrison%2C+New+York+10604%2C+United+States&lat=41.042003&lng=-73.748969&r=10] leads to providers at 15 North Broadway, White Plains, NY 10601 to appear first before locations at 1111 Westchester Ave, White Plains, NY 10604.

*Desired Behavior:* The client believes this is a confusing user experience since the expectation should be that they would see results that match 10604 first. It sounds like the client's desired is more about sorting results as opposed to the calculation of distance.

We appreciate your consideration in this request as it is very important to the client that the search work in a manner they deem to be most correct.
workaround:
The services and product team came up with two potential work arounds, however both have serious drawbacks:

-Two Search Calls: we first do an exact match on zipcodes and then depending on how many results are returned, we do a separate search call to then do another search for other locations within the 10 mile radius.

Trade-off: This means that each search would be considered two searches. We would also lose on accuracy of reporting which was a big part of the reason the client (Ed) wanted to migrate to search. 

-Front-end Sorting: Our team would have to do front-end manipulation to get Ed's desired sorting.

Trade-off: Front-end sorting would conflict with the backend search product. This would be more difficulty debugging search issues and inaccurate analytics.

summary: Update Location Conflict Process for Resellers
description:
Reseller location conflict is a constant pain point for resellers. So many of their new customers get frustrated by the cancellation process with other vendors that might work with Yext in order to get listings live. This generates frustration with our partners because they have to go through a lot of back and forth with their customers to finally get locations canceled.



We spoke to a partner who had the idea to do something similar to Duda where you basically upload like a proof of cancellation to the platform and get access to the location for listings (in Duda’s case it is to manage the domain for the website). There are partners that could abuse this in bad faith but apparently Duda flags those and limits their ability to utilize this tool. Could we do something similar for listings to make it easier for our partners to take on new locations/listings when customers move to them from another partner?
workaround:
We have our location conflict process as outlined below: 

Inform the client that they already have Yext Listings through another Yext Provider, and we cannot support duplicative subscriptions.
If they know who they are working with:  let them know that they'll need to reach out to their current provider to cancel their services:
“I would like to cancel my Yext Listings service with you so I can work with another Yext Partner.  Please cancel the Listings service effective today.”
If they do not know who they are working with, please have the end customer email LocationConflict@yext.com
“I currently have listings service through Yext or a Yext Partner, but I would like to work with Momos.  My information is below: Business Name, Address, Phone Number, Contact (Name / Email).  Can you please provide the Name and Contact information of my current provider so I can contact them to cancel?”
Once they have that information, they can contact the Partner to cancel using:
“I would like to cancel my Yext Listings service with you so I can work with another Yext Partner.  Please cancel the Listings service effective today.”
Once Services are canceled, you should be free to add services.
If the Service Addition fails again, reach out here.
Oftentimes it is helpful to have proof of cancellation, so you may need to ask your client to provide a screenshot of the client’s current provider confirming cancellation
With this Yext can reach out to the Partner and request the services be canceled.

summary: Listings Insights - Dashboards Visibility/Map
description:
We had a feedback meeting with *Signal Iduna* about *Listings Insights* as a replacement for the previous Competitive Intelligence Dashboard, which we would like to share with Product.

*TLDR*: Signal Iduna's feedback on the new Competitive Intelligence dashboards is predominantly negative, criticising in particular the significant changes compared to the previous version.

Quote from decision-maker: we would not have purchased this version!



+The main points of criticism are:+

* *Lack of communication*: Signal Iduna complains that changes were made without informing them in advance.
* *Loss of the main metric*: The central and well-explained metric to date was ‘Share of Intelligent Search’. This could be explained very well in discussions with insurance agencies and used as a KPI. With the new display format, this is no longer possible.
* *Deterioration of visualisation and storytelling*: The derivation and visualisation, especially with regard to the Local Pack, is perceived as significantly worse and criticised as too technical for the target group – insurance agents who are not specialised data analysts.
* *Complexity and usability*: The new dashboard is perceived as more complex; the preselection of folders is considered pointless and the column sorting unclear.
* *Data quality*: The competitors' figures are considered questionable.
* *Impact on motivation and price*: The old dashboard served as a motivational tool for brokers, which is no longer the case. Sigal Iduna states that the price of the product is no longer justified with the new dashboard and that he would not have purchased it in this form.
workaround:
As a workaround they created Reports/Dashboards to mimic the former Competitive Intelligence Dashboard.
But due to its unique UI/visualization those Reports/Dashboards can't - in their opinion - not keep up with the old one.

Since Signal Iduna is our Competitive Intelligence lighthouse client, probably another feedback session with Product might be of interest.
With 80k Euro it's a quarter our total ACV of 320k, which is at risk at the next renewal.

While the generative review response feature can trigger based on star rating and templates, the client is looking for a *fully hands-off solution*: any review, regardless of rating, should receive an automatic AI-generated reply without manual intervention.
workaround: Tropical Smoothie Cafe is requesting the ability to fully automate AI-generated review responses within Yext, meaning no manual approval required per review. Their goal is to allow AI to respond instantly to new reviews that come in across all 1,550 cafés.

Linked Zendesk Ticket ID: [2254405|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2254405]

Client is having trouble knowing which fields on the updated entities were updated. Currently the connector Entity Results mark which entities were updated, but won't get more granular than that and denote which fields on that entity were updated and how.

summary: Rich Text Field edit enhancements
description:
I wanted to highlight a pain point raised by a financial customer, who are currently building a Knowledge Base and heavily relying on the RTF editor. Due to the absence of API integration, they’re entering data manually — including complex table structures.
When editing tables, users need to click into each individual cell to apply formatting changes like center alignment, heading styles, or font colors. This is extremely time-consuming and inefficient.
At present, it doesn’t appear possible to select an entire table and apply these formatting changes in one action.
Is this kind of bulk table formatting functionality something we are considering for the product roadmap? It would significantly improve usability for clients working with content-heavy or structured data in the RTF.
Thank you for looking into this.
workaround: Users need to click into each individual cell to apply formatting changes like center alignment, heading styles, or font colors.

Stephanie

Being able to see, which user scheduled an update in the entity history, after it has gone live is not effective for them, as it is essential for them to be informed, who is making changes to their content- particularly content which is regulated on their end, or Legal such as Terms and Conditions, before they are applied.
workaround: We have been able to pull a report with upcoming scheduled updates and which user scheduled these on a one-off basis for the client. Product has, however, also confirmed that this can’t be proposed as a supported feature with any regular cadence to it.

The partner phase2 says “When protecting fields against user editing, it's actually the built-in fields that are the most important.  Fields like NPI number, Address, etc we don't want users messing with when importing via connectors.  And we can't replace those kind of fields with custom fields.”

Hence the need for this Write Scope optionality to also work on built-in fields. Please let me know what else is needed
workaround: You can mostly use custom fields but there are some built-in fields that must be used so there is not a full workaround

summary: Home Screen feature suggestions
description:
Stroer had a few product suggestions for the Home Screen page. See summary below. Please let me know if you need any screenshots or if have any questions about their feedback.



# *Display Name & Line Title: Character Limit Indicator*

     Currently, there is no indication of the remaining character count, meaning users must guess whether they have reached the maximum number of characters.



# *Allow More Links & Sections (Rows):*

At present, the module is limited to two sections (referred to as rows), each containing four link fields. It would be helpful to allow additional sections and more links within each section. For example, being able to store six links in a single section would be beneficial.





# *Remove the Mandatory "Display Name" Field:*

Reason: In some cases, display names may be duplicated within a module, making this requirement redundant.



# *Add a Description Field for Each Row (200-250 Characters Maximum):*

This would allow for additional context or explanations to be displayed alongside the links.



# *Module Resizing & Layout:*

The module currently has a fixed, unchangeable size. It would be helpful if the size could either adjust automatically (to avoid scrolling within the module on the homepage) or if users could manually resize it (both in width and height). If the width can be adjusted, the sections (rows) should also align next to each other horizontally.
workaround: n/a

[https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/agreements/services#operation/listServices|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/agreements/services#operation/listServices|smart-link]

We want them to engage with our product and, responding to customer questions is part of that - we need email notifications to alert our clients first and encourage them to respond.

summary: Google Selectable Services
description:
Fonecta ($690K ACV) asked me last year about integrating selectable services from Google. In March 2023, Calvin said there were no plans to integrate this. Now almost 2 years later, Fonecta has asked again because one of our EU competitors, PinMeTo has launched this recently - see below. Here's Google's API documentation: [https://developers.google.com/my-business/content/services|https://developers.google.com/my-business/content/services|smart-link] 

And below are screenshots from BeeDigital's ($942K ACV) GBP account - they showed me how Google displays the options in their UI. They're interested in this too!
workaround: Not available at all within Yext, the workaround is to do this in Google's UI, which undermines the purpose of Yext as we want clients and resellers to use Yext as much as possible

summary: Field-Level Sync Control for Listings on a Per-Entity Basis
description:
Client would like the ability to *disable Listings Sync for specific fields (e.g., Categories, Hours, Photos)* on *individual listings (entities)*, while still maintaining sync for other data types like *Reviews*, especially for *Google Business Profile*.

Currently, the “Update Listings” setting can only be toggled globally per publisher, which means turning off sync applies to all listings across the account. This limitation prevents the client from customizing sync behavior at a more granular level, which they believe would significantly improve their service offering to their end customers.

The client has identified multiple use cases where this functionality would improve their offering to their customers and provide more flexibility in managing listings content.

Support ticket for reference - [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2228273|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2228273]
workaround: There is no viable workaround at this time. The only option available is to globally disable sync for a given publisher via the “Update Listings” configuration, which is not suitable for clients who need entity-level or field-specific sync control.

summary: Request for social post validation
description:
Hi, this is the request from Fonecta after they encountered an issue with a social post not being published to Google - unfortunately our platform does not alert that phone numbers are not allowed in posts to Google and there is no error message indicating that the post has failed. 

_“Could you add an error notification in the_ *_Posts_* _section when a user includes a phone number in the post text and selects_ *_Google_* _as a publisher?_

_This notification is essential because Google doesn’t publish posts that contain phone numbers in the text, because it violates Google’s content policy. (Example below)_

_Notifying users before publishing would help prevent confusion and failed publications._

_We’ve received feedback from clients who assumed their posts—such as offer posts—were published, when in fact they weren’t. They had no idea there was an issue with the post content._

_Unfortunately Yext doesn’t indicate any issues for unsuccessful post._

_We only notice this when we sign in our admin Gmail and Google has sent an email about post removal. Or, if we check the GBP in Google and the post hasn’t been published._

_In the Post Calendar, the Post Status is incorrectly ‘Published’.”_

Thank you!

Liz
workaround: None

summary: Fixing Average Review Calculation in Analytics API
description:
The Analytics API returns {{0.0}} for rows with a {{null}} value in the {{AVERAGE_RATING}} field, while the Report Builder omits these rows entirely. This inconsistency arises due to differences in how {{null}} values are handled between the two systems.



* Running a API query in Snowflake revealed 17 rows with a {{null}} {{AVERAGE_RATING}}, which matches the 17 {{0.0}} values in the API response.
* The Report Builder omits rows with {{null}} {{AVERAGE_RATING}}, so the {{0.0}} values are not visible there.
workaround: Users interpret 0.0 in the API as equivalent to null

summary: API key inheritance for Suggestions API
description:
Localsearch is planning to use our Suggestions API to pull Computed Field suggestions into their UI. In order to do this they will need to query sub-accounts with an API key from the parent. Today the Suggestion API does not support API key inheritance. 

An example: [https://api.yextapis.com/v2/accounts/localsearch-1/suggestions?api_key=c638b84876cf225d845809cb71f2d041&v=20241128|https://api.yextapis.com/v2/accounts/localsearch-1/suggestions?api_key=c638b84876cf225d845809cb71f2d041&v=20241128]. This is a request with an API key of the parent account ([https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/4079861|https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/4079861]) trying to pull suggestions from a sub-account: [https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/4388468|https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/4388468].

This is a blocker for them rolling out the feature, as they have no way to pull these suggestions in at scale (an API key for each sub-account would require them to set up 150,000 Developer Apps and store the keys somewhere on their end + they would need to do this for every individual new account).

The request is to add API key inheritance to the Suggestions API. If we do not offer this, they will start building out their own integration with OpenAI or Perplexity, which we want to avoid as it means they rely less on Yext features.
workaround: None

summary: Use Page ID to Pull in Competitors
description:
Hibu would like to utilize the Page ID for competitor analysis, we could add this as a field option in Storm when filling out competitor info and then pull in the competitor data based on the Page ID for FB. 
 
The reason for this addition is because Facebook's search tool does not perform well without very specific information (like a parent page ID, account ID, etc) which is causing competitor analysis for FB to not return results most of the time.
workaround:
None - this expected for Facebook due to the nature of their search tool.

Although we are scanning on a set cadence for competitors on Facebook, Facebook's search tool does not perform well without very specific information (like a parent page ID, account ID, etc). Searching just for a business name, which is the only strategy we can use here when searching for competitors, is unlikely to actually find the page.Which means that we aren't finding any data to pull in for the competitors in this case. Even though the pages may exist, Facebook simply doesn't have a good way to pull in information for them unless you are an admin of the page or have the page ID.

summary: Template import/export
description:
Hi Team!

The L2 Team and I were discussing features that would be nice to have in Content.

One of these would be the ability to upload/update Template files and export them. 

Ideally, we’d love to have real upload functionality—similar to how Content Upload works—using Template IDs as a reference.

It would be really useful to have an upload functionality that uses the Template ID as a reference. This would allow us to upload or update templates accurately without the risk of overwriting important data. The upload process should automatically recognize the Template ID and associate the correct data.

Another feature we would love to see is the ability to add new fields or sections directly during template upload. This would make template updates more flexible and reduce the need for manual adjustments after uploading the files.

A pre-upload validation that checks the structure and content of the template (e.g., ensuring required fields are present or verifying the correct format) would be extremely helpful. In case of errors, users could receive detailed notifications, preventing issues from arising after the template has been uploaded.

In addition to uploading, it would be important to have the option to export templates in an editable format (e.g., .json or .xml). This would allow us to make updates offline and then easily upload the modified templates without disrupting the workflow.

It would be helpful if the system allowed us to export a backup version of the template before each update. This would ensure that, if necessary, we could easily restore the previous version without having to manually search for the file.

Thank you!

Diana

summary: Review Gen | API update
description:
*Pulling invitation changes*

Problem:
In order to pull changes and store them on our side, we need to check for updates for all invitations that:

* has !REJECTED status
* has any empty field: sent/opened/clicked


There are several scenarios where these fields may remain unfilled for a long time:

* the user has disabled SMS/email notifications in settings, causing invitations to remain in pending status until they are re-enabled
* the contact may never open the email, in this case the invitation will never have all its 'sent/opened/clicked' fields filledThis can lead to a linear increase in the number of pages we have to pull during collection because '[Get Review Invitations List|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/reviews/reviewgeneration#operation/listReviewInvitations]' endpoint returns invitations sorted by requested date DESC.So the question is: Is it possible to add the 'updateDate' field to the invitation model and modify the '[Get Review Invitations List|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/reviews/reviewgeneration#operation/listReviewInvitations]' endpoint to return invitations sorted by updateDate in DESC order? This would greatly help in reducing the number of queries to Yext API, as we would be able to collect only invitations that were changed since previous collection
workaround: None that I am aware of.

summary: Update Rich Text Field UI/UX
description:
Today, AIB leverages the rich text field type quite heavily which powers their Knowledge Card Page Type (screenshot below) within their KB experience. They typically add both text and images (securely hosted) into the {{Document Body}} field in their {{Knowledge Card}}  Entity Type. When they add Entities into the Entity Type, they usually copy & paste from the original internal document into the field (there is no automation set up and they don’t intent to set up a connector or ETL at this time). They usually go chunk of text by chunk of text and add in the relevant images where needed (mimicking the original document’s structure). When they add an image, they need to scroll to the top of the rich text field (which might take a while as some of the entities are *very* long) to click on the add image button, add the image, and then scroll all the way back down within the field to where the image was just inserted, update the size, format…etc. and then scroll to the top again for to insert the next image and so on. This process can sometimes take up to 15-20 minutes to just add one entity into the KG (understanding this will always be quite manual since they’re copying and pasting). 

The idea they had was to update the tool bar to a floating one so that it follows the user wherever they are in the field to eliminate the scrolling up and down to upload the images and then format it and therefore cut down on the time it takes to add content into the KG.
workaround: See above, they do lots of scrolling to upload the images into the field and format them correctly.

summary: Change in Phone Validation library to consider phone number extensions
description:
*Product Request Description:* 
The current *mainPhone* field on location entities validates phone numbers using the Phone Validation Library, which only accepts phone numbers with *10 digits* for the US. However, many businesses—especially larger organizations with multiple departments—need the ability to list *phone number extensions* alongside the main number to facilitate proper call routing. This request proposes the addition of a separate field for *phone number extensions* to allow businesses to provide more detailed and accurate contact information for their customers.

*Why the customer wants this:*
Many businesses, especially those with customer support centers or large teams, rely on phone extensions to direct calls to specific departments or employees. Currently, businesses are limited to listing only a 10-digit phone number, which means that departments or employees requiring extensions cannot be directly reached through the main listing. Allowing extensions in the phone number field would enable businesses to provide more complete contact information and improve the customer experience by routing calls more effectively.

*Why we should build:*

* *Enhanced Call Routing*: Businesses can display both the *main number and relevant extensions* for specific departments or employees, streamlining customer communication.
* *Better User Experience*: Customers would have immediate access to the correct department or employee without needing to navigate through multiple layers or wait for operator assistance.
* *Business Growth and Scalability*: As businesses grow, the need for internal extensions becomes increasingly important. Supporting extensions will better serve growing organizations and allow them to scale their operations efficiently.
* *Compliance with Industry Standards*: Many phone systems and business directories, like VoIP providers, allow extensions. This would align GBP with broader industry practices, making the platform more useful for businesses.
* *Improved Accuracy in Contact Information*: By allowing the inclusion of extensions, businesses can ensure that customers have the correct information, reducing the chances of misdirected calls and improving call accuracy.

*Slack Thread link:* [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/CDA4M8ULS/p1743087416638729|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/CDA4M8ULS/p1743087416638729|smart-link] 
*POCs:* [~accountid:5d24af4c2c5a110c1d474b07] [~accountid:633ff2603ac41ebde76ec981] 
*Products Impacted:* Content, Listings

summary: Ability to Duplicate Reports in Report Builder
description:
NOTE: This has not been specifically requested by a client but every single client I know would benefit from this, not to mention that Yext CSMs and anyone who works with client data would have a lot of time freed up if it was created 🙂 .

Having the ability to duplicate reports created in Report builder would result in substantial time savings and minimize redundant work for clients and Client-facing Yext employees. 

Example use case (in the client’s voice): I’m a Healthcare client and I made a report for total listings impressions by month, for the past 12 months, compared to the previous period. I did not use any filters on this report because I wanted it to be for all entities, but now I want to have individual versions of this report for each specialty (I have 30+), which I indicate via folders and labels. Currently, to do this, my options are to:

# Have only one report but have to modify and reset the filters each time I access it, which leaves significant room for error or risks the wrong information being reported up the chain if I (or someone else on my team) don’t know to do this or forget, or
# I have to create a new report entirely from scratch, which (depending on what’s in the report or how many versions I want to make; in my case I’d have to do this 30+ times) can be tedious and time-consuming.
# My CSM told me that I could technically add the report that isn’t filtered by specialty to a dashboard, filter my labels on the dashboard, re-open the report, and then create a new report that way, but that’s still a lot of steps to do 30+ times and feels too convoluted and/or I don’t use dashboards.
workaround:
1. Have only one report but have to modify and reset the filters each time I access it, which leaves room for error or risks the wrong information being reported up the chain if I (or someone else on my team) don’t know to do this or forget

2. I have to create a new report entirely from scratch, which (depending on what’s in the report or how many versions I want to make; in my case I’d have to do this 30+ times) can be tedious and time-consuming.

3. My CSM told me that I could technically add the report that isn’t filtered by specialty to a dashboard, filter my labels on the dashboard, re-open the report, and then create a new report that way, but that’s still a lot of steps to do 30+ times and feels too convoluted and/or I don’t use dashboards.

summary: Restrict User Access Scope by Search Experience
description:
We are building another search experience for Lloyds and they have raised a security risk around user access. Currently it isn’t possible to restrict user access scope to a particular search experience. That means new users who will be managing the new search experience have to be given access to all search experiences, rather than just the one they’re managing which introduces risk, given that they are a bank. 

This will now be the 9th search experience in their account and each search experience is managed by a different brand within the group so while they are all part of the Lloyds group and have a group MSA, brands should not have access to other brands search experiences.
workaround: We do not currently have a workaround; they have to be given to access to all search experiences.

summary: Improve Filtering Behavior for the Description Field in Entity Search
description:
When filtering for entities without a description in the UI, the query currently takes into account all profile languages associated with an entity, rather than only the selected profile language. This results in an inflated number of entities appearing in the results, as the system includes entities where the description is missing in any language profile, even if it is present in another language.

TechOps ([https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-13711|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-13711]) has confirmed that this is expected behavior, as the query defaults to including alternate language profiles (e.g., both German and English in this case). However, this behavior is not explicitly conveyed to users, leading to confusion. The only way to obtain accurate results per language profile is to export the data and filter manually.

Plus, even when filtering on the primary profile language (here, German), the number of results is still incorrect, as the filter does not isolate the data strictly to the selected language profile.

This issue creates inefficiencies for clients trying to manage their data, as they cannot reliably filter by description field within the UI alone. It would be beneficial to either (see screen recording attached - the current number of entities without description in the primary lang - german - is 42).

# Provide an option to exclude alternate profiles from the filter directly in the UI.
# Surface clearer messaging in the UI to explain the current behavior.
workaround: Currently, the only workaround is to export the data, select the specific profile language, and manually filter the results outside of Yext.

summary: Ability to Export User IDs in platform

For example, the export contains the External ID 8511411251777918850 for the user linked [*here*|https://www.yext.com/s/3557648/user/14204804], but not the User ID 14204804 which is shown in the URL [https://www.yext.com/s/3557648/user/14204804|https://www.yext.com/s/3557648/user/14204804] and needed for the bulk delete tool linked [*here*|https://www.yext.com/admin2/configuration/bulkDeleteUsers].

Use the below Snowflake query to pull these IDs. However, this is not ideal as it only pulls users who have logged into the platform, not a full list of all users. This could also be limiting to teams who do not have Snowflake access or the resources to pull the data.

select * from YEXT_DATALAKE.DB4_ALPHA.apps_users
where business_ID=3557648

summary: Schedule License Assignment
description: Currently, KG does not offer the option to schedule a 'license assignment,' similar to how we can schedule a license removal. This limitation becomes evident when a client is not using the SFLA option but requests a location launch for a future date. Introducing a scheduling feature for license assignments would help streamline this process and improve overall efficiency.
workaround:
Introducing an option to schedule license assignments directly, without requiring integration with Saved Filters. This enhancement aims to improve efficiency for agents and enhance the client experience.

Key Benefits
1. Simplified Process
Eliminates the need for custom fields and saved filters, reducing setup time and complexity, especially for clients who don’t require them.

2.Improved Agent Workflow
Allows agents to track tickets more efficiently without placing them on hold, leading to faster resolutions.

3.Enhanced Client Experience
Enables proactive education for clients, reducing the need for additional requests when the open date is within 90 days.

4.Potential for Self-Service Integration
Can be linked to Listing Flags, allowing clients to manage license assignments independently in a self-serve system (if no major limitations exist).

description:
Hi, this is a request from the client - see attached screenshots. 

“We've noticed some updates to Yext Entity History, along with certain functional improvements. However, we’d like to highlight a few usability concerns.

*'History' tab in the Entity view*

When accessing the Entity History via the 'History' tab in the Entity view, the default "Group by Operation" mode only displays actors and timestamps, without specifying which fields were modified. Unfortunately this lack of detail doesn't provide enough information to be usable and we can't edit the view. Ideally, *the default view should be "All Events,*" as it provides more comprehensive insights, including updated fields, event types, and other relevant information.

*'All Events' view*

Additionally, in the 'All Events' view, the previous and current values of modified fields should be displayed by default. Currently, users must *manually add the "Value (Rendered)" and "Value (Raw)"* columns for each account, which makes it less user-friendly. 

Restoring this functionality as a default setting would improve usability and efficiency.”

summary: Analytics Request: Adjusting Color Indicators for Ranking Metrics in Analytic
description:
Request from the client: 

“We have observed an inconsistency in the way percentage changes are visualized in the analytics dashboards. Currently, positive percentage changes are marked in green and negative changes in red, which generally makes sense for metrics such as impressions or clicks. For example, if page views increase by +50% (from 1000 to 1500), the green highlight correctly reflects a positive trend.

However, for ranking-related metrics - such as 'Average First Organic Match Position by Keyword' - the interpretation is reversed. A lower numerical ranking (e.g., improving from position 12 to position 2) is a positive outcome, whereas a higher numerical ranking (e.g., dropping from position 2 to 12) is negative in this metric. In these cases, the color coding should be adjusted so that a decreasing percentage (indicating an improved ranking) is marked in *green*, while an increasing percentage (indicating a decline in ranking) is marked in *red*.

Implementing this adjustment would enhance clarity for clients and sales presentations and ensure that the analytics dashboard visually reflects performance trends more accurately.

We’d be grateful if you could consider these improvements for analytics.”
workaround: None

summary: Reviews and Response Analytics / Reporting Deficiencies
description:
# No reporting for Automatic Response. We need to be able to report on how many automatic responses were posted. This quantifies the value of the feature and the time/cost savings in using the features. It’s also important for the client and for Yext internally to distinguish between responses posted by Yext MRR and responses automated by the platform.
# No reporting for responses by user. We cannot report effectively and accurately on response output based on who did the posting. The client and Yext MRR needs to distinguish between responses posted by our team and responses posted on the client side. We cannot filter for Response User in Reviews either. I raised this issue [in another PFR|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/PFR-2153].
# Response reporting differentiated by Update and Review Date. Response data reads very differently if the report is based on Update Date or Review Date. Response Rate and Response Time are based on Review Date. In other words, if 100% of 1000 reviews posted in December were responded to 30 days later in January, the Response Rate for Dec. would be 100% and the Response time would be 720 hours. However, the Response Count for Dec. would be 0 and for Dec. would be 1000. We need to be able to distinguish between the two in reporting. 
# The data formatting for Response Rate has changed from 100 = 100% to 1.00 = 100%. This has changed the look for >100 MRR clients' dashboards that show Response Rate and Time in a single report. Before RR=95 and RT=24 made for an intuitive visual; now that shows 0.95 and 24.
workaround:
1. We can only track automated responses in Review Monitoring, for example. [https://www.yext.com/s/501290/reviews#c0=site&d0=false&sc0=date&p0=uep&p0=reviewdate&p0=sites&p0=status&p0=automatic-response&p1=includes&p1=gte&p1=250%7C283%7C71&p1=1%7C3&p1=true&p2=293054&p2=01%2F23%2F2025&p2=includes&p2=includes&p2=&p3=labels&p3=&p3=&p3=&p3=|https://www.yext.com/s/501290/reviews#c0=site&d0=false&sc0=date&p0=uep&p0=reviewdate&p0=sites&p0=status&p0=automatic-response&p1=includes&p1=gte&p1=250%7C283%7C71&p1=1%7C3&p1=true&p2=293054&p2=01%2F23%2F2025&p2=includes&p2=includes&p2=&p3=labels&p3=&p3=&p3=&p3=]
2. We can see if the response was posted in Review Monitoring. Response User is also a field included in the reviews export. This process is cumbersome and time consuming.
3. Everyone just has to know how to interpret each report in the dashboard [https://www.yext.com/s/501290/reports/insightsDashboard/213147|https://www.yext.com/s/501290/reports/insightsDashboard/213147]
4. I separated Response Rate and Time into separate reports.

Hi Team,

This should not change but there is a cohort of listings that fall outside of the matching criteria that are considered rogue listings and should be dealt with on the side of the client. Currently we can provide clients with broader scans that open up the criteria and result in large files or results that could possibly contain rogues for the client to work through but as you can imagine the files are large and cumbersome for the client to go through one by one. Ideally we would have a system by which we could rank likelihood of rogue status in this file to help our clients prioritize what to look at or some other system by which we could help alleviate the work involved int this task. 



Please note this request is Mount Sinai but other clients like MSK and Columbia have brought this up as well. 

Wilhelmina

summary: Timer for Automatic Review Response + Half Rating filters
description:
We are doing a 6 month MRR pilot for Allstate. We had auto response set up for Google and First Party rating-only reviews. [https://www.yext.com/s/501290/tasks/workflows|https://www.yext.com/s/501290/tasks/workflows]

We make custom pages for Allstate as well as custom review generation where a rating and feedback are gathered separately. Allstate requests a rating on the first page, which then pushes a new FP review to Yext. Allstate then requests feedback on the second page. If the customer submits that, that comes through as an update to the review. This means that First Party reviews are always getting auto responses because they always come in as rating-only first. 

*ASK:* Can we add to the workflow configuration to include a timer for when the auto response is triggered? I know this idea was discussed in early developed but not sure if it is still being prioritized. 

Another issue we are seeing as a result of the custom pages/review generation we have for Allstate is fielding half-star ratings. They allow ratings on a scale of 1-10, so 7 is pulled in as 3.5. If we are filtering for negative reviews (<= 3 stars) or positives (=> 4 stars), these are not captured in Reviews > Response or in Workflows. This is also an issue in Hilton Hotels which has a [Booking.com|http://Booking.com] reviews integration; Booking reviews are also 1-10 and surface in Yext with half stars. 

*ASK:* Allow half-star filtering all Reviews features, including Monitoring/Response, Workflows, and Auto Labels.
workaround: We are monitoring FIrst Party reviews separately in Reviews > Response without filtering for ratings to ensure nothing is filtered out. I also removed FP reviews for the auto workflows; all FP reviews are now being manually managed by the MRR team.

summary: Apply saved filter to entity dropdown list in entity edit view on Yext Content
description:
Feature request to add a dropdown list on the linked entity relationship fields when editing an entity in Yext Content. Often entities have fields like "type" and you only want to allow the user to choose an entity with a specific value of this field. For example, within all Healthcare Facility entities you only want to choose from Urgent Care facilities. Another COMMON use-case is to mark records as "Deleted" or "Archive" to remove them from search rather than deleting data. When this is done, any entity-relationship field still shows all the deleted/archived records. Yext already has the ability to create a Saved Filter. Would be extremely useful to assign a Saved Filter to a Entity Relationship field so it only shows entities from this filter in the drop-down list.

*_This is currently a blocker for a client project._*
workaround: Currently no way to filter out "deleted" entities that are linked.

summary: Yext Content to support "Name" holidays
description: Could Yext Content support built-in “Named” holiday values like Thanksgiving, Easter, etc. Healthcare Facilities where they have Holiday Hours but instead of a specific Date, they want to set the hours for a "Named" Holiday (e.g. Thanksgiving) because the actual date of the holiday will likely change year to year?
workaround: Currently when you need to update the date of a holiday that is on a different day each year, you need to bulk-update the locations with the new date. Or, the current recommendation from Yext is to manage Hours/Holidays in a spreadsheet and then periodically import that spreadsheet via a Data Connector to update the hours. Another option is to create a custom compound field with a subfield reference to a "Named Holiday" taxonomy entity and a subfield Hours value. But this compound field would not be used by the "Is Open" search functionality so isn't really viable.

summary: Adding additional linked entity fields to the Excel export functionality
description:
The use-case for this in Healthcare is very common:

* Client needs the ability to get a csv export of their Healthcare Professional content, with columns for the address field.
* So the export needs to be able to traverse the entity-relationship between Healthcare Professional and Healthcare Facility to export the street, city, zip of the doctor’s location address field 
* However, currently our excel file export does not let users pull linked entity fields, only the fields that are on the entity

summary: Ops Report Download via Filter ID
description:
h2. *Feature Request: Enhance Ops Report Generation with Filter ID Option*

h3. Current Process:

Currently, when downloading Ops Reports for suppressions, we rely on using *label IDs*. This requires us to manually add labels to the relevant entities, download the report, and then remove the labels once the task is completed. While this method works, it involves a lot of *manual effort* and is quite *time-consuming*, especially when handling large datasets or entities with MDS licenses.


h3. Proposed Solution:

The proposed solution is to introduce an option to *add filter IDs* into the Ops Report generation process, instead of using label IDs. By doing so, we would be able to create a *saved filter* with the required criteria (e.g., "subscriptions include 'Manage Duplicate Suppressions'") just once. Then, for any subsequent suppression tasks, we could use the *filter ID* to generate the Ops Report, without needing to manually add and remove labels every time.



Here’s how this would greatly benefit the workflow:

* *Time Savings:* With the ability to use *filter IDs*, we would save an estimated *6-8 minutes per ticket*. This is because the label creation, application, and removal process would be eliminated. For larger entities and datasets, this could save even more time—up to 10-12 minutes—especially in cases where manual upload of labels is required.
* *Reduced Manual Effort:* The ability to use *filter IDs* would automate the filtering process, removing the need to manually manage labels for every ticket. This would reduce the amount of time spent on repetitive tasks, and it would be especially helpful when handling recurring suppression tickets (typically created every 90 days for a client).
* *Improved Efficiency:* A saved filter can be used repeatedly for future tasks, making the process much faster and more consistent. Rather than creating a new label each time, we can simply use the saved filter’s *filter ID* to download the Ops Report quickly and accurately.

To implement this change, we simply need the ability to *add filter IDs* into the Ops Report generation process. This small adjustment would significantly improve our ability to manage suppressions with much less manual intervention, saving both time and effort.

*Ops Report Generation Link:* [https://www.yext.com/sms/report/generateOpsReport|https://www.yext.com/sms/report/generateOpsReport]

----
workaround: Until this feature request is approved and deployed, we will continue to use the existing approach of creating labels and downloading the Ops Report using label IDs. Although this method involves more manual effort, it is the current solution until the feature for using filter IDs is available.

summary: Add Location-Level Scope for Manage License permission (95)

Currently, license management is restricted to those with full Account-level access, which can be too broad and impractical for organizations with regional or location-specific administrators.

The idea would be to enable the "Manage Licenses" permission to be scoped at the *Location level*, in addition to the Account level.

Additionally, would it be possible, when creating or editing custom roles in Storm, to display the scope access (e.g., Account-level, Location-level) required for each permission - we currently don’t have this level of information displayed which can be misleading for the client (see SS attached).
workaround:
Suggested workaround would be to grant these users full account scope access (similarly to Isabelle Maher access set up). This adjustment would allow them to assign licenses to entities.
To ensure each user can still focus on their specific scope, they can filter their content, entities, or analytics views by applying filters based on their designated locations or folders.

summary: Listings: Dismiss or Resolve Non-Actionable Data Issues
description:
CI Web Group adds UTMs to all their website URLs for tracking purposes. However, various publishers do not accept UTMs, and this results in a "Data Issue" in the Listings module. The company has no plans to resolve the issue, as they do not intend to remove the UTMs.

Please see the attached screenshots. The *Data Issue* states: _This publisher does not support website URLs that contain Google-related UTM parameters. As a result, the URL may display without all the given parameters. All other data is syncing correctly._ *Solution:* _Navigate to Content, and remove any Google-related UTM parameters from the Website URL field._

The client wants a way to either hide or 'resolve' this Data Issue. If a customer requires a UTM on their website URL and is unwilling to remove it for just the few publishers that don’t accept it, the presence of this issue gives a skewed picture of listings with actual issues that require action.
workaround: N/A

summary: Ability to reference external images (via URL) without Yext needing to pull in its own copy of the image
description:
From the partner developer: 

This is a feature Sutter Health would want to have in 2025. Right now you have to upload an image (like Provider image) directly into Yext. When the client has these images in a digital asset management (DAM) system, updating the image in the DAM doesn't cause the image in Yext to be updated. So it requires a regular job to run to re-import images. Same with images that are part of a web-crawler import: the image gets uploaded into Yext and the SRC of the image is changed to point to Yext instead of it's original URL from the DAM.

The connector they have set up only runs for the initial data import.  They’d need a regular job to run and update the images...but then it would update ALL images with no idea which ones had changed in the DAM. Yext *really* just needs the ability to reference an external image via a URL.  That is what external DAMs are for.
workaround:
With Sutter, once they start managing their provider profile images in a 3rd party DAM we will probably stop using the Headshot/Image field in Yext itself and just have a text URL field and then let the front-end app fetch the image from that URL (usually the DAM has a CDN that makes this performant).
But then Sutter won't have the ability to see the images in the Yext UI itself.

For the web crawlers, they will need a way to keep the IMG tags in the HTML body content untouched and not have image urls converted to point to Yext.

summary: Dynamic booking links/links on Search Entities
description:
Client has 1000s of independent pubs in the UK. Their search experience powers each of these pubs for questions like “how do i make a booking?”, this currently directs the customer to the top of the page to find book now. Client wants the search answer to have a dynamic link, that relates to the location entity, such that it can hyperlink to that location entities own booking link. 

Right now, the FAQ can have one link stored against it, so each location entity would have to have it’s own FAQ (1000s of the same FAQ, but just a different URL). 

What the client wants is an easy way of having one FAQ that sits across all location entities, but that the link/booking link can be dynamic or relate to another field - like an entity relationship. 

This would be apparently very complex for us and be outside of their 5 MSH to fix this ([https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C02AA2AL8FK/p1734009109060999?thread_ts=1732810972.156529&cid=C02AA2AL8FK|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C02AA2AL8FK/p1734009109060999?thread_ts=1732810972.156529&cid=C02AA2AL8FK|smart-link] ). 

See feedback from the client here: 

Hi Will,

 

As discussed, we’re having some issues with Yext FAQs as we’re unable to add individual links to the FAQs, which means we have to be really vague with the answers and ask guests to find the correct page themselves rather than linking them straight there. This means a longer guest journey to find what the guest is looking for and also means we cannot track the bookings we’re getting from Answers.

 

We’ve previously setup FAQs with links but it has to be one link for all or we’ve created 100s of versions of one question to enable separate answer links. It would be helpful if we could pull through the entity URLs into the answers so they’re dynamic per pub.

 

This is starting to cause real issues for us as we’re wanting to invest more time into Yext Answers, we would like for this to be added as a feature request asap.

 

Thanks

Abbie
workaround: Directing searcher to look at the "book now" button

Only content HH articles, and generic saved filter HH docs, not the SFLA help doc.* 

My confusion with the documentation is relating to the links below. I have no clue which one is correct/which one clients should be using. 

* [Start the Listings Launch Flow |https://hitchhikers.yext.com/tracks/listings/lis220-launch-listings/01-launch-get-started/]- This one is odd because you technically need to set up the SFLA first which is the prior HH unit, and does somewhat allude to that in step 1 of this guide, but in my opinion, the SFLA preparation should be _within_ this guide as its all part of the launch process. 
** And if SFLA is already set up, that can be called out as “If its set up, you can skip to part X “ or whatever

Hi Team - Several clients have reached out to me with the new all listings screen UI Changes asking how to do things in the platform now that everything is different.

I also think both things can be true where release notes/release emails matter but so would showing our customers the changes and how to leverage them inside of their account are helpful as well because its not only been throwing my clients off as they are pinging me, but other AEs on my team as well.
