summary: Embedded fields in a Google CTA URL when social posting
description: Kroger is starting to use social media posting more, as recommended by its CSM. However, due to the nature of its various brands, it is unable to utilize the custom URL option when posting on Google. Just like how the body of the post allows you to insert embedded fields, it asked if we could add the option to include embedded fields in a Google Custom CTA URL.
workaround: Right now, they submit a ticket anytime they want to post on social media, which is an extremely clunky experience.

summary: Send Listings Accuracy Field Value via API
description:
[Owner.com|http://Owner.com] would like to use the Listings Accuracy metric, and instead of returning a % accuracy value, include (via API) the actual field value.  

If an Entity is 90% accurate, and the 10% inaccuracy is Phone Field incorrect on Yelp and Bing, they want to be able to pull the actual Phone Value that we have in the platform for those sites.  

Currently, via the Reports endpoint, we only surface the % accuracy, but the field values exist within the Platform. 

*Not to take away from this request, but Resellers have asked for a “Post Scan” for years, and Accuracy is _very_ close to what they are looking for.  If we returned field values, this could satisfy a lot of the “Post Scan” request to evaluate how their clients exist across different publishers post implementation, at scale.
workaround: In Platform Only - No export functionality.

summary: Ability to pull listing accuracy scan data via API
description: Details from the listing accuracy scan can be pulled via API (as a report from the analytics endpoint) however the data of the last scan (shown in screenshot) cannot be pulled. This limits the ability for a reseller building off our API to showcase this information in their platform and also makes it difficult to determine the data ranges for structuring the accuracy report.
workaround: N/A

summary: Review Approval: Bulk
description: The client requested for the ability to bulk-approve review tasks when approving review responses - Burger King is looking to increase locations using the reviews tool and bulk approval would help greatly with the volume.
workaround: n/a

summary: User Permissions
description: The client requested if new users be assigned to labels when creating a new user account - currently, they can only be assigned by individual location or folder
workaround: Sub folders

summary: Review Workflow UI
description:
Hey team - Burger King mentioned a good point about the workflow’s UI - when you’re creating a workflow rule - none of the fields are flagged as required. They requested that the red required dots be added to the required fields.

Kevin
workaround: n/a

summary: SEMrush | Review invite Templates | Custom Field Params
description:
See: [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1CfJaVi4H57xo6De16em9G3xGNIqvsN03_El5lfLX_mI/edit?tab=t.0|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1CfJaVi4H57xo6De16em9G3xGNIqvsN03_El5lfLX_mI/edit?tab=t.0|smart-link] orange text, Page 16 begins: Each custom field has its own unique id in the RG template.



# SEMrush wants to create a custom template whose button points to a specific URL rather than using the balancing algorithm. 
# When using Sub-accounts, a reseller has to create a new custom field every time. 
# This results in a new custom field parameter for each sub-account. Example: {{$cf_1110945}}
# Ideally, we would like to use the API ID, for example: c_googleOnlyReviewTemplateURL
# This would allow consistency rather than needing to edit the template every time with a new param. 



Ideally, it would be great if custom fields and templates could be inherited.
workaround: There is no workaround; it is a highly manual process, which is very challenging for a scaling reseller.

summary: Social URL Default
description:
HighLevel noticed that  when they update a social profile URL via the API or directly through the Yext platform, if any social URL is entered in the format {{www.facebook.com}} (or similar, without the {{http}} or {{https}} prefix), Yext defaults to saving it as {{http://www.facebook.com}}. It only saves the URL as {{https://www.facebook.com}} if we explicitly include the {{https://}} prefix.
 
Shouldn’t {{https}} be the default protocol when a URL is entered without a prefix, especially since {{https}} is the standard for secure web connections?
workaround: Have to explicitly include the https:// prefix.

summary: AI Suggestions for Competitive Intelligence
description: Suggestions made by AI to user to improve share of intelligence search, for example.
workaround: N/A

summary: Listings Notifications by Label
description: Currently there is no way to filter by Label for Listings Notifications
workaround: Filter by Entity or Folder

summary: Generic Source for Yext Webhooks/Apps
description: The new generic destinations provide great additional functionality for adding logic on top of Yext’s existing dev console apps including a webhook. Rather than having to configure an app/webhook separately (or at a minimum create it and pull in the API details) it would be helpful if dev console apps were a generic source in Connectors that would let you select from (or create) the existing apps in your account.
workaround: Push API source with the creds/URL from your app.

summary: Add Yelp Customer Actions to Snowflake Data Dictionary
description:
[Owner.com|http://Owner.com] is looking to pull Yelp Customer Actions via Snowflake, and currently only Yelp Page Views is available. 

Transparently, Resellers expect that any Location Listings Metrics that are available in Reports or via API, would also be available via Snowflake.
workaround: Unsuccessful workaround: pulling via Total Listings Actions and dimensioning for Site: Yelp, and Actions.

summary: Gate Permanently Closing >10% entities in Product
description:
Hi Team,

For years, we’ve seen numerous customers accidentally permanently close all of their locations via ETLs, APIs and manual file uploads. It’s a mess, and so stressful. This year I’ve had 1. UCSF Health’s providers all closed via an erroneous upload by support, 2. KeyBank close all of their locations via an API twice, 3. Regions close 300 branches via an ETL, and 4. UCI’s ETL close 90% of their providers.

For a long time, we’ve relied on Services to build breakers into the platform, and we have this logic in connectors, but there isn’t a consistent fail-safe across accounts. Customers should be able to set a breaker at their level that if we receive >10% of requests to close entities in platform, that they receive an alert OR some sort of API breaker that prevents it from being sent to publishers.

I know that Tapestry and a number of other customers have experienced this…and I think we could all be spared of one type of fire drill (meaning man hours saved and much happier customers).
workaround: Services work, or custom connector

summary: Social Post Feed
description: The Social Post Feed needs to be ordered by Scheduled/Published Date. Also, it needs a Calendar View. I'm struggling to see what dates I have posts scheduled so I don't duplicate post dates.
workaround: N/A

summary: Pull First Name as Embedded Field in Text Assets for Review Response
description: The reviewer name embedded field pulls the entire name field. For most clients, this is suboptimal as the preference for most is to address customers by first name only. This makes the embedded field for reviewer name of little value. It’s also something I completely recommend against if setting up assets for automatic response since there is too much variance in names, such as username or capitalization.
workaround: Clients either accept the efficiency gains of using the entire name or take the hit in efficiency to manually enter the first name.

summary: Reviews Filtering for Listing Status
description:
Previously synced reviews from opted out or unsynced listings (and we also see new reviews still coming in for these listings) appear in Review Monitoring and Response and appear available for action. In many cases, the Yext MRR team or users on the client side run into an error when trying to respond to a review from a listing that is opted out or unsynced.

If the intent is to show reviews in Monitoring regardless of Listing status as long as they’ve been pulled, then we should have the listing status as part of the review data so people don’t waste time trying to respond. We should also be able to filter based on listing status to quickly identify such reviews.
workaround: We have to attempt a response to trigger an error then we can apply a Review Label to it.

summary: Add "Where You Live Matters" to publisher network
description:
Hi,

This request came from a very small customer and Kate N mentioned she has not seen this company requested before.  Just submitting for tracking purposes in case it comes up in the future and becomes a consideration.

Thanks,
Leigh
workaround: Suggesting they consider leveraging our APIs to provide location data to this website.

summary: Notification emails with large attachments
description:
Hi, we experienced an issue for a reseller that has a notification setup. Unfortunately, we’re unable to deliver the email because the attached report is too large and email servers can’t support sending or receiving something of that size. I’d like to request a workaround when files are so large, if we could include a link to a drive where recipients can download the report they need. 

Here’s the Techops ticket: [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12970|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12970] and here’s the Jira item: [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/NSYNC-3661|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/NSYNC-3661]



Thank you!

Liz
workaround: None, the emails simply fail to send at the moment.

summary: Listing Feature Slides
description:
Hi Team,

I’d like to request a few additional slides for the Listings Product Pitch deck to help AEs better communicate the value of Yext Listings. While I understand the importance of solution selling and the RON story, we need more tangible collateral to leave behind with prospects. 

These materials are often reviewed independently by decision-makers when evaluating Yext against competitors, and right now, we lack clear, feature-based slides that effectively convey what Listings offers and how we stand out.

Currently, we have feature-based slides in the Reviews and Social decks, but we’re missing them in the Listings deck. Some key features that would be valuable to include are:

* *Listings Overview Screen* – highlighting its value
* *Listing Escalating* – how this process works and benefits the user
* *Un-Synced Listings Status Features* – demonstrating how we make it easy, transparent, and quick to fix
* *In-Platform Bulk Verification Readiness & BV Status* – showing our readiness and the status of bulk verification
* *Recommendations & Publisher-Required Fields* – emphasizing these features for ease of use
* *Bulk Edits* – while this is technically content, it’s highly relevant for Listings
* *Scheduled Updates* – again, technically content, but highly relevant for Listings

These slides would help AEs present a clearer, more defined picture of what Listings is, how we’re different, and the key features that set us apart.

Thanks for considering this request!

Best regards,
Micah
workaround: make slides manually

summary: Allow Multiple Publishers in Create: Post request
description: Creating a post for multiple publishers via the API is currently not supported. Since this isn't supported, to mimic this behavior multiple Post: Create requests need made in parallel to post a single post to the necessary publishers. Besides requiring multiple requests, managing and consolidating the post IDs and viewing these in platform becomes a bit of a mess. It would be helpful to allow an array of publishers to be provided in the request to generate multiple posts with a single call.
workaround: Make multiple individual requests

summary: Yext Pulse (Yext Advisor Feature)
description:
Goal is to get monthly insights from customers on their digital marketing focus areas through a lightweight pop-up chat feature. Customers would answer targeted questions such as “What are your top KPIs right now?” or “What challenges are you facing in digital marketing today?” The goal is to align better with their goals, provide actionable tips and identify opportunities for recommending relevant products. 

This addresses a key gap in understanding customer priorities *proactively* and drives engagement with the platform. Customers have evolving focus areas (and trouble keeping up with search trends) and this tool will allow Yext to anticipate needs rather than react to support tickets or issues. Bonus: they can be honest with their responses given it’s not directly with sales. 


*Features*:

* *Trigger Frequency*: Pop-up appears once a month during active sessions, ensuring non-intrusiveness.
* *Dynamic Questions*: Rotate between questions like:
** _What are your top KPIs right now?_
** _Which reports do you find most useful?_
** _What are the biggest challenges in your digital marketing today?_
* *Pre-filled Suggestions*: Offer quick-response buttons for common answers (e.g., "Improving local SEO," "Increasing review volume").
* *Real-Time AE Alerts*: Automatically flag responses for AEs to review in their dashboards
* *Integration*:
** Embed the popup as part of the *Yext Advisor Chat* interface
** Responses populate somewhere or alert AEs (not sure 🙂 )
workaround:
AEs manually engage customers via emails or calls to gather this information but this is time-intensive and not scalable especially when 90% of the calls are support or publisher related. There is no automated or in-app way to collect these insights regularly and in a structured way. This would benefit AEs, marketing and our data science team by gathering the changes in focus areas / trends. 

Screenshot I took was built using bolt

summary: Fetching review invitations by date in the API
description: Clients want to be able to pull and filter review invitations by specific timestamps if possible. I believe we track the info in platform as I can see it within the Review Invitations breakdown page but I do not currently think there is a way outside of pulling this in the API as it’s not an available dimension.
workaround: No workaround, just pulling everything all at once

summary: Request to add date account was linked to Entity History for: Google, FB, LinkedIn, TrustPilot, etc.
description:
Our entity history/activity log would be so useful if we could also record the date that each of the publishers with a Linked Account was connected in Yext. It would also be good if it logged when the account was no longer linked/the connection switched to Unavailable. Please add this for all the publishers that require a linked account:

Google

Facebook

Instagram

Truspilot

[Booking.com|http://Booking.com]

Any others I’m forgetting. 



Thanks, 

Liz
workaround: There's no way to see this data in Storm, only visible to users in admin.

summary: Tracker in platform and notification when a LinkedIn account token needs to be re-set
description:
When we launched LinkedIn for posting, we were told that connecting the account would give a 1 year token and that the account would need to be re-linked each year to keep the active sync. At the moment there’s nowhere in the Partner Portal that gives visibility into which customers have connected LinkedIn, or how much time is remaining on their token/when they need to be re-linked. This is a request for visibility at the sub-account level and the Partner Portal level into how much time remains, and also a request for a notification that can be setup so the user receives a reminder when there’s 30 days left before they need to re-link their LinkedIn account. This visibility is essential to complete the functionality for this LinkedIn feature that launched in Summer '22. 

Thank you, 

Liz
workaround: None at the moment

summary: Hide Report Builder access from Analytics for custom user roles that don't have Report Builder access
description:
Hi team,



Creating a product request for this issue based on the feedback in this techops ticket:

[https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12977|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12977]



Summary of issue:

Basically our partner Spotzer created a custom user role for all their customers:

[https://www.yext.com/admin2/roles/8265|https://www.yext.com/admin2/roles/8265]

For this custom role all permissions to the Report Builder have been removed, the users should only have access to the normal Analytics dashboard. Except when navigating to the Analytics dashboard the users can click on the 3 dots and select “View in Reports”. And since this custom user can’t access the Report Builder the link will go to a 403 error page. I made this quick video for what I mean: [https://www.loom.com/share/947b145f244d429789a4bd19e835f3aa?from_recorder=1&focus_title=1|https://www.loom.com/share/947b145f244d429789a4bd19e835f3aa?from_recorder=1&focus_title=1|smart-link] . Here is an user and account you can test this with: [https://www.yext.com/admin2/users/15264367/access|https://www.yext.com/admin2/users/15264367/access].



Product request:

Remove the “View in Reports” option for Analytics dashboard metrics for users that don’t have permission/access to the Report Builder feature.
workaround: None, custom users without report builder access are redirected to a 403 error page.

summary: Review Gen on Glassdoor and Indeed
description:
Hi team,

MUSC shared that [rep.com|http://rep.com] can generate reviews on glassdoor and indeed and that they are sharing major success on those sites. Since MUSC has some locations that have pretty poor ratings on these publishers could we consider this functionality? MUSV is considering a review gen pilot with Yext.



Thank you!
W
workaround: Generating outside of Yext on these pubs.

summary: Make the Tasks Inbox customizable
description:
When reviewing a Review Approval task in the platform, a user is able to view the entity’s name and ID from the platform. For large brands where a location’s external name is consistent (Eg. Tesco Express, Haagen-Dazs), businesses often use an internal name to identify a location, and not the unique ID/Store Code. 



This feature isn’t just specific to the review approval task here. But one can imagine how different businesses may want to add/remove some details from the default tasks view.
workaround:
* Navigate to the entity in platform, find the internal name field and see the information.
* Take an export of locations and work with that on the side.

summary: User Permissions by Entity Type
description:
Hello Team,

For my client Accor we have had the use case a couple of times where permissions to profile fields differ by Entity Type.

For example the User Group “Hotel Manager” is not allowed to have edit access to the Website URL field on the Hotel Entity Type, but needs to have edit Access on The restaurant and Location Entity Type. 

We have considered create a custom user role but after discussing this with the Concierge Team, this was a big piece of work as we would need to modify all the past users, which is high risk.
workaround: Giving access to a CF on the Restaurant and Location Entity and building a Y2Y connector that synchs just that field to the website url.

summary: Display an error if all Hour intervals are not mapped
description: If a customer does not map all hour intervals they’ll get an entity creation error. The mapping takes 14 distinct fields (21 if you do closed as well) so it’s tough to keep track if it’s mapped properly. Would be ideal if it displayed an error message if only some of the hour fields were mapped.
workaround: Manual review and dry runs.

summary: Include text name for related entities in exports vs just having the entity ID
description:
Hi Team!

When the team is looking to pull data for related entities it only includes the entity ID vs the name. Having the name would be helpful for ease of use of the data especially when the exports need to be used by other vendors like Geonetric.



Thank you!

W
workaround: Mapping the IDs to the names

summary: Reviews and Reports Filtering for Response User
description:
We cannot filter for Response Users in the platform. This is a problem for both clients and for Services. 

In Hilton’s case. they utilize Yext Managed Review Response to manage the majority of their Google reviews in NA and LATAM. They have some very active hotel managers on Google reviews, and much more activity on [Booking.com|http://Booking.com] reviews. Hilton would like to be able to identify when/which of their hotel managers are responding to reviews. 

For Yext MRR, we need to label reviews that are managed by us vs them. There is no efficient way to do this because we cannot filter by Response User.
workaround: We can see the user attribution for responses in Review Monitoring if the response was posted in Yext. There is also a Response User column when exporting Reviews into Excel.

summary: Dynamic + Multiple Date + Entity Filters for Analytics Dashboards
description:
The client is looking for Analytics dashboards that can support reports with different date ranges. Currently, dashboards apply a blanket date range to all reports. The client would like a single dashboard that has reports with recent history (last 7 days) and reports with all time data. There is no way to do this now except with separate dashboards. If you create a report in Report Builder for the last 12 months and add it to a dashboard set for the last 7 days, that report automatically falls under the dashboard filters.

Ideally, each report in a dashboard can have its own date and entity filters so that clients can compare data from different periods and between different groups. 

Additionally, the entity filter only allows you to select for entities, but select against entities, i.e. clients would like to do things like Folder is A and B, AND Label is NOT X and Y. 

Lux is a $2M ACV client that has been with Yext for 11 years. We are currently implementing a massive rip-and-replace Reputation Management solution from [Rep.com|http://rep.com/]. Review management, including analytics and various types of notifications, is essential for the client and thousands of users who are now moving over to Yext.
workaround:
We can only duplicate dashboards and apply different filters to each

Weekly: [https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/insightsDashboard/211591|https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/insightsDashboard/211591]

All-time: [https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/insightsDashboard/211643|https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/insightsDashboard/211643]

summary: Analytics Dashboard Notifications Include Reports in the Email Body
description:
Many clients are unsatisfied with Analytics Notifications, particularly the attached PDF with a screenshot of the dashboard. They find it unhelpful and archaic. 

Raising this specifically for Luxottica who would like the reports to be within the body of the email.

Lux is a $2M ACV client that has been with Yext for 11 years. We are currently implementing a massive rip-and-replace Reputation Management solution from [Rep.com|http://Rep.com]. Review management, including analytics and various types of notifications, is essential for the client and thousands of users who are now moving over to Yext.
workaround:
We have this Analytics dashboard: [https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/insightsDashboard/211591|https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/insightsDashboard/211591]

This is the Analytics Notification: [https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/notifications/4435145|https://www.yext.com/s/427914/reports/notifications/4435145]

summary: Social posts - need only eligible/linked pubs to be visible or selectable to reduce confusion
description:
Hi, for ease of use, can we grey out publisher icons on the Social Post screen if we can’t actually post to those pubs because there isn’t an active sync? 

Here's an example: [https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/4263889|https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/4263889]
 
This account only has a Google account linked. However, when you go to create a social post, all the social pubs are available and selectable - is this a bug? We should only display the pubs that have a linked account, or at least only be able to select the publishers with a linked account. A post to Facebook would fail for this customer because they haven't linked an account, but I can select FB anyway on this screen (see screenshot). 

This problem affects all accounts and locations, even if there is only one location entity in the account. For instance, single-location businesses without any synced post publishers are experiencing this issue - please see additional screenshots. 

We’re launching the social eligibility API which does exactly this - can we please bring this simple feature into the platform as well? 

Thank you, 

Liz
workaround: Currently, someone can create a post and select all post publishers and they will only know it won't be published when they get an error after trying to submit their post.

summary: Translate Reviews Received
description:
Hi team,



This is a high importance functionality for Exxon:

"HIGH IMPORTANCE : The automatic translation in French of all the comments & reviews is mandatory for us, very helpful if we want to answer to all these comments received in a foreign language."
workaround: No workaround

summary: Need visibility into Booking.com linking status
description:
Currently the platform doesn’t have a button to initiate the setup/config for [Booking.com|http://booking.com/], it must be initiated with an external link. In addition, if the account is linked properly, there is no way to confirm the status of the connection - there is no status on the Publishers page, on the Linked Accounts section in Content or in the Linked Accounts section in Account Management. There is simply no visibility anywhere to tell that there is an active (or not) connection with [Booking.com|http://booking.com/]. This is a poor customer experience - please help! We really should be able to have a button on the Publishers tab to initiate the setup like we do for linked accounts such as Facebook, Google, LinkedIn.

I’ve included screenshots of the Publishers screen, filtered to show the publishers that require linking, the [Booking.com|http://booking.com/] screen on which no action can be taken, the Entity screen from Content with no option to add [Booking.com|http://booking.com/], and the Account Settings> Linked Accounts screen with no option for [Booking.com|http://booking.com/]

Thank you,

Liz
workaround: None

summary: Task Completion outside of Yext Platform
description:
Melon Local wants to send a suggested Review Response (generated with Generative Review Response) via email to their end clients, and have the end client accept (or edit & accept) or reject the suggested response via email without logging into the platform.

Melon Local’s client base is 100% insurance agents and they were recently told that it goes against compliance to have 3rd party vendors respond to reviews or post socially.  There are 3 other US based Resellers (totaling $600k in ACV) that are also looking for this functionality specifically for insurance agents. 

Thanks!

Kim
workaround: A workaround would be to give the end client a very limited access user role, however, our platform is not truly whitelabeled, so that is not an option for most Resellers.

summary: Asterisk for Entity ID field when creating new entity
description: The client wants to have the entity ID field marked as mandatory (with an asterisk) when creating a new entity in Content.
workaround: N/A

summary: Automatically flag spammy reviews for removal
description: We are requesting the ability to automatically flag spam reviews, possibly with an in-platform feature where a client can add specific terms, or possibly in admin for Yext to control, including terms like “WhatsApp,” since spam reviews typically contain that term. The client here has received spammy first-party reviews and would like to spend less time manually flagging these for removal.
workaround: Manually flagging these reviews when notified that they have been posted

summary: Restaurant Guru
description:
Accor is currently using Yext to increase the number of restaurant listings.

They mentioned Restaurant Guru as a publisher that is becoming more popular in Europe but it is currently not part of our network.
workaround: n/a

summary: Restaurant Guru
description: Accor Restaurants are requesting to be able to sync their data to the website [https://restaurantguru.com/|https://restaurantguru.com/|smart-link] and also get the reviews from it if possible. Can we try to get them in our publisher network ?
workaround: No workaround for now

summary: Review Approval Metrics
description: This client would like to have metrics for approved replies to reviews and the number of replies edited.
workaround: There are no metrics for approved replies to reviews and the number of replies edited.

summary: Filter Content by Date/Time of Entity Creation or Last Updated
description: The Church would like to be able to pull exports of their Event entities, based on the time/date that the entity was created.  This is particularly relevant to them because they want to be able to gauge the extent to which their end users are engaging in the Yext platform.  However, there is no way for them to get a filtered view of their content based on the date in which an entity was created
workaround: The closest workaround the church has been able to use for now has been to sort their Content dashboard by "last updated".  However, this doesn't give an accurate view of when events are actually created, as an entity "Update" can include content edits, etc.

summary: Setting Permissions Scopes based on Entity Type
description:
In order for the Church to maintain 55k of their Location and Event entities via management API, they have had to store both entity types in the same folder tree (all are stored under the “Areas” folder). This has thus far been non-negotiable as they do not believe they cannot maintain via management API without this folder structure.

This causes a problem from a user access perspective.  They need for some roles to ONLY have access to the Locations entities within the Areas folder tree.  However, there is no way to do this in Yext sustainably, as permissions can only be set based on folder/subfolder, or on an entity-by-entity basis.  



So ideally, we’d love for the role permissions to be segmented based on entity type as well- this would easily solve the problem for the Church
workaround: Thus far, the Church has had to simply live with allowing users to have access to both Event and Location entities in the folders for which they have access.  There isn't a way for them to handle the permissions user-by-user due to the volume of users and entities.

summary: Google Advanced Consent Mode
description:
DHL UK noticed a difference in analytics between GA4 and Yext analytics related to this report:

* Report: [https://www.yext.com/s/3741141/reports/68899?b=3741141|https://www.yext.com/s/3741141/reports/68899?b=3741141]
* Timeframe: 26 Aug - 22 Sept 2024

The client is seeing in GA4 15 download clicks, whereas in the Yext reports, we have 5 download clicks registered. We have investigated this with techops multiple times (see below) and confirmed that we do not track any analytics if a user rejects cookies. This explains why there are fewer clicks in Yext reports and we flagged to the client that even if a user rejects a cookie it seems Google is still tracking the clicks.

The client explained they are using Google Advanced Consent Mode and shared [*this guide*|https://www.tdmp.co.uk/insights/google-consent-mode-v2-everything-you-need-know#:~:text=This%20means%20that%20any,Browser ].

As per [*this guide*|https://www.tdmp.co.uk/insights/google-consent-mode-v2-everything-you-need-know#:~:text=This%20means%20that%20any,Browser], they are asking us to track non-personal data to understand how users interact with pages like:

* Device type
* Browser
* Conversion type
* Location (country)
* Time of conversion

Is this a feature we could support via enableYextAnalytics() function or in any other way?



Techops tickets for reference:

* [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12623|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12623]
* [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12753|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-12753]

summary: More comprehensive scrubbing for 1st party reviews
description:
Hi Team! 

MUSC uses Yext to ingest 1st party provider reviews for the purpose of publishing on their provider pages. As part of that process they need to review all the reviews in quarantine before they go live. This is a lot of work for the team given it is thousands of reviews. On a recent call Kendal (champ) shared that if we cannot figure out a way to make this review of the first party reviews more scalable they will go to press gainey for this functionality. She shared that their new CXO said that Press Gainey has a more comprehensive scrubbing functionality that would cut down on the work for them. Currently we have the reviews go through PHI scrubbing and also our system marks them for any inappropriate content but MUSC needs more to be removed automatically. Below Kendall shares what Press Gainey would be able to scrub for them and is asking for us to be able to do this as well.

“As we discussed in our meeting on Friday, here is the review criteria we are being asked to evaluate. We are being told by our new CXO that Press Ganey is following similar criteria for one of their clients:


Contains personal health information (PHI)
Profanity/vulgarity
Not related to MUSC
Advertising/spam/out of context (e.g. a comment about the survey itself, N/A, etc.)
Hateful/violent/inappropriate
Refers to a resident
Names a different provider, service, specialty, clinic, or department
Patient dismissed from the practice
Claims of malpractice, criminal activity, misconduct, or pending litigation
Irrelevant to provider or patient care (e.g. about billing, insurance, parking etc.)
Provider’s physical attributes (e.g. race, appearance, disability, English as second language, etc.)
Inaccurate as substantiated by evidence”



If we are not able to do this while we may not lose the monitoring and response business outright since they use it for 3rd party too but it will create a lot more risk in the account since PG will then be in the nest. It will open the door for them to then poach the 3rd party business and maybe even listings. I would prefer we just accomodate the request vs risk losing functionality to PG. That being said this is a business decidtion the Yext team needs to make. 



Thank you for the consideration,

W
workaround: Currently the reviews are scrubbed by our teams for PHI and foul language but that is not enough. We have also explored using workflows to distribute the work but Kendall does not have confidence in her teams to actually get the work done.

summary: "Confirm Sync" guardrails to the standard and holiday hours fields for GBP Listings
description:
Clients and implementation teams may often create new entities in Content without hours data, which then defaults the Standard Hours and Holiday Hours fields to "Specify Hours" = "false", which means any pre-existing GBP hours will be cleared out as soon as listings are opt in. Many accounts will not add hours upon initial implementation because it is not a required field, they plan to add it at a later date, and they can sometimes misunderstand the field’s behaviour as GBP was known to *_not_* clear out some fields with empty content if there is existing content in GBP. Many users will also not go as far as to research the field behaviors even if they were to be publicly documented (which it currently isn’t).

Having guardrails in place that ask users to confirm before opting in that they are aware their GBP hours will get cleared out with empty Yext data will prevent cases like an escalation we had with the account VVF Villages - 100004165 where they needed someone at Yext to help them recover their old GBP hours.
workaround: Pubops has to manually fetch historic data from GBP, which them services or support agents have to reformat to attempt a reupload either into Yext or directly into GBP. Example ticket thread: [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2081930|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2081930]

summary: Connector Configuration Labels / Versions

summary: External Bulk Move Accounts API Access
description:
Hibu ($3.9M) acquired RevLocal, and the RevLocal portal has been moved directly under Hibu. This is the current structure.

*Current Account Structure*

* Hibu
** RevLocal
*** Sub-account 1
*** Sub-account 2
*** Sub-account 3
*** etc.

As Hibu upgrades RevLocal’s sub-accounts to use additional Hibu services, they need the RevLocal sub-accounts migrated from the RevLocal sub-portal level to the Hibu parent account level.

*Desired Account Structure*

* Hibu
** *_Sub-account 1_*
** RevLocal
*** Sub-account 2
*** Sub-account 3
*** etc.

Hibu is currently sending in 1-2 tickets per week for the Support team to process these account transfer. Support has a processing time of ~2 business days, which prevents Hibu from being able to access their accounts under the main portal immediately after SKU upgrade. Support also has to provision the account to NONE in order for the Move Accounts tool to work. This freezing of provisioning causes the account to be in an undesirable state during the account transfer, and can result in additional tickets where the client thinks their account has been canceled. 

Ideally, Hibu would like to be able to manage their own account migrations so that (1) they don’t have to rely on Yext Support, (2) they can automate these account migrations via API, and (3) they can immediately access their accounts under the main Hibu portal post SKU upgrade.

Best,

Eden
workaround:
1. Hibu submits ticket to Support team with list of accounts.
2. Support provisions account to NONE
3. Support uses [https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/moveAccounts|https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/moveAccounts] tool
4. Support provisions account back to YAM

summary: NPS Score
description:
Our current listings client, [Palmetto Dunes|https://yext.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/0010y00001kzGo7AAE/view], utilizes Birdeye for reviews. When discussing the possibility of having them use us for reviews now that we can also do 3rd party review generation we learned that they rely heavily on the NPS score they get within Birdeye. summary: CHN + Yext | Feature Request: Predetermined Synonym List for Healthcare Listings to Boost SEO
description:
*Objective:*
To improve SEO for healthcare providers by integrating a pre-built list of common healthcare synonyms into Yext Listings. This will ensure listings appear in a wider range of search queries, increasing visibility and driving more accurate traffic. Our client wanted more direction and we currently don’t have a list of synonyms and they’re having to manually add.

----

h3. *Problem:*

Patients often use different terms for the same healthcare services (e.g., “doctor” vs. “physician,” or “heart doctor” vs. “cardiologist”). Without including all relevant synonyms, healthcare listings might not show up in search results, leading to reduced visibility and missed opportunities.

----

h3. *Proposed Solution:*

Yext should create an automated synonym list for healthcare listings that includes common, technical, and layman terms (e.g., “ENT” ↔ “Otolaryngologist,” “Stomach Doctor” ↔ “Gastroenterologist”). This feature would automatically optimize listings for various search terms without manual input from users.

----

h3. *Benefits:*

# *Improved SEO:* Listings will appear in more searches due to the broader range of terms.
# *Time-Saving:* Reduces the need for manual addition of synonyms.
# *Better Search Accuracy:* Patients can find relevant providers even with non-technical terms.
# *Competitive Edge:* Makes Yext a more attractive platform for healthcare providers.

----

h3. *Next Steps:*

* Collaborate with healthcare experts to curate the synonym list.
* Implement and test the feature.
* Regularly update synonyms based on evolving trends.

This feature will help healthcare providers optimize their listings more effectively, improving search rankings and patient engagement.
workaround: Currently for an idea, I had exported some terms from our HC taxonomy for them to use that was recommended by product to do in the meantime.

summary: In-Platform Response Status for Booking.com Reviews
description:
Yext recently launched a Reviews integration with [Booking.com|http://Booking.com]. All review responses are subject to approval by Booking, and there is a notice in the platform indicating it may take 24-48 hours. Booking only permits one response submission. No removal, no edits. We also learned recently that Booking prohibits providing phone numbers in responses.

We have observed a lot of inconsistency in how response posting and pending approvals are surfaced in our platform.

# We submit a response and it instantly posts in-platform, but we know it’s been sent for approval. Checking the listing, we can see that the response is not there. The response nevertheless remains in platform.
# We submit a response and it instantly posts in-platform, but we know it’s been sent for approval. Sometimes if we refresh, the response is no longer there. If we attempt to post a response again, it fails, suggesting it is being blocked by a pre-existing response submission.
# We submit a response, it gets rejected, but it appears in the platform. We cannot submit a new response.
workaround:
Hilton's digital marketing agency has access to their Booking account and can view response approvals. They or the hotel manager can resubmit a new response directly through Booking. This is a bad look for us as the intended solution is for us to manage their reviews.

This is the ideal functionality in platform:
1. We submit a response and it appears in platform as "Pending approval from Booking.com", similar to how we see responses pending approval when they're submitted by users with "response with approvals only" permissions. 
2. We have the approval status updated in platform, receiving an update from Booking. 
3. We have the ability to clear the rejected response and submit a new one for approval.

summary: Analytics > Report Builder > Dimensions || Enable Time Dimensions to based on Rolling Dates vs fixed week/month/year periods
description:
Problem:

This recently came up via a Zendesk ticket ([here|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2079176]) where a client was confused because our data was showing a dropoff at the end of a range for a report using the “Weeks (Time)” Dimension. The end date was 7/31/24, a Wednesday, so because the date was not a Sunday, the data showed as 0 for that range, as expected, since the full “week” (as we define a week as Monday-Sunday) was not included in the report. 

While our system _does_ auto-correct if a date falls on a non-Monday for the start date or a non-Sunday for the end date (i.e. when I chose 7/31/24 the end date would show 8/4/24), there is no explanation for why so clients will think this is a glitch and go back and select what they believe to be the right date (in this case, 7/31/24) instead - and when they do so a second time, the end date does not autocorrect. Then, when they see the dropoff, not only did they think there was a glitch when selecting the dates, but now they think there’s a glitch due to the dropoff as well. This results in a confusing experience that degrades confidence in our analytics tool.



Proposed Solution: Allow the option for any Time-related dimensions to be configured on a rolling basis vs a fixed basis. For Example, when looking at “weeks” for this specific instance, we’d group this range by 7 days as opposed to if the start date falls on a Monday or a Wednesday. In addition, if the range did not evenly divide by 7, (i.e. maybe the last group has 5 days instead of 7), that is not an issue and the correct data for those dates still shows as opposed to dropping down to zero.
workaround:
There are technically two different workarounds, but one is only partial and one requires cumbersome data manipulation and steps outside of Yext to fulfill:

Workaround 1 (partial): Instead of using the Weeks (Time) dimension, clients can use the Days (Time) dimension to not experience the dropoff. While this does account for the full range of data, the visual readout is not as clear as there are then 7x the datapoints in the visual model vs using Weeks.

Workaround 2 (cumbersome): In addition to using the Days (Time) dimension mentioned above, clients can then download the .xlsx or .csv file and manipulate the data to show WoW comparisons. This requires additional work on the client's part, taking them out of Yext to get the report they need, and still does not solve for the initial confusion encountered by the client.

summary: Scheduling Crawler Start Time
description:
We have had a significant escalation from Fidelity, as one of their crawlers (which pulls content in their search experience) keeps running into peak trading hours, causing major functionality and performance issues on their website. The issue stems from the Yext Crawler's limited functionality, as it only allows scheduling based on the crawler's end time, not its start time. Despite them going in and manually running the crawler at midnight, given that run times change, the end time keeps creeping forward over time, which then impacts the start time of the next daily crawl. 

This results in the crawl time moving into peak operational time and means they are considering turning off the crawler completely. However, this is not an option as the crawler is currently a critical data feed for their main [PI search experience|https://www.fidelity.co.uk/search/].

We have an upsell and 3-year contract renewal out for signature currently so the timing is very sensitive.
workaround:
The only way to ensure that the crawler runs outside of business hours is for someone from their team to go into Yext in the middle of the night and hit 'crawl now', which isn't a sustainable solution. 

Long-term, we are working with them to transition to an API connector. However, this will take significant resource and time on their side and we need an interim solution.

summary: Suggested Questions Feature in Generative Answers Beta for Community Health Network
description:
*Problem:* Patients in the *Community Health Network* often face challenges navigating healthcare information, leading to missed follow-up questions critical for their understanding of treatment options, next steps, or preventive care. Many struggle with medical terms and don't always know the right questions to ask after receiving initial information.

*Solution:* Integrate a *‘suggested questions’* feature into the Generative Answers Beta platform used by Community Health Network. This feature would generate context-aware follow-up questions based on the returned content, guiding patients through their care journey and ensuring they receive comprehensive information.

*Example:*

* *Query:* “What are the treatment options for Type 2 diabetes?”
* *Answer:* Lifestyle changes, medications, insulin therapy, surgery.
* *Suggested Questions:*
** “What are the side effects of Metformin?”
** “When is insulin necessary?”
** “How can I improve my diet for diabetes?”

*Key Benefits for Community Health Network:*

# *Empowers patients* across the network to ask relevant follow-up questions, improving health engagement.
# *Closes information gaps* by guiding patients through their care journey, ensuring they get the right answers at the right time.
# *Increases health literacy* among patients by simplifying complex medical concepts into actionable next steps.
# *Enhances doctor-patient communication,* resulting in more informed discussions during appointments, improving care quality.
# *Personalizes care* for Community Health Network’s diverse patient base by tailoring follow-up questions to specific conditions.
# *Reduces patient anxiety* by offering clarity on conditions and treatments, helping patients feel more in control of their care.
# *Encourages early interventions* by prompting preventive care questions, improving long-term patient outcomes across the network.

*Conclusion:* For *Community Health Network*, a ‘suggested questions’ feature in Generative Answers Beta would improve patient understanding, reduce uncertainty, and empower patients to take a proactive role in their healthcare journey. This leads to more informed, engaged patients, improved communication with providers, and better care outcomes across the network.
workaround: N/A at this time. The org is wondering how they can do this since they currently testing for the GAB.

summary: Multiple Metrics on one Dashboard
description:
Our TA Operating client was trying to make a central dashboard where he can view all the metrics that are important to him, cut different ways depending on the metric.  Unfortunately, despite being able to build all the necessary reports, when he tries to add them to the dashboard, all the report filtering is overridden by the dashboard filtering, so he can’t get the views he wants.  For example, in the Review dashboard he was trying to build, he wanted to see his overall Star Rating and rating distribution for all locations, then underneath wanted to break out the same metric by business unit (Gas Stations, Restaurants, Truck Service).  In the reports he built, he filtered by folder to get to the biz unit breakout, but when he added those reports to the dashboard, the folder filtering went away and all three biz unit metrics were the same.  Here’s a link to the dashboard he built so you can see what I mean by the metrics being the same:  [https://www.yext.com/s/1325170/reports/insightsDashboard/206801|https://www.yext.com/s/1325170/reports/insightsDashboard/206801]

This is the first SaaS company I’ve worked for where the Dashboards had to have one unified filter.  The client was also surprised to learn of this limitation, and made a big deal of showing me how he can have different metrics, cut different ways, in other platforms he uses (e.g. Google Analytics).  He stated this ease of use as a consideration in the renewal they have coming up in April.
workaround:
His primary workaround has been to take screen shots of each report and drop them into a powerpoint deck, which is not a very elegant solution.

Other departments within TA will be purchasing Tableau licenses, so he's hoping he can get a license too and hook our data into the report he builds so he doesn't have to spend so much time screen shotting reports.

summary: Reviews API Endpoint Improvement (Rating Distribution)
description:

More info on the topic is discussed here for full transparency: [https://yext.slack.com/archives/C0666T500PN/p1726691221668859?thread_ts=1726685891.162349&cid=C0666T500PN|https://yext.slack.com/archives/C0666T500PN/p1726691221668859?thread_ts=1726685891.162349&cid=C0666T500PN|smart-link]
workaround: So far we've been having them query the review endpoint and filter by rating. That means on every page load, the API is going to get hammered with individual requests as it will look to pull back info for multiple ratings and multiple entities

summary: Change default set up for display fields to not display all data
description:
The current default behavior of Search API display fields if they are not configured in the backend is to return all fields on an entity (documentation here [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/modules/search130-vertical-level-config/02-searchable-display-fields/?target=display-fields|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/modules/search130-vertical-level-config/02-searchable-display-fields/?target=display-fields|smart-link] ). This recently posed a serious security issue to Utility Warehouse, which is detailed in this post-incident report [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1BjMn47KoWPNjdstm4UWGnNvFBR7zDoY2ffI1abmMqCI/edit|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1BjMn47KoWPNjdstm4UWGnNvFBR7zDoY2ffI1abmMqCI/edit|smart-link] . For European clients who are more sensitive to exposing data, having this Display Fields default behavior presents serious risk. 

*Could the default for Display Fields be changed to show no fields by default?* This will prompt search developers to be more conscious of what data they are choosing to expose in the frontend Search API and require them to configure the fields for Display Fields in advance. If this is not possible, then there should be an explicit warning added on the Display Fields configuration screen (example [{color:#0747a6}here{color}|https://www.yext.com/s/3688573/search2/experiences/partner-search/verticals/locations/displayFields?version=71&verticalKey=all&locale=en_GB]) that clearly communicates the default behavior. It currently states that “The API response will only return fields that have been configured in display fields.” but this contradicts the statement in the documentation that “By default, the Search API response returns all fields on the result entity, but not any fields of related entities.” Thank you!
workaround: Configure the display fields, although this is not presented as a required part of search configuration and could be easily missed if not specified by the client.

On this page, request logs are truncated (see attached screenshot). This makes it difficult to conduct a thorough investigation of these logs. Full logs would be preferable.

summary: Reviews Roadmap
description:
Hi team,
I hope all is well.

I have a client who would like the following changes:

# No replies for deleted or updated reviews: If a review is deleted or updated after a Zendesk ticket has been created, a reply from the Zendesk ticket is no longer possible and users will receive an error message in the ticket. In these cases, reviews must be edited directly in Yext. The desire is to be able to respond to updated reviews from Zendesk and to be informed about deleted reviews.
# Workflows can only be edited in Zendesk after re-authentication: Existing configurations cannot be edited without the need for new authentication. In practice, this is annoying for admins and feels like a bug because the configurations are not initially displayed.
# New trigger when adding workflows: As soon as a new workflow is created in Yext, Yext creates a trigger in Zendesk for feedback. In the first workflow, this is as expected. This also happens when additional workflows are set up, which causes users in Zendesk to receive multiple replies from Yext for replying to reviews. Admins must therefore always remember to delete triggers that are not needed.
# Zendesk tickets are created on behalf of the admins: Zendesk tickets from Yext Reviews are created on behalf of the admin (requester) who created or last edited the configuration. For agents in Zendesk, it initially looks like the review comes from the admin. The client is currently using a workaround in the form of a function user in whose name the authentication is carried out. Could this be defined in the workflow in the sense of a fallback - in the event that this data should not be taken over from Yext (GDPR problem)?

Many Thanks
workaround: n/A

summary: Support for Workflows to Connectors/APIs
description:
It would be super beneficial for supporting workflows with connectors or APIs to help customers in several product areas. Listings, reviews, social, etc. 

For example, you can set up a workflow where an event is triggered, goes to connectors, and could do something like: 

* Send a slack notification to a team or person
* Create a Jira ticket for a team or person
* Create an Asana task for a team or person
workaround: NA

summary: Yext Analytics Extension
description:
When looking to debug pages that are built on Yext to see if the pixel is firing, you need to know how to do things like go into the network tab and search for the API request that is going out for the analytics event type that is firing (pageview, cta clicks etc.). We should make it easier for anyone building on Yext to easily debug pages and the analytics events by creating a nice extension. For example, I was looking at the Snowplow extension which does a really nice job of showing you analytics events - [https://files.slack.com/files-pri/TT8ARFBFY-F07LF3S3FD4/screenshot_2024-09-09_at_4.16.45___pm.png|https://files.slack.com/files-pri/TT8ARFBFY-F07LF3S3FD4/screenshot_2024-09-09_at_4.16.45___pm.png|smart-link] 



Yext Services built out something called the Yextension - the problem is this doesn’t work on client built or partner built experiences. This would be great to productify for anyone building on Yext and make our software even more user friendly.
workaround: You can use the developer tools within the inspect tool on browsers. This is not a must have, but a nice to have to help make the product more accessible to users!

summary: Ability to edit date and time for a scheduled social post
description: Fonecta needs the ability to edit date and time for a scheduled social post, in addition to being able to edit the content.
workaround: The workaround is quite tedious, they'd have to delete the scheduled post and create a new one from scratch with the content, settings, etc.

summary: In-platform notification
description:
Accor mentioned to us the lack of in-platform notifications where users would get notification when a listing in unsynced for example, the actions that need to be taken etc, some recommendations being sent that, notifications about google updates, etc.

Anais is member of the CAB and apparently she mentioned this during the meeting as well other clients. She also mentioned that most of our competitors have this functionality
workaround: No workaround for now, it needs to be done by the account team

summary: Zillow Listings Sync
description: PNC Mortgage would like to be able to better manage listings on Zillow. Right now, Zillow moves MLOs between brands based on the NMLS directory. However, it doesn’t update the branding so a PNC MLO can have Bank of America content on their Zillow listing. Ideally, they’d like to manage this via us.
workaround: Directly updating these. They know Zillow deprecated this API ability.

summary: Only generating reviews for providers that have BOTH webhook location id (department ID) and NPI filled out
description:
Hi Team, 



This request is really for NYP, WCM, and CUIMC and is a high priority request. We were told on our call today that this would be a blocker for WCM launching on review generation. 



Basically we need a way for clients on legacy pricing to exclude providers from the review generation program. Our thought was that if we don’t have the webhooks location ID present then that could be a good signal to not have the platform generate reviews for that provider. However if the provider only practices at one location then it will go solely based on NPI. We need it to be set up so the system requires BOTH NPI and department ID for our solution to work. 



Can this be done please? Feel free to reach out if there are any questions



Thank you!
W
workaround: We are not sure of a work around at this time and it would be a blocker for us to launch WCM and severely disappoint the NYP/CUIMC teams if we do not have a way for them to exclude specific providers from the program. This cannot be done in epic as that is sent based on department. Also the epic team and the redox team should no be managing a one off list of this data. It needs to be stored in Symplr and fed into Yext that way so that the client has full visibility and control at all times.

summary: Implementing Reviews from additional Publishers
description:
The client would like to be able to manage reviews for multiple new publishers: Better Business Bureau (BBB), WalletHub, ConsumerAffairs, PissedConsumer and Deposit Accounts.

As they can’t integrate them with Yext, they have to individually check each publisher and manage their reviews from multiple sites.

I could not find a workaround for this; I did not work with someone from Product or the SME team to address this.
workaround: NA

summary: Branding review gen URLs in SMS messages

Hi team, 

NYP has reached out asking to brand the urls included in their SMS messages, for example [nyp.leavefeedback.app|http://nyp.leavefeedback.app/] rather than just [leavefeedback.app|http://leavefeedback.app/].  



I was told this is not possible today but think it would be something alot of our clients would be interested in. 

Thank you!
W

summary: Redirected pages can be detected with connector for the deletion
description:
The client implement their closing pages within the site to redirect to the index page. Currently those entities cannot be deleted via connector and remain, and those old entities will continue to be displayed in Search. Therefore, the client requested the following function that allow the connector to detect redirects.

* To be able to obtain the URL at the time of obtaining the HTML separately from the URL to be crawled
* To be able to detect on the connector side that redirect has occurred (both JS redirect on the browser side and 301/302 redirects on the server side), retain that as data, and control that accordingly.
workaround: They are adding the target URL to be the blacklist, etc. if existing content within their site is redirected.

summary: Grid Ranking Ability - Search Tracker
description:
My client, CVS Veterinary PLC ($138K ACV) have requested our search tracker to measure map pack presence using a grid ranking tool a la Bright Local or Local Falcon vs. a singular pin drop location. 



[https://www.brightlocal.com/local-seo-tools/rankings/local-search-grid/|https://www.brightlocal.com/local-seo-tools/rankings/local-search-grid/|smart-link] 



[https://www.localfalcon.com/|https://www.localfalcon.com/]

summary: Ability to remove fields that have been added as displayFields in the Search backend from previous configurations
description: Currently when fields are added to the displayFields property in the Search backend, they cannot be removed and all the previously added fields will display. This is not an ideal user experience and can be unintuitive as well as inefficient. It would be great to add a feature to allow developers building on search to purge previous configuration versions or remove display fields that were previously configured.
workaround: Recreating the search experience by copying the JSON and then fixing the display fields that are added

summary: Competitive Analysis Tool
description:
The client (reseller) reached out to us about a project consisting in enhancing the capabilities of our competitive analysis tool. The project will focus on Google only and not include Facebook. The details related to the project are compiled [here |https://docs.google.com/document/d/1U6Mf5T8VkZZHyU37t2KZP91TPkJxkGRE/edit]and would consist in making the Competitive analysis tool dynamic so the user can select a service area & a specific keyword (both pulling from the related entity in the KG) and see the Google tile updated with what the associated Google search (Location/Service Area + Keyword) would return.

Please note that this would be enabled for all Hibu sub-accounts
workaround: None since the user would need to get out of the Yext platform to get the results they would like to see

summary: More automation in License Management -> saved Filter auto removal after 8 weeks
description:
Spar wants to automate the process of creating new stores / removing old ones.

We discussed several ways and think that the auto assignment via savedFilter works best for them. If we can add an option to say “Remove license after the field has been set to no after x weeks” would really help. 

Creating locations works really well: Create new entity with key data, apply a template, and set the field for the savedFilter - Done.

Not so with closing locations: they send an update via API, apply a “Closed” template and check from time to time the ones set to “Closed: yes” and are going to set the “Live on Listings” field to “no” manually.
workaround:
As a manual workaround would be to check closing dates of the store and set a scheduledUpdate for the "Live on Listings" field that is being used for the savedFilter.

This works, but is a manual effort.

The way through API would be: Call the API, see what license packs have been assigned, create several API calls with a dynamic close that (today + 6 weeks) to schedule the license assignment. Which, according to their it department, seems very complicated

summary: Notifications for licence consumption
description:
Hi team,



It would be great if we could have notifications set for licence packs (in a similar way to how we do for capacity based products like API calls, page views and entities)



Use case is CubeSmart who ran out of licences this month but had no idea. They want to be proactively notified when they are running low and there isn’t a way we can do this (to my knowledge) [https://www.yext.com/s/3230091/licenses/subscriptions/licensePack|https://www.yext.com/s/3230091/licenses/subscriptions/licensePacks]



Thanks in advance for your thoughts

Laura
workaround:
I don't think there's a proactive workaround

As a workaround, I can set up an automated export of “open = yes” “licence = no” file, but this would be reactive and too late for new openings.

Best,

summary: Opting listings in based on a date field in content
description: This more around content, listings, and even connectors. But again with Papa Johns, they have a ton of new locations that are Coming Soon. Since the only real publishers that allow coming soon are Google and FB, they have to remember to opt in the rest of the publisher network for specific new locations. Therefore, it would be amazing for some type of automation to exist either in content, the listing dash, or connectors, where we could say “if open date = today, opt in on all publishers”.
workaround: Manually remembering to opt in OR having support do it

summary: Allow transformations to be conditional on a date being equal to "Today"
description: For Papa Johns, they have locations that are not open in Yext where they have “Coming Soon” content filled out to be able to push to the listings publishers as well as their pages. Although, they find it meticulous to have to go and schedule an update based on the various open dates of their locations. I submitted a similar request for scheduling bulk updates [here|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/1102/PFR-1965?created=true], but this is a little bit of a separate request as another way we could accomplish this idea is that if connectors understand within transformations what the current day is. So being able to say “if open date field = today”, apply the transform.
workaround: Manually scheduling updates

summary: Include the ability to schedule an update based on a date field / bulk schedule updates based on a date field
description: For Papa Johns, they have locations that are not open in Yext where they have “Coming Soon” content filled out to be able to push to the listings publishers as well as their pages. Although, they find it meticulous to have to go and schedule an update based on the various open dates of their locations. Ideally, they could bulk schedule an update for new locations that hinges on the open date field of the entity. I think overall this is a great use case for a ton of our clients with coming soon locations that do not have an ETL/API updating these locations.
workaround: No real workaround today. You have to manually schedule the update on an entity by entity basis.

summary: Reviews Status on the Daily Reviews Notification Spreadsheet
description:
Hello team!

This client here - [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2048695|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2048695] - has a [*Yext - Google Reviews*|https://www.yext.com/s/4141956/reviews/notifications/4406813] *Notification* through which they receive a Daily Spreadsheet with their Reviews.

What they actually want is that the spreadsheet contains a column with their Reviews' Statuses (Live, Quarantined, Removed). 

Currently, there is no option in the platform to export the Reviews Statuses.

Client notes: _We are currently working on optimizing our data analysis and would greatly benefit from having the review status included in the export files. This addition would enable us to filter and analyze reviews more efficiently within our BI tools. We need the Status column in the automated Notification export file._
workaround:
Exported all the reviews and manually created a column with Review Statuses (Live, Quarantined, Removed) - Please, see file attached.

The client mentioned this is not a solution on the long run and require this to be automated.

Oliver

summary: Improve Reseller Scan Matching Logic
description:
Currently the Reseller Scan relies on this matching logic, which is prone to deliver inaccurate results. This has become a significant pain point for one of our Hibu day to days (Karen Boas - Manager, Systems Introductions).

For example, under the current logic, these 2 listings are flagged as duplicates of one another. 

* Rocket City Movers of Huntsville / 103 Spenryn Dr. / Madison, AL 35758 / (256) 337-6888
* Rocket City Pool & Spas / 103 Intercom Dr., Suite A / Madison, AL 35758 / (256) 251-3089

This is a *roadblock in the Hibu sales process*, and we are unable to manually fix the scan results, which results in a lot of frustration for the Reselller.

Current matching logic here: [https://wiki.yext.to/index.php/Duplicate_Search|https://wiki.yext.to/index.php/Duplicate_Search]
workaround: NA

summary: Automated Review Response
description: Client wants to be able to configure Automated Review Response to only fire during specific hours so their team can respond during online hours and they turn it over to automated response when they are offline.

summary: Add Sorting Logic to Content Delivery GeoSearch
description:
The Content Delivery endpoint indicates that it supports GeoSearch and Sorting, however these two conventions can’t be combined to sort geosearched responses by distance. This is not clearly indicated in our documentation and is generally expected behavior when retrieving location type data. 

Recommend adding distance to the geosearch response and sorting functionality for this endpoint.
workaround: N/A
