summary: In Platform escalation tickets - change text
description:
Hello everyone,

I hope you’re doing well.

We have noticed that now the in platform escalation tickets via ZD are coming in with a public reply instead of an internal note (example: [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2040337|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2040337]). 

Unfortunately in the public reply there is an Admin link the client will see and doesn’t have access to (screenshot attached) and shouldn’t be able to see at all.

We should adjust the public comment Body to reflect the below information so its more public-facing for customers.

---------------------- 

_Yext Listing Escalation_

_Thank you for escalating the below issue. Our technical support team will review further and follow up with you via this ticket directly._

* _Entity ID: <ID and Storm Link>_

* _Publisher Name: <Name and Link to Storm Listings Page>_

* _Other fields customer asked to fill out depending on modal selection_

_Additional Information: <text user added>_
workaround: no

summary: Adding additional tie break behavior for inferred filters for healthcare taxonomy specifically
description:
For a healthcare related search like “Urgent Care Center near me”, would it be possible for the inferred filter to trigger both “Urgent Care” and “Care Center” separately? Right now only “Urgent Care” is triggered which eliminates any results under the “Care Center” filter. This feature request entails investigating any additional tie break that can be added to inferred filters where this is the case. 

Relevant Slack Thread: [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1724101203206129|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1724101203206129|smart-link]
workaround: Currently, the search algorithm uses a greedy approach for inferred filters where it will choose a filter based on a few things, like number of tokens and typos (the more token matches in the filter the more likely it is to choose that filter, and vice versa with typos).

summary: Allowing the built in hours field to be filtered by open and closed
description: Right now, there is no way to filter based on the hours of a location (whether it’s closed or open). We can only filter by “Reopen Date”. This would be useful for creating saved filters.
workaround: No workaround - using different fields to filter for closed vs. open

summary: Being able to use any photo for review gen sms vs just the brand image
description:
Hi Team,

Columbia would like to be able to use the provider headshot vs the brand image in their sms messages. This is not possible today as you have to use whatever is in the logo field. 



Best,
W
workaround: The work around is to put the headshot in the logo field.

summary: Review Gen options for Email and SMS work together
description:
Hi Team, 



Piedmont is in the progress of launching on review generation and something that they brought up was that in their ideal world they would like to be able to send both an sms and email. This would also be helpful in case they ever ran out of sms capacity instead of the sms going into pending the platform could send an email.



Best,

W
workaround: Right now they have to choose either sms or email and if they run out of sms it will go into pending until the month resets vs sending an email.

summary: Chat Calendaring & Toggle On/Off Ability
description:
Hi team, my customer Pulte is interested in launching Chat, however, we hit a snag in the evaluation since they _need_ a calendaring feature that would allow Yext users who are logged into the Yext Platform to:

# Toggle on & off the Chat widget on the respective websites that they have added the code on 
# Be able to set specific dates & times that the chat feature is live (eg. Chat only live from 5pm - 6am in after hours so that Chat can respond to questions during this time and when available Live Chat or a Lead form can be inserted in place from 7am to 5pm during work hours)

According to Pulte, other Chat competitors like Podium & Chatmeter have these capabilities built OOTB. 



Out DM said “If this isn't on the roadmap it's a deal breaker”
workaround:
The workaround shared from our team is:
They could handle putting up and pulling down Chat on their website through the CMS - however this requires resources on their team and prevents flexibility of having a single team own Chat usage and design.

Additionally, Pulte could wrap the init() in a check for the current UTC time. Its not bulletproof but it'd work fine, and they'd retain full control. - This also received pushback since other competitors allow this to be configured in their respective platforms.

summary: Increase Ability for Search Result Modification
description:
Feature Request is related to this slack thread: [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1723654120752409|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1723654120752409|smart-link] 

Issue: Partner is trying to set minimum relevancy limits on results for Basic Search for Sutter Health. For example - they have a specialty field that is set as Phrase match and then a builtin.location field as a inferred filter. If you search for “cardiologist” you get the cardiologist results (354 out of about 9k) but if you add in “cardiologist in sacramento” then you get 1794 results out of 9kish. This is expected behavior because the inferred filter is returning the 1794 doctors in sacremento and then phrase match is boosting cardiologists to the top. However, they want to instead filter by the speciality “cardiologist” as well without another inferred filter because the client is on Basic Search and it would cost more. 

If they were to try to do something like this with Basic Search and have the FilterSearch up front for the speciality field is that the front-end react code needs to specify the entire list of allowed terms rather than being an autocomplete of the existing value for the field. So to get the list, the partner needs to do additional queries to get the list of values for each static filter in the account - lots of work.
workaround:
Solutions: Net net - the solution is to upgrade to Advanced Search, but could we have a better solution for Basic Search where either: 

1. There is a way for an implementer to set a minimum relevancy score on results and have a way to remove the results below the set relevancy score from the results. They basically want a way to remove results with no keyword matches. 
2. And/Or the FilterSearch component is simplified and the work to have to specify the list of value for the field in the code is automated by the product and instead is autocompleted by what is already in the content fields 

Let us know if any other questions! 

Thanks!

summary: Templated Response Assets are not available via AI Generated Review Response
description: ALDI noticed that when a user is trying to generate a review response via AI and does not like the response or would like to double check how the asset for this type of review looks like, they cannot switch between the “Response” & “AI Generated” functions or they cannot reach the templated response assets from the AI Generated interface.
workaround: Currently they have to open a new tab, find the review, click on the Response instead of AI Generated to find the asset or go directly to the templated response assets. They cannot leave the AI Generated tab because the generated response would disappear (no option to save a response draft without using an approval flow). Anyhow, somehow they would need to copy & paste either the asset or the AI generated response to align the best of the two worlds.

summary: Analytics Metric on How Many Responses Were Generated by AI
description: ALDI would like to have an Analytics metic on how many responses were generated by AI. It doesn’t matter if the response generated was modified and was not sent unaltered but that could be a next step. For now, they would like to see for how many responses was the function used at all.
workaround: The words generated information does not help them further and while they tried to label the responses with AI, this solution did not work properly either  (KI_übernommen for responses solely responded to with the AI response & KI_angepasst for responses where the generated text was altered by a human).

summary: Saved Filtering Option for Reviews Monitoring & Response
description: ALDI would like to use Saved Filters just like in Content under the Reviews Monitoring & Response tab. Currently, you can only be automatically notified or label reviews based on the set filters but you cannot save the filters you clicked together.
workaround: The current workaround is to save the URL of the filters set among your browser bookmarks.

summary: Track, Show and Report on Which User Responded to Which Review
description: ALDI would like to see in the Reviews Monitoring Tab, which user responded to which review and ideally have a weekly or monthly reporting for this also for compliance and performance tracking purposes.
workaround: The current workaround they use is assigning Review Labels but this is cumbersome and anyone can change the assignment of the label and falsify their reporting. See screenshots of their agent labels.

summary: Editing Rights for Templated Review Response Assets
description: ALDI is a heavy user of Templated Review Response Assets and they noticed that only the creator of an asset can make changes to it. This is a problem for them when colleagues are on holiday or leave the company because the project team cannot change the assets if they are not the creators. Who created an asset could also be a filter option for the filter mentioned in [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/PFR-1932|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/PFR-1932|smart-link]. Expanding this functionality would increase ALDI’s trust in the product and would benefit the user experience according to them. (Please note that ALDI has a lot of assets, 295 sites of Value Statements…, see screenshot)
workaround: No known workaround.

summary: Filter Function for Templated Review Response Assets
description: ALDI has so many assets saved under [https://www.yext.com/s/2471702/account/reviews/response|https://www.yext.com/s/2471702/account/reviews/response] that they are requesting a feature to be able to filter these based on differing criteria. The main reason for this request is user friendliness and the time saving aspect, as currently they spend a lot of time and effort searching for certain assets. They usually search for quality assurance purposes or to change an asset following a feedback from a customer or their stores on a review response given.
workaround: There is no efficient workaround as you cannot download the assets, you cannot even use Cmd + F to search for them as they are not displayed on the same page and the only option is to click through them manually, which is time-consuming and frustrating, especially with 295 pages of assets (see screenshot).

summary: AI Prompts for Review Response Content Generation at Folder Level
description: We currently only support one set of prompts per account for the AI Content Generation for Review Response, while many of our clients, incl. ALDI wish to have separate prompts per folder. The reason for this is that prompts should differ at country or brand level due to differing requirements for language, brand, culture/tone, etc.
workaround: There is currently no real workaround for this. ALDI's prompts are already so long that you cannot paste enough prompts under each other to deal with the complexity of their differing requirements per country/brand. Therefore, they currently only use this feature for HOFER in Austria but cannot even respond to the reviews in Germany and Switzerland, even though these are all German speaking countries because the branding and the culture is still so different.

summary: Add filter options for AI review response playground
description:
Hi team, 



For the new AI response playground it does not let you filter by review site or rating in the test reviews which could be helpful for clients that pull in first party reviews into Yext and want to filter the playground just to Google etc.



Best,

Wilhelmina
workaround: Testing your own reviews or swapping till you get to a review that is relevant for the purposes of the prompt.

summary: Request permission from Google reviewers to share their reviews in marketing materials (on a client's website & elsewhere)
description:
Primo Water wants to post Google reviews on their website. Their legal team is pushing back (despite examples we shared from Bass Pro and LensCrafters) due to Google’s policy requiring consent from the reviewer to share their reviews in a brand’s marketing materials:

”_You must obtain consent from the reviewer if you want to use customer reviews of your business for your own marketing purposes, such as on your website or in print or digital ads. You can_ [+_reply to customer reviews_+|https://support.google.com/business/answer/3474050] _to ask if you can use them in your marketing materials. Google cannot provide you with additional contact information.”_

Primo Water would prefer to not reply publicly in their review response saying something along the lines of, “Thanks for your review, can we share this on our testimonials page?” and would rather message them directly through some method of direct communication like an email or a DM. 

With the deprecation of Google Messaging and Google’s position on never sharing additional contact info for reviewers, I’m curious to see if there’s a solution Yext could provide. 

A few things that came to mind were:

# Social Inbox: Use this to communicate directly with the reviewer
# Review Response: Toggle public/private (also a great use-case for customers who have an offline-first approach to dealing with sensitive topics such as childcare, medicine, etc)
# Hearsay’s technology for messaging
# 3rd party review generation: Add a disclaimer in the review submission form stating the review may be used in marketing materials, or give them a box to check yes/no for permission

With the challenges presented by Google’s deprecation of messaging and Yelp not allowing review gen for partners it might be a hard problem to solve. This would give us a competitive edge and it would make sense with our current set of solutions. Primo Water merged with Blue Triton to create a massive $6.5B water brand and they are looking to grow the Yext partnership. 

This would be massively helpful for the upsells we are working on, and really open the door to on-site opportunities for Primo like API/ Pages/ Search.

Let me know if any ideas come to mind or if you want any additional context! 

Thanks,
Vaughn


cc: [~accountid:62f37db3c1b3a10ac3ac6000] [~accountid:712020:0f877113-b262-46ca-9f1c-df6909c065af] [~accountid:61d6db6d9ee70a00687c661f] [~accountid:712020:6661a543-dae6-491d-85fd-cd56995e9f9c]

summary: Yelp Services Field Sync
description:
Hi Team,

Ferguson is currently a SOCi customer and one of their biggest feature requests is the ability to sync Yelp Services from our platform. It looks like Yelp has an API for this, so I am hoping we can connect one of our service fields to this Yelp field.

Here is an example of their listing
[https://www.yelp.com/biz/ferguson-bath-kitchen-and-lighting-gallery-north-charleston-3?osq=Ferguson+Showroom|https://www.yelp.com/biz/ferguson-bath-kitchen-and-lighting-gallery-north-charleston-3?osq=Ferguson+Showroom|smart-link] 


They would be paying for Yelp Listings with Yext.

documentation [here|https://docs.developer.yelp.com/docs/data-ingestion-api] and [{color:#0747a6}here{color}|https://docs.developer.yelp.com/reference/v3_business_service_offerings] that suggests something like this could be possible

summary: Social integrations- tik tok, X, Pinterest
description: Client is using hootsuite and loves what we will be rolling out in regards to social, but having integrations with X and Tik Tok are deal breakers at the moment. They also mentioned Pinterest being important which is a platform that has been coming up more frequently lately. Do we have a timeline for any of these integrations?

summary: Short and Long Descriptions for different publishers
description:
Our publishers have very different length restrictions when it comes to the Business Description field. Google has 750 characters as the max, but Facebook has 5,000 and Foursquare goes as low as 300. 

Many of our competitors (Uberall notably) use a short and long description field to differentiate between publishers with short character limits and ones with longer limits. This allows users to send the most complete and detailed description to each publisher. 

Ströer has asked for us to implement the same logic, so that their customers can use longer descriptions where possible without impacting other publishers.
workaround: For some publishers you can use custom sync settings to sync a custom field instead of our built-in field, but this does not scale for reseller accounts in Partner Portal.

summary: Move or add a "close" option to the Yext "Help" pop-up button in Storm
description: The new “Help” button that appears in Yext blocks clients and internal Yext users from being able to click “Submit” or “Continue” on various pages through the platform.
workaround: You can change the zoom of the page to get around this, but no other workarounds seem possible at this time.

summary: Export from entity history
description: Hello. When it comes to entity history from Yext Content, there is no option to export the data. It would be nice to have this possibility as us/clients want to see the changes that were made for multiple locations and to cross check the data with what they have at the moment. Since we cannot export, it needs to be checked manually. Thank you.
workaround: N/A

summary: Bulk category override
description: Hello. When it comes to bulk override categories, we have this tool [https://www.yext.com/admin2/locations/bulkCategoryOverride|https://www.yext.com/admin2/locations/bulkCategoryOverride] however there is no option for add to existing. We’ve had several cases where clients wanted to add one more category for Google and the option “add to existing” is not present, making things a bit more complicated and time consuming. It would be great if we had the option to add to existing. Thank you!
workaround: N/A

summary: Geocode bulk tool
description: Hello. When it comes to geocoding locations in bulk, the tool is very helpful - [https://www.yext.com/geodata/tools/bulkEndorseGeocodes|https://www.yext.com/geodata/tools/bulkEndorseGeocodes]  , however the limit is 2k. We recently had a case for FedEx where they required us to bulk geocode 78k locations and since the limit was 2k this took quite some time. It would be nice if the limit was increased to 10k. Thank you.
workaround: N/A

summary: Notifications
description: Hello. Creating custom notifications for clients is quite time consuming. It would be nice to have the ability to create notifications in bulk via file upload. We've had instances where we had to create over 1k notifications and we need to create them manually one by one. Thank you!
workaround: N/A

summary: Restore deleted entities
description: Hello. It would be very helpful if we had an option to restore deleted entities. Mistakes happen and then in order to resolve everything needs to be added back manually.
workaround: If the account has a connector and the entities were included in the connector, we can make another run and try to restore them by downloading the connector result and upload entities back in the platform.

summary: Clarified Error Message for Submitting Duplicates
description: It would be great if we could add a more detailed description to the error message stating why the suppression request didn't go through (1/3 NAP match).
workaround: Reseller thought this was a tech issue so they reached out to CSM. Support confirmed they can submit additional information for a 1/3, but this is a manual process - no way to do this in platform

summary: Apple Showcase Description Length
description: Apple showcase description character length has now been increased to 100 characters.
workaround: No workaround currently, character limit is set at 58 characters in platform right now.

summary: Automatic Image Watermarking based on Location
description:
The prospect has many different schools in his school system, each with a different logo. When making a social post for these schools, he wants to add the correct logo for that school before posting.

Ideally when he makes a social post with images, and it automatically adds the correct watermark for the school it's posting from based on the entities selected. This is so he doesn’t have to add a different watermark manually to each image before making a post for multiple schools.
workaround: No current workaround aside from adding the watermarks manually outside of the Yext platform, then uploading them into the asset library. Still very time consuming for this use case.

summary: Services Field
description:
Hello everyone,

I recently joined Yext : before that, I was sales at Partoo and I stole (L’Occitane, Air France, Uniqlo) or won (Marionnaud) several deals in front of Yext : in particular thanks to a feature, “Google Services” that is :

# not well used by Yext customers
# not well highlighted by Yext platform

*Not well Used by Yext customers :* 

Yext customers do the job of filling their services but they need a Yext approval to get access to Products & Services tab in Content. 

So, if their account managers do not communicate well : they will fill Yext platform in the wind because the products and services field would not be activated. That’s a shame. On Uberall, Partoo or Evermaps platforms services field are not hidden : they are clearly highlighted which help customers being autonomous.


*Not well highlighted by Yext platform* 

It can be confusing for customers to understand the relevance of these fields (additional attributes)

* brands
* products
* services
* specialities
* keywords

if they only have the Listings Products, these fields are useless because they are not linkedin with GBP :  lot of our customers (Biogroup, Krys, Afflelou, Jules, Thom…) fill them thinking it helps their local SEO.
workaround:
automatically activate the services field
reorganize the fields and downgrading the useless fields such as specialities, brands...

summary: Embedded Field Descriptions
description:
As an example, when setting up an asset for automatic review response natively in the tool, you can use author, name, or nickname as embedded fields, but it's unclear what those fields actually correspond to. In this case, the client wants the automatic review response tool to only embed the author's first name in the review response.

When creating an asset for review response in content, you can use author, first name, last name, name, nickname, etc.

Nowhere in the tool does it show which embedded fields correspond to fields from the entity, review author, etc. and it's equally as unclear why the available embedded fields vary based on where you create the asset, even if the assets are both used for review response.
workaround: Ask a SME channel

summary: Create new algorithm
description: For CSG, the client wants the search results to be ranked with the following logic: The top results should be for content with similar/exact phrase matches in the document body. The results below that should only be for keyword matches. The similar/exact phrase matches should be ranked higher even if there are fewer token matches. Essentially this part of the algorithm should always be weighted higher than keyword matches.
workaround: Currently we have text search set as the algorithm for their body content. From speaking with Product, this seems to be the best option today (especially in consideration with the volume of content they have.) However, this still does not return results in the way they want.

summary: Add "normalize by location" to more metrics and chart types

CubeSmart wants to look at impressions over month, normalized by location to see if they are actually increasing or not:

It looks like we have this for maps but not many other chart types… 



Can we add this to more metrics? Particularly for the line graphs



Thanks,

Laura
workaround: none

summary: Assets Library Nested Folders
description:
Would there be the possibility to create a social assets folder rather than utilising folders that are already in the platform as they don't want information shared in these folders with the social team. For clarity, the Assets Library to have the functionality to create Nested Folders each aligned to User Access Rights / Permissions of different Yext Users who are allowed to view the respective folders/nested folders and subsequent assets within those folders different Asset Library folders.

This was a the clients ask after Yext provided a social demo.
workaround: None at this stage.

summary: Google Business Profile Export - Amendments
description:
Hi team, I can see we have a Google Business Profile Export scheme which is a great idea and so I'm wondering please could we add all of the fields that could go onto Google as part of this export scheme? At the moment it only seems to have a select few? E.g. Missing FAQ's, Google Attributes etc.?
 
Currently there is a lot of manual work in getting exports for clients to show their field completion rates.

This isn’t a request from the client specifically but CSM’s are having to do manual exports to show completion rates to clients (especially if on the EU server as Tableau isn’t working) and it would be great to have a ‘recommended’ export scheme for each entity type.
workaround: Doing bespoke exports from the platform and then adding them into the Core Field Completion file for CSMs.

summary: Export category overrides for all publishers
description:
When trying to audit the current category overrides for an account the UI export only offers options for Google, Bing Places and Yelp. 

We have queried this in Snowflake and managed to export it however, it only shows the category IDs, but due to the volume of category overrides they have, it's unmanageable to somehow map all of those pub-specific IDs back to the appropriate names. There seems to be a gap in the product as we are able to download publisher specific categories and bulk upload category overrides in admin/UI but we cannot export the current settings for those overrides.
workaround: query in Snowflake, match the category ID to the name (admin) in Excel

summary: Dynamic photo assets
description:
Team - We’re working a competitive deal with Milestone Education and are at the finish line however the CMO has brought up a specific need that’s important to them re: social posting. They want to know if we currently offer, or if we will soon be able to allow them to for example have a photo saved where there is a dynamic square/section in the corner where they could input a watermarked logo based on which one of their brands is posting the photo (so they don't have to save a ton of unique photos for every single brand). 

Is this capability something that is on our radar/roadmap?
workaround: n/a

summary: In Platform escalation tickets
description:
Hello everyone,

Support noticed that in some platform escalation tickets the full content is missing, which can definitely result in client’s frustration and dissatisfaction.

For example in this platform escalation ticket [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2035300|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2035300] some information are displayed in the Yext platform (*URL of the duplicate listing*) but are not displayed in the ticket Support receives (see screenshots attached of Yext platform vs Support ticket generated from the platform).  It doesn’t seem expected behaviour to me. Is there any way to fix this?

 I am asking because I am afraid this piece of information will get lost in the ticket, resulting in client's dissatisfaction. 

I have already spoken offline with Carl and Adrienne from product who suggested to raise a product feature request. 

Thank you for the help and let me know if you have any further questions. 

Giulia
workaround: Support can log in every single time into the platform to check if the info are actually correct or missing from the ticket, but this results in a lot research time instead of solving the ticket immediately.

summary: Adding line breaks in the UI of list custom fields to visually indicate different records in each field
description:
In the Yext UI when editing a Custom field (with subfields) that is a LIST, there is very little indication in the styling between different records in the field.  For example, the screenshot attached of the Sutter "Location Services" shows a field where there are multiple subfields for each instance.  Each instance starts with the "Service" label and you can see it's very hard to tell where the previous record ends and the next record begins (just a small break in the vertical line along the left side).  Any chance of a styling update to make the record boundary more obvious?

[Slack thread|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1723063660565169]

summary: Customizable Quick Links in Enhanced Dashboard

Nobel Tech is a reseller that has organized some of their customers into Enhanced Dashboard accounts. The Enhanced Dashboard Home screen/Welcome screen has a panel with Quick Links on the side, but these link to Yext support and resources. As a reseller, they would like to be able to link to their own Help site and resources.

summary: Assorted improvements for Listings Recommendations
description:
Make recommendations visible at the account level so that you can work through all recommendations across entities instead of having to go into each entity individually.

Don’t display a recommendation for a field that has already been filled out.

Customers are going to be confused by the wording of the recommendation %. They're going to understand the % as the % lift they're going to get versus the probability they will experience any lift.

Can we limit the recommendations based on the business category, as we’re seeing many recommendation that are not relevant to the business type. Every entity I’ve looked at recommends all the URL fields, and a menu URL is not applicable for a scrap metal business, as an example.

Resellers would like us to call out missing opening hours or missing business descriptions as recommendations. They feel intuitively that these fields would be more important (especially hours) than something like a Menu URL.
workaround: There aren't any for these requests. These improvements are essential to deliver the value of the concept for Listings Recommendations.

description: BeeDigital would like to be able to export the listings recommendations from multi-location accounts so he can see what they are across entities.

summary: API support for Listings Recommendations

summary: Social Eligibility API
description:
BeeDigital ($1.02M) wants to integrate Social via API and has pro-actively asked for a Social Eligibility API, as there currently is no way for them to know which one of their customers and locations is eligible to post to Instagram. This is preventing them from rolling out the feature to their customers.

To post on Instagram you need to:

# Link your account granting all permissions
# Be an admin of a Facebook page with an instagram account - we automatically pull in the instagram accounts associated with the Facebook pages in a given account

Prior to us syncing to Instagram, clients only gave permission to sync to FB. In order to sync to Instagram, clients have to re-link their accounts. They need to know whether a particular entity is eligible to post to Instagram, as there is no way to find this out today using our other APIs. This is why they were eagerly awaiting our Social Eligibility API, which has been put in the Graveyard: https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/PB-20076

This is a very important feature to get Resellers to adopt Social and not having it will make the offering they can offer to their customers much worse.



When Eden Green and I consulted about this, Emmy Sohn said “So from a Facebook API perspective this is possible, however, in our own system it's difficult for us to pull the access_token of the user in order to check this.. this is something we've asked eng to help with and let me see if we've made any progress on that and I'll let you know if this is something we could implement in the near future.”

It was recommended by Emmy and Eden to submit this as a product request because as of Aug 8, 2024, Emmy hasn’t made progress with Eng.
workaround: Trying to post to Instagram and receiving an error message afterwards indicating the user hasn't set it up correctly. This is a bad user experience. Alternatively, they can leave out Instagram support altogether, but that reduces the value of the posting solution massively. None of these is a good  workaround.

summary: Ability to apply report dashboards with custom insights across sub-accounts
description: This has been a sore point for Nobel for at least 6 months, and having the ability to replicate dashboards with custom insights across sub-accounts will be hugely appreciated by other resellers as well.
workaround: Build the dashboard manually in each sub-account separately - this will not work when we have resellers in a portal structure with many customers like Nobel.

summary: Ability to apply report dashboards with custom insights across sub-accounts
description: This has been a sore point for Fonecta for at least a year, and having the ability to replicate dashboards with custom insights across sub-accounts will be hugely appreciated by other resellers as well.
workaround: Build the dashboard manually in each sub-account separately - this will not work when we have resellers in a portal structure with over 15K customers like Fonecta.

summary: Translation of reviews
description:
If Reviews come in in another language than the main language you need to copy that into Google Translate / Deepl to translate it. 

Thats especially the case if you want to centrally want to reply to reviews in all stores in the EU.
workaround: Using Google Translate twice

summary: Snowflake Data Sharing - Adding Reviews DB's to Data Sharing
description:
Hi Product,

We have a client (Bagel Brands) who is trying to pull average rating and related metrics via Snowflake Data sharing - but the databases haven’t been exposed to Data Sharing yet! 

Just submitting this product feature request in the hopes to bump the priority on this for the future and keep it on the roadmap! Let us know if you have any questions.

The priority of this is normal / low - but it would be nice to expose more and more data as it gets cleaned!
workaround: They can utilize management API / traditional report builder for this.

summary: Remove mentions of Yext from Dev Console
description:
Our German Reseller Ströer has a couple of Enterprise clients that use our APIs. These clients know that they are using Yext under the hood because of our API documentation and the URL they are doing the API calls too, but Ströer does not want them reaching out to our support directly. When you click the "Increase Quota" button in the Dev Console there is a mention of Yext and reaching out to [apisupport@yext.com|mailto:apisupport@yext.com]. 

they would like to have this removed. I've been trying to push back and convince them it's not a big deal, but they keep bringing it [up.|http://up.Do] Is there a way for us to hide this for them?
workaround: None

summary: Apple Showcase Length
description: Apple showcases are currently set to a 30 day max. Apple has increased this limit to 365 days.
workaround: We need to update our validation that prevents showcases at over days to be at over 365 days. I have tested the endpoints and confirmed they will now work

summary: *Time Sensitive* Language Support Requests
description:
The Church off Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints will be expanding their Event and Unit Page templates globally, and thus will need support for a variety of languages that either a) are not in Yext, or b) use a locale which does not match the locale that the Church uses in their own internal database.  The full list of requested languages can be found here.  For reference, the “Materials Language Abbreviation” column refers to the code the Church uses to store the language-specific data (so they are interested in knowing if we can update our own locales to match for some of the languages we already support in their account).  The Unit Count refers to the number of Church districts that use the language.



Ideally, the Church would like to have support for all of these languages enabled prior to August 16th, if that is possible.

||Materials Language Name||Materials Language abreviation||Unit Count||Yext to Add?||
|Tagalog|tl|237|Y|

summary: User Photos to be able to see in Review Response
description:
Currently the photos that user added for the review is not attached in Review Monitoring or Review Response.

Photos are stored in User Photos section but it is hard to recognize the connection with the review.

Knowing what kind of photo user added (on top of the review content) would be helpful for the clients to fully understand the review. 

Ideally there is a blue link or something next to relevant review that says ‘photos’ or something.
workaround: GBP shows review and photos togeter so check the author's name, look into User Photos tab and recognize by the author's name which takes a lot of time.

summary: More in platform / product support around how to automatically handle trailing slashes on reverse proxy implementations
description: Feature for sitemaps where entries would preserve the slashes for RPs. Full Slack thread [here|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C02UVSE7P6W/p1721933768531859]
workaround: For now though the clients would still be expected to provide the logic in their RP to do the transformations that they need to serve their content after having preserved the slash.

summary: Custom field mapping for Google Business Profile photo gallery
description:
Greene King want to ensure content parity (as much as possible) between Google and the Extended Network. When we pulled imagery from Google (this was paramount for them to have Google imagery in platform) and then launched on GBP listings, we created duplicate imagery due to the format in which we store Images (URLs generated by our CDN). The duplicate imagery was an issue for Greene King so we paused the image sync and cleared the duplicates using a script that TPM helped us run.

Greene King want to retain the impressions on their currently Google imagery, due to which we cannot enable the ‘override photo gallery’ option within the Publisher configurations. With this caveat and that we don’t match to specific images on Google, we then proposed a custom field for a Google specific photo gallery. However, the Publisher configurations tab currently remains greyed out (as seen [+*here*+|https://app.eu.yext.com/s/100003861/listings/publishers/250#sortCol=DEFAULT&sortDir=asc&s0=0&l0=25&tab=config]+*, on this page*+ [https://app.eu.yext.com/s/100003861/listings/publishers/250#sortCol=DEFAULT&sortDir=asc&s0=0&l0=25&tab=config|https://app.eu.yext.com/s/100003861/listings/publishers/250#sortCol=DEFAULT&sortDir=asc&s0=0&l0=25&tab=config]) even if an eligible CF is created. This being greyed out along with the visibility of a drop-down is misleading which caused us to believe that we are able to customize the field mapping for this field. The functionality to be able to customize the Photo Gallery field mapping for Google would be great functionality to have here.

Additional context on [this ticket|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1986538] ([https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1986538|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1986538]) and [this ticket|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2024303] ([https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2024303|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/2024303]) with TPM for reference.
workaround: The image sync is paused for Google at this time. We're pushing imagery to the ExNet. We can enable the sync after clearing out the field, but that would mean that they can only add new imagery to Yext for all publishers.

summary: Only send out another email/sms review gen if the patient has not already left a review for that entity
description:
Hi team,



NYP was wondering if there was a way to customize the max contact frequency setting so that it only reached out to a patient again if they had not left a review already. I know this is not a feature today but it could be pretty helpful!

Best,

W
workaround: Just setting regular max contact frequency and sending review invites as currently set up in yext.

summary: Accessibility Attribute Expansion
description: Hilton wants more accessibility attributes available in Yext to be pushed out to publishers. Yelp is a key focus as they display several accessibility attributes on Listings. However, they would be interesting in maximizing for any publisher.
workaround: NA.

summary: Total Row for Average Rating is a SUM instead of AVG
description: Current total row incorrectly calculates average rating as a sum instead of an average
workaround: none

summary: Show Google User email address within "Manage Admins" section
description: While we are able to see the user name and access type, customers cannot see the actual email address associated with the GBP account. This is important for them to see so they can access or advise clients who forget their email tied to the account.
workaround: Currently no work around.

summary: Remove Google Users from Yext
description: Currently, we have the ability to add / send invitations to GBP users to a profile in Yext. However, there is not a way to remove users. Screenshot attached. Is it possible to implement a feature to remove users?
workaround: Manually remove in GBP.

summary: View Match Query Token Scoring in Search Backend Debug Screen
description: For users configuring search experiences that are leveraging the Match Query Token relevance setting, it would be helpful to be able to see the scoring for those tokens on the search debug screen for better debugging and understanding of search performance.
workaround: Currently no workaround, you aren't able to see the scoring for match query tokens on the debug screen.

summary: Computation Method Configuration in Portal Accounts
description: Currently, computation methods are only configurable per sub account. In order for AI Content gen to be scalable, managing computation methods needs to also be managed at the parent level. That way, fields can be turned on in bulk and rules can also be set in bulk to reflect all sub accounts.
workaround: Currently there is no workaround. Only option would be to manually enable each field per sub account.

summary: License assignment reporting
description:
My customer has a franchise model, much like many other customers I work with. They are struggling to invoice their franchisees appropriately since we are not able to show LA when Listings licenses have been assigned to individual entities. Other competitors like RIo & Moz do this for their customers and it has been a common complaint of franchisee type businesses.

Can we please create a report of an area within the account profile to display the date and time when a license has been assigned, and to what entity that licenses has been assigned. Additionally, if a license has been unassigned from one location and assigned to another they client would like a time stamp and the ability to see when that happened.
workaround:
Today Eden Green attempted to query the license assignment history, however, AEs no CSMs have access to snowflake and we cannot see this information.

[https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05S1872BEW/p1721855063517679?thread_ts=1721318028.499429&cid=C05S1872BEW|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05S1872BEW/p1721855063517679?thread_ts=1721318028.499429&cid=C05S1872BEW]

It would be a huge help to have this in the platform for customers to help with their own invoicing process and for AEs to maintain clients at renewal like LAZ who have threatened to leave over not having this feature and causing major invoice challenges for their business.

summary: Zapmap Publisher Integration
description:
Hi Publisher Team 



I’m speaking to the EV Charging department within Tesco. 

They have said that we need to have an integration with Zapmap as they are a big player in the UK market, have we spoken to zapmap about integrating with them? [https://www.zap-map.com/|https://www.zap-map.com/|smart-link]
workaround: No workaround that I know of

summary: Twilio app partner
description: Allow for text message distribution utilizing an app that feeds Yext data into Twilio. Similar to Sengrid integration (potential to combine the two since Twilio acquisition).
workaround: No workaround.

summary: Set 'AND' search as default when searching by any keywords
description:
Client wants to make space-separated searches into AND searches.

so if the user searches '{{AAA}}     {{BBB}}’, the resut to contain both {{AAA}}and {{BBB}} but anywhere in the string. 

We have *Add Filter Query Rule* but need to set specific words so having the config to set the search to function 'AND' would be great
workaround: Add Filter Query Rule - but needs to specify the keywords

summary: Scheduled Update for Temp Closed Field
description:
Sephora has planned renovations that will take months at a time and thus the store will be temporarily closed. Sephora wants the customers to know when its planned to reopen and ensure customers know that the store isn’t permanently closed.

Renovations will span months time so holiday hours won’t apply.

Currently, the client has to log in on the day that the construction starts and mark it as temp closed, which is a very manual process. There are 121 stores that will be having planned renovations, so they need a more scaleable approach.
workaround: Currently, the client has to log in on the first day that the construction starts and mark it as temp closed, which is a very manual process. There are 121 stores that will be having planned renovations, and more to potentially come in the future.

summary: Dimensions for search tracker data
description:
Hi team,

Cubesmart uses search tracker pretty heavily and would love to split the share of intelligent search data by publisher, and for Google by app (search V maps)

Example report where we cannot do this today [https://www.yext.com/s/3230091/reports/67474?b=3230091|https://www.yext.com/s/3230091/reports/67474?b=3230091]

Thanks,

Laura
workaround: none

summary: Separating brand category from category columns in exports
description:
Hi team, 



When we take an export today in the category 1 column it will include the brand category if that is populated vis just having the primary category. If the client has the primary category it will show in the category 1 column. The result is that the category 1 column is a mix of primary categories and brand categories which makes lining things up and really assessing who has what very very confusing. In an ideal world we would have brand category (which my understanding was historically just used for launching on Apple and serves no other purpose after initial Apple launch) in a separate field from the actual categories we sync to publishers. 



Thank you!
W
workaround: Excel file manipulation that is beyond me to get the columns to line up

summary: Ability to export Entity History in UI
description: The client wants the ability to export entity history via the UI. When considering the roll out of user access to Regional or Site Managers, the ability to keep on top of changes and usage is extremely important to the success criteria.
workaround: You can use the Logs API, but the team who want to monitor changes do not have access to internal resources to set up an API, additionally this used to be possible via the UI

summary: Pages Performance Tracking/Alerts in Platform (All Pages Customers)
description:
Historically, Services maintains alerting/monitoring for all Pages customers with Services hours. This includes technical alerting (such as missing or robots.txt, canonical URL checking, Yext analytics not firing, certificate expiration, data update failures, url writeback missing, GSC verification issues, reverse proxy misconfiguration, and more) as well as performance monitoring (pagespeed insights, seo/schema, 404 spikes). While customers with Services hours benefit from this monitoring, any Pages/Search customers without Yext Services are often left in the blind.

It would be great if we had more alerting/monitoring of Pages sites available in the platform as a feature of Pages. Being able to tell with a glance at the Pages tab in Storm if your site is registered correctly in GSC (for Search tracking) or if you’re not syncing your Pages URLs to listings, or if your PageSpeed took a huge dive due to your last template update would help customers/partners self serve and ensure they are actually getting the most value possible out of Pages. It is very easy for one small Pages configuration mistake, like accidentally disabling Yext Analytics or your robots.txt being misconfigured, to tank the value of your Pages.

I’ve submitted this under Charlotte Tillbury as there was recently some concern with their Pages performance with a Partner as implementor, however this applies to all Pages customers.
workaround: If they have Services hours, Services maintains/monitors this alerting

summary: Full Access in SubAccounts to Sharing Analytics
description:
Submitting this product request at the request of a current support ticket:

To summarize, this reseller wants to share one of their subaccount dashboards with a specific user from their main parent account. The user has full control and access to "all accounts", but when they try to share the dashboard, they are unable to locate the user: [https://www.screencast.com/t/yqK6nkUAKR|https://www.screencast.com/t/yqK6nkUAKR|smart-link] .
 
This is expected behavior since the user is not directly in that subaccount, but also seems like this functionality should work since the user in question has full access to all subaccounts. So the best course here would be to submit a product request to see if product could add the functionality to share subaccount dashboards with full access users in the parent account.
workaround: N/A

summary: BK - Review Response Agent Metrics
description: Unable to filter for Responder within Yext. Client will have multiple agents responding to reviews, and will want to pull Response Metrics for each agent, such as Response Count, Response time.
workaround: Export responses to filter by responders.

summary: Review Response Agent Metrics
description: Unable to filter for Responder within Yext. Client will have multiple agents responding to reviews, and will want to pull Response Metrics for each agent, such as Response Count, Response time.
workaround: Export responses to filter by responders.

summary: IHG - Expansion of Pub Network Request
description: Client note: “I'm reaching out to see if you can recommend any Yext partners who specialize in expanding our publisher network to include local area travel websites. As we analyze backlinks from hotel websites, it's clear that competitors are focusing on state and city tourism sites to secure valuable dofollow links, potentially boosting their Google rankings. These links typically appear in the form of 'hotel listings' or 'business listings'.For instance, here's an example placement: [https://www.visitflorida.com/places-to-go/southwest/marco-island/|https://www.visitflorida.com/places-to-go/southwest/marco-island/|smart-link] Do you have any leads on companies that can assist us in identifying these relevant sites and aggregating our listings on them? Ideally, they would integrate with Yext to streamline the publishing of hotel information by city and state.”
workaround: Working with individual sites. Not very scalable for their 6000 hotels.

summary: More Advanced Criteria for Filters
description: Currently filters in analytics allow for inclusion behavior (i.e., you can specify what to keep in the dataset). Often times a report needs exclusion behavior (e.g., excluded blank search terms) which is not currently supported.
workaround: N/A

summary: Provide a tooltip when unsupported metrics + dimensions are selected
description: Currently, if a user selects metrics and dimensions that cannot be ran together, the Apply button greys out, with no indication as to why. It would be helpful to provide a dynamic alert that specifies that X metric(s) is incompatible with Y dimension(s).

summary: Adjust Available Metrics based on Account Licenses
description: Within Analytics, an admin will see metrics for all Yext products, not the products they have purchased. It would be easier for admins if only metrics related to the products they have active subscriptions for showed in Analytics.
workaround: N/A

summary: Label Search Duration with Unit of Measure
description:
The current Search Duration dimension mentions that it’s in seconds in the tooltip, but once included in the report it does not mention the unit. Recommend adding (Seconds) to the name of the dimension once it’s shown in a report. 

Also, it would be helpful to allow a user to convert this measure to minutes or another measure, thousands of seconds is a pretty unusable way of assessing search duration.
workaround: N/A - external reporting

summary: Publisher Integration with Avvo.com
description:
Hey Team, 

Surefire is asking if there was any potential integration with [Avvo.com|http://Avvo.com] in the works. They are expanding into the Legal Industry and this is a big Publisher for their clients.

summary: OpenTable API - request for more flexibility from Four Seasons
description:
Hi team,

Four Seasons would love more flexibility in our OpenTable API.

The issue is they don't want the Yext Business Description to go to OpenTable. They want different content for this than all the other publishers. As this [field|https://www.yext.com/integrations/opentable#:~:text=Description,attract%20more%20consumers.] is part of the integration I was hoping we could either:

# opt out of this field
# remap to a custom OT description field to manage in Yext

Thanks,

Laura
workaround: Sounds like the only solution is to either opt out of OT completely, or update the description (and then if they want a custom description field for Google etc we can change that if the Pub accepts).

summary: Automatic review response - ability to add rule that applies for entity's country
description:
BeeDigital wants the option to isolate entities by country when they set up Automatic Review Responses - they haven’t found a way to ensure that specific templates in a language are only used to reply to entities for a specific country with that corresponding language. 

sample: [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1987273|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1987273]

summary: Translatable widgets
description:
“Currently, we have defined a menu for each entity and for each language as well. This means, we also have one widget per language per entity. Thus, if a website for a restaurant is presented in 3 languages and we want to integrate a menu widget, we need to create 3 separate widgets for each language.

We would like to just create a single menu widget for an entity and Yext would display the correct language depending on the visitor's browser? (If it's programmatically possible by passing a URL parameter, it would be helpful as well!)”
workaround: Creating a widget for each language

summary: Apple linked account messaging confusing for clients who have already delegated their account through adding business and company IDs on entity

Hi Team, 

MSK reached out bc even though they were one of the first accounts to delegate their ABC account to Yext via the method of adding the IDs from ABC into their Yext entities, in the linked account section it says that they have not linked their ABC account. This is very confusing since they have linked their account via the original method. Can we make this clearer for our clients?



Thank you!
W

summary: Yelp Attributes from Yext
description:
*Client: TracFone*
*Admin:* [*https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/1197005*|https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/1197005]

Brendan

See [here|https://yext.enterprise.slack.com/archives/CDA4M8ULS/p1721160986136699]

Vista Print - Prospect is looking for a simplified content system for an SSO, where clients manage their content directly in Yext.

Competitive situation with Synup.

summary: Filter a specific Search Term in a Raw Search Term analytics dashboard
description:
The client asked for an Analytics Dashboard in which they can filter how many “cerca de mi” searches have been done based on specific areas. 

When setting up the dashboard, it is not possible to filter it for a specific term, so all the searched terms are being displayed.

Regex filtering would also be very useful for the client.

As a workaround, I've advised the client to set the time frame they prefer and export the dashboard - [https://www.yext.com/s/1029251/reports/insightsDashboard/204958?b=1029251|https://www.yext.com/s/1029251/reports/insightsDashboard/204958?b=1029251].

Once exported, the search terms-specific column can be filtered. 

However, the client would like to suggest this feature to be added to the Analytics dashboards.

summary: Custom Email Templates
description:
We noticed that the new user welcome email (attached below) is off-brand with the Reseller use case. Since a lot of the work done with our partners is custom, we were hoping that we could potentially have the ability to edit components of this email.

We recently had a partner come to us and say that this content does not apply at all to his business or his clients - thus he was looking to change the copy. Is this something we could potentially do for them and subsequently other Reseller Partners?
workaround: The current workaround is to not send anything at all.

summary: Duplicate Workflow Rules
description: The client has requested the ability to duplicate an existing workflow rules set for Automatic Review Response. They are building separate rules for reviews with the same rating but differing content, so they want to use almost all of the same rules from an existing flow without having to manually re-add each one.
workaround: Current workaround is just to manually create another workflow rule and add each of the same criteria.

summary: Xing field support for sites Acompio, Cylex, Dialo, Finderr, Hotfrog, MeineStadt, Stadtbranchenbuch, WhereTo and Wogibtswas
description:
Based on Matt’s feedback here: [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C03B2AA5W11/p1720101852599299?thread_ts=1701101021.132959&cid=C03B2AA5W11|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C03B2AA5W11/p1720101852599299?thread_ts=1701101021.132959&cid=C03B2AA5W11|smart-link]  creating a product item. 

There has been a long and old tread with the Product team - [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C03B2AA5W11/p1701101021132959|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C03B2AA5W11/p1701101021132959|smart-link] . To summarize, we have added a new build-in field called “Xing” as part of a Social Media field within Content. This field is available in Content for all German customers. Xing is the biggest Linkedin competitor as a Professional Social media platform.  Next step is to ensure we are pushing this field to as much German publishers  as possible.

As you can also read in the Slack thread the following sites have all showed interest in supporting the Xing field through the publisher integration. Those sites are: Acompio, Cylex, Dialo, Finderr, Hotfrog, MeineStadt, Stadtbranchenbuch, WhereTo and Wogibtswas. Most of these publishers/sites already have a build-in Xing field on their platform, so integrating this field should not be an issue for the publishers.

Let me know if you need anymore info. Our reseller Stroer has been waiting for this feature for over 8 months now, plus this will also be of strong value for all our direct German Enterprise/Midmarket customers.
workaround: N/a

summary: Include Asset Name in the Review Response feature and improved filters
description:
When a  customer creates Review Response Templates they are able to give each Response an Asset Name, example here: [https://app.screencast.com/9l5bHIZpLAD0r|https://app.screencast.com/9l5bHIZpLAD0r|smart-link] Unfortunately within the Review Response feature we don’t show the Asset Name of each templated response. For account who have dozens of different templated responses its now a complicated scroll to find the right templated response. The solution I propose is that we add an extra “Asset or Element Name” to the Response section, like this: [https://app.screencast.com/72AVSkMgyWso2|https://app.screencast.com/72AVSkMgyWso2|smart-link] 



Additionally for accounts who have over 20 different Review Response Templates it would be an improvement to have Filters in the Asset section that allows them to choose for which filtering rule the Review Response Template should be available to. For example that “Value statement 1” only applies for Reviews with 1 star without any Content. Basically the idea would be to add this type of Filters to the Asset section for Review Response Templates: [https://app.screencast.com/KMBBamu0MFqPs|https://app.screencast.com/KMBBamu0MFqPs|smart-link] .
workaround: Currently for accounts with dozen of templated reviews responses the Yext Response UI is confusing and requires them having to scroll through a long list, causing chances of selecting the wrong response. Adding an extra Asset/Element Name to the Response section and adding Filter options to the Review Response Asset Template section will improve the UX drastically

summary: Extra "Temporarily closed" slots/dropdowns/entries for locations
description: There isn’t a way to add more temporarily closed and reopening dates within the field (i.e. temporarily closed on July 12/reopens July 14, temporarily closed July 14/reopens July 16) - “I'm convinced the best outcome would be to have multiple lines/date selections in the Temporarily Closed field. My dream state would be that I could have *Temporarily closed /**___​_**​/__/__, Reopens /**___​_**​/__/__* lines that duplicate as I need them.”
workaround: Scheduled updates - aren't super intuitive for customers

summary: UX for assigning licenses is confusing to clients
description:
Hi Team!



We have a client who is used to clicking licenses pack when assigning licenses but when they did so today it turns out that they have to select each licenses individually since premier support and MRR are at the account level. Mixing account level licneses and those that need to be assigned individually is confusing and makes the client think that they are running out of MRR and premier support when that is not the case. Can we make this clearer?

Thank you!
W
workaround: Explaining this to clients and having them deal with the confusing view.

summary: Social Posting per Publisher
description:
Hey team,

The Co-operative Funeralcare is part of a large brand under the name Co-op in the UK which also owns banks and supermarkets.

Their funeral business uses Yext for Listings and Reviews and they have 900+ funeral homes across the UK logging into Yext to update Listings with accurate information and posts.

However, the Co-op would like their funeral homes to post on Google but not on Facebook as they would like to manage their Facebook pages centrally.

I have been investigating this in detail and unless I’m mistaken:

# Currently, there isn't a way to deactivate social posting for one specific publisher only on account level. It's either on/off for all publishers.
# There also isn't a way in the user permissions for me to prevent social posting for a specific publisher either which is why the funeral homes can select Facebook for social posting.

Could you please share more information on the feasibility of the above two options being built?
workaround:
Therefore, as it currently stands, there are two ways for us to prevent funeral homes from posting on Facebook:

1. We inform them that they shouldn't be posting on Facebook, only Google and therefore Facebook should not be selected when creating a post. We would therefore trust that they follow our instructions.

2. We completely cut the connection between Yext and Facebook for the entire account. This would therefore mean that we no longer update the listings and pull in the reviews for the Facebook listings.

As I'm sure you can imagine, this is not ideal.

summary: TripAdvisor Experience Reviews & Viator
description:
Hey team,

It currently isn’t possible for Yext to pull in “experience” reviews from TA.

Our customer, Merlin Operations, owns the majority of large and famous tourist attractions globally such as the London Dungeons and the London Eye.

For this reason, a lot of the reviews they receive via TA are related to the specific experiences that are offered at each attraction.

They also have a partner company called Viator where customers can purchase tickets for specific locations and experiences and a lot of the reviews come via this platform.

I’m unsure why we’re unable to sync the experience reviews from TA - I know it’s a limitation in the API but it’s not clear on the Telescope card why it’s a limitation. Could this be an easy fix?

Thanks,

Ashley
workaround: n/a

One of their markets is South Korea where the usage of Naver is high.

It’s basically the Korean version of Google and currently Accor is having to contact them directly due to data inconsistencies compared to what is in Yext.

summary: Enable scheduled updates for Temp closed field

Currently Temp closed field does not support scheduled update.

Thanks,

Kotoko

summary: Order consistency in Subscriptions List
description: In storm, most accounts include a column in their Content to display the Subscriptions associated with each of their entities.  When an account has more than one subscription, the licenses are displayed as a comma-separated list.  However, it appears that the licenses can be displayed in any order.  I’m not sure if the order of the list is dictated by the order in which licenses were applied or some other criteria.  However, the result is that Subscriptions list looks very “random” for clients.  This results in it being very difficult to discern whether a license is missing for a particular entity.  For example, if a client were to look at the entities in [this filter|https://www.yext.com/s/2970756/entities2#p0=entity-type&p1=is&p2=0&p3=&p0=folder&p1=contains&p2=592681%7C570706%7C415564%7C557041%7C547296%7C556853%7C434422%7C526345%7C518537%7C606164&p3=&p0=fields-with-data&p1=location.google_account_id%7Clocation.gmb_linked_account&p2=&p3=&p0=entity%7Eboolean%7Elocation.custom.2970756.active_on_listings.0&p1=is&p2=yes&p3=&selectedSavedFilterId=253732], they would not easily be able to tell.  The randomness of the lists also just looks a little less organized than I think we would like.
workaround: NA

summary: Listings recommendations take into consideration competitor data and are more actionable
description:
Hi Team!

I have shared the listings recommendations feature with a number  of clients and while the hype fore this was very high I have to be honest a lot of clients are disappointed. They do not feel like the recommendations are actionable and many times they are not relevant. Piedmont specifically would love it if this tool could be combined with data we are able to gather from local competitors to provide recommendations if possible. Maybe this could be accomplished by tapping into the CI tool! 



We have all been very excited for this feature and I still think its a step in the right direction but I do think there is more that can be done here to make this valuable for clients! 

Happy to talk through this if that would be helpful or share gong recordings of client reactions! 

Wilhelmina

summary: Thumbs up/down to correct Search Term Cluster results
description:
Within Search Term clusters, items often get incorrectly clustered based on semantic similarities in terms that do not logically correlate. For example, queries with similar leading actions (e.g., download, view, etc.) might group together entirely different items (e.g., download forms, download invoice). 

It would be helpful to have the ability to provide feedback on the groupings to further training the models and adjust what’s viewed in the screen.

It would be helpful to add a dimension to account for all events mapped to another event to support this type of reporting.

summary: Search - ability to have Document Search on multiple fields in one entity

I have a client with Help Article configured with multiple body of fields [Example|https://www.yext.com/s/3141402/entity/edit3?entityIds=88205506#:~:text=Help%20Article%20Section%201] and when it comes to applying Search Algorithms to the Help Article [vertical|https://www.yext.com/s/3141402/search/experiences/configuration/argos-search/verticals] I can only select 1 for document search. I want to be able to select multiple to enable picking featured snippet from the most relevant section of the help article 

Additionally, I am finding that featured snippets is not supported for [custom field types |https://www.yext.com/s/3141402/account/customFieldTypes/type.c3141402.articlecontent/edit?b=3141402]

summary: Definition & Permissions of Customised Roles
description:
The client would like to give an existing user the option of administrating another person for their particular location.

Example:
User A was administered by Admin for location A.
User A is now responsible for location A.
User A would now like to administer user B for location A.

This means that user A should not have access to all locations.
workaround: NA

summary: Ai Span Identification Tool for Reviews
description:
Managed Review Response flags reviews on behalf of clients. Individual team members make subjective assessments of each review based on publisher guidelines, which are similar to Yext First Party Review guidelines. This includes looking for spam, profanity, and offensive content. 

The team may mis-identify reviews as flags or mis-identify flags as legitimate reviews. 

The client called out some reviews that we should have flagged. They said they used online AI resources that were able to determine the likelihood a review was spam. They also reverse-image searched an image in a review to find that it was an online picture and not a legitimate customer photo. 

Their request was incorporating AI spam identification into Reviews.

I think this is in the ballpark of the upcoming Reviews Insights dashboard. If we’re using AI to identify qualitative customer insights, sentiment clusters, etc., is it feasible to also use AI to identify spam and reviews with questionable content that may not warrant a response?
workaround:
Identifying flaggable reviews is entirely manual and subjective. You have to read the review and make a determination on its validity before going to the listing itself to flag it. 

If we mess up and respond to a review that should have been flagged, we delete the response and task multiple people to flag the review to raise the likelihood of the publisher removing the review.

summary: Smart Analytics Triggers: Trend and Outlier Notifications
description:
Client is having trouble identifying trends or outliers in reports.

An example being spikes in review volume for particular, e.g. a location received 3x the amount of reviews in a single month compared to the monthly average in the prior 6 months, signaling potential spam behavior. 

While we have all the data and Analytics can build the reports, the client is looking for more interpretation to be delivered from the report as well as notifications for significant changes. The client identified this when receiving a few spam reviews for a location and then looking at a report of its review volume.
workaround: The client uses ThoughtSpot ([https://www.thoughtspot.com/|https://www.thoughtspot.com/]) for internal analytics. TS enables them to set certain parameters for notifications/alerts, e.g. if data spikes or dips by x amount in y period.

summary: Resubmiting Review Removal Requests
description:
Hi team - Allstate is requesting that their insurance agents DO NOT have the ability to resubmit review removal requests after they’ve already been declined.

They have one corporate person managing these requests and have already experienced agents trying to resubmit removal requests after they’ve been declined - which creates more of a lift for the team managing.
workaround: N/A

summary: Set Category Overrides before license assignment
description: The platform currently only supports viewing/editing category overrides _after_ listings licenses are assigned to an entity. It would make more sense to allow this field to be viewed/edited prior to license assignment, since accounts can be configured such that entities are automatically opted in to publishers when licenses are assigned.
workaround:
1. Make sure nothing will be opted in with license assignment
2. Assign licenses
3. Edit category overrides
4. Manually opt in

*note - I believe you can still set overrides via a Connector or Upload before licenses are assigned, but there is no way to view the overrides / advanced category settings until after licenses are assigned, which is the issue

We should have the ability to hide these recommendations from the platform or ensure they don’t surface at the top (like with review notifications - possibly a filter to only view new reviews, instead of new reviews + updates)
workaround: Add review date as a filter, and order with review date instead.

summary: Remove Yext mentions and URLs from Listings Pages
description:
Healow will be iframing Yext into their own UI, and will need our pages to be entirely whitelabeled.  There are a couple instances of breaking whitelabel on the Listings pages below: 

#  Duplicate Suppression: 
## _"Learn More About Duplicate Suppression"_ leads here: [https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000000863-Suppress-Duplicate-Listings|https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000000863-Suppress-Duplicate-Listings]
## _“Status > Unable to Suppress”_ leads here: [https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=232923|https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=232923]
# Listings Status Details: 
## _“Please first Opt Out of the listing and re-assign the location to the correct Google location group. This can be done by restarting the matching flow. For help on this process, please visit the_ [_Help Center_|https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005989746-How-to-Link-Google-Business-Profile-]_, or feel free to reach out to healow Aware Support for assistance. Please do not opt in your location until you have confirmed the right Google Location Group is assigned, as you will risk creating a duplicate.”_ leads here: [https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005989746-How-to-Link-Google-Business-Profile|https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005989746-How-to-Link-Google-Business-Profile|smart-link] 
## Example: [https://www.yext.com/s/4127191/listings/all#sortCol=DEFAULT&sortDir=asc&tab=overview&s0=0&l0=25|https://www.yext.com/s/4127191/listings/all#sortCol=DEFAULT&sortDir=asc&tab=overview&s0=0&l0=25]

There are also some instances of “Yext” or “Yext System” in entity history, and Content > Suggestions.

I think ideally we would find all instances and have a Parent Level On, Off toggle for whitelabeling the platform under [Branding|https://www.yext.com/resellers/4088525/cobranding].  Some Partners are fine to have mentions of Yext, and others are strictly not revealing Yext as the solution provider.
workaround: Cannot iframe the page – API Integration.

[https://www.findmeglutenfree.com|https://www.findmeglutenfree.com/]

[https://www.dogfriendly.co.uk|https://www.dogfriendly.co.uk/]
