[https://www.scoresonthedoors.org.uk|https://www.scoresonthedoors.org.uk/]
workaround: N/A

summary: Publisher addition request
description: Hi team, Greene King are keen to look into Yext adding [Sociability|https://www.sociability.app/] to our publisher network - this is an app for disabled web users to help them find accessible venues/locations.
workaround: None currently - only utilising current publisher network which has limited disability/accessibility information

summary: Export entity history logs
description: Currently this cannot be exported and they want to track activity / download it
workaround: None that I'm aware of. Looking to see if support/services can assist

summary: Automatic review response
description:
Hi team,

Love the review response enhancements! Is it possible to considering adding a scheduled delay for the automatic review response app? Couple of reasons why:



# It seems a little creepy / spammy to respond 2 seconds after the response was left, and actually doesn’t make the customer feel that thought about when it’s sent in such an obviously automated way (in turn reducing the value of some of the personalized embedded fields)
# Client is also worried this instant could look spammy to Google’s algorithm. Any comments to address that?

Thanks,

Laura
workaround: NA

summary: Custom redirect to external site based on specific search key words
description:
The customer would like a way to redirect customers that search for certain keywords to external sites from their search experience that contain legal and regulatory verbiage.  Currently, this can only be done using a custom serverless function with a query rule or custom code inserted into the search front end.  

Also, see this slack thread:
[https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C0457TME3QU/p1719241225690439|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C0457TME3QU/p1719241225690439|smart-link]
workaround: Have an entity that contains the verbiage and boost it in search results for the specific keywords.

summary: Custom Referrer Source For Page Analytics
description: What's the feasibility of adding 3rd party sites as referrers for page traffic? JPMorgan Chase wants to know if we can track page views from the awards sites like Forbes and Barrons since the Yext powered page URLs are linked there.
workaround: maybe a one time manual Snowflake pull

summary: Ability to Filter Publishers by Country/Brand/Folder/Label/Entity Type etc.
description:
When clicking at Listings/Publisher one can only see an account wide total number of Publishers & Publisher Status. There is no filtering option on this site. 

When clicking at Listings/All Publisher, you can filter but this site will show individual listings on all the publishers.

So there is no place in Yext to look at the publisher status of a brand, a country, a folder, a label or any other sub-selection of the account including all publishers.

E.g. when the Leadership of my client asks on how many publishers are we live in Italy, we cannot respond.

We desperately and urgently need this function developed and released, please. It affects all clients and would significantly improve user experience, and save time.
workaround:
Currently, one can only filter unter Listings/All Listings e.g. by folder & publisher to get the status for one publisher but one has to repeat this 40-60 times for all publishers. Or one exports the listings publisher status and tries to count the publishers per country/brand manually. All very labor intensive and a bad user experience.

We do have a list of Publishers per country on our website ([https://www.yext.com/de/integrations/publishers|https://www.yext.com/de/integrations/publishers]) but it is not guaranteed that the client is live on all of them given unsupported categories and opt-outs.

summary: Better Filtering or Viewing for All Redirects on Sites
description: The Marketing Team was trying to debug an issue with redirects to a certain subset of pages on one of our Yext Pages Sites and we noticed that it was rather difficult to navigate the list of redirect pages since it only shows a Max of [25 redirects per page in the UI|https://www.yext.com/s/919871/yextsites/43020/branch/2431/deploy/sbes7gnpgm/urls#tab=REDIRECTS], it doesn’t show how many total redirects there are and there is no way to filter the redirects
workaround: The only work around was to post in Slack and have the product engineering team pull the list for us: [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C02UVSE7P6W/p1718736211542829|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C02UVSE7P6W/p1718736211542829]

summary: Increase Item Limit for a Field
description:
We use the c_expertiseMappings and c_specialtyMappings fields to power the filters on [NYP's FaD|http://doctors.nyp.org/]. So, when a user makes a search for ["Joint Pain"|https://doctors.nyp.org/?aff=false&ep=Joint+Pain], we reference the list to determine which Specialties should appear in the dropdown filter. Currently, it only displays 3: Pain Medicine/Management, Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation, and Sports Medicine. However, there should actually be 10+ specialties that should appear in the filter. NYP is not yet on Search, which is why we're leveraging these fields. The issue is that the NYP FaD is incorrectly displaying the number of available filters for Specialty and Expertises. Joint Pain is only one example, but this issue is occurring for 100+ instances. 

Please note - NYP is not yet on Search. The FaD is on Classic Pages.
workaround: None - NYP specifically only called out the issue with Joint Pain, but Services has uncovered that this is impacting a larger number of Specialties and Expertises. The workaround I had attempted was to pause the ETL y2y syncer and manually edit just the Joint Pain mappings. However, the issue is that due to the larger number of items in the field, the page fails to load my edits.

summary: Customer User Role | Listings Status Details No Access Option
description:
Healow has requested the option to hide the Status Details column so that their clients cannot see Status Details.  There are two reasons why they ultimately want this feature created: 

#  Status Details include non whitelabeled resources, like the attached photo [which directs to help.yext.com|https://help.yext.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005989746-How-to-Link-Google-Business-Profile]
# Status Details for Synced listings might confuse their clients.  For instance, if a GBP listing is synced but we are showing Status Details to flag photos not being sent to GBP for sizing issues, they believe it might generate support calls / requests for their team. 

Is it possible to create a section under Customer User Roles > Listings where they can choose to hide Status Details?
workaround: No workaround today other than no providing their clients access to the entire Listings tab, or not whitelableing that piece of the platform into their own soltution.

summary: Ingest .txt files in Connectors
description: Currently the Connector framework can only ingest HTML and PDF content and cannot ingest .txt content. This prevents client data from being ingested into the platform that they would like used for their Search experiences. This data cannot be consumed in any other way as this is being crawled from their pages. I have confirmed with product that this is currently not possible.
workaround: NA

summary: Adding Yango to publisher network
description: Yango is significantly growing within the region and our Middle East customers are asking about this publisher
workaround: n/a

summary: Twitter integration
description: VPM let us know today that Yext not having the ability to post to Twitter/X may be a dealbreaker here, especially being that SOCi, Birdeye, and Rep all have that capability. This has been coming up more and more with prospects/customers recently.
workaround: n/a

summary: Review Monitoring publisher request from Flagstar
description:
Hi team! 

Flagstar was promised by an account team member who is no longer with Yext that we would have the ability to monitor reviews from the below pubs: 

* *BBB* (i know this is on the roadmap but is there a more firm answer than end of year?) 
* *Deposit Accounts*
* *WalletHub*
* *Zillow*
* [*ConsumerAffairs.com*|http://consumeraffairs.com/]
* *Pissed Consumer* (i know we used to work with them and there isn’t a plan to reengage since they weren’t a good partner)
workaround: N/A

summary: Correct Validation in Search for Canadian Postal Codes
description:
Background: We see inconsistent behavior for Canadian Postal Codes in Search based on their structure and have had a request in for Product to correct this. Likely this will be impactful for all Canadian customers that use a Search locator. This will be impactful for adding additional lines of business, as well as other international customers with Search Locators.

See this ticket: [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1938766|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1938766]

Most recent update:

“Still no movement on the postal codes. I added myself to the item sitting with Team Watson to monitor for any movement, but it seems the product team is focused on other priorities at the moment. If we want to get this prioritized more highly, I'd recommend submitting a feature request through SFDC to make sure we tie any active ACV or opportunities to it so they can better assess priority and need. “
workaround: Manually setting query rules for postal codes that surface incorrect results.

summary: Yelp Inbox Messaging
description: Yelp Advertising has functionality where you can respond to listing messages all from one interface. However, when partnering with Yext, this functioning is removed and separated into separate inboxes since the client is no longer using Yelp Advertising. If we had this functionality native in the Yext platform and part of the Yelp Review SKU, this would really help with franchisees and maintaining Yelp functionality with Yext.
workaround: There is currently no workaround other than continuing to be a part of Yelp Advertisers to maintain the one inbox interface.

summary: Linked Account to DNS Provider
description:
For Reseller customers, managing many sites linked to various domains owned by their end customers is quite challenging. Most of their customers are not marketers or developers, and walking them through accessing a DNS provider like GoDaddy and changing DNS records is complicated.

Rather than requiring a user to manually changes CNAME and A records, it would be helpful to use the Linked Accounts infrastructure in platform to allow Resellers to link their end customers DNS provider (i.e, GoDaddy) and provide a approval workflow to making the record changes required for launching a site on Yext Pages.
workaround: Manually setup

summary: Ability to Include Facets & Filters in Universal Tab
description: Client shared that they are receiving negative feedback on their Search experience because the Universal tab does not have Facets: [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1997564|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1997564]
workaround: There's not a work around, perhaps you could make an additional single vertical that Searches across all verticals

summary: Multi-Account Access for Delivery Partner Users
description: Hi Platform! We’ve had several delivery partners request the ability to authenticate into multiple Yext accounts they are servicing through a single username and password, similarly to the access our services team has, but restricted to only the accounts the partner services. One of these partners is Phase2, who is currently building out Search for Sutter Health. The existing user experience is very inconvenient for our delivery partners who are working on multiple client projects with us, and having a single username and password for all Yext accounts will increase implementation efficiency. I have spoken to Carl Palmquist about the current problem and feature request. Thank you!
workaround: Currently the PSM team sets up a unique username for the partner for each new user of their client accounts and the partner needs to manage all the usernames across all their accounts.

summary: Saved export schema where you can change the entities being included in export.
description:
Hi Team, 



The client would like a way to have a saved export scheme where they can change the filter of which entities are included in the export. 



There is not appear to be a way to do this when trying to edit the entities included in a saved export or if you try to select a pre-configured scheme there does not appear to be a way to create your own in these options (see screenshots of both situations).



Thank you!

W
workaround: Manually selecting the fields included in the scheme every time you want to do an export for a different group of entities.

summary: Review Deletion
description: Client on Yelp Knowledge has removes removed from Yelp frequently, and compliance settings adjusted to remove them from the platform. When Yelp fails to fire a webhook and notify of review removal, we only run the manual scan for Yelp Knowledge once per month. Even adjusting the frequency to weekly, which was discussed with TPM, the client would still like the ability to manually remove reviews.
workaround: Must create a ticket for TPM in these cases and TPM must write a new script to remove the review manually.

summary: Social Eligibility API
description: Advantago is integrating Social via API and has pro-actively asked for a Social Eligibility API, as there currently is no way for them to know which one of their customers and locations is eligible to post to instagram. This is preventing them from rolling out the feature to their customers.
workaround: None

summary: Google Oauth Flow
description:
* RevWell leverages *Reviews Only* with majority of their locations in Yext. 
* They have integrated reviews feature set into their own platform 
* When going through the Google Oauth flow for a multi location sub account, the UI requires every location to get matched before they can move to the verification step.   

Here’s a video showing the issue.
workaround: Logging ticket with support to manually match everything on the backend.

summary: Saved filter in report builder
description:
Hi team, 

Is there a way we could be able to use saved filter on report builder? For complex accounts like Accor this would be very helpful 

thank you,
workaround: currently its a manual work

summary: Include Query Rule Entity-Level Impacts on Debug Screen
description: Currently it’s difficult to directly tie the impacts of a query rule to a particular result. It would be helpful to indicate whether a result or vertical has been boosted or buried by a particular query rule directly on the result cards present in the debug screen.
workaround: N/A

summary: Support Rating Email
description: when the client receives a “How would you rate your personal interaction with ____ today?” email, the client doesn’t know what ticket it was for
workaround: guessing

summary: Platform Escalation Ticket Name
description: Escalation tickets client do in the platform aren’t easy for the client to understand what location it was about unless the support agent mentions the location in the follow up due to the generic nature of the title of the ticket like this “Walt Disney Parks and Resorts: Google Business Profile: LISTING_SUSPENDED_OR_DISABLED”
workaround:
They have to guess what location. Disney submits several tickets at a time.

THIS IS A SENSITIVE CLIENT WITH A MAJOR LOGO. THEY COMPLAIN A LOT. THIS WOULD LIKE THEIR LIFE SO MUCH BETTER.

summary: ALDI requests new publisher: Odpiralni Časi (odpiralnicasi.com)
description: This is a very important site in Slovenia to find opening hours of stores, pharmacies, banks, restaurants, etc. As well as for searching for nearby ATMs. Also available as an android/ios app.
workaround: N/A

summary: The ability to track status of online booking field overtime through report builder
description:
Hi Team!

UNC has had this request for a while but it was recently resurfaced. They would like the ability to track the status of how many profiles in Yext have online booking set to “yes” over time. Our understanding is that this is not possible today but we are open to any creative solutions the team may have!



Thank you!

W
workaround: Our understanding is that this is not possible today but we are open to any creative solutions the team may have!

summary: Google Flag Review Feedback
description:
Client states that when they want to flag a review for removal, they initiate with Google before going to Yext, but when they flag with Google first, they don’t get any response or acknowledgement that they’ve received/actioned on the flag. So they don’t know where it stands before asking Yext to flag



Client is asking if Yext has a way to add transparency to status of flagged reviews.
workaround: Currently no way to check what the status of a flagged review for the client.

summary: Add validation for Service Areas
description:
Localsearch has a lot of businesses that want to use Service Areas and they often add service areas outside of the allowed 100 mile radius for Google. The only way they find out about this is via the Unavailable Reason on the Listings tab. 

Localsearch would like to be warned while editing already that a particular service area is not suitable for this business and have asked if we can build in additional validations on the field for this.
workaround: None, other than reviewing service areas manually and then updating accordingly.

summary: Show Character Count
description: Would love to see the character count of a field, as well as the max character count, while editing in entity edit!
workaround:
Copy/paste the field contents into an editor external to yext.

Important because if Yext web team does not like our system they will have to buy a solution! Want to increase their productivity!

summary: Allow Users to delete Google User Photos from the platform
description:
Currently Google User Photos keeps showing photos that have been deleted from Google. Yext admins have the ability to delete these photos from Yext, but customers do not.

Ströer would like to be able to do this themselves. Can we make this functionality available to Full Control users?
workaround: Email support and ask them to remove the photos

summary: Social Eligibility API
description:
Yell has scheduled the work to integrate our v2 social posting APIs into their mobile app to allow their customers to post using the Yell IOS and Android App. 

They need to know whether a particular entity is eligible to post to Instagram, as there is now way to find this out today using our other APIs. This is why they were eagerly awaiting our Social Eligibility API, which I see has been put in the Graveyard: [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/PB-20076|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/PB-20076]

This is a very important feature to get Resellers to adopt Social and not having it will make the offering they can offer to their customers much worse.
workaround:
Try a post to instagram and only after receiving an error message tell the user they didn't set it up correctly. This is a bad user experience.
Alternatively they can leave out Instagram support altogether, but that reduces the value of the posting solution massively.

None of these are workable workaround.

summary: Bulk Export and Import Assets
description:
This is a common request for clients with Managed Review Response (MRR). AutoZone is ~$1.4M ACV and spends ~$150k on MRR annually. 

AutoZone and other MRR clients want to export Text Assets from Content. This is a common request as MRR clients want to be able to review their MRR response templates outside of the platform in an easy way. Why?

#  Review the quality of the templates and ensure they still conform to current brand standards. 
# Share and review the templates with multiple shareholders that may not have Yext access.
# Easy way to share, view, edit outside the platform. 
# Easy way to bulk edit and/or upload the new assets. 
# When there is turnover on the client side, new contacts need to review the content.

This is also a request internally for Services. When we implement MRR, we add text assets one by one. This is a long and cumbersome process.
workaround:
Clients and Services have to add and edit new text assets one by one. 

If someone wants to review all assets outside of the platform and we don't have all of them up-to-date in an existing file, we would have to transcribe all of them one by onen.

summary: Add Error Code to Status Details message of Canceled Add requests
description:
Hi -

A while ago we release a feature for Localsearch that shows them whether a Canceled Add Request was canceled due to a conflicting entity in their own account or in another account in the Add Requests: Get (Location) / Add Requests: List (Location) endpoints. This helps them more efficiently work through cancellations and handle channel conflict better.

Localsearch has requested a small change to this feature to make it easier for them to automate on their end. They have asked if the 3 potential outcomes can get an error code in our response, so that they can rely on that instead of on the plain text of the detail message. 

This would be similar to how we handle Unavailable Reasons in our Listings endpoints.

Hope this makes sense, lmk if you have any questions



Thanks

Tim
workaround: n/a

summary: Add in additional Degree info into the Degrees built in field
description:
There’s been a request to add in the following additional degree/education info to the healthcare providers degrees field:

·         *ACNP*: Acute Care Nurse Practitioner

·         *BSN*: Bachelor of Science in Nursing

·         *BCh*: Bachelor of Science in Chemistry

·         *MB*: foreign title for medical degree

·         *MS*: Master of Science

·         *MSN*: Master of Science in Nursing
workaround: It's currently built in option field so they have been leaving certain degree info off the providers

summary: Add Support for Videos in Google User Photos
description: Besides Photos it’s also possible for Users to Post videos to GBP listings. Ströer has asked if we can include these videos in our Google User Photos feature to ensure all UGC on the GBP Listing is visible in our platform.
workaround: None

summary: Add API Support For Google User Photos
description: Ströer is interested in rolling out Google User Photo to their SMB customers, but they are fully integrated via API. There is no API support for this feature today. The request is to add a Google User Photos API.
workaround: There is no work around

summary: Multiple sets of generative review response prompts per folder or entity
description:
Hey team,

Since Ströer has some larger enterprise customers with a multi-location structure, they need an option to create individual sets of prompts for different brands/locations. Therefore, it would be really helpful to have the option to create multiple sets of generative review response prompts for specific folders or entities.

Thanks 

Hannah
workaround: Currently we can only create on set of instructions

summary: Including built in reservation and website URL into options for Google social posting CTA
description:
Hi Team!



Pet Vet would like the option to us the built in website URL or reservation URL as the link for the Google post CTA. I connected with the reviews SME who said that product made the legacy decision not to have this and that I should submit a product request. 



Thank you!
W
workaround: The work around is to create a custom field and populate it with that same info from the website URL

summary: Escalate Multiple Listings Issues at Once/Attach Images
description: PNC actively uses our in-platform workflow to escalate listings. However, they cannot 1. see the ticket that is created until Support responds, and 2. Often find it confusing to know which entity the issue is for, and 3. Don’t have the option to add storefront photos that are necessary for Google-specific escalations.
workaround:
"I think it would be helpful if there was a way to attach store front photos or other artifacts when escalating a listing issue.

From what I can tell a Zendesk ticket is created when the escalation is submitted.  Unfortunate that ticket isn’t accessible as a client, so I must wait until support responds to the ticket so I can reply via email.  Additionally, the response from support doesn’t include the entity ID or entity details so if I have multiple open tickets, it’s difficult to know which entity I need to provide photos for.

Happy to talk more about this."

summary: Listings Visibility for Reviews Only Entities
description:
Reviews Only clients rely on the Home Screen to sync their GBP listings within Yext.  As there is no Listings tab with Listings Status, they are unable to see when a listing is Synced, Processing or Unavailable.  The only way they know if a sync is successful is if Reviews pull in. 

Additionally, for multi location clients, they have no way to Opt Out and restart the sync flow.  [Here|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1984919] is a ticket where 3 entities are synced and 1 is not.  The Home Screen shows the task as complete for GBP sync, however, one location is not synced as no reviews are pulling through.    They cannot fix this on their own and it requires help from support.
workaround: Reaching out to Partner Support.

summary: 2-step Approval Flow for Review Response
description:
Hi team,

The partner wants to have 2-step approval flow for one of their clients.

ex) Store manager A submits a response → Area Manager B approves it → Then go to HQ C for the final approval → The response is officially posted (Please refer Review Response 1.png)

Their other idea is to notify specific users when the response is created and when the response is approved. (Please refer Review Response 2.png)

The current feature does not support neither, can we add more features to the Review Approval flow?

Best,

Nami
workaround: N/A

summary: Ability to View API Response from Debug Screen
description:
Currently the debugging screen and the screen containing the API response are a separate screens. To effectively debug a query you often need both of these items (since core elements like _distance_ aren’t exposed in the debug screen). When doing debugging via the Search Logs, there’s no way to view the API response, only the debug screen.

It would be helpful to combine these screens so I can admin can view these alongside each other. Also, exposing the _distance_ alongside the result score would be extremely helpful.
workaround: Re-run query in test search

summary: Custom Geocode Fields in Content
description: Currently, the only geocode field within Content is the built-in address field. For many search use cases, entities need to be searchable / sortable by geolocation when only a city, region, or zip are present. It would be helpful to at a minimum geocode the Service Area field associated with address and/or at a custom field type that supports a geocoded city, region, or zip.
workaround: Use built-in address field.

summary: Testing/Staging Versions for Connectors
description:
When making updates to a Connector there is no way to save changes without those going immediately into production (particularly for Connectors with a schedule run cadence). It would be helpful to add a staging/testing version for Connectors that would allow an admin to make changes to mapping, transforms, etc. that wouldn’t impact previous versions until it was pushed to production and/or a dry run was accepted. 

Additionally, it would be helpful to “roll back” changes to previous versions of a Connector based on the outputs of a dry run. For example, if a dry run produced undesired results, the admin could revert back to a previous version of the Connector’s configuration.
workaround: Duplicate connector and test changes.

summary: Social media links for hotels
description:
Hi team,



Can we look at adding the social media links for hotel chains too?



Full context [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05DPGC206R/p1716472644880139|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05DPGC206R/p1716472644880139|smart-link] 



Doesnt say anything in the release notes [https://www.yext.com/release/fall23?target=additional-google-social-media-link-syncing|https://www.yext.com/release/fall23?target=additional-google-social-media-link-syncing|smart-link]  and unsure why we didn’t include hotels…



Thanks,

Laura
workaround: do in GBP manually

summary: Holiday Hours field update
description: When client is adding holiday hours in the platform, both hours boxes default to AM vs one to AM and the other to PM. Client would like the second hours box to default to PM
workaround: Change manually to PM

summary: Switch And/Or logic within a Connector Transform
description:
Currently within the conditional logic of a connector you have to pick And or Or logic - once a selection is made for any additional criteria it has to be all And’s or all Or’s; it would be helpful to toggle for each additional criteria so you could construct something like:
Do X if:

* X is true AND
* Y is true OR Z is true
workaround: Mutiple transforms

summary: Google Photo Rejection Identifier
description: Google will notify us that a photo was not accepted as it sits outside of their guidelines, however we do not mark in the UI which photo is triggering the message.
workaround:
[https://trikeryext-google-photo-failures-home-obtvfo.streamlit.app/|https://trikeryext-google-photo-failures-home-obtvfo.streamlit.app/]
^^ this is a great tool, but isn't necessarily a scalable solution. 

The ideal solution would be to mark the photos in platform that Google has rejected in Photo Gallery.

summary: Add a "Primary Domain" Integration Method
description:
For client’s using Yext as their primary CMS / website host the current integration options (Subdomain and Reverse Proxy) and the associated descriptions can be confusing for someone hosting their main/apex domain through Yext. 

Recommend adding another option (and supporting documentation) for organization’s using Yext to deliver their primary domain.
workaround: Add primary domain via subdomain integration

summary: Event Markers in Analytics
description:
To be able to track impact of various changes (e.g., launch of listings, a page, etc.) within Yext Analytics it would be helpful to add automated and custom event markers. Example event markers could include:

* Markers for when a specific entity(s) went live on particular publishers
* Markers for when a specific entity(s) Page was pushed to prod/went live and/or of a specific deploy
* Markers for when a configuration change was push to prod for a Search or Chat experience
* Customer markers with a defined date and event from the admin

Example from Google screenshot attached.
workaround: Document dates

summary: Payment Options to GBP
description:
Hi Team,

Currenltly paymentOptions field is only particlly synced to GBP. (Only some of the credit cards like Visa,MasterCard etc..)

Reference pubops ticket - [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1974003|https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1974003]

Kotoko

summary: Monitoring/Posting on Reddit
description: Not positive if this would fall under social or reviews, but First Command brought up that they actively manage their brand presence on Reddit. They have to monitor if there are threads about their brand and then combat any negative sentiment with on brand responses. They said they would love to manage this all through Yext somehow
workaround: they log directly into reddit

summary: Give Access to Move Entities between Subaccounts (Portal)
description:
Partners have requested the ability to move entities between subaccounts within the Platform.  

Subaccount 1: Location A, Location B, Location C 

Subaccount 2: Location D, Location E

Give a Parent level user the ability to move Location E into Subaccount 1 with Locations A, B, C. 

Help them to work independently and not overwhelm Partner Support. 

Likely not a standalone issue to impact Renewal, but impactful alongside other shortcomings for their use case.
workaround: Currently Partner Support can manage this on the clients behalf.

summary: Make Linked Accounts Inheritable from Parent Account (Portal)
description: Often times, Partners have 1 catch all GBP linked account, or 1 catch all FB account where they manage thousands of their clients.  Instead of going through the Linked Account process for every subaccount, it would be ideal to have the ability to add the Linked Account at the Parent Level and inherit that Linked Account down to all Subaccounts
workaround: None at the moment.

summary: Parent Level Users - Access to Some Subaccounts, Not All (Portal)
description:
Healow would like the ability to give a Parent Level user access to some subaccounts, but not all.  A user has to sit at the Parent level to add / remove services, and they must have access to the entire account & all subaccounts, but not every user should have access to all subaccounts.  

Some resellers have different arms of the business that should not be pulled together.  This might be challenging to manage at scale, but is being requested by their team.
workaround: Create a user role with its own UN/PW on every subaccount the user needs access to.

summary: Review Generation, Invite Templates via API
description:
RevLocal is looking for the ability to create and update Review Generation Invite Templates via API.  This would allow them to work at scale (they are in Portal), and would also allow for the use of newly release *Review Invitation Follow-ups:*

You now have the ability to send follow-up review invitation emails automatically within review generation email templates. From the new *Email Follow-up* tab under each template you can enable follow-up emails, and select the maximum number of follow-ups (3), as well as the number of days that pass before the follow-ups start.
workaround: Manual creation / editing, working out of 1400 different subaccounts.

summary: UI Flexibility for updating Font / Color of Nav
description: Ability to change color schemes and font within the platform.  Utilizing the Branding section to create better brand consistency.
workaround: Integrate via API

summary: Multi Account Reporting (Portal)
description:
Yahoo Small Business (once Aabaco, now Infinite) has requested a Parent Level Dashboard to see high level metrics of all entities supported in all subaccounts.  

We currently have exportable reports, but they are looking for something more comprehensive, and in one view. 

-Location Count, Listings Live Count, GBP Sync Rate, FB Sync Rate, User Login Rate, Duplicates Detected, Duplicates Suppressed, Review Count, Review Average Rating, Service Additions, Service Cancellations, the list goes on. 

It would be great to have this in one view, but then be able to drill into the tile and see more detailed information by subaccount, etc.
workaround: Exportable reports, not helpful at scale.

summary: Add Entity Scope for Listing Duplicate Notification
description: For all of the current Listing’s notifications (e.g., Invalid Location Data Issues, etc.) the admin can select which entity(s)/folder(s) they want to monitor that notification for, with the exemption of Possible Duplicates. It would be helpful to include the same selection mechanism for this notification type.
workaround: N/A

Can we get analytics slides to show the various, great features of this product? 

* Dashboards

Also - this is a relatively unique product and helps to have a visual when explaining the differences, pros and cons, etc of Knowledge Tags/Live API to Yext Pages
workaround: just showing examples on a call, but would be nice to have something to send to clients after calls.

summary: Yelp Slides
description: We have a few outdated, somewhat not-great-looking Yelp slides that have been tossed around for several years, but would be great if we can have PMM make new, on-brand Yelp Knowledge slides as this is a huge ACV booster/upsell for AEs and prospects/clients always have a ton of questions for the Yelp Knowledge Add-on.

summary: Listing Feature Slides
description:
Hi Team - 

It would be awesome if we could build slides for listing features in addition to the listing solution slides. 

A lot of the time during a deal cycle, its helpful for prospects to have content on the features of the products we have in scope so that if they are looking at multiple vendors, they can easily see things in the form of a deck, that set us apart. 

We show the market the listing features on the listing page on [Yext.com|http://Yext.com] (see screenshot) but would be great to have those in slides to use in-market as sellers. 

We have a ton of listing features that set us apart that are not in decks like: 

* The Verifier
* In-Platform LIsting Escalations
* Unique user roles (soci can only have 2 user roles) for listing management 
* self-serve listing launch flow
* listing un-synced messages 
* bulk verification readiness reports
* scheduled listing updates etc
* Listing Recommendations
workaround: Finding slides that other AEs have hacked together or building them manually

summary: User management and login data
description: Would it be possible for users to only see the user management and login data for users that have a similar scope or lower scope than theirs: so for example if someone has access to the ibis France folder, they should only see the login data for all the users who have entities in that folder
workaround: We created another custom role that allows the users management only and apply it to the entire account level - this works but the user sees it for everything globally and not only the brand that they care about

summary: "View as" option for clients
description:
My client ifm Technologies is requesting a "view as" button in the platform, so he can log in with the “view” of another user. 
As context, my customer is based in the head office and is currently onboarding field users for different countries. He is doing the trainings himself, and would like to do the trainings in a way that he logs in as a user in the region, so that during the training, the screen/admin account only looks like it would look for the user.
workaround: Login as user by logging on with their password

Currently it can be filtered by Actor type but client wants to filter by the Actor.

summary: Changing the behavior of typing in Japanese in Chat
description:
Hi team,

When typing in Japanese in Chat, for example, “エンティティを編集するには？ “ (How do I edit an entity?), I first type “えんてぃてぃ“ in Hiragana and then convert to Katakana “エンティティ” by pressing the Enter key and keep typing the rest of the message. But when I do this, the word “エンティティ“ is sent to chat while I’m still in the middle of the message because I have to please the Enter key to convert.

Also, the text I typed remains in the message bar.

To solve this problem, is it possible to change to pressing the Enter key twice to ask a question in chat in Japanese?

Please check out the video I shot of the behavior when typing in Yext Chat and when typing in Chat GPT. the Chat GPT behavior is the ideal one.

Thank you,

Koki

summary: Access control permissions for connectors
description:
Hi team - one of our partners (Phase2) building a FAD for a HC client (Sutter Health) is looking to leverage a File Upload connector to update holiday hours. However, they need to limit who can actually run that data connector, ideally by giving a certain user role the ability to run a specific connector without being able to access any other connectors. 

This is tied to an existing product feature request ([PRODIDEAS-115|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/jira/polaris/projects/PRODIDEAS/ideas/view/3959414?selectedIssue=PRODIDEAS-115]).
workaround: No current workaround. This is tied to an existing product feature request ([PRODIDEAS-115]).

summary: Transform that brings in more data from Yext
description:
Hi team - one of our partners (Arc4) manages a connector for Pizza Hut, which keeps their location data accurate. The partner is running into an issue where several custom functions are failing due to spurious API errors or exceeding the 10-second limit, which is causing the runs to abort. These failing functions make an API call to Yext to get the existing value of a given field (like GMB attributes). While it shouldn't take 10 seconds to make that call and do some logic, they are randomly sometimes hitting that time limit and or randomly sometimes getting an [error|https://www.yext.com/s/1906947/addData/connectors/17109/runs/18042452?tab=runErrors] from the Yext API (they've added a retry, but still getting errors).

An ideal solution here would be a feature that exposes an entity's current data in the connector without requiring a developer to make an API call. In the meantime, they are making a single Yext API call per entity (for this Connector and all future ETLs that they turn into Connectors, too), either in the function that is the source for the connector or in a function they use for a transform. This change will require some care/testing however, as it's a change that impacts multiple fields and functions (including open/closed functionality). As a quick fix, they will move some problematic transforms/fields to their own Connector (which they did with the GMB field/transform previously). This will allow updates for core fields (i.e., hours) that they are not having problems with.
workaround: Arc4 is making a single Yext API call per entity (for this Connector and all future ETLs that they turn into Connectors, too), either in the function that is the source for the connector or in a function they use for a transform. This change will require some care/testing however, as it's a change that impacts multiple fields and functions (including open/closed functionality). As a quick fix, they will move some problematic transforms/fields to their own Connector (which they did with the GMB field/transform previously). This will allow updates for core fields (i.e., hours) that they are not having problems with.

summary: Suggested Query Analytics
description:
Hi team,



Cricket has a big focus on analytics and wants to be able to track searches that have originated from their suggested queries. Our current workaround using Search labels isn’t accurate (example iPhone 15 - some people might select the suggested query, but others might just be typing in iPhone 15/might’ve been searching that anyway).
workaround: Our current work around is to apply search labels to match the exact query that is suggested, but this isn't the most accurate especially for shorter searches like iPhone and requires extra steps for the team whenever new queries are added or when queries are removed.

summary: Allow GIFs (Static and Non-Static) in Image Fields
description: Dyson would like to be able to display GIFs on certain Search cards, as well as certain Promo Banners. Currently, all image-related fields only allow for the upload of static GIFs, meaning that if a GIF is uploaded, the movement of the image will not display. This request came directly from Dyson’s Creative Team, so getting this feature implemented would be a big win at a higher visibility, which would allow for future upsells and build confidence in their current contract.
workaround: We do not currently upload GIFs for the Dyson team given the limitation.

summary: Increase limit on bulk remove access tool
description:
A common request for resellers is to use the bulk remove access tool: [https://www.yext.com/admin2/users/bulkremoveaccess|https://www.yext.com/admin2/users/bulkremoveaccess] to remove role permissions across multiple sub accounts. The issue is that some of these requests are for removing thousands, and sometimes tens of thousands of user permissions. The tool currently can only process 500 users at a time, so for some of these requests we find ourselves having to use the tool dozens of times before working through our entire list. 

If we could increase the limit on this tool, that would be a huge help to our team in processing the often last-minute user changes we get looped into. Since we've increased our frequency of product releases, we've also had an increase in Resellers asking for help making bulk user changes to hide different Product features from certain users before GAs.
workaround: Remove permissions 500 at a time

description:
Unable to configure the Olo Menu App. We have added additional menu item attributes such as ‘Spiciness’, Preparation Methods, Allergens as well Dietary Restrictions. These are all specified directly in Olo, but we are unable to configure the mapping of these to these fields, so they are being pulled in as Options. 

Bloomin' has all nutrition info as well as photos in Olo, but we are unable to pull in as we are unable to configure the app.

summary: Configuration of Olo Menu App

These are all specified directly in Olo, but we are unable to configure the mapping of these to these fields, so they are being pulled in as Options.

summary: Google Photo Rejection Identifier

[https://trikeryext-google-photo-failures-home-obtvfo.streamlit.app/|https://trikeryext-google-photo-failures-home-obtvfo.streamlit.app/]

summary: Bypass System Check for Reviews Only SKUs
description:
ServiceTitan utilizes two Reviews Only SKUS for Location Conflict, and also as a base sale to upgrade. 
Review Monitoring (GLOBAL-00000340)
Review Response (GLOBAL-00000349)

Although Reviews Only SKUs should always be passed through with no location conflict consideration / system check, they still hit a system check where support has to manually approve. This generally takes around 24hrs and are sometimes rejected in the system check process.  These SKUs are part of a purchase flow that is immediately followed by a ServiceTitan client self serve onboarding. The system check is slowing their sale to onboarding process and we want to remove the system check.

We know we cannot remove the system check from the SKUs above as other Partners are using them as well.  A [suggested solution from Carl & Sheila is to |https://yexttest.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/1088/DS-11773][_add additional parameters in the api add requests to allow resellers to skip duplicate checks._|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/1088/DS-11773]   

This would improve their onboarding flow and client experience.  We would see a near immediate boost in revenue if this feature was opened up and would signal partnership to Service Titan. 

Thanks,

Kim
workaround:
-Ping support to ensure service additions are approved
-Create custom, Service Titan only RM + RR SKUs with the system check removed

summary: Adding existing-filter logic to new saved filters on Knowledge Graph
description: Hi team, my client has asked for a way to set a Saved Filter that includes pre-set parameters from an existing filter. Essentially, something like '[pre-existing filter] AND [closed=yes]'. Waitrose is doing some testing on their prod account and they want to set up a filter based on the filter used for live pages. This risk with the current set-up is that if the original filter gets changed, that will mean their newly-made one is no longer valid.
workaround: A workaround would be for the original filter to never change, or the client will be notified if this does happen.

summary: Filter Analytics by Metrics
description:
Allstate would like to filter a Report by a metric, not just dimensions. Basically, I have a report where I want to filter out 100% and 0% results for Listings Accuracy since I only care about those with <100 and >0. This is so the Allstate team can take action on fixing listings accuracy issues. 

Here’s what I have to do instead as a workaround: 

* [This report|https://www.yext.com/s/501290/reports/getCustomReport?requestJson=%7B%22filter%22%3A%5B%7B%22filterValue%22%3A%5B%22location.address%22%5D%2C%22name%22%3A%22LISTING_FIELD%22%7D%2C%7B%22filterValue%22%3A%5B%22559%22%5D%2C%22name%22%3A%22siteId%22%7D%5D%2C%22tableSettings%22%3A%7B%22knowledgeGraphFieldIds%22%3A%5B%22location.store_id%22%2C%22location.business_name%22%2C%22location.address%22%5D%2C%22sortSettings%22%3A%7B%22sortAsc%22%3Afalse%2C%22sortColumn%22%3A%7B%22id%22%3A%22LISTINGS_ACCURACY%22%7D%7D%7D%2C%22metric%22%3A%5B%22LISTINGS_ACCURACY%22%5D%2C%22businessId%22%3A%5B%22501290%22%5D%2C%22displayFormat%22%3A%7B%22displayType%22%3A%22TABLE%22%7D%2C%22userId%22%3A%220%22%2C%22dimension%22%3A%5B%22ENTITIES%22%2C%22LISTING_FIELD%22%5D%2C%22timeFilter%22%3A%7B%22comparison%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22endDate%22%3A%222024-05-09%22%2C%22dateRangeType%22%3A%22DAYS_7%22%2C%22startDate%22%3A%222024-05-03%22%7D%7D] shows the accuracy of the Address field on FB for each entity
* Download as excel
* Filter to exclude 0% and 100%

* 0% are *Hidden Address = Yes* and FB is actually hiding address
* 100% is *Hidden Address = No* and FB is displaying address

* This will leave you with a handful of locations where *Hidden Address = Yes* and FB is still displaying the address, hence the inaccuracy.
workaround: Download to excel and filter there (as described above so I can hyperlink)

summary: Update User Password Criteria and MFA
description:
TUPSS had a security breach the week of 4/29/24 and is now looking to have us update our password criteria. They are requesting the following:

# Update password criteria: Minimum password length shall be 12 characters with MFA OR 16 characters without MFA, and have complexity rules, get that in place quickly
## Password Rules:
### Minimum of 12 Characters
### Cannot contain first name, last name or User ID
### Cannot use previous 24 passwords
### Must meet at least three of the four conditions:
### Uppercase Letter (A-Z)
### Lowercase Letter (a-z)
### Number (0-9)
### Special Character (e.g., !, @, #, $)
# Support not allowing substring.
## In this particular example, they don’t want us to include “UPS”, which will cover TUPSS, iShip, Store, and MBE
workaround:
They are working on integrating Yext into their SSO, which should take care of all password criteria. I just need the project scope and proposed timeline to give them as this is top priority at the moment. They also sent over an email that stated the above criteria needs to be met if we want to remain a UPS vendor, so trying to cover as many bases as possible here. 

I have attached their SAML apps documentation to review as well.

summary: GBP: Add Language Assistance Attributes to the API

summary: "Or" Operator in Content Filters

While they see they can do one filter AND another, it does not appear they can do OR another. (attached screenshot)
workaround: NA

summary: Google Photo Rejection Identifier
