Working with Owner.com (a new partner that supports restaurants) and they are looking for an in platform marker for photos that Google flags / cannot accept. 

[https://trikeryext-google-photo-failures-home-obtvfo.streamlit.app/|https://trikeryext-google-photo-failures-home-obtvfo.streamlit.app/]
^^ this is a great tool, but isn't necessarily a scalable solution. 

The ideal solution would be to mark the photos in platform that Google has rejected in Photo Gallery, but also include this same marker for Menu Photos.

summary: Auto Liking Google Reviews
description: We were walking through options for automated review response with IHA and they mentioned that it might be helpful to have a tool that auto liked reviews.
workaround: The client can go into Google and like the review manually.

summary: Route new entity adds through Suggestions
description: Currently only field changes on an existing entity can be routed through Suggestions. When new entities are created, it would be helpful to allow these to flow through Suggestions prior to the entity being available in Content.
workaround: N/A

summary: Client Facing: Export of Notifications by User
description:
Hi team, 

I have a support request from a client where they are requesting an export of all of the notifications set up for a user within their account. 

I let the client know this is not a product functionality and they can view the notification by user using the workaround below (and provided a screenvideo with steps for filtering). The client wrote back in escalated because they have had this done before in another support ticket. In tracking back the previous ticket this was able to be pulled via a product escalation: [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C04KFQRB86P/p1711993329196839|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C04KFQRB86P/p1711993329196839|smart-link] 

The client now has an expectation for this and is insisting that it is pulled again. If we could create this as a feature then we can prevent further one off escalations via the product escalations channel.
workaround: Using filters within the Notification dashboard (Any Owner or Recipient) to view the notifications within the dashboard.

summary: Data Recovery/History
description:
Hi Content team - one of our partners (Phase2) building search for a HC client (Sutter Health) was editing an Entity Reference field, and instead of changing the "Validation destination entity types" to remove an entity type, they instead modified the "Entity Type Availability" setting and removed "Healthcare Facility" from the list.

Not only did this remove the field from Healthcare Facilities, but when they realized this and went back and re-added it to the Availability, all the data in this field was lost. Fortunately they are still doing migrations and were able to re-run their Data connector to restore that data. However, this raised a red flag for them which was how we backup/restore content, if deleted. 

We have been informed that the data _should_ be available via the Logs API, and that there may be some queries the partner can use if they need an export, but this requirement increases the importance of the data recovery/history project. The partner does not fully understand this part of Yext and how to talk with the client about how they need to manage their content, which is becoming increasingly important for accounts that intend to author content in Yext directly.
workaround: Leverage the Logs API or use queries (from Rachel) to export the data and then re-import

summary: Google Q&A Sentiment Analysis
description: We don’t have a tool to pull out sentiment or common keywords in Google Q&A feature.
workaround: Manual work in excel

summary: In-platform notifications for approved/rejected review responses
description: Hi team, currently there is no way for review response requesters (eg. Franchisees) to get in-platform notifications when their response is approved/rejected by HQ.
workaround: No current workaround, aside from getting email notifications which is not preferred by the client as they can get lost amongst other emails and won't allow franchisees to focus on Yext when logging into the platform.

summary: Expansion of UR 443
description:
Bob’s Discount Furniture recently uploaded a template to all of their locations, with the intent of only updating photos and attributes; but the template included additional categories that the user was unaware of. The upload updated additional categories on all locations, and the update triggered re-verification across 146 locations. 

Google re-verification can be caused by any updates to business information, which include additional categories. To avoid any accidental category updates in the future, we would like additional category updates to also have a guardrail in place, similar to the guardrail for primary category updates, which trigger UR 443 and require a user to confirm sync before actually making the updates. 

While this idea came from a recent incident with one client, implementing this can be beneficial to all of our other clients who have listings on Google. If we have this implemented, it can reduce risk of accidental updates to business information, and reduce risk of triggering re-verification unintentionally.
workaround: No workaround, once re-verification is triggered on Google, a client has to manually verify their locations on Google in order to go live and make updates again. This is not an ideal situation, especially when bulk locations get affected as it can take a long time to manually verify each and every location.

summary: Share Posts on Facebook via Yext
description: PNC likes a lot of the posts that are on their brand page, and since it’s brand approved, they’d like the option to share that via Yext.
workaround: Since my contact, Anne, isn't an admin directly on the stand-alone MLO pages, she can't share a post from the PNC brand page to the MLO pages.

summary: Content Suggestions: Update Show Changes Behavior
description: Update the Show Changes functionality in the Suggestions UI to only display the updated portion of a field when selected. For clients like Morgan Stanley and Merrill Lynch (Bank of America) that have large and complex custom field types with several subfields implemented, the Show Changes functionality as it currently exists highlights the updated portion of the field, but that section is often below the fold when loading the Suggestions page, which causes confusion for users who are looking to review the updated field content.
workaround: No current workaround - users have to scroll down on the page to view the updated portion of the field.

summary: Apple Showcase Posting Improvements – Action Links & Image information for better user experience
description:
# Apple does not support links in the content of the post. To add a link to an Apple Showcase post, you will need to create an [Action Link|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/listings/apple-action-links/], and then select the Action Link as the Apple Showcase Call To Action Type. → Deutsche Bank filled out the fields for [one entity|https://app.eu.yext.com/s/100003011/entity/edit3?entityIds=100424027] as a test and find the user experience very limited that they have to go into content and fill out these fields instead of filling them out under Listings / Post when they try to create a post on Apple. 
# Apple rotates the image by about 10 degrees and this caused their first test to cut some of the text on the image. I guess the social posting preview window will help to solve this, but currently, there is no mention of this in the platform and no suggestion on how to avoid the rotation cutting parts of the image. They even tried with whitespace but Apple still cut parts of the image/text. A pop-up window or a asteriks with some information on this would improve the user experience.
workaround:
1. Making the changes to the Apple Action Links in Content one by one or in bulk.
2. Lots of trial and error but Apple does not allow to delete posts, so Deutsche Bank will not use this feature until the preview is not possible because they can't be certain what will be cut off of their images/texts.

summary: OutdatedProduct Training Videos
description:
*At the end of just about every single implementation, PCs send a platform training video that was made over 2 years* [*ago.*|http://ago.My]

[*My*|http://ago.My] *fear is that customers, franchisees of customers, etc. are missing out on a ton of valuable training content and are getting training that was relevant over 2 years* [*ago.*|http://ago.Do]

[*Do*|http://ago.Do] *we have any plans to improve this video or how we are going to keep these videos relevant?*

Video here - [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zSZRf-Wt7QAU9cWrdCDSc6HBfZVeoiZQ/view|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zSZRf-Wt7QAU9cWrdCDSc6HBfZVeoiZQ/view|smart-link]
workaround: Unknown

summary: Apply Custom Fields To Sub Accounts
description: Currently, there is no way to create custom fields at the parent level and apply them to sub accounts. Deploying the pages repo to an account will fail if the required custom fields don't exist there. This will create scaling issues for portal account clients and foresee this as a potential deal breaker.
workaround: Currently, there is no workaround except for manually created fields on each sub account.

summary: Pulling additional attributes from reviews
description:
Hi team,

Can we pull in additional attributes for Google reviews (hotels)?

Is this a new feature on Google? Is this on our roadmap?

Thanks,

Laura
workaround: NA

summary: Increase Search Volume Capabilities
description: Walmart needs Yext to be able to scale their Search volume to 8 Billion Searches/Year.  Product is already aligned with this request but formally submitting this request.
workaround: n/a

summary: Language Detection for Reviews
description:
Hi team,

This client had a review notification set up to alert them of any new reviews with the filter set for language to not include English. The client wrote into support confused as to why they were still receiving reviews that did not match the filters for any of their notifications (English Reviews over 3 star ratings). User [rebecca.leblanc@cypresshp.com|mailto:rebecca.leblanc@cypresshp.com].



Matt Hupman confirmed in a techops [*ticket*|https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-11688?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiMjU3YjkzZDk5NjJkNGRjNDk1Y2ZjMzdmOThiMzM4MzkiLCJwIjoiemVuZGVzay1qaXJhIn0][ |https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS-11688?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiMjU3YjkzZDk5NjJkNGRjNDk1Y2ZjMzdmOThiMzM4MzkiLCJwIjoiemVuZGVzay1qaXJhIn0]that this issue was due to Language detection not being present among publishers including Google - which essentially caused for their reviews to be pulled into notifications that were English and other languages. The client was frustrated by this limitation and asked for the MRR team to assist them in monitoring and responding to their reviews that were coming in other languages than English since they have no way of receiving alerts for these specific reviews. 

Is there a way we can implement Language Detection into the platform where even if publishers do not include language detection it can still be categorized by language when the reviews are pulled into Monitoring?
workaround:
MRR team has been given feedback provided by the client: 

"I would imagine as part of the MRR system, the goal is to respond to all reviews left on your client’s pages. Since we have patients of all demos and want to remain inclusive, we want to make sure to respond to revies in other languages. Given our scale, it is not practical to monitor these as they come in and respond to a non English review as a one of ourselves. I just want to relay this feedback in the hopes your MRR team is open to making adjustments to your workflows and is understanding of client needs. I understand the volume of MRRs your team handles per day is extremely high, but feel this should be a consideration in your process."

summary: Entity History Exports
description:
Hi team,

A client has reached out requesting a report of the entities they have had created between the timeframe of January - May and another report for their entities that were closed in this time frame. I provided them with Entity History links with the filters for for this data. But they would like to have an option to export the Entity History page.
workaround: For this request I told them they can refer to the data on the Entity History page with the filters applied but there wasn't a way to create an excel report unless manually transferring the data into a spreadsheet.

summary: Creating setting overrides based on folder in Redox
description:
Hi Team,

Currently as it stands today it is possible to create overrides to redox review gen settings (ie delay feature) based on labels in the account. The client thinks they need an additional layer to this and believe that folders provide this. With that in mind they would like to be able to set overrides based on folder and not just label. 



Thank you!
Wilhelmina
workaround: Setting overrides for redox settings is possible via labels today

summary: Time of day review generation invite send time customization
description: The client would like to customize the exact time of day that the review invite is sent out via redox review generation.
workaround: My understanding is that batch sending is an option but can it be customized based on time of day?

summary: Review Invitation Webhooks
description:
RevLocal sends Review Invitations via API and surfaces invitation progress in their client facing UI the same way Yext does.   They have to make full Review Invitation: Get calls to see the status of an invite: requested, sent, opened, clicked, reviewed. 



Ideally we would support Review Invitations with Review Webhooks so they are pinged when a ReviewID goes from requested to sent to opened to clicked, etc. 

Thanks,

Kim
workaround: Consistently sending Review Invitation: Get calls to pull status.

summary: Additional Status Detail for Ownership Conflict
description:
When Filtering down to Status Detail “Verification” it currently shows both Ownership Conflict and Verification needed Listings and there currently is no way to drill down on Ownership Conflict for example, unless you Export the Listings Statuses and use Excel to filter down on the Unavailable Reason.

I have had some complaints / concerns from Accor that this particular part of the platform is very confusing and does not have the best UX.
workaround: Export the Listings Statuses and use Excel to filter down on the Unavailable Reason.

summary: Specify Field Permissions based on entity type
description:
Currently it is only possible to specify permission entity, folder, and account but it is currently not possible to specify by entity type. 

We currently have a field that is used for 3 different Entity types for Accor, but the users should only have access to the field for 2 of those Entity Types.
This can currently not be set.
workaround:
* Remove the Field from the Entity Type 
* Create a separate CF

summary: Syncing languages to Google
description:
Hi team,



Client would like to be able to sync languages to Google. This is not currently available. 



Best,

Wilhelmina
workaround: No work around

summary: Syncing insurance data to Google
description:
The client would like to sync insurance data to Google. I understand that on Google this is a multi select field and for us it is free text but it would be VERY helpful to be able to manage this data via Yext.



Please please can we consider?



Thank you!
W
workaround: Adding via google

summary: Way to view all scheduled updates in account in centralized place, log, or export
description:
Hi Team!



The client is asking for a centralized way to view all scheduled updates within the Yext platform. This could be in the form of a page on Yext, a log, or an export. 



Thank you!
W
workaround: Looking at each profile individually

summary: Add Staging and Production Tags to Sites Deployment Endpoints
description: On Services, we use the sites deployment & generation endpoint to get the status of a deployment or any failed data updates. We then use this data for internal alerting but we only want to report on the production deployment. In the current state, it is impossible to determine which deployment is the production deployment.
workaround: We don't have a workaround at the moment so we are just alerting on the most recent deployment and likely missing several alerts. Ideally, we could filter to the production deployment with query params or the response would contain a flag for this info.

summary: Open Hyperlinks in Unavailable Reasons in a new tab
description:
Localsearch has requested that we open links that appear in Unavailable Reasons (typically to the Google Help Center) in a new tab. 

Localsearch iframes our Listings tab and Google does not like their Help Site to be iframed. So every time a user clicks one of these links they get taken to an error page instead of seeing the relevant Google Help Article.

See the attached GIF for an example. I hope this is a quick fix, since it’s causing a lot of frustration with Localsearch and their clients.
workaround: Technically users can CMD-click to open in a new tab, but that's not a viable option. So there is no work-around.

summary: 3rd layer for Sentiment Analysis keywords
description:
Hi Team,

Client suggested to add 3rd layer for Sentiment Analysis keywords for further analysis.

Currently we have only till 2nd layer.

Thanks!

Kotoko
workaround: N/A

summary: Review Monitoring/Response Columns
description: The customer would like to see additional columns for folders/labels from entities so they can filter and see which groups aren’t answering reviews.
workaround: Built a report in Analytics to show this, however, the customer would prefer to see it in the Monitoring platform.

summary: Editing review responses as an approver
description: My client (First Stop - renewal in Jan 2025) would like to be able to edit review responses as the approver when they are looking at submissions from franchise users. This is seen as essential to keeping them with Yext at renewal, so anything we can do here would be much appreciated.
workaround: Approvers can currently leave a comment for the requester to change the review and re-submit for approval, but they cannot make the changes themselves.

summary: Site-Level permission-ing
description: Right now when you create a SMB user that has access to 1 loc and they also have pages, the live domains do not respect the user permissioning.  So user 1 will see all active domains for other SMBs that they shouldn’t have access to.
workaround: no current workaround

summary: Upload Category Names Via File Upload Connector
description:
Hi team,

When the client was trying to use the new file upload via connectors tool it was not letting them upload categories based on name. This is challenging since in order to upload the categories via connector they would need to map the categories to the IDs. Do you think a simpler way could be considered?

Thank you!
W
workaround: Client can map the categories names to the IDs before uploading

summary: Content: Generate Geocode Data on Address and Service Area type custom fields
description: Generate geocode data on Address and Service Area type custom fields for use in Locators/Search
workaround: None, currently

summary: Content: Expose Address and Service Area Address Type Fields
description:
To support the Virtual Professional entity type feature, this request is to expose the Address and Service Area type fields in Content for use in other Custom Fields. This field type: 

# Should support the service area component in case the data being stored in there is simply a service area.
# Should allow incomplete addresses, ie only a service area, region(state) or city.
workaround: Creating a custom field type to store this data, with no built-in validation to support valid addresses

summary: Content: Saved Filter warnings when converting Entity Types
description: When changing the entity type of existing entities to a new type, a user should ideally be prompted with a warning about existing Saved Filters that the entity currently falls into that it would be exempted from as a result of the data change. For example, if a location entity is being updated to a Financial Professional entity, and currently falls into filters that leverage an Entity Type = Location criterion, the user should be prompted with a warning before the update is pushed through. More details can be found in the document here.
workaround: The user has to proactively check for any filters that leverage that entity type criterion before making entity type updates

summary: Content: Change Entity Type via API
description: To support the rollout of the Virtual Professional entity type for existing clients like Allstate, we’d like to request the ability to modify the entity type of existing entities in Yext Content via API. More details can be found in [the Virtual Professionals Services Feedback document here|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1QCrxNYCch0SAgdHE6xLYeikjr3ESHQd6RLMrSEYfTKA/edit?=&query=product+feature+request].
workaround: Currently, we can modify the entity type via the UI individually or in bulk, but making that update asynchronous to other updates to Pages/Search/ETLs for features that are dependent on the entity type puts deployment and use of these new features at risk.

summary: Additional Context Regarding Failed Entities in Connector Run Notifications
description: This customer has requested that additional details be added to the connector run results that explain why an entity has failed to update. Specifically, they asked for the notifications they receive regarding the connector to include those details, but because the notification contains the connector run results export, it seems likely that those errors would need to be added to the export. We would like to have a column on the “Failed entities” page that displays the error message we see in the “Entity Errors” tab.
workaround: Currently, there is no context provided in the run details. This means that, when the client receives a notification, they have to log into Yext to understand what the errors are that have caused their entities to fail to update.

summary: Ability to Segment User Access Based on Entity Type
description: The Church Yext userbase includes 4.79k users, many of whom should only have access to specific entity types.  Because of an organizational need on the Church’s side to have their Yext folder structure align with their internal, global content management strategy, most of their Events and Locations occupy the same “Areas” folder structure in the account.  This is causing significant issues for content access management on the Church’s end, as their users that manage their local Meeting Houses have access to a separate Church division’s Event entities.  There is currently no way to sustainably manage content access- we don’t have the option to build out a custom folder structure to prevent these access issues, and we don’t believe that we can manage entity-level access at scale.  This is becoming a major pain point for the Church, and one without a great work around. It would be great if it were possible to manage access at the Entity Type level as this would solve the vast majority of the Church’s user access problems.
workaround: It seems the only current workaround would be to delegate access at the Entity level, but this is not sustainable long-term for this many users + locations.

summary: Sandboxes  - Caribou Coffee - Working with Hannah Ho
description:
Worked with Hannah Ho on this. 



“this came from a client:

I could use someone to spot-check the way we’re using our sandbox environment. It gets the job done, but I’m hoping Yext has made the process better since we set it up. For example:

* No ability to switch between accounts. One uses SSO, one doesn’t.
* No ability to sync/promote content between accounts (I like the “[code moves up; content moves down|https://docs.wpvip.com/development-workflow/code-moves-up-content-moves-down/]” philosophy)

 

It isn’t bad enough to trigger a call right away. Just as Heather and I talk, it is a pain that’d be great if Yext could cure.”
workaround:
The customer said
to get logged out of Prod and into non prod

* log into single sign on
* log out of single sign on
* then log in to non prod separately

Ideas to help this From Hannah Ho - 

its no small project - for your context, it involves:

* deprecating the sbx environment and moving to prod
* limiting usage in test accounts / figuring out if/how we charge for it (prob some amount of free usage but if you start wanting to do really large tests, it does cost capacity since we do incur costs for supporting the tests)

and is in very early stages

summary: Ability to send both SMS and Email messages for review generation within the same account
description:
*Summary request:* Ability to send both SMS and Email messages for review generation within the same account.

 

*Feature outline:*

* *Ability to send via 2 modalities for a single request*
** Example: Send initial review request via both SMS *_and_* email
* *Customizable by touchpoint*
** Example: An SMS for initial request; emails for reminder messages (pending development of reminders feature)
* *Customizable by division/folder/label/provider*
** Example 1: Primary Care division would like to send SMS messages for initial solicitation + reminders
** Example 3: ENT division has one physician who does not want text messages sent. Configure him up to only send email messages; set up rest of department to use a combination of both.
workaround: Right now we can send either an sms or an email and not both.

summary: Generate reviews on Glassdoor and Indeed
description: CNO would like to be able to generate reviews from their agents on glassdoor and indeed. I know these publishers are on our monitoring roadmap, but will we be able to generate reviews for these sites?
workaround: They don't currently generate reviews for these sites

summary: Customize review balancing
description:
CNO wants to be able to change review balancing depending on the folder AND depending on the customer data file.

They generate reviews for people that interview to be an agent that they want to collect but not publish, but because they have review balancing on for the whole account half the time the interviewee is sent to Google.
workaround: we have review balancing on for the whole account

summary: Increase Rich Text character limit
description: Block uses a rich text field for their community posts that come up in search. If there are several comments on a post it could be longer than our 100000 character length. Can this character limit be increased? Are there any recommendations for another workaround in the meantime?
workaround: unknown

summary: Opentable Cover Photo field
description:
Hi team,

Cote a restaurant client here in the UK is having admin challenges with updating their cover photo in Opentable through Yext. When they upload new images to the photogallery in Yext they need to manually change the position of the photo they would like to be the cover photo for each restaurant. With 80 locs, this can take around 2 hours to get the right photo back in place. They have expressed a risk of churn if we are not able to present some way to alleviate the admin involved in the process today.
workaround: Introduce a cover photo field for Opentable (PubOps has confirmed with Opentable that this is feasible).

summary: Syncing logo images from Yext to Acompio
description:
Hello team,

My reseller, Ströer, would like to synchronize the logo images of their customers with Acompio. I believe Acompio has already worked on implementing this, and now we need to complete the work from our side to ensure that this field is also synced.
workaround: Manually uploading logo images on Acompio

summary: Ability to Dimension by State in Report Builder
description: Currently clients cannot dimension reports by state which causes them to be forced to manually manipulate exports from Yext report builder. Its a cumbersome process for clients who have a multi-state presence and want to pull reporting for something that is broken out/dimensioned by state.
workaround: Create a table chart, dimension by entity, include state as a column and then export to Excel to filter by state.

summary: Custom Role: Option to restrict the user roles that a custom role can select when creating new users
description:
When creating a custom user role, it would be helpful to have the option to choose which user roles this custom role is allowed to select when creating new users.

We don't allow users to create users with roles that have more permissions than they have, but there isn't an option to specify which roles they can select.
workaround: No workaround

summary: Exporting Text Assets
description:
Hi Team,

Piedmont uses MRR and stores their review responses in the asset section of the platform. They would find it very helpful to be able to export them so they could provide edits vs working via a Google doc. Can the team consider this please?



Thank you!
W
workaround: They can edit the assets via an export provided by our team.

summary: Custom User Roles For Portal Accounts
description: For portal accounts, it would be good to create custom user roles at the parent level and customize which sub accounts users can have access to. For example, some partners may want their sales teams to have view access only to their accounts but don't want them to see anything else in the portal. It would be good to create one username log in at the parent and when they log in, they see all of their sub accounts specific to their territory.
workaround: Current work around would be to multiple logins and usernames for the various sub-accounts. This would not be scalable in many cases.

summary: Factoring in data for providers who have left the Yext program in analytics
description:
Hi Team,

We had a call with Columbia today about analytics and during the conversation it came up when providers leave the Yext program we take their data out of analytics because we are only looking at data for profiles that have licenses. This was very concerning to the client who feels that this is not an accurate picture of what actually happened in terms of impressions/clicks to listings. They feel that this is an incomplete picture and would like us to be able to keep the historical data for the profiles are no longer in the program. We would also like to understand the context for why it is currently set up this way vs being able to include that data?



Is this something that could be considered?



Thank you and I am here to answer questions if needed!

Wilhelmina
workaround: I do not see a work around to this at this time.

summary: API Authenticators
description: Right now, anyone with the API key (and JWT, if enabled) can access the Live API. For security purposes, it would be helpful for financial services clients to have a functionality that authenticates that the originator of an API request is internal to the client or Yext. Ideally, this restriction will return a valid API response only if the originator is from the client or Yext IP addresses.
workaround: none

summary: Label logic to help scheduled updates
description:
What we need is the ability to schedule changes based on labels. For example at the moment, there are scheduled changes in place for all locations under a specific label. When we open new stores and add them to that label, we still need to individually add the scheduled changes for those specific stores. 

What we actually need is a rule in place scheduled changes are made to *all entities within X label, on a recurring basis (if required).*
workaround: None currently - manual effort

summary: % Sync Rate & Filter by Folder on The Publishers Tab
description:
Tesco want to easily be able to see the % Synced by Publisher in one view, they also want to be able to filter the [+Publisher Tab+ |https://www.yext.com/s/1296041/listings/publishers#s0=0&l0=25] by Entity / Folder / Label like you can do in the All Listings Tab



This is also really important with the current RON talk track for clients to easily be able to see their sync % 



Is it also possible to have sync % as a metric in Report Builder - at the moment the Listings Live isn’t the best view as it doesn’t also state the Total Listings for a client to work out their sync rate 



Requested also from CEMEX & ALLEGO
workaround: Export Listings and work it out manually example of CEMEX Sheet, but if there are a lot of Locations the sheet frequently breaks.

summary: Enable Phone Number Field Type as Subfields
description: Hi team - a partner (Phase2) building out search for a HC client (Sutter Health) is looking to put a phone number into a subfield of a custom field. However, “phone” does not seem to be an available field type for a subfield (see attached screenshots). They’ve identified using a Single Line Text field as a workaround, but this is not ideal. The partner has strongly requested that we support phone number field types as subfields for custom fields.
workaround: They’ve identified using a Single Line Text field as a workaround, but this is not ideal.

summary: Bank Branch Locator Publisher
description: Wells Fargo mentioned they have seen wells locations on this site [https://www.bankbranchlocator.com/|https://www.bankbranchlocator.com/|smart-link]

summary: Notifications - Trigger Alert Based on Changes to Count of Entities in a Saved Filter
description:
*Ask:* Add functionality to the Notifications product that allows for a user to setup a notification based on the entities in a Saved Filter changing.

User should have the ability to receive an alert in all of the following scenarios:

* Entity Adds:
** Every time an entity is added to the filter
** When greater than a single number (i.e. >10) entities are added to a filter
** When greater than a threshold of total entities in the filter are added (i.e. count of entities grows by more than 10%)
* Entity Removes:
** Same as all the above, but based on entities no longer qualifying for and being removed from the filter

User should be able to configure the frequency & summarization of the notification:

* Real-time updates
* Daily summaries
* Weekly/monthly recaps

*Related Slack thread for context:*[https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C03KFKFN7PV/p1713464271311729|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C03KFKFN7PV/p1713464271311729|smart-link] 

*Impact:*

* Inappropriate changes to entity data or Saved Filter criteria, from data integration automations or from manual adjustments, can inadvertently cause massive amounts of incorrect changes to Yext products:
** Pages to be created/removed incorrectly
** Listings licenses to be assigned/removed incorrectly
** Search results to be added/removed incorrectly
** Chat results to be added/removed incorrectly.
* It is possible for these changes to go completely unnoticed for long periods of time because the actual product itself is still functioning as expected and nothing has broken, but the content being returned is no longer correct.
workaround: There is a pseudo-workaround that attempts to prevent bad scenarios from happening upstream, by using Runbreakers in ETLs and Connectors. This can prevent bad data from being filed in the first place to ensure that there aren't mass closures or openings of locations, but this does not account for manual changes and does not account for when Saved Filters rely on criteria the runbreakers don't natively account for.

summary: Entity IDs Added to User Exports
description:
Hi team,

With the current Export Tool for User the Unique Identifier that is exported is the Yext ID (Access Controls: Scope ID Column). I’ve had a ticket where a client requested Entity IDs - as most clients typically use Entity IDs more internally (while on the Yext side we may use Yext/Locations IDs when troubleshooting). I was able to provide the clients with the Entity IDs for in their User Export with an Index Match from their account but think it would be a great added feature for Entity IDs to be included in this export as well! 

In addition, the client wanted to have the blank User Roles in the Column A populated ( when a user has access to multiple entities Column A-D is not repeated and blank in the sheet). The client wanted these rows populated because they wanted to be able to filter the sheet by Entity IDs to confirm what users have access to each entity filtering by the entity. When they would filter by the entity some of the Columns A-D would be blank because in the spreadsheet without filters, they are listed above the blank row as the user. 

Lastly, potentially moving Unique Identifiers to first columns? This was just an idea from me not a request by the client.

All the best,

Christina Perrier
workaround:
For the Entity IDs
Support / PC/ CSM/ Client pulling an Index Match or VLook Up from an Export of their Entities from Content

For User Blanks repeated for different entity access:
Highlight Column A > Ctrl G > Special > Blanks > In First Blank Row "=(row above ex. A163 for A164) > Ctrl + Enter

summary: Add Request Logic
description: BirdEye is running into issues where we are canceling a high number of add requests that should have been approved. When the address matches an existing location (even if the address is hidden in Yext), it shows up as a duplicate address in the matching file. This process was set up years ago, so I'm not sure if this was an intentional decision or an oversight. But SABs hidden addresses are often unique (especially for small businesses), so it's possible whoever designed this process was still using the hidden address as a proxy for whether the SAB was net new.
workaround:
As an immediate solution, Eden confirmed we can match Birdeye add requests separately. Our existing process does allow us to filter by parent business, so we can filter to Birdeye, and approve matches that meet the following criteria (and would have otherwise been rejected as an internal channel conflict)

Unique Phone

Existing Brand + New Geomodifier

Medium-term, we want to see if to see if we can update our existing matching logic so that our file will take SABs into consideration and to see if long-term SABs with different geomodifiers and unique phones can be approved automatically.

** Profile completion: Whether based on entity views or admin-selected fields, indicate fields that are not populated and a % indicator of profile completion
** Suggestions: Highlight pending content request and/or approval actions
** Connectors: recent/upcoming runs and statuses 
** Content Freshness: Entities that have no been updated for a set duration of time
* Modules for Search - there are currently none, could include modules for Experience Training suggestions / approvals, search term clusters requiring attention, etc. 
* Other Platform Modules - there’s a series of somewhat recent platform features that don’t have modules. These include:
** Notifications - a module for showing recent notifications (similar to the left nav window)
** User Login / Active users
** Open support tickets

summary: Automatic Intent Page Entity Generation
description:
While today it is possible to automatically create directory entities in the KG with the directory manager, unfortunately there is no automatic way of creating intent pages entities in the platform. It seems that in order to do it, it needs to be managed externally at yext.



This request is not associated to a specific client, but comes from tests that I have been doing on my own.
workaround:
Looking for some example on how the process works today I got to the account of Dolce and Gabbana, which is handling it in a smart way.

This is how they currently manage it:
Location ->[https://www.yext.com/s/3024330/entity/edit3?entityIds=15442473#section.Intent%20Page|https://www.yext.com/s/3024330/entity/edit3?entityIds=15442473#section.Intent%20Page]
Location Pages-> [https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home|https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home]

In the section intent page there are 3 "launch to intent pages"  entities selected (entities can be product categories, iconics, occasions/gifts, services )

The entity linkage basically triggers under the hood a webhook that creates 3 new Intent Page entites [https://www.yext.com/s/3024330/entities2#p0=entity&p1=contains&p2=corso+venezia+7&p3=&p0=entity-type&p1=is&p2=140982&p3=|https://www.yext.com/s/3024330/entities2#p0=entity&p1=contains&p2=corso+venezia+7&p3=&p0=entity-type&p1=is&p2=140982&p3=]

Which then creates the three following intent pages
[https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home/fine-furnishing|https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home/fine-furnishing]
[https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home/furniture|https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home/furniture]
[https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home/home-gifts|https://boutique.dolcegabbana.com/italy/milan/corso-venezia-7/milan-corso-venezia-7-home/home-gifts]

it seems that as of today it can only be done externally from Yext as Spotzer is doing:
1 - Create a webhook for entity updates
2 - Write a serverless function that receives the webhook payload
a - Check to see if location entity is being updated
b - If yes, check to see if product was linked to location
c - If yes, make management API call to create new intent page entity
3 - If new intent page is created, it should now appear on the site so that it can be accessed from the location entity

summary: Native Survey Functionality in Reviews
description:
To support more holistic survey creation, management, and analysis a series of features would be helpful to add to the Reviews product to support surveying use cases, including:

* More flexible Review object data model to store additional unstructured and unstructured fields alongside existing Review fields (in addition to existing labels) 
* Expose additional survey fields to Analytics and Sentiment Analysis to support holistic analysis of Survey and Review responses 
* Ability to trigger webhook/function hooks based on Review criteria and/or as a part of Workflow logic (for purposes of triggering case creation in 3rd-party systems)
* Ability to create survey templates and forms directly within Yext (similar to creating Review Gen templates)
* Ability to to easily ingest CDP/CRM data into a survey to ensure customer level data (e.g., customer type, related transactions, etc.) can pre-populate in surveys and be accessed by analytics for dimensioning and/or filtering reports
workaround: Various workarounds

summary: Review/Survey Gen for NPS score calculation
description: This client is very interested in bringing reviews (monitoring/response/generation) for their ~170 locations over to Yext from a competitor (currently using Rep) however one thing that they need and that is a dealbreaker is the ability to generate reviews/surveys for NPS score purposes. summary: Staging Version of Entities in Content
description:
There is no first class support for drafting/versioning content in the KG.

The ability to preview content changes within a pages template is clunky. Currently, the only way to preview a content change in a staging environment of the page is via a suggestions workflow with permissions set up where the user suggesting the change has to be different from the user who can ultimately approve the change. This is more or less okay if the customer has a requirement for a suggestions/approval workflow where different users/stakeholders need to have different permissions, but for a customer who does not have a requirement for an approval workflow and who simply want to provide their content teams with the ability to view/test content updates on the page before pushing it live, this is complicated (and potentially impossible if the customers log into Yext via SSO and therefore do not have the ability to switch between users/accounts with different permissions).  

A lightweight solution to TJX’s use case specifically would be the ability for the same user to suggest the change, preview the change and approve the change & push it live - instead of needing to navigate between multiple accounts to suggest & view the change and then approve it. 

This is currently negatively impacting Location Pages implementation and if the customer is not okay with this workaround, this will put the renewal at risk. We’ve had tons of customers request a kind of draft mode for Entities (to then flow downstream into all of our products across the board) and have lost deals as a result of not supporting this feature is a robust way.
workaround:
1. Add users to TJX's account
2. Make sure each member of TJX's content team has Content Requester permissions which will trigger a suggestions workflow which will enable the pages preview feature – in this role, the content team members will be able to suggest content updates and view them within the pages template but they will not be able to approve the change and push it to production
3. Set up a superuser account that the team has access to with Content Approver permissions
4. Once a content team member suggests a change, previews it on the page, and is happy with the change, they need to log out of their own account and log into the superuser account to push the change to production (if a customer logs into Yext via SSO, this might not be possible and therefore this workaround might not work in all cases)

summary: Support Relative URLs in HTML
description:
Hi team - one of our partners (Phase2) building a search experience for a HC client (Sutter Health) is importing an HTML body field with embedded images. The images have relative URLs, such as {{/images/my-image.jpg}}. When they try to use the Convert HTML data transform, they get the below error:

{noformat}Error uploading photo: Get "/images/my-image.jpg": unsupported protocol scheme "".{noformat}

Their current fix includes doing a Find/Replace and adding {{http://www.sutterhealth.org/}} to the image {{src}} field, but they don’t want to have absolute URLs in the HTML field, they’d like to keep them relative. They are currently using a Rich Text v2 field because the client will ultimately author content directly in Yext. 

Per Sam’s feedback, this is an issue with converting from HTML to AST. The feature request in question will be to support that conversion in Yext, as opposed to having clients/partners need to use another HTML to AST converter and upload directly to the AST.
workaround: Their current fix includes doing a Find/Replace and adding [http://www.sutterhealth.org/|http://www.sutterhealth.org/] to the image src field, but they don’t want to have absolute URLs in the HTML field, they’d like to keep them relative.

summary: Exact Match option for Plain Text
description:
Hi team - one of our partners (Phase2) building out a connector for a HC client (Sutter Health) has been trying to map fields in a JSON source to option-select values in Yext. However, sometimes the “Plain Text” option isn’t a full exact match of the field, which has been throwing off errors indicating that the fields do not align, such as “Value must be a valid option” or “Values not working”. 

* Would be nice if the Preview tab had a way to show the records that were generating the validation errors
* Would be nice if there was a way to perform an Exact Match in the Find/Replace without making everything a {{^xyz$}} RegEx pattern
* Might update the docs to clarify that the "Yext ID" for the Category references can be the "API Name" and not just the API numeric id value
* Would be nice to have an easier way to map source->dest values rather than having lots of Find/Replace rules.

Here is a link to the thread for this request (which includes more detailed context on the problem): [https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1711398750968749|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1711398750968749|smart-link] 

The partner has devised a workaround (see below), Rachel is aware, and this is _not_ part of an upcoming renewal (rather a current project), but we are now in a motion with the partner where they feel comfortable providing these feature requests to us.
workaround: They have been using RegEx and ^ and $ anchors.

summary: URLs per location on GBP offer posts
description: I'm a big fan of the new release's GBP Offer Posts test. This is a feature I've been waiting for and am excited to use. However, the offer posts lack a feature that Yext's standard GBP posts have: There's no ability to make each location's posts have a unique URL. Instead, there's just a "Redemption URL" field which is the same across every location the offer is made on. Given how hyper\-local GBP Offers are meant to be, this is a huge disadvantage, since posts made to multiple locations can't drive clicks to that store's unique local page.    We ran a test to see how using unique local URLs versus a single URL for all posts affected our conversions. We selected 208 stores and first made manual offer posts to each store, with the offer linking to that store's local page on our website. These posts ran for 8 days and resulted in 37 conversions total. We then took the same group of stores and made a post through Yext using a single URL for all locations with identical copy and images. The single URL post ran for 15 days and resulted in just 10 conversions.    There's a clear difference in the effectiveness of GBP Offer posts that link to a unique local page versus a single landing page. Local pages are far more effective for getting conversions. I'd like to see the same URL options for Offer posts as there are for standard GBP CTAs.
workaround: We could submit each post manually for every location but this takes quite a bit of time and effort to do.

summary: Add support for bulk GBP Offer posts
description: Google Business Profile natively supports the creation of multiple types of posts, including Update, Event, COVID Information, and Offer posts. That latter type, Offer posts, is an extremely effective tool for business promotion. Offer posts place a CTA directly under business listing in Google Maps searches and have more advanced field support than other post types, including:    * Headline    * Start Date    * End Date    * Offer Details    * Coupon Code    * URL    * Terms \& Conditions    In our manual tests of offer posts, we've seen stellar results. We made an offer post to 200 locations and an identical update post to 1000 locations on Friday, 3/17. By Monday morning, 3/20, we had 41 clicks and 11 reservations on the offer post, versus just 7 clicks and 1 reservation on the update post. The return is nearly good enough to be worth spending hours doing manual posts.    A bit of searching around tells me that competitors already have bulk offer posts available in their interfaces. It would be great if Yext could prioritize adding support for this incredible tool.
workaround: NA

summary: Opt Out LinekdIn & Twitter

We are unable to do this for LinkedIn and Twitter so it always appears as though there is a task to do regardless of weather the customer wishes to include that platform or not.    We use the statuses on the dashboard to send emails, sms and app notifications as well as display a tasks to complete tile on the app to show the customer what additional value they are still yet to open with the product they have purchased.    This means that customers who don't want Twitter or LinkedIn linked up have no way of opting out of those communications as they are unable to indicate their preference via the dashboard and in turn us use that to omit them from comms.
