workaround: Working on a connector transformation that updates two custom fields and then sorts those two fields separately on the vertical

summary: Search Config to map Keyword with Lat/Long to enable NLP filter for nearby search
description: We want to map Latitude Longitude information with certain keywords.    i.e. Disneyland is at XX/YY then I can search locations near Disneyland    In Japan, there are "neighborhoods" that are not in the location address, but people expect to be able to search by that term.    i.e. "Kanda" is a neighborhood in Chiyoda, Tokyo, but a standalone "Kanda" does not actually exist in Chiyoda.    "Kanda XX YY" exists, but since the addresses in Japanese do not have spaces in them like in English, searching Kanda will not return these results.    Also, many Japanese users expect to be able to search near a certain area, even if the entities are not in the query.    i.e. no actual entities located in "Kanda", but when searched for "Kanda", entities near "Kanda neighborhood" is expected    If we can map "Kanda" to a certain point on the map, this issue can be solved, as searching "Kanda" should enable NLP filter as "Locations near Kanda".    Also, we need this functionality to map train station data with its Lat/Long with the same reasons.
workaround: Add the keyword "disneyland" to all the entities that are near disneyland.    Add all the stations near the entities as keywords in the entities.

summary: Ability to set a Radius on "Near me" Functionality
description: At the moment when you search for a query using "near me", sometimes results appear that are very far as there is nothing "near me". From a User Journey POV this is not great as it is not really close by.    Therefore it would be great to have a functionality on the front or backend to make the max radius allowed for that "near me" query configurable.
workaround: This is possible to do using hooks, however this is more complex to do and clients would not be able to do this themselves without yext dev hours.

summary: Direct Answer on FAQ Answer
description: When an user enables Direct Answer on the Answer field for the FAQ Entity Type, the Answer should be returned if the query matches the name of the FAQ Exactly regardless if other entities are returned in the set.
workaround: Current workaround is to enable Document Search on the Answer as described [here](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/community/t/direct\-answer\-with\-faqs/1380/7). There is a limitation though if the Answer's text is not close enough to the Question to not trigger a Document Search Featured Snippet.

summary: Filtering Searches Based on Domains
description: It would be great if Searches could be filtered based on Yext Pages vs. outside domain like a location page vs. a corporate site. This would be super helpful in telling a more comprehensive story or having a better understanding of a users journey.    Recommendations for search improvements based on location pages traffic may differ from the recommendations that may be provided if the traffic was coming from the corporate site/none Yext domains.
workaround: Not that I am aware of.

summary: Allow Negative Vertical Bias
description: For certain verticals such as Links, it's important for it to always return returns but it should almost always follow not appear lower in the SERP than structured results. If a query rule is added to demote the vertical it interferes with the Seach Merchandiser (becomes Read\-Only) and setting a threshold may cause the vertical to be omitted from the results. It would be helpful to have the ability to set a negative bias that effectively demotes a vertical, without having to hardcode query rules.
workaround: Demote vertical, but causes issues with Search Merchandiser

summary: And/Or Boolean operators with the Answers Algorithms
description: Hi HH team    My suggestion would be for the continued expansion the search algorithm to cater for and/or operators. For example, with Yext powering recipe searches, website visitors may search for:    Recipes made with chicken and tomatoes  Recipes made with chicken or tomatoes    Both searches should yield different results.
workaround: Text Search across a custom ingredients list, where AND based searches would default to appear at the top (even when the search query says OR)

summary: Answers - Advanced Search Operators
description: Hi Team,    I recently had an inquiry from a support client asking if we have [advanced search operator](https://ahrefs.com/blog/google\-advanced\-search\-operators/) functionality. For example, using AND or OR in the search to fine tune a query. We don't currently have this, but I think this type of functionality could be helpful because some competititors already have it, so adding it to Answers removes it as a value\-loss from transitioning from one of these competitors.    Best,    Jon
workaround: None

summary: Adding a "dial" style facet
description: Currently, facets are limited to checking boxes or applying a radius. I think it would be great to have a dial style facet for factors like price or time that would benefit from having a range selected by the user.
workaround: None!

summary: Bot Blocking in Answers
description: I'm working on an account that has had recent "bot attacks" in Answers, in that bots are searching the same query many times per minute / hour. This has an impact on the analytics and capacity of searches. It would be great for the Yext CSM / TEM teams and eventually Yext customers to be able to identify, flag, and ultimately remove these bot searches without having to consult engineering. (Perhaps once customers can do this, they can only request the removal, then the CSM or another team member has to review and approve it)
workaround: Submitting a ticket to engineering. I can share a relevant email thread that includes Zendesk and Jira links if that'd be helpful.

summary: Answers - Random Relevancy with NLP
description: Add "random" sorting criteria that does not conflict with the Relevance criteria. Currently, we cannot sort by a field in between relevance and random relevance.    EX: Sorting criteria as:    * Relevance    * Field    * Random Relevance    Client use case:    Cedars is looking for a different form of behavior like the following:    * Match query is NLP filter on clinical term, specialty.    * Boost Employed type who match the query (Employed, Affiliate, Private)    * Randomize the results of the Employed type
workaround: Sort based on Relevance, then the c_sortingPriority field (and leave out Random Relevance). However this is not exactly the intended end result.

summary: Increase Answers API Raw Results to 100 or More
description: Currently, in the Answers \> Home view in platform, a user can see a maximum of 10 entities per entity type when testing the Answers API response. This is helpful, but since it only shows 10 entities, its limiting for QA scenarios where we may expect to see dozens or even hundreds of entities in a search result page, but can only see the top 10; this limits our ability to thoroughly QA the full results set. It'd be great if this view in Answers could be set by the Answers Admin/ Developer to show a certain amount of entities (perhaps from a picklist of 10, 25, 50, 100, or 250 to start).
workaround: In platform, there is no workaround; you can only see the top 10 entities in each vertical.

summary: Exact Text Match for Queries in Quotes
description: Google offers new results when a user submits a query in quotation marks. These results only include pages where the exact text is included. This feature would help users looking for a very specific term amongst a set of entities.
workaround: No current workaround (i don't think)

summary: Getting confidence scores for individual search results instead of verticals so that results from different verticals are intermixed.
description: Not being able to do this currently means we're stuck with more than 1 result from a particular vertical. For example 4 results from "Community forums" appear before the first result from "Docs" because the community vertical has a higher confidence score.    This is incorrect, as the best search result might be a *combination* of community forum and docs results \- not a sequential list.
workaround: There isn't currently a workaround.

summary: Search Logs Data Available Via API
description: Hi Team,    My client is very interested in getting the data that is in the search logs interface in search either via API or an exportable format. The reason they want this is to do more advanced manipulation on the information that is available currently in the analytics suite. They tried to export the data but since the data set appeared to be too large since there is no way to filter by date the data did not pull.    If the team would consider this it would be of great interest to my client!    Wilhelmina
workaround: No work around now other than just viewing data directly in Yext

summary: Option to add variable weight to query rules and sort rules
description: Query rules and sort rules are completed on a yes/no basis (i.e. boost or bury). It would be great to be able to create rules with a specific weight. I.e. boost "Cardiology" by +1 and "pediatric" by +2 so that a search for "pediatric cardiology" would return pediatricians and then cardiologists.
workaround: Layering different query and sort rules

summary: Search: Code Snippet Generator UI Update
description: Two UI updates for interacting with the Search Code Snippet Generator:    1. Make the integrate hyperlink that opens the code generator overlay more visible: Replace the hyperlink with a button. Some of my client's didn't notice they could click on *Integrate Search on Your Site* as the underline appears only when you hover over the text.    2. Make it easier to understand that the code generator consists of two steps: Show both results page and search bar code snippets on one big screen instead of letting the user click through multiple steps.  Some clients close the generator after copying the results page snippet instead of clicking continue to proceed with the search bar steps.    Both save us time answering questions about where exactly to find the code snippets for both elements and guiding users through the UI multiple times.
workaround: NA

summary: Copy Configuration Version
description: Hi team,    I recently ran across a ticket where a client was requesting a Search config update, but the Configuration Version in Production was 80 versions behind what was in Staging. The differences were significant, and I didn't want to apply any updates to Production other than what was being asked in the ticket I was working on. As a workaround, I downloaded the config for Production and pasted it in the JSON Editor to save to a new Configuration Version. However, this method was clunky, and the config JSON I downloaded as a text file was ordered differently from what was in the JSON Editor, which made for a very confusing experience. It would be nice if there was an option next to the "Download Config" button that let you copy a previous Configuration Version to avoid applying unwanted updates. Example scenario:    STAGING \- 176    PRODUCTION \- 100    Goal would be to copy Version 100 as Version 177 so that updates between 100 and 176 aren't included    There would likely have to be some sort of workflow because when I used the workaround of downloading and pasting the config, I had to address a few errors before I could save e.g., an Entity ID for a deleted entity had to be removed from the config.
workaround: Download the config you're looking to base off of and copy that into the JSON Editor

summary: AND Search
description: **Currently,**    Yext search currently allows user **only "OR" search** when the search query is combined with some words.    If, for instance, you search for "Washington hot springs," separate results for "Washington" and "hot springs" will be returned, which means articles related to hot springs in Tokyo will also be included in the search results.    This is not what they intend.    **Ideally,**    We'd love Yext Search to provide **"AND" search condition**, too.    This feature is considered essential for multiple search queries and natural language queries.
workaround: NA

summary: As an admin, I would like to be able to export search logs that include bot searches
description: Bot searches are excluded from the search log export today. I would like to see them all.
workaround: NA

summary: Boost Relevance by Source
description: In some instances, organizations want to boost/bury results based on the source of the data. When multiple data sources are combined in a single vertical it becomes difficult to use thresholds/biases to achieve this behavior. Rather than having to group entities into filters to create open\-ended boost query rules, it would be helpful to add the ability to assign a bias based on the source of entity (either a specific Connector or a URL pattern for website based indexes).
workaround: Query rules or assign sources to vertical 1:1 and utilize thresholds/biases

summary: Create Results Sorting Rules
description: I've got a lot of situations where I'd like to not only boost content, but sort that content one the basis of one of the fields in it. It would be great to have the ability to do that either with an addition to existing Query Rules by, say, allowing a "sort filter" to be applied, or perhaps to add another capability (e.g., Query Sorting) to Search.
workaround: NA

summary: Search Diff in Backend Configuration with User History Logs/Export
description: Feature to easily perform a JSON diff comparison between search configuration versions natively inside the platform. In addition, the ability to perform/configure an automated export in order to store this history of changes in an external archival system for highly regulated industries where this is required.
workaround: 1. Click the configuration version tab and identify the versions you want to compare    2. Look at who made the changes, when, and then download the JSON config    3. Use a common JSON diff tool to compare    4. User history and JSON diffs are separated and there is no way to export to archival besides manually downloading and uploading into archival tool but still missing the user data.

summary: Remove Internal Searches from Search Capacity
description: I have had several clients ask for more clarity on how many searches their corporate team is making in their search experience to ensure that their capacity is not being used by their team.
workaround: NA

summary: Make nlpfilterOrder Configurable via UI and Force User to Define
description: It would be great to add a drag and drop to Vertical Configuration to allow an user to define the nlpfilterOrder via the UI. It would also be good to automatically do this to force users to define that order so that they think through how they are applied and how tiebreakers should be decided. In this idea, any new fields set as nlpfilterOrder would be last in priority until re\-organized by an user.
workaround: JSON Editor

summary: Option to use React Components without TailwindCSS
description: **Add namespaced selector classes to the React Components, which are not included in the** `customCssClasses`    Currently if I want to use. just the `<SearchBar />` React component and use the default styling \- I would need to import `@yext/search\-ui\-react/bundle.css`. Unfortunately, this bundle contains all the TailwindCSS styling, which overwrites the default styling of our app and conflicts with Bootstrap. We also don't really want to import so much styling just for 1 component.    If I choose NOT to import this CSS bundle and style the components myself, there is no way to add custom classes to all of the used elements, because not all elements are included in the customCssClasses object and their classes are hardcoded. Also the customCssClasses don't overwrite the existing ones, but merge with them, which still causes issues if we are using Bootstrap in out app instead of TailwindCSS.    It would be helpful to have a way to target all the necessary elements for custom styling and not be dependent on TailwindCSS.
workaround: NA

summary: Limit Results based on Semantic Similarity
description: A client has raised that it does not make sense that you can only dd a threshold for the entire vertical and not be able to set a limit on what results is displayed based on what the semantic similarity is.
workaround: N/A

summary: JSON Diff Viewer for Search Configuration Versions
description: It would be super helpful to have an in\-platform JSON diff viewer to compare changes between two config versions for debugging, validating changes recently made, reverting to previous version of a config, etc. Taking some inspiration from the Monaco editor: [https://microsoft.github.io/monaco\-editor/playground.html#creating\-the\-diffeditor\-navigating\-a\-diff](https://microsoft.github.io/monaco\-editor/playground.html#creating\-the\-diffeditor\-navigating\-a\-diff) (we already use Monaco in the platform, so this shouldn't be too much to implement!)    This would be a good opportunity to deprecate/change the current implementation of version comparison, as it is not widely\-used or as intuitive of a use case as this.
workaround: Currently, the flow is:    1. Download the config versions to compare    2. Open VSCode and create two files    3. copy and paste the configs into those files and check diffs in a split window    OR:    1. Follow step 1 from above, but for step 2 go to a site like jsondiff.com and paste the configs there

summary: Allow users to set “Entity Type” to Dimension and set search related metrics to Metrics at the same time.
description: Please enable to set "Entity Type" to Dimension and set search related metrics including "Searches", "Search Clicks", "Searches with Knowledge Graph Results" to Metrics on the Analytics report.
workaround: No work around so far. Currently, users can only set Entity Type on Advanced tab after setting All Entities. That's not what users want to do.

summary: Direct Answers Return Yes/No Based on Whether Content Exists
description: We're currently using an NLP filter on long list of supported features on my publishers vertical. When someone searches for a specific feature, only the publishers with those features show up. We'd like to go a step further to support queries like "Does X publisher support Y feature?" Ideally we would just get a Yes/No direct answer based on whether that specific feature is included in this field for that specific publisher.
workaround: * Currently we're just using an NLP filter for the feature field and since the publisher is searchable too, the relevant one would surface first in results.    * We could also make a Yes/No field for every possible feature and set all those feature fields as direct answers, but since we have a long list of features, this is not feasible.

summary: Defining Phrase Matches in Answers
description: In the Answers Search configuration it would be helpful if we could define explicit phrase matches. There are terms that when they appear together have a different meaning than separately. Defining phrase matches would require the engine to treat the phrase as if quotation marks surrounded it.    Terms such as: **dress shirt** versus **shirt dress**. These terms have very different meaning and the order of the terms dictates what a relevant result would be.    This feature works hand\-in\-hand with another Idea Board request to allow users to enter quotation marks as part of the query.
workaround: None that I am aware of.

summary: Include API Key Param in Experience Preview API Request URL
description: In Experience Preview, after exposing the API request associated with your query, there is a "Copy URL" button which allows you to quickly copy the request URL to be used elsewhere. The only issue is that the request URL within the platform does not have a API Key parameter which is required when hitting the API outside of Experience Preview.    Right now, if I copy that in\-platform URL and paste it into my browser, I immediately see an "Invalid API Key" error. This copy and paste feature is really useful, so it would be great if the api_key parameter was included in the URL for requests being made in Experience Preview to avoid having to add it manually when using the request URL outside the platform.
workaround: After copy and pasting the URL via Experience Preview, you can manually add the api_key parameter to the URL before making the request.

summary: Buttons to Automatically Copy Experience Details
description: It'd be great if there were automatic "copy" buttons on the Experience Details section within the Answers tab. I've mis\-copied API keys a few times while sharing details or embedding Answers on external sites.
workaround: Highlighting, copying, pasting. Obviously manageable, but error prone.

summary: Markdown Support for Direct Answer (Not React, But Themes)
description:
Background * markdown for Direct Answer is supported for React, but not for theme. Issue * Since the crawled HTML tags cannot be directly utilized, pages on the client's website that are displayed as tables or lists are presented with different HTML structure, such as `

` tags, in the case of Direct Answers.
workaround: No workaround

summary: Option to delete or deactivate Query Rules when removing search vertical
description: When deleting a search vertical in the search configuration, admins will receive an error message when attempting to remove the vertical. Rather than requiring users to delete all associated rules it would be helpful to have an option for deleting or disabling all query rules associated with that vertical as a part of the removal flow.
workaround: Manually deleting all associated rules

summary: Salesforce search results page component - expand width to show facets on lefthand side
description: For clients using the Salesforce search results page component: facets/sorts are available via link on the top righthand side of the results, but would like to have the ability to place them on the lefthand side. This is an example: [++https://communitysearch.influxdata.com/?query=iox1++](https://communitysearch.influxdata.com/?query=iox)
workaround: N/A

summary: Search検索ログの項目「日付（UTC）」の表示方法
description: 検索ログの項目「日付（UTC）」の表示方法を、日本のお客様にも利用しやすいように、変更もしくは可変にしていただきたいです。  （現状）02/25/2024, 01:29 PM → （要望）2024/02/25 13:29  ※年月日の順で、24h表記にしていただきたいです。
workaround: NA

summary: NLP Reset for Incorrectly Spelled Terms in NLP Training
description: Hi Team,    Out client reached out because they saw NLP was taking a search for Nephrology spelled wrong and NLP was changing that to Neurology and the client made a correction to say it was a bad fix and both spell check. That being said in order fully correct for this issue it appears the client needs to go through all instances where this term is getting flagged by NLP before it can get corrected.
workaround: To find all instances of the NLP filter acting on that term and reject them but it would be easier to do this for all instances at once.

summary: Remove Facet Cap
description: Hi Team!    We would like to request that the facet limit of 1000 facets is removed. We are requesting this because the cap is causing the following issues for our client:    1) This is inhibiting our client from creating filtered FAD views that will populate on Location pages in their new experience.    2) Users of the FAD experience do not know a Location search on FAD caps at 1,000. This means for this client who has 1,495 locations in Yext, almost 1/3 of them will not show up in the FAD Location search.    If this could be considered it would be greatly appreciated!    Thank you!    Wilhelmina
workaround: There does not appear to be a work around.

summary: Answers - Average Search Volume By Site Visitors
description: On the analytics page for Answers, we should have a stat box that shows the average number of searches and site visitor performs per visit.
workaround: Currently, we have to go to the report builder and create a repost based on total searches and search sessions. Once that data is pulled we have to go perform quick math and divide the two numbers to get our average searches by a site visitor.

summary: Adding an 'ignore' option for NLP filter trainings
description: Instead of just offering the options to accept/reject suggested NLP filters, it might be useful to add an option to 'ignore' a suggestion.
workaround: The current workaround is to actually ignore the suggestions which results in the suggestions to pile up more and more over time.

summary: Boost Results by Source
description: Reposting due to error.    In some instances, organizations want to boost/bury results based on the source of the data. When multiple data sources are combined in a single vertical it becomes difficult to use thresholds/biases to achieve this behavior. Rather than having to create a series of query rules for large amounts of entities, it would be helpful to be able to apply a boost/bias to a particular source (e.g., by Connector or URL attributes) or by Entity Type.
workaround: Query rules or mange or map entity types 1:1 to verticals and use biases.

summary: Answers data on query structure
description: Hi Team,    We do a really great job of providing data on the ability to group together queries that have similar intent. That being said it would be helpful if we could have a way to group together queries of similar structure. An example of this would be queries that all contained "near me" in the search.    Thank you,    W
workaround: There is not a work around that I am available of today.

summary: Related Document Indexing
description: Identify related documents using a score. For example, the ability to surface a relevance score for results matching a query using the API? Ability to identify the most similar documents in an index?
workaround: Similar to our search term clustering, what we can do is look at queries and the most popular articles clicked on after someone searches. This will give us an idea of duplicative content. Otherwise, we can make suggestions for content gaps or where we'd recommend archiving specific discussions.

summary: Simplified Answers Search Format Report
description: Hi Team,    We have a client who is trying to understand HOW users are searching for providers/locations/services on their answers experience. For example, when they search for a doctor do they type in First Name \- Last Name or some other format? We do a great job of grouping searches together based on what they mean but is there a way to understand the format of searches at scale?    Thank you,    W
workaround: Going through the data manually and trying to make sense of it. This is challenging at scale.

summary: Allow consumer messages with specific duration period
description: Communicte a specific mesage to consumers which is relevant only for a specific period of time
workaround: I am not aware

summary: Adding Location for Entities to appear when that location is searched (not map)
description: Adding an option to entities if they are relevant to specific locations such as countries/towns they would appear when the relevant search is made automatically which would ensure it doesn't appear for everything or potentially nothing without having to do separate query rules each time.
workaround: Currently creating a new query rule each time a new town/place is searched within a location for the entity to show.

summary: Embedded Fields for Automated Review Response
description: For the new "Reviews Automatic Response" app, it is currently unable to utilize embedded fields in the response assets. Can we add this functionality in a future release? Clients loves the idea of using this tool for a particular criteria (ex: 5\-star reviews without text), but they want to make sure they reply with the reviewer's name.
workaround: There is currently no workaround since Automated Review Response does not support assets with embedded fields

summary: Review Column Expansion
description: Expand the review content column for easier reading when on the monitoring tab and when viewing the platform via cell phone. The review content column is so small that only a few words are able to fit per line. It's not efficient and is difficult to read, especially for our exec team viewing on their cell phones.
workaround: There is no workaround within the platform. The only other option is to export to Excel and format the cells, then send via email. This is not ideal for a quick view, especially with so many locations/reviews to read through.

summary: Reviews Automatic Response App Improvements
description: Hi,    That would be great to add some features to the automatic response app:    * by language. Even within 1 country, we can get reviews in multiple languages    * by folder (we are organized by country)    Ideally, we should be able to set up several variations of responses for each scenarios so that yext can pick up a random one.    5\-star Google review in UK location, no content =\> response in English, EN template 1    5\-star Google review in UK location, no content =\> response in English, EN template 2    5\-star Google review in FR location, no content =\> response in French, FR template 1    5\-star Google review in Spanish location, no content =\> Spanish not defined, then default answer in English
workaround: Manual responses

summary: Review Monitoring and Response
description: Hello! I think it would be great to be able to expand the ability monitor and respond to reviews from multiple sites, including from the BBB and Consumer Reports.
workaround: NA

summary: Naming "Reviewer Name" vs "Author Name"
description: Naming "Reviewer Name" vs "Author Name"
workaround: NA

summary: Review Response Workflows for Pooled Teams
description: The Yext platform's workflows don't allow for a pooled model for responding to reviews in Yext. It should be possible for multiple people (level 1) to have access to a set of reviews, and then multiple "response reviewers" (level 2) to have access to the draft responses. This is not enabled by our platform today.
workaround: Limit Yext customers to the single\-person linear workflow for all items requiring review. (This is not limited to review response; it also applies to KG updates and probably a few other workflow use cases that aren't coming to mind right now)

summary: Notifications Workflow (multi-step)
description: Based on several conversations with prospects it would be great to add multi\-step workflow capabilities to our notifications.    One specific request has been with reviews:    * Based on a specific keyword in a review the incoming review is routed to a team (member) being responsible for that category (keyword).    * If the team (member) does not answer the review within 48 hours, the review is routed to the responsible supervisor.    Based on different triggers a multi\-step workflow would be great to cover those scenarios.
workaround: NA

summary: Possibility to extend Review Response window
description: When you respond to a review, a new window opens. This window has a fixed size, it is not possible to make the window bigger which is not user fiendly for detailed answers.
workaround: NA

summary: Intelligent Review Response - bulk upload and edit via file
description: Currently, if we need to upload review responses assets, we have to manually input them one by one.    For context, we currently have more than 500 value statements alone.    Would it be possible to upload them via file upload?    To edit them, the only way we can is also manually, one by one.    There is no search bar, and you can only view 5 at a time.    ***Bulk upload and bulk edit Intelligent Review Response Assets.***    Thanks for upvoting the idea.
workaround: NA

summary: Google Q&A - Intelligent Response
description: **Client Feedback**    It would be helpful for reporting, labeling structure/rules, intelligent response features that are available with Reviews Monitoring/Response to be available for Google Q\&A.    * Labeling Rules for Google Q\&A    * Intelligent Review Response for Google Q\&A    * Reporting in Report Builder for Google Q\&A
workaround: NA

summary: Notification for Reviews which were deleted from Google
description: I know that once a review is deleted directly from its author, we need to deleted from Yext ourselves. But is there not the possibility to have a notification or something that informs us that the review is not on Google anymore?    This would save actually some time, since we could avoid clarifying and elaborating an answer that in the end we can't publish, and since we can't check that each review is still there before starting to elaborate an answer.    Thank you very much in advance for your time!  Giada
workaround: There is no current work around. I'm answering 100 reviews each month, which are not so many, but still some. I can't check everyday if each review was deleted from Google by its author or not.    The other day we received a review for a pharmacy. I contacted the pharmacy to elaborate an answer. When I tried to publish that answer I discovered that the review was deleted by its author. However, in Yext the review was still standing. This already happened twice or three times and costs me min. 30 minutes work.

summary: Flag as Inappropriate: To translate in French
description: Translate "Flag as Inappropriate" into French for users whose language is French.
workaround: In Reviews \> User Photos, "Flag as Inappropriate" is not translated into French for users whose language is French.

summary: Adding pinned filters to the reviews platform.
description: My client is requesting the ability to save applied filters in the review monitoring and response tabs. For example, they want to pin multiple filters around specific star ratings, publishers and its review content/sentiment keywords and easily apply them, vs. having to manually add these filters every time they're in the tool. Can we look into adding this functionality?
workaround: manually applying filters during their workflow.

summary: Integrate Google Chat into Yext
description: Integrate the Google chat feature within Yext's interface so users can respond to consumers/reviews within the platform
workaround: NA

summary: Bulk Delete Review Labels
description: Hi Team,    There is no place in the platform to manage all the review labels available/used within an account. This would be a great feature that would allow clients to manage their review labeling easily.    Currently, even if a review label is removed from all reviews, it still shows up under the Review Labels as a suggestion when adding a new label. There also is no workaround to simply delete the review label from the platform, which removes it from the respective reviews as well.
workaround: No workaround at this time for deleting review labels.

summary: Label and Folder Filtering Option For Review Gen Invite Page
description: Hi Team,    We have a client that would benefit greatly from being able to filter invites by folder and labels on the review generation invites page. Can we look to add this functionality?    Thank you,    Wilhelmina
workaround: No work arounds.

summary: Hide Reviews Overview Tab From Those Without Access
description: Currently, you cannot access the Reviews Overview Tab if you don't have permission and if the entity the user is in charge of does not have the Review Generation license.    Can we either hide this tab or update the error you get when trying to view this page if this criterion hasn't been met? For now we just get a "you don't have access to this page", but instead can we get a "you're missing ___ "or just the hide the tab from the user?
workaround: None, Support has to check what's missing either via Permissions or the Entities the user in question is in charge of

summary: Show Asset Names/Labels in Review Response Screen
description: Hi team! Currently in the Review Response screen, the dropdowns to select response assets only show a pre\-set preview of the content. A client has specific review response assets created to be used for specific location regions and are designated as such through the names/labels. These names/labels aren't visible in the review response screen so it makes it extremely difficult for a user to determine which are the correct ones to use. It would be very helpful to either have additional filtering abilities in the asset selection tool for name and/or label or be able to adjust the asset preview for us to add in custom indicators.
workaround: Only workaround currently is to toggle back and forth between the Assets screen and the Review Response screen to cross reference

summary: ADD PUBLISHER: Healthgrades
description: Please consider adding 'Healthgrades' as a publisher to the YEXT platform with regards to third\-party online physician reviews. Our healthcare system receives substantial positive patient feedback from Healthgrades, and it is a trusted source for many of our healthcare providers.    Thank you.
workaround: None.

summary: Reviews Tabs
description: Hi team,    I've had the below feedback from a client:    *I think I've mentioned this before, but here is my understanding of the way the Reviews options work:*    * *Reviews \- Response: This option shows all reviews that are awaiting a response*    * *Reviews \- Monitoring: This option shows all reviews that have been responded to*    *Can you confirm if my understanding of these options are correct?*    *I'm pretty sure they are, but there are reviews that haven't been responded to in the 'Monitoring' option and I can't work out why.*
workaround: Is there a way to avoid this confusion and differentiate the UI of these two tabs a little more? Removing the "respond" button from the monitoring tab would clarify that the response tab is where to go, and perhaps make it feel more separate? Although I appreciate that's there for UI ease.    I wanted to pass on the feedback as I think many of our clients find the difference between the two confusing as they look the same and you can respond from both

summary: Easier Mobile Experience
description: The client has been complaining about not being able to read Yelp reviews directly in their email reports and even when they try and click through the link from Yext, it opens the review but they can't read the full thing on mobile (screenshot attached).    The mobile web page also won't let them zoom out or move around the page to see the full review, which ends up leading them to have to close the review and search it by location which is inconvenient on mobile.    Is there anything we can do to make this an easier experience on mobile?
workaround: For now, to search by location on mobile but it's very inconvenient to do so.

summary: Ability to monitor and respond to Door Dash and Uber Eats reviews within Yext.
description: Can reviews left for us on Door Dash and Uber Eats be pulled into Yext so that we can monitor and respond to them?
workaround: We'd have to respond to reviews within each of those platforms.

summary: Insights- Number of Reviews received by Competitor
description: At the moment we can only see Google's average rating of competitor in the platform. It would be great to have insights into the number of reviews generated by them.    For instance, we can have a rating 4.5 and the rating of the competior is 4.4 but we have only 10 reviews and competeitors reviews are 30. This will help to analyse better the performance of the country and to undersand if we need to focus more on this area.
workaround: At the moment there is no option to get this info.

summary: Engaging with Yext via Mobile
description: Hi Team,    Hope you are doing well.    It would be useful to develop a Yext mobile App to access some parts of the platform, or at least a mobile friendly version of some aspects of the Platform, especially the option to respond to reviews without having to log on to a Desktop. This would help with engagement and importantly enable field users to easily access the platform.    Moreover, it would also be great to have Knowledge Assistant available in French.    Many thanks,    Olivia
workaround: None to my knowledge.

summary: Competitive intelligence, Review Number
description: In the Competitive intelligence tool to compare average ratings for competitors, would it be possible to also scrap and display in the platform the number of reviews for each competitor?    Clients and prospect are asking about this because it can be hard to compare a store to a competitor if one has thousands of reviews and the other just a few but a better average rating
workaround: nothing possible for now

summary: "Requester" added to the API calls for Zendesk Reviews integration
description: When receiving the reviews in Zendesk using the integration with the app, we are receiving them with the requester appearing as the system admin.   We need to have the API call pushing the cases into Zendesk, to be able to populate the "requester" filed by customer name that can be found already inside the case. This is, update the requester name to become customer's name. If the same customer contacts again, this should also need to be logged under the same customer name. Additionally, the customer should have a unique ID to avoid issues with clients under the same name.
workaround: No workaround currently

summary: Sentiment Analysis - download more data by location
description: Is it possible to export/download the top keywords with modifiers by location?
workaround: There is currently no report option available in the Report Builder that shows the most common keywords or the modifiers for the keywords.

summary: App Store Integration
description: We use YEXT to respond to our Google Listings, Tripadvisor and Yelp reviews. However, we also have an app on both the Google Play Store and Apple app store which receives reviews which we can't track through YEXT.    Is it possible to feed these reviews also into our YEXT account to avoid having to check multiple accounts when responding?    I am sure this would be a transferable feature for other businesses with both physical stores and an app.
workaround: NA

summary: Include review generation metrics within analytics
description: For our review generation clients they would like to be able to include review generation metrics within report builder and dashboards. My understanding is that this is not possible at this time but please let me know if I am mistaken!    Thank you!    Wilhelmina
workaround: Going into the review generation section of the platform.

summary: Generating reviews for both location and provider in one email/sms
description: Hi Team!    We have a client who is asking about being able to give patients the option to generate reviews for both the provider and the corresponding facility in one sms/email.    Thank you!  W
workaround: Toggling between redox review generation settings to alternate prioritizing generating for providers or facilities on a set cadence.

summary: レビュー返信のアセットの選択の際、「アセット名」を表示させたい。
description: レビュー返信のアセットに関して、アセットを選択する際、登録した「文章」が表示されている状態ですが、  こちらを「アセット名」を表示するように設定は可能でしょうか？
workaround: NA

summary: Create Review Notifications with OR Functions
description: We have been trying to create a review notification filtered by content. We added 6 different keywords, however, each of the keywords added are "and"ed together which means that these notifications will only trigger if a review comes in with all 6 keywords included.    It would be great if we could get the ability to choose between "and" and "or" conditions instead of just "and" by default. This would allow us to create the notification with the keywords "or"d together, which would cause the notifications to trigger if a review comes with any one of those keywords instead of all of them.
workaround: The only workaround for this would be for us to create 6 separate notifications, each one containing one keyword.

summary: Export links to leave a review
description: Google does not allow us to export links to leave a review for several shops at the same time. You need to go to each GBP profile, click on reviews\>get more reviews and then copy paste the link.    YEXT gives the possibility to export the map links of all GBP listings (when exporting the reviews), but I was wondering if it would be possible to export these links to leave a review in order to share them directly with the customers.
workaround: NA

summary: new filter to sort user-submitted questions for Google Q&A
description: Currently there is no way to filter user\-submitted vs owner submitted questions in Google Q\&A section.    This would make it more easier to see new questions asked by customers.
workaround: NA

summary: Delete Reviews
description: When reviews are removed they remain in the Yext platform and we no longer have the option to delete the review from the platform. The reviews should either update daily or provide the ability to delete the reveiw.
workaround: NA

summary: Setting Max Contact Frequency Based For Review Gen by Entity or Subgroup (Department)
description: Hi Team!    We have had several healthcare clients ask us about a way to set the maximum contact frequency for review generation by entity or subgroup. The reason for that is currently how it is set up is that if the max contact frequency for an email or phone number is 30 days visits they have to other unrelated doctors will not generate reviews. We would instead prefer to have it so people are not contact to leave a review on the same provider within a given period of time but can still generate on other entities.    Thank you!  Wilhelmina
workaround: There is currently no work around for this.

summary: Reviews from HR Resources - Indeed, ZipRecruiter, Glassdoor, Monster, etc.
description: The majority of our reviews come through job postings/human resource listing sites. These are the primary reviews we need to monitor. As we consider Yext Reviews, we also need to be able to monitor these reviews. Our employees are what make our business successful.
workaround: NA

summary: Add dates and codes to list of scrubbed 1st party reviews PHI
description: Hi team,    We would like to request that dates and any form of numeric code is scrubbed from 1st party healthcare reviews during the PHI scrubbing process.    Thank you!  Wilhelmina
workaround: The current workaround for this is to remove those reviews in the quarantine period.

summary: Response Approval Automation Via Email
description: Is there any feasibility to get response approvals sent to individuals via email and have that automate either an approval or suggestion back into the platform? My client doesn't want to give individual users access to the platform and needs their input into responses.
workaround: NA

summary: When responding to a Google/Yelp/FB review, please include the telelphone number as part of the Name/Address/Entity # info at the top right of the box.
description: When responding to negative reviews for our clients, we always include a contact phone number for the reviewer to call personally to discuss concerns.
workaround: We have an Excel spreadsheet with client name/address/phone number listed \-\- which means we have to toggle back and forth between Excel and Yext to complete a response.

summary: Add a note when escalating 1st Party Reviews
description: When escalating a first party review for removal in the platform there is no where to leave a note as to why the review is being escalated.
workaround: NA

summary: Reviews Widget
description: * Review content gets cut off with three **"..."** at end of review. Is it possible to customize that (i.e "read more")? This would improve user experience.    * Spacing between each review is not customizable. Right now, the reviews are tight together and there is nothing they can customize to easily fix.    * Pagination of reviews. Right now the limit is 5 reviews per page. They would like the flexibility to show more than 5 per page.
workaround: NA

summary: Saved Filters on Review Response Screen
description: Hi Team,    Similar to how a user can have Saved filters setup in Yext Content, it would be great if that feature was also available in the Review Response portion of the platform as well!    Additionally, preset date range filters (like those available in Report Builder) would also be very beneficial to have available (i.e. last 30 days, last 6 months, etc.).    This will help cut time/margin for human error whenever a user is trying to filter to a specific set of reviews they are trying to respond to.
workaround: No. The user currently has to re\-configure the filters every time the Review Response screen loads.

summary: Recognising Italian reviews in sentiment analysis
description: Currently the platform only recognises English, Spanish and Japanese as languages for sentiment analysis.    So our idea is very simple: **include Italian reviews in the sentiment analysis**.    We work in Italy, so our customers give us feedback in Italian, but we don't have any way to evaluate this feedback as sentiment analysis because this value is at 0 on platform for italian language.
workaround: NA

summary: Different colours for review labels
description: We work with different labels and since they all have the same color, it quickly becomes chaotic. I think it would be great if you could assign different colors to the labels.
workaround: There is none.

summary: automatically generate a response for reviews without having to click the magic wand/generate button
description: Hi! We love the content generation feature for reviews but we just get so many reviews that we'd rather have the responses get automatically generated and then we just review them before they go live rather than needing to manually click the generate button first.
workaround: NA

summary: Generating responses for reviews without text
description: The help section suggested providing ideal answers to reviews which generated much better results when generating responses. However, when reviews are left without text (stars only), the generator pulls the ideal response every time and inserts it. I have tried adding a prompt that specifically tells the tool what to do when there is no text, but that has not worked.
workaround: Manual text input

summary: Multiple custom email domains in one account for review generation
description: Hi Team,    We have a client that needs to have multiple custom email domains in one account for review generation. They have providers who practice at other organizations living within their account and need to be able send review gen emails from an email domain that corresponds to their respective organization.    Thank you!    Wilhelmina
workaround: Currently you can only have one custom email domain per account for review gen.

summary: Managed Review Response Tracking
description: MRR responses should be tracked similarly to other capacity products so that the account team and client can accurately track and forecast any additional capacity that may be needed.
workaround: Currently the MRR team tracks responses manually. This has led to numerous overages among several different clients.

summary: Larger Text field for Reviews Content Gen
description: Currently, it is not possible to change the size of the text window for Review Content Gen text.    It should be possible to change the size (smaller, larger)
workaround: No workaround

summary: Review Badges for Pages/Reseller Websites
description: Hi Team! Can we create Reviews Badges (see this site for an example: [https://licketysplitfl.com/furnace\-services/heat\-pumps/](https://licketysplitfl.com/furnace\-services/heat\-pumps/)) that sticks to every page of a website and shows Google reviews, similar to how the reviews widget shows GBP reviews today? A reseller would love this as a product feature! Thank you!
workaround: None but this idea does show on the Reviews Widget, just not as a badge that will follow on every page of a website.

summary: Add Content Generation Capabilities to Google Q&A
description: Hi Team \- It would be great if Content Gen could "suggest" responses to Google Q\&A that comes in through a customer's GBP Listings. The questions that come in are usually answered through the entities' hours, address, services offered and it is typically very difficult for a corporate point of contact to efficiently answer the magnitude of questions that come in. This seems like a valuable and efficient Content Gen use case.
workaround: NA

summary: Update "Flag Review" Process
description: When a review is flagged and Yext decides not to remove the review, the determination email from Yext should include the name and email of the reviewer and a link to the specific review. The subject line should be more specific and say "removed" or "no action taken" instead of the subject saying it is "resolved".
workaround: Currently, when a review is flagged and Yext decides ***not*** to remove, the determination email from Yext does not have the name or email of the reviewer, nor a link to the review. Clients have to track down the review to find the name of the reviewer, then search through emails to find the reason it should be removed.

summary: Review templates - Make review templates (greetings, value statements and closings) unique at an entity level
description: Review templates that help us quickly respond to online reviews are currently shown across the whole of our business, meaning if you work in a single brand you are seeing review templates for other brands. It would be great to be able to assign templates to an entity, label or folder.
workaround: None, other than search through every entities pre\-set templates.

summary: Allow Clients to Sort Listings on Reviews Publishers (E.g. Trustpilot)
description: Replicate the Sort and Search function to Reviews Only Publisher, aka publisher that do not have a listing tab.    Users currently cannot filter or sort on the Publisher Tab, requiring them to constantly maneuver around pages and manually searching through their "listings".
workaround: NA

summary: Allow Clients to Sort Listings on Reviews Publishers (E.g. Trustpilot)
description: Replicate the Sort and Search function to Reviews Only Publisher, aka publisher that do not have a listing tab.    Users currently cannot filter or sort on the Publisher Tab, requiring them to constantly maneuver around pages and manually searching through their "listings".
workaround: NA

summary: Allow Clients to Kick Off Review Linkage Tasks (Opt In button, but for Reviews)
description: Currently, "listings" on reviews only publishers have no way of being launched in the existing storm UI. Review Linkages tasks are kicked off automatically and cannot be started by clients at will.    This has frequently led to issues on Trustpilot (a reviews on pub) where clients are not ready to match to their review linkages, but we started the tasks ahead of them. This alway resulted in support + techops ticket.    Bonus Ask:    Furthermore, we want to allow clients have more flexility on control their matching on Trustpilot. Giving clients a better view of what their "listings" are matched to would be a great start. This also does not exist in the current Storm UI, users are only able to see whether their listing is "match" or "not matched", no option to "View Listing" or see what they are matched to.
workaround: NA

summary: Show a hint when a review includes a photo.
description: Google Users can add Photos to support their reviews. Yext adds those Photos into the user generated content. Is there a chance to add an hint or direct link to the photo for reviews with photos?
workaround: NA

summary: Yext Integration of Android Play Store & Apple App Store Reviews
description: The idea is to retrieve \& monitor reviews not only from publishers such as Google Business Profile, Yelp, and much more, but also from the app store and play store, as being able to respond to these reviews \& ratings will allow us to keep track of how the business is performing from all platforms/devices/areas.
workaround: NA

summary: Knowledge Assistant in German, French, Spanish, Japanese etc.
description: Dear all, according to [this Telescope Card](http://knowledge.telescope.yext.com/board/board-reviewsdifferentiatorsproductinformation?selected=d0b1a1c2-cd94-4981-8bb9-5ba88a7a9333-knowledge), the Yext Knowledge Assistant currently only communicates in English. Thus, our German clients cannot use it for a very relevant use case, to respond to reviews via SMS (older financial advisors, franchisees, etc. tend to not have access to the platform, they don't speak English and this feature in German would be a great way to engage them nonetheless and increase our clients' review response rate and stickiness.
workaround: NA

summary: Add a timestamp OR "Time left in Quarantine" stamp on First-Party Reviews + Option to Filter By
description: For clients publishing first\-party reviews to their consumer\-facing pages, it would be valuable to understand when the review entered the quarantine period from de\-identification so that users reviewing the content understand how much time they have to review the comments/ratings prior to the review record publishing onto a page.    Additionally, it benefit users in\-platform to filter by "days left in quarantine" OR by the time at which the review entered the quarantine period.
workaround: There is no workaround currently. The time left in the quarantine period should roughly correspond with the date of the review, but there is no concrete way to determine this.

summary: Review Monitoring Scope for Specific Sites
description: For many marketing organizations, specific teams or individuals are responsible for a particular third\-party site or sites (e.g., Google, Facebook, Yelp, etc.). There is currently not an option to scope a user's role/access to only include reviews coming from a particular site(s) \- it would be helpful to include this as another item that can be configured within custom roles to define which roles can view, response w/ or w/o approval, etc.
workaround: Site Filtering.

summary: Apply Embedded Fields To Custom Email Notifications
description: Currently the custom email tab for review notifications does not support the use of embedded fields. We would like the option to add embedded fields for data such as the username and business name to our custom messaging.
workaround: The current workaround is to add a separate notification with custom messaging for each user in the account which can be very time consuming for resellers.

summary: Filter reviews by time period instead of specific dates
description: Currently, reviews can only be filtered by specifying dates (e.g., between 1/1/2023 and 3/1/2023), but more often than not, clients prefer to filter reviews by time periods (e.g., last 30 days, last 60 days, last 90 days, etc.).    Instead of always having to indicate \& update specific dates to filter for reviews in a certain time period, it would be nice if clients can filter by time periods on a rolling basis (for example, selecting last 30 days auto\-detects what the date 30 days ago from today is).
workaround: NA

summary: Content Generation for Review Response: Enable to create multiple instructions
description: Hi team,    Today, we can create only 1 instruction for Content Generation for Review Response. It would be great if we can create multiple instructions and enable to select which instruction to use when generating review response.
workaround: NA

summary: Separate Review Generation Prompts by language
description: Setting up prompts for global (or Swiss accounts) with multiple languages is challenging due to varying tones, greetings, and endings.    While techniques like few\-shot training and specific prompts work well for individual languages like English or French, they become complex when applied to different markets and multilingual contexts, such as Swiss accounts involving German, French, Italian, and English.
workaround: You could create 15 few shot trainings, but the LLM seems many times to ignore a few of them.

summary: Content Generation for Review Response: Enable to use Intelligent Review Response and Content Generation together
description: Hi team,    It would be better if we can use the assets of Intelligent Review Response (Greeting, Value Statement, Sentiment Keywords, Closing) in the "AI Generated" tab to add responses to the AI\-generated response.
workaround: NA

summary: Review Rules for Primary CTA
description: I would like to be able to create a review label rule to add a label for all reviews generated by the primary CTA on the page
workaround: Maybe custom dev work

summary: Notification of new reviews with link to Yext CA + reviews
description: Hello together,    currently the notification of new reviews only includes the link to the publisher (Google...) review.    It would be a significant improvement for the customers / users of the Yext CA in the processes, if the notification of new reviews to the link to the publisher (Google...) reviews could also be the link to the Yext CA and to the reviews there.    This streamlines the process for customers / users of the Yext CA enormously.    Greetings    Sina
workaround: Only the link to the publisher reviews at the publisher.

summary: Analytics on agents responding to reviews
description: Hi team,    Currently there aren't any report metrics we can use to monitor **who** has responded to a review.    I was on a call with a customer complaint manager who wants to understand which of her agents are responding to the most reviews, how quickly etc. Ideally I'd be able to get their email as a dimension in the report builder... Thanks!
workaround: None

summary: Date update for updated reviews
description: For example, if a google user submits a review on 2011\-11\-01 and changes that review on 2022\-08\-24, then the change will be classified as New by Google.    Not in Yext CA. This irritates the customers, because they pay attention to the date and do not look for the Google user in the CA.    Is it possible to import the updated date into the CA?    Greetings    Sina
workaround: none

summary: Wir möchten Reviews nach Usern filtern und suchen können.
description: Wir möchten Reviews nach Usern filtern und suchen können.
workaround: Alle Reviews nach Label filtern und manuell nach dem User suchen. (Bei einer hohen Menge an Reviews ein großer Aufwand.)

summary: Pre-filtered view of reviews tab
description: Hi Team,    My client would like a pre filtered view of the reviews tab to only show reviews that the team is responsible for responding to (currently the client shares an account with 2 other brands). Is this something we would consider?    Thank you,  Wilhelmina
workaround: I suggested that we create another login with limited access to just view her organization's entities so that she could see the pre\-filtered view when looking at reviews.

summary: Add zero count for Reviews
description: Are we able to please add zero as a count for reviews by location. I have a client who monitors this very closely as they pay out bonus' based on review ratings / volume across all sites.
workaround: Currently when they look at this across the full estate, they have to manually add in the missing locations to say that they have had no reviews over a certain time period. This is because we only count reviews from 1 onwards. However knowing a location has had zero reviews is a valuable piece of information to them nonetheless.

summary: Review Posts Template
description: We would like to be able to create a new template that applies to all Google review post notifications that get sent out to our clients. As the wording in the current built in template is not very suitable for us, and it can be a bit confusing for our clients. [https://www.screencast.com/t/lYuAVkct1UR8](https://www.screencast.com/t/lYuAVkct1UR8)
workaround: Only workaround would be to edit each review post manually. This would not be the best option because it will take more time than usual, and we are also looking for a more permanent solution.

summary: Embedded Field with 'Employee Name' in Review Response
description: Hi Team! I think it would be great and useful for clients to have an embedded field for 'Employee Name' available for responding to reviews. Example below of a client use case \- thanks!    *'We are so glad to hear that \[employee name\] provided such excellent service! \[employee\] truly lives our values and always exceeds our expectations.'*
workaround: Not possible using embedded fields

summary: Filter Option for Reviews that were answered via Yext on GMB
description: Several clients of mine of asking for a possibility to track how their users are using Yext to respond to reviews on GMB. Currently there is no option for filter for this in Analytics. Reports only show the total number of reviews that were answered on GMB (total of directly via GMB \& Yext).
workaround: Currently we need to look via review monitoring for responses by registered users which is not a real option.

summary: On Screen Tips
description: For the metrics at the top of the Review Monitoring and Review Response tabs, there should be explanations of what each metric means
workaround: N/A

summary: Translation: "Message" > "Publication" in French
description: In the French User interface, the features related to Publications are called "Message" and not "Publication".    Is it possible to edit the translation wherever it appears?    So also in Reviews \> Message for each Review.
workaround: In the French User interface, the features related to Publications are called "Message" and not "Publication".

summary: Review Generation Follow Up Communications
description: Multiple Yext customers expect the ability to send follow\-up communications (SMS text and/or email) to a customer (or patient) after they have an encounter with the brand (Yext customer). Right now, there is no way to set up logic such as "If the patient hasn't opened the text in 3 days, send a follow\-up text" or "If the patient doesn't submit a review from the email within 1 week, send a follow\-up email".
workaround: I think Yext customers can re\-submit (via API, flat file upload, or manual input) the customer/ patient's contact information in the Yext platform, and this will trigger another outreach (text or email) to the customer. However, this is completely manual and doesn't involve any logic about whether anything happened with regard to the original outreach.

summary: Review Invitation Reminder Emails
description: Hi team! I would like to be able to re\-send review invitation emails or SMS messages from the platform if the text/email has not been opened or clicked within a given time period. I would like to be able to filter for review invitation opened/clicked, select review invitations that are false for that criteria (i.e. not opened or clicked) and then resend or send another review invitation to those users in an effort to nudge them to write a first party review.
workaround: There are fields in the review generation endpoint for 'clicked' and 'opened' so I could leverage a developer to develop against that endpoint to check if an email/SMS has been clicked on, if not, send a review invite.

summary: Reviews Workflow
description: Collaborate on reviews and review responses. Be able to comment back and forth internally on the review that was submitted, assign to another team, auto create labels based on certain keywords in the reviews that automatically land in a folder that certain teams may own (for ex. legal or compliance teams), if the user is suggesting a review response then that would show up and the corporate user could recommend changes (if needed) and it would route back to that field user to update, etc.
workaround: N/A

summary: Differing Content Generation Prompts based on Folder/Country/Brand
description: For now, Yext only supports one set of instructions for Content Generation for Review Response in each account, but according to Product, we're exploring improvements for the future. So clients who have several folders for their different brands or countries with various languages, it is difficult to come up with one set of prompts that would cover all of their entities. When responses are generated by the AI, the results are often not helpful if the differing language requirements and brand guidelines are not taken into account.
workaround: NA

summary: Auto refresh Review monitoring Page in Yext
description: It would be great if new reviews automatically flowed into the platform without having to do a hard refresh of your yext account.
workaround: NA

summary: Apply Review Labels to Text Assets
description: Apply Review Labels to Text Assets so that specific text assets only populate for specific Review Labels when responding to reviews.    If a client adds 158 assets, it is difficult to sort/filter out specific assets to respond quickly \& efficiently to reviews. If we were able to assign a Review Label (ex 5\-star) to a Text Asset, then that Text Asset only populated when a review had that label, it would provide more efficiency when responding to reviews.
workaround: No workaround today.

summary: Freehand Text for Review Generation
description: Can we add the ability for users to create freehand messages for Review Generation? Currently, they can only send out emails and text messages with prebuilt templates, but this is very limiting. Clients should be able to use the templates, but they should be able to edit this text (similar to Review Response) in the "send single invite" workflow.
workaround: Currently there is no workaround for this. They only thing we can do is create subjects and bodies with imbedded fields, but users are still limited by the templates created in their account. This is very limiting for clients with many users sending out messages (like franchise businesses). They want the ability to customize their invites like how they can customize their review responses.

summary: www.provenexpert.de bei den Reviews anbieten
description: Ich arbeite sehr stark mit der Plattform [https://www.provenexpert.de](https://www.provenexpert.de) zusammen, um Bewertungen zu bekommen und auf einer Plattform zu sammeln.  Können Sie ProvenExpert auf die Liste der Bewertungsplattformen aufnehmen, damit ich zukünftig nicht nur Google\- sondern eben auch ProvenExpert\-Bewertungen über YEXT bearbeiten kann? Danke im voraus!
workaround: NA

summary: Review translations
description: It would be great to have translated reviews in english under original reviews.
workaround: NA

summary: Setup prompt in the platform for linking Booking.com
description: Hi, there's currently no way to initiate the linking/setup to Booking.com from the platform. Since the rule cannot be applied retroactively, it will help both users and the support team to explain why the label has not been thanks to the creation date or when the label has been updated (with different criteria than the ones initially set).    For instance see video [https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/3757429](https://www.yext.com/admin2/customers/3757429): the client thought that the label was not applied because of the technical issue which was not the case after we investigated. summary: Count Adding Review Labels as an Update to the Review
description: As part of Managed Review response, review labels can get added to reviews that need to be called out to the customer. In order to notify the customer of those reviews that are labeled, we can create a review notification. There are two options in a notification set up when deciding on which reviews to filter on.    * New reviews matching all of (filters)    * New reviews, updates on existing reviews, new or updated Facebook comments matching all of (filters)    If you are to do a daily notification where we filter off of review labels, reviews would only count as "new" if they are left in the past day. If a review is not labeled until the following day, it would no longer be new and would not qualify under the first option above. However, it would also not fall under the second option because adding a review label to a review is not considered an update.    I'm suggesting that we consider the addition of review labels as updates for review notifications so we can more accurately send notifications based off this filter. Otherwise, we would have to label within a time constraint in order to fall under the "new review" piece. This can lead to missed reviews in our notifications.
workaround: The workaround would be to manually export from the Review Monitoring module.

summary: Review Detail Open Fetch
description: Pull review updates when opening review detail e.g. clicking on View or Respond. Updates include:    * existing response when user clicks to respond to a review  \-if fetch fails, user submits response and overwrites  \-if fetch succeeds and no response, user submits response  \-if fetch succeeds and existing response, user can choose to overwrite existing response    * update status if review has been deleted    * update review if changed and retain original record (timestamp, rating, and content)
workaround: * responses do not get pulled via API, they are only pulled in via scheduled review scans   \-if user posts a response to a review, it will overwrite an existing review that is on a listing but has not been pulled into the platform yet * review status is updated in the platform but I believe that is via scan as well    * there is no record in the platform of review updates. the only way to know if a review has been updated is:     * create a review notification with the filter for Update Status     * if reviews are being responded to, you can compare the timestamps. an updated review will have a timestamp that is ahead of the response
