workaround: No current workaround.

summary: Bulk Uploading Users
description: Currently in the bulk upload users flow, you have to specify in a new row each entity that a person should have access to (if not a folder or the entire account). You should be able to input a list of entities (comma separated) instead of this rigid upload format.
workaround: Having the BPO manually format the upload form to fit the current parameters

summary: Ability to export users with aligned locations
description: Currently the content provided in the existing user export is limited to name, email and last login date. We would like to also be able to pull the specific locations that each user manages, for our customers auditing purposes.    For example, we have a client with 261 active users with more often than not different locations aligned to each email. It would be great to be able to hit export and include the locations aligned to each user.
workaround: No workaround available.

summary: Embedding Help (Answers) search bar and FAQ topics within each section of the Platform UI
description: I'd like to suggest embedding the [https://help.yext.com/hc/en\-us](https://help.yext.com/hc/en\-us) answers search bar within the Platform UI itself. Today, if I have a question about some task that I'm performing in the Platform UI, I have to click on the question icon in the bottom left hand corner (somewhat hidden) or I have to manually navigate to hitchhikers.Yext.com. Ideally, I would be able to ask questions and search the Yext Help \& Hitchhikers Knowledge Graph without leaving my account.    In addition, it would be helpful to pull in FAQs onto pages within the UI. For example, including FAQs on the 'Connector's page for things like tips, hints, and best practices.
workaround: Visit help.Yext.com

summary: Display incident and maintenance information in the Platform
description: The [Yext Trust Site](https://www.yexttrust.com/) provides subscribers with updates on ongoing incidents and upcoming maintenance periods, but it requires users to either visit the site regularly or subscribe individually to get updates.    To reduce the friction involved in getting the word out, it would be nice if the Yext Platform and/or the Hitchhikers site displayed information about ongoing incidents and upcoming maintenance windows.
workaround: NA

summary: Add additional components/widgets for Home Screen
description: When setting up Custom Home Screens, there are limited options to select for getting a sufficient view of outstanding actions the user might need to perform. Recommend adding a series of additional platform widgets including:    * A summary view of pending/unread Notifications    * A summary view of Suggestions, including actions requiring that user's approval or items that are pending approval/have been approved/rejected    * A summary of platform usage stats
workaround: N/A

summary: Improve Filtering On User Management Page
description: Hi Team,    A client of mine has recently set up SSO logins, and they want us to delete users with their corporate emails that don't currently have the SAML setting enabled. Using the current filtering capabilities, I can search for users with the specific email domain, and I can search for SAML/non\-SAML users, but I can't search for users that meet both of these criteria. This is because when you click on the Advanced Filter option (to filter on SAML users), the search bar disappears.    ![0](data:image/png;base64,)    Ideally this filter would function similarly to other filters in the platform, where you can search for a specific token within the data while also being able to select various other filters.    Best,    Jon
workaround: None I can think of besides checking each user individually

summary: Ability for Users to Be Multi-account (For Delivery Partners)
description: Currently as a delivery partner, you need to have the Yext team set up a user ID for you, which is usually First Initial, Last Name underscore "_" customer name and then we (the partners) create a password, the problem is 2 fold.    First, we have to create this for each and every single user within the account, so my recommendation or ask would be the ability to create a group in which we all get added to, so we don't have to add each person at our company that would want to access the account each time.    Second, we'd like the ability to not have to create a new user for every account that we have, we'd recommend the ability to be able to have a user access multiple accounts, so we can limit the context switching from account to account, it's very time consuming to go from account to account
workaround: N/A

summary: date format in filters
description: When using filters on date across the platform (reviews, analytics,...) When you choose a custom date and are in Europe, the format is DD/MM/YYYY but when you save the filter it appears in US format with MM/DD/YYYY in the filter bar.    So it gets confusing for people working on the platform to see both formats simultaneously.    Is it possible to correct that and make sure that the format is same across the whole platform ?    Maybe this can be an option to choose in the personal setting
workaround: NA

summary: User Admins
description: Users should have the ability to see a full list of 'Power Users', i.e. Admins that they can reach out to in the event they need assistance with the platform, increased role/scope, or have other general questions.
workaround: Reach out to Yext Support for assistance.

summary: Automatically Approve Requests to Add Reviews-Only Services
description: When users submit requests for Services to be added to entities, these requests go through a review process to ensure that duplicate Listings features are not being added to different entities representing the same business.    I'd love if requests that do not include Listings, such as requests to add Reviews\-only SKUs, were approved automatically so that users do not have to wait for their requests to be approved (or rejected) by the review process.
workaround: NA

summary: Quick Find AI / Derived Answers
description: **Searchable Derived Account Answers in UI using Quick Find**    I wish I could use Quick Find to search for answers that can be derived from my knowledge graph, entity history, logs, integrations, and configuration of my account. Here are some example queries below:    * Entity history     * When was 123 Main St marked as closed     * Who closed the 123 Main St location    * User roles/history     * Users with edit access for xyz Folder     * Who was the last user to log in     * How many people have logged in this month    * Connector/ETL history     * When was the last successful Connector/ETL run     * List of errors from the last Connector/ETL run    * Pages history     * When was the location page for 123 Main St last published    * Field mapping     * Where does XYZ custom field get publisher / what does it power on my pages    * All advanced filters     * Show me all locations in Arkansas    * Licenses     * How many Pages licenses are available     * Which locations are not on Listings licenses    * Listings     * Is this location live on XYZ publisher     * Why is 123 Main St unavailable on Facebook     * Which content policies are enabled for FB    * Bringing Admin settings into Storm     * What is the geocode endorsement     * What are the matching alt naming policies     * Which flags are selected    I can see this being valuable for both customers and our own delivery teams. A quick query assisted with some AI can answer questions that currently take a lot of navigation, multiple pages, and calculations.
workaround: Manual investigation.

summary: Allow Clients to Whitelist Their Own URLs for iframing/SSO
description: At the moment, in order to whitelist a URL from iframing/SSO, clients need to reach out to Yext directly. The URL is added manually via the **Frame Ancestor Host Sources** tab in Admin 2. There are certain partners that have a constant flow of URLs that need whitelisting.    The idea is to hopefully set up a tab in the platform to allow clients/partners to whitelabel URLs on their own. That way we can avoid such a reliance on the Yext support team in setting these URLs up.
workaround: Currently the workaround is to send us a direct request to white label the URL in Admin.

summary: Move Entities Between Sub-Accounts Under Portal
description: I'd love if portal Account Managers had the ability to move entities between sub\-accounts under the same parent account (similar to how a user can move entities between folders).    Currently, if an entity is signed up under one sub\-account and needs to move under a different sub\-account *without disrupting service*, a ticket must be sent to Yext Support in order to process the change.
workaround: Canceling the entity and signing the entity up under different sub\-account

summary: User management - restricting ability to delete from Analytics and recovering deleted dashboards/reports
description: 1st part \- introducing restrictions for users automatically not having the ability to delete dashboards/reports from the analytics field. This could be a separate type of access which can be granted to minimise the risk anyone being able to delete everything if they wanted.    2nd part \- introducing a recovery mode for if things are deleted in error from the Analytics (or anywhere else in the system) to ensure that if something in error has occurred we are able to retrieve it back or have a log of what happened.
workaround: No workaround \- currently all users have the ability to delete reports/dashboards which has happened and we don't have the ability to recover once deleted and have to create from scratch again.

summary: User Management - Updating Last log in to Last Activity
description: To remove 'Last log in' as users can select to remain logged into the platform and update it to 'Last activity' where it will show when a user was last in the platform pulled through from a click within it. This will show a more up to date view than getting users to log out of the platform to log back in to update the 'Last logged in' field.
workaround: None \- having to message users to manually log out and then back into the platform for the date to update within our specified time period.

summary: Link Sandbox and Prod Accounts
description: Customers are generally more comfortable doing testing for integrations, configuration changes, etc. within sandbox accounts but it can be somewhat difficult to map changes back to a production account and populate a sandbox accounts with sufficient data or product\-level configuration to perform meaningful testing.    Although some of this can be done in the admin console today, it would be helpful to add a more tight coupling between production and sandbox accounts, potentially including:    * Sandbox creation flow within production account    * option to select which data/entities, entity configuration, and product configuration (e.g., search configuration) populates into sandbox    * ability to publish changes made in sandbox account (e.g., creation of a connector) back to prod account
workaround: Admin console and/or Yext Account Connector

summary: Reset user password, without requiring them to click on email link
description: Currently, if you go to a Yext user as an admin and click to "Send user a password reset email", that is all it does. It send a link to the user so they can reset their password. Unlike other systems though, this does not automatically trigger a password reset to the user and requires them to open the email and manually click the link to reset their own password. As we look for ways to add layers of security for our user base, the need to automatically trigger password resets for inactive users is necessary.
workaround: NA

summary: Messaging for 403 Error due to Permission Issues
description: When you run into an access issue a 403 error is returned advising you aren't able to access a specific page but doesn't call out why. This then requires users to contact Yext for clarification.    Ideally, the messaging would be adjusted to confirm they don't have access and to have their admin make adjustments to their role if they need to access the page. Additionally, it would be great to note what permission set exactly is missing and preventing them from accessing the page.
workaround: NA

summary: Customize Suggestions Actions by Roles/Permissions
description: Today, regardless of roles/permissions, a user sees three CTAs in the suggestions screen when reviewing a suggestion: Approve, Reject \& Assign. I'd like to be able to show/hide certain CTAs based on the user role. I.e. if there is a role that should never be able to approve any content, I'd like to hide that CTA from their view.
workaround: Remind users who should not be approving content (when there might be multiple layers of approvals for example) not to click approve when they're happy with the content change and ready to pass on the suggestion to the next layer in the approval workflow \- they should only ever click Reject or Assign.

summary: Banner or In-platform notification for upcoming Platform Maintenance
description: Have a banner or in\-platform notification for upcoming system/platform maintenance so partners and users are aware when there will be service outages.
workaround: Currently notifying partners verbally and via email, and have them sign up (if not done so) to our Trust Site page.

summary: Add Permissions/Endpoint Filtering to Developer Console Apps List
description: An account may have many apps, and you may want to filter to a certain set of apps. E.g. those that call Live API, those that can "write" (vs read\-only), etc. Showing a permissions summary and/or allowing the list to be filtered on permission set would be useful.
workaround: Click into each app.

summary: Allow Admin to Define Password Requirements
description: Allow admin to define password requirements (character limit, use of special characters, etc.) that users must meet when establishing new platform accounts.
workaround: Done outside of Yext via IDP

summary: Users Manager Subscriptions at Subaccount Level
description: Currently, we have a partner that wants to give their clients the ability to manage their subscriptions at the sub account level.    We are running into issues with this request because they are on YAM and not services. In Enterprise accounts, we can enable the automate listings license flow to give users permissions to add and remove services. For accounts on YAM, there is not a feature that would allow the partner's clients to manage YAM subscriptions at the sub account level.    Can we create a feature that would allow the partner's clients to control subscriptions within the UI? Currently we only see this option if you are an account owner at the parent level or an internal user.
workaround: NA

summary: Enhancement to ticket dashboard
description: Create a filter drop down for the ticket dashboard to enhance the search functionality on tickets and make it easier to separate your tickets into groups. This would be especially helpful for users that work with larger companies and have many tickets that they work with globally. The functionality would also be useful to filter by regular Yext support tickets, along with White Glove Support tickets to keep track of tickets that have been submitted by franchisees outside of the internal network.
workaround: None.

summary: Support tickets : Ticket-Status
description: Hi,    the current status of each ticket (support ticket overview) would be great.
workaround: open every ticket to check the status

summary: Change language option in Knowledge Graph
description: Many web services such as airlines pages and Facebook offer changing their platform's language by an option, usually a flag\-like button on the top right/left of the screen. It would be beneficial to have that also in our platform for all users. Sometimes a screenshot of what is seen is required. Yext employees can benefit from being able to adapt these screenshots to the language of the client, our clients can benefit from faster troubleshooting by being able to provide support screenshots in another language if needed.
workaround: Currently, the platform display language may be changed from Account Settings and it is tied to having a user account.

summary: Support tickets: CCs do not see ticket description in first automatic ticket email
description: If you put people in CC when creating a Yext support ticket, the first automatically generated email for the ticket never directly shows the message of the ticket creator. The CCs just receive an empty email with the mere information that they have been put in CC and the (generic) subject of the email does not tell you what the ticket is about in detail either. Only attachments are displayed to the CCs. Can you please take a look at this and fix it in a timely manner?

summary: Export functionality for support requests
description: In order to increase efficiency during the weekly calls business/Yext when we go through the open topics, it would be handy to have an export function for the support requests
workaround: None

summary: More prominent help icon in enhanced dashboard.
description: Currently, in the new enhanced dashboard, the help icon is in the bottom left corner in a light grey against the black background. This is causing some challenges with clients being able to see it/instinctively know where to click for support.
workaround: na

summary: User role customization
description: this request for custom roles needed for simple SSO\-iFrame integrations.    Custom user roles can help limit the view of the whitelabel dashboard. if this is flexible enough, the partner /client does not need to re create their own navigation and generate multiple frames per solution they want to show, but just use one frame of the custom view associated to the custom user role.    would be great to be able to do the following:    1. hide home screen in user role settings (not as feature suppress as there can be different user groups and some should see the home screen and some shouldn't. feature suppress would just disable home screens for all.    2. hide knowledge graph \- why? firstly, most partners integrate via admin API and make the updates in their CRM or let the client do it in a native UI and then don't need/want to show it to their clients. especially if its an SMV secondly, especially for internationally operating clients who don't speak English the term Knowledge Graph is not understood.    3. make it possible to only show analytics \- dashboards sub navigation. This is currently not possible. One either needs to hide the complete navigation \- analytics\- or show analytics \> with sub navigations: dashboards, report builder and activity report    4. not urgent but its only possible to show All listings \& Publishers subnav together. It's not possible to e.g. only show publishers sub nav or only all listings sub nav.    Thanks!    Mali
workaround: we created a custom user role that comes close but isn't really what we were looking for. Its for approx 15k smb clients so the imperfection of the role gets displayed to quite an amount of SMBs.    Thanks team!!

summary: Add Tab Functionality to UI
description: I am constantly opening new tabs of the same Yext account. It would be amazing to open an entity page and have the overall entities page in another tab on the same page.
workaround: I open everything into a new tab in my browser. Typically end up with 2\-10 tabs when working through something in the platform. Especially when making Custom Field changes or bulk editing entities.

summary: Entity fields | Change at parent level.
description: After a scoping session, the ideal logic for partner portal accounts is the ability to change entity types (such as location) at the parent level, which would then filter down to all sub\-accounts below.    For example, the ability to add custom fields to the Location entity that would populate for any net new location or existing location. Also the ability to add Search Tracker fields to the content graph on entity creation instead of once a subscription is added.    This use case is based on a custom solution but will become relevant for more users over time.
workaround: NA

summary: Adding API Request Payloads to Export Function of API Logs
description: It would be great if we could add the Request Payload of an API call to the Export function of API Logs. Many of us want to retry failed requests and currently, it is not possible to retrieve all of the original attempted Request Payloads when using the export function. Adding this would make it easier for developers to retrieve this Request Body info and retry their failed calls.
workaround: Current workaround is to copy every Request Payload for each individual call from the Logs page in the Developer Console\- which is not really feasible for dealing with calls in bulk.

summary: Typescript Deno SDK for Yext APIs
description: With the increasing use of Plugins/Functions there are a lot of use cases for interacting with Yext APIs through Typescript. It would be helpful if Yext provided sdks for doing this instead of requiring users to implement the functionality from scratch.    There is an sdk here: [https://www.npmjs.com/package/@yext/api](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@yext/api) but the functionality is very limited. I've seen a couple cases of people starting a project using it and then needing to replace it after running into its limitations. It also isn't usable directly from the Deno plugins because they don't support npm imports.
workaround: Implement api interactions using raw `fetch` calls.

summary: XML to JSON Converter Connector
description: Some retail clients BOPIS inventory feeds rely on XML files; some of their CMS require store information is imported/exported in xml file format. When trying to import/export from the KG, they have to convert XML to JSON (or vice versa) via API \& would like to skip that step in between (or keep it centralized)
workaround: converting from XML to JSON then importing\> upload via api or using csv files to upload into KG

summary: API Endpoint to return Created Date of Entities
description: Need to be able to filter out recently created Entities via API, since we have a Connector that needs to run when new Entities (with Alternate Language Profiles) are created, but Content Endpoints cannot send all the Language Profiles in a single Push.    ASK:  \-API endpoint returns the Created Date of Entities    \-API filter parameter allows Matchers such as $isToday to handle Created Date
workaround: NA

summary: Override category via the ETL
description: If we want to overwrite categories using the ETL, we cannot do it as there is no clear track of the categories overwritten in the knowledge graph structure.    At the moment, we can only sync the generic categories but, if we need to overwrite something because a category exists only for a specific publisher, we cannot do it. The only solution is to overwrite categories manually.    It would be great having the possibility to overwrite categories using the ETL, to have the possibility to automatize that process.
workaround: We can overwrite categories only manually

summary: Outbound links available for Welcome page
description: **Welcome page Quick Links should be outbound links**    We added 'Quick Links' module to Yext Welcome page that is visible for all of our accounts. Quick Links include link to our user manual, YouTube tutorials, our company's website etc. But when a user clicks those links, they are opened in the same browser tab and user is drifted outside Yext site. It would be much better and usable if these links were outbound links and will open in new tabs, so users can stay in Yext and have the needed link in the next tab.    # Quick Links # outbound links # welcome page
workaround: Suggested to Yext

summary: Add Custom External ID Field for User Adds and Bulk Uploads
description: Can we add a "Custom External ID" field to the user creation process?    A field for the single user add, and a column to the file upload for the bulk user add process.    Thanks!
workaround: NA

summary: Aggregated View for App Logs in Developer Console
description: Any given account may have 10s of Developer Console "Apps" \- between segmenting apps by use\-case, contact email, permissions, functionality (e.g. webhooks), testing, etc, it is easy to reach that number. Occasionally you may need to determine figure out what *app* might making certain API calls. While it is possible to click into each app to and view its logs one\-by\-one, this is tedious. It would be useful to have a consolidated view of app logs. It could even take the form of adding "app" as a filter in the normal app log view.
workaround: Click into each app one by one.

summary: Being able to use the schedule updates function to assign licenses on a certain date without manual work on that day
description: Some of our team was recently given the option to assign licenses to our entities without waiting for support to assign them for us. Being able to then schedule the licenses to be applied on a certain date using the scheduleing updates feature would be pretty cool, a lot like how you can set a date for the licenses to be removed in the future for entities that have closed.
workaround: Assigning licenses manually on the date of opening.

summary: Export users, their roles, and scopes
description: I want as a customer a **full** list with all the user\-names, permissions/scopes and the other mandatory fields as an export in excel.    This would enable us to change roles for many users at once and bulk uploading the list with the updated permissions.
workaround: Currently its only possible in the Tool itself by clicking on each single user and changing the role/scope manually.

summary: Indicate Whether a Streamed Record is Result of an Update or Newly Created Entity/Record
description: It would be helpful to be able to determine in Streams, and therefore in Streams Webhooks, whether a streamed record is a result of an update or a newly created record.    I'm hoping to setup a webhook that invokes a function only when a new entity is created.
workaround: The closest workaround I've come up with is only including the Entity ID field in the field definition of the Streams endpoint; this ensures editing any other field on an existing entity wouldn't trigger the function. That being said, I'm curious if there is way to specify only new entities as the Streams Webhook trigger.

summary: Include User Access + Permissions in Users Export
description: Exporting Users from the Users tab in an account does not include the permissions or access of a given user. This information would be helpful to export in genera (and is already something you can see when looking at the Users tab)l, but also for scenarios where user access + permissions needs to be changed or verified.    * Example: Turtlehead Taco's uploads franchises + regional managers as users and wants to verify that franchisees only have Entity Manager access to their stores and that Regional Managers have Account Manager access over their regions, which are setup as Folders in the Knowledge Graph. After uploading users, there's no way to export each users access + permissions to verify that they were setup correctly. Ideally, Exporting from the Users tab would include the data for the access + permissions    Additionally, the ability to pull *historical user* information (including access and permissions) would be extremely helpful to have since accounts with complex user setups usually include folder level access, which can very easily be changed and impact user access as a result. If users are changed because of folder restructuring, there's no way to confirm what their previous roles + access levels were.    * Ideally, in the same way that we can pull historical exports of the entities in the Knowledge Graph, it would be nice to have the ability to pull historical users roles + permissions.
workaround: I believe you can query user roles + permissions for an account in SQL, but that is obviously not something clients can do and there is also no way to pull historical user roles + permissions.

summary: User Creation Emails for API Generated Users
description: When generating users for an account or subaccounts for partner, this can often be done via API. If done manually each user gets an email notification for the creation but for those set up via API it does not. Currently this is a pain point because for large partner accounts with different users per subaccount, we can't use SSO since they aren't all from the same company so ETL for users is our best solution which requires the API. This then leads to the problem where none of those users will now know about their access to Yext.
workaround: Either manual additions but for large amount of users to add across numerous subaccounts, this isn't feasible.

summary: Partner Channel - Apply Custom Fields Across All Subaccounts
description: Currently there isn't a way to create a custom field in Partner and apply to all subaccounts. Currently custom fields can be created within the subaccounts but if there are 100 subaccounts, I don't think there's a way to add 1 custom field to all 100 in bulk.
workaround: We've built custom integrations to get this accomplished in the past but it usually requires a complex ETL and keeping the fields in sync usually is a challenge.

summary: Allow Yext admin users to reset password in bulk
description: This account is a new user of the Yext platform. Yext Implementation and support team have helped them to create an important amount of user and send the welcome email. However all the users have not used the email to login for the first time and hence the link to create the password (within the welcome email) have expired. As result the admin of the account is not able to reset the password for their users in bulk but need to do so one by one, which will be very tedious
workaround: In addition for the admin user to reset the password one by one, the support team can do the bulk password reset in the backend but the client admin user is dependant on us to do so, which I believe is not very efficient for both us and him.

summary: Deleting Users in Bulk
description: There should be the ability to bulk delete multiple users. Bulk User Deletions could be completed via a File Upload OR there could be a platform user interface for "Bulk User Deletions."
workaround: There is only the ability to delete users one\-by\-one. This is time consuming, especially when large "platform user cleanups" are taking place.

summary: Setup Notifications via API
description: Multiple Partners would like to use the API to set up Review and Analytics notifications for their clients.

summary: Scheduled Updates via API
description: The ability to schedule updates in Entities API would provide useful functionality and continue to expand upon the flexibility of our platform. This has been [discussed previously](https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C02HCA2PYBG/p1665499358636129?thread_ts=1665496548.371309&cid=C02HCA2PYBG), but I wanted to document it here and give people a chance to upvote if it's a popular idea.
workaround: Schedule updates manually (or upload via the Vertical Uploader?)

summary: Upgrade Yext CLI to Handle Entity Linkages More Elegantly
description: Hi Team \- Today when I run the Yext CLI, I often run into errors creating entities via "resources copy" as the CLI will error out on creating entities when their linked entities don't already exist. It would be great if the CLI on the first pass avoided performing any linkages, and then "came back through" and handled all the linkages. I believe this would avoid this type of error, and make the tool more useful to folks that are a little less technical.
workaround: First download resources, copy entity resources, write a script to remove all linkages, upload without linkages, then upload with linkages.

summary: Filter for Support Ticket List
description: There is no possibility of filtering for Support Tickets.
workaround: NA

summary: Sync directly with Lyft
description: Lyft now uses their own mapping system so data is not getting to Lyft (when previously they used Google).
workaround: NA

summary: Custom Role: Option to restrict the user roles that a custom role can select when creating new users
description: When creating a custom user role, it would be helpful to have the option to choose which user roles this custom role is allowed to select when creating new users.    We don't allow users to create users with roles that have more permissions than they have, but there isn't an option to specify which roles they can select.
workaround: NA

summary: License Removal Notification
description: Ability to create a notification or automated report for locations that have had licenses removed (similar to the entity add notification). Many clients ask for a weekly automated report of locations that had their licenses removed.
workaround: N/A

summary: Export Notifications
description: It would be a great help to allow users to export notifications so that in the event that a high level user is deleted or needs to be deleted offboarding can be done more seamlessly so that account notifications are not lost with the user. Currently, there is an ability to transfer notifications from user to user but no way to do this in bulk by exporting the notifications but instead each notification needs to be opened one by one and the notification ID be captured from each link individually.
workaround: Manually opening each notification one by one and capturing the notification ID from the URL. In an account with dozens of notifications this can become quite tedious and cumbersome.

summary: Ability to add Filters into Notification Management for Report
description: Currently Notification settings are fairly limited in the ability to filter reports. It would be great to have the ability to manage notifications and filter existing reports by "Entity"/"Folder"/"Label" and then also by date range. This way you can have one report across the whole account, but then notifications can be quickly updated to display the appropriate subsets to different groups.    This is very helpful when we're sharing with non\-Yext users.
workaround: Creating individual reports for all of these different filter use cases, and then setting up the notifications.

summary: Update Message in Reseller Listing Scan When Google Quota Exceeded
description: Update Message in Reseller Listing Scan When Google Quota Exceeded
workaround: Contact Yext Support, who then directs Reseller to follow [Step 9](https://help.yext.com/hc/en\-us/articles/115005975363\-Add\-Google\-to\-your\-Listing\-Scan) in this Yext Help Article and this subsequent Google Article, "[Manage Your Cloud Billing Account](https://cloud.google.com/billing/docs/how\-to/manage\-billing\-account?_ga=2.267829390.\-1626875908.1540923110)."

summary: Copy Configuration from Sandbox to Production
description: Similar to this [idea](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/4928291828113021235), it would be helpful to provide a more streamlined process for cloning various resources between sandbox and production accounts. Many customers/partners test Connectors and specific transforms within sandbox accounts before pushing to their production accounts.    Providing simpler functionality, within the platform UI to supplement what is currently available via the CLI/Admin Console would be helpful for initial configuration cutover as well as ongoing testing between sandbox and production versions of Connectors.
workaround: CLI / Admin Console

summary: Customize Default Email Notifications
description: Currently the default notification message contains dynamic fields relevant to the type of notification (e.g., \[Connector ID\-name\], \[count of new entities\], etc.). These dynamic fields can only be used within the default response and the language surrounding them cannot be changed.    Requesting the ability to include these dynamic fields, and including documentation on the corresponding syntax, to enable more flexibility for custom email notifications.
workaround: No direct workaround.

summary: Add a flag to the CLI's "resources apply" command to create entities despite invalid linked entity references
description: It is currently very difficult to apply linked entity resources to an account using the CLI.    If you try to apply two linked entities to an account via CLI, they will both fail and show the following error because the other of the two entities doesn't yet exist: **Value must be a valid entity reference**.    It would be immensely helpful to have a flag that tells the CLI to run and simply leave the linked entity blank if it can't find the entity that is being referenced. Then you'd be able to apply the resources once to create the entities and apply them again to fill in the entity references now that everything exists.
workaround: No workaround that I'm aware of

summary: Update Flow for Scheduled License Removal
description: Hi Team,    When clients get to the page where they are asked if they want to keep licenses or remove them there is a blurb in the question that suggests they keep licensees assigned. This is confusing because what the blurb should be directing clients to do is to remove licenses at a date scheduled in the future. Updating this language would help clients when going through the license removal flow.    Thank you!  W
workaround: NA

summary: Flexible Default Export Configuration
description: Hi team,    Currently, we are unable to change the default export settings for accounts. I have run into an issue with entity IDs, to where if you export via CSV it shortens the Entity ID and broke the API connection my client was using to power the information from Yext to their pages. Ideally, I'd like to be able to edit the default export/import settings so in case me or the client goes to update information via spreadsheet, we mitigate risk of breaking the Entity IDs.
workaround: currently we can add export/import configurations, but without a reminder to use it, there is risk of people not using the configuration and breaking it.

summary: Optimization Tasks for Common, User-Actionable Data Issues
description: Currently, Resellers can use the [Content Capture](https://help.yext.com/hc/en\-us/articles/115005989566\-How\-to\-use\-Content\-Capture\-) tool (which generates Optimization Task links) to allow their users to setup Listings and add core data without needing to log in to the Yext platform.    In addition to initial Listing setup, I'd love if we had Optimization Tasks for common user\-actionable data issues.    Examples:    * Low confidence geodata    * Missing phone number    * Missing category    * Invalid photo dimensions    Often users or resellers who do not log into Yext directly reach out to Yext for assistance in resolving common reasons that a Listing might be not\-synced. Any data corrections are communicated via ticket or email and are then updated directly in the Yext UI by a user with login access.    Allowing users to action common data issues via Optimization Tasks would allow Resellers to manage their users' Listings at scale and would give users greater control over their data in Yext. This would reduce the back and forth that is often needed to ensure data quality in the platform and would result in higher Listing Sync rates for users who do not log in to the platform directly.
workaround: NA

summary: Indicate Whether An App is "Active" in the Apps View of Developer Console
description: An account may have many apps. Some may be in\-use, others dormant. It would be useful to indicates in the Apps list what apps are "in\-use". Perhaps this could take the form of:    * 14 day # of calls    * Last "call" date    * A spark line of # calls/day for the past 14 days    * etc
workaround: Theoretically you could use the graph at the top of the screen, but it is difficult to use with a non\-trivial number of apps (e.g. 15+)

summary: Pause Webhooks
description: I would like to be able to temporarily pause a webhook from being triggered. The use case is if I notice that it is causing issues for some reason, or just maybe I'm doing some things that would normally trigger it but for some reason I want to avoid.
workaround: Currently you can delete and recreate the webhook.

summary: Invoke plugin without an event
description: It would be nice to be able to invoke a plugin on a schedule instead of being triggered by an event.
workaround: Create a function as a source connector to trigger the plugin, even if it's not actually creating entities.

summary: Allow email notifications for sandbox accounts OR update the UI for sandbox
description: Currently, users who create sandbox accounts (including those created off a Hitchhiker profile) can set up in\-platform and email notifications in the platform. However, they never receive the email notifications. Based on some internal digging, it appears this is intended behavior. However, this is confusing for sandbox users who set up their notifications expecting emails to be sent, but they never get the emails.
workaround: There are no workarounds for the sandbox users. My understanding of the situation is there are at least two ways to fix this moving forward:    1. Update the sandbox settings so email notifications can be sent from sandbox accounts to any email address    2. Update the notifications UI in sandbox so that users can see the email notification option, but its greyed out with a tooltip explaining that this feature only works in production accounts

summary: Authenticate via Linked Accounts from within a plugin
description: When writing a plugin to be used as a function connector source or transform, it is common to be making third party API calls that require authentication.    In cases where an API uses OAuth, it would be really nice to be able to reference an existing linked account from within a plugin to authenticate any API calls instead of needing to write code that fetches an access token each time the plugin is invoked.
workaround: As of now, you would need to hardcode the client ID, secret, etc., into the plugin function and write code that fetches an access token every time the plugin is invoked.

summary: Ability to Define "Scope" for Developer Console Apps
description: Currently, Apps (and the associated permissions) are setup on at the product level (Listings, Reviews, etc.), however for accounts with multiple users who only have access to a subset of entities or experiences in the account, a user could make an API call that impacts entities or experiences that are outside of their role's scope.    To prevent this, it would be ideal to add an additional permissions or scope selection to isolate which entities or experiences a particular app can influence.
workaround: NA

summary: Indicate what fields have been changed in Streams Webhook
description: It would be great if in Streams webhooks we could see which fields were updated in each webhook notification so we can use that in function hooks.
workaround: NA

summary: Transfer notification ownership
description: The ability to transfer "ownership" of a Dashboard Notification to a different user in the same account.  It is not uncommon that a member of a business with heavy usage in this feature may leave the business. As the control and visibility of the notification is only available to the original creator of the notification, once that user leaves the account, remaining members are not able to make any further edits to the existing notifications without reaching out to Yext support.    There is some overlap in feature to [this](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/6502204763629078550?timestamp=1665671623664)HH idea re: visibility of notification settings, but ideally we would like to have a feature within the UI to allow the original user to designate a new owner of their notification before the log out for the last time.
workaround: None. Whoever wants to be the new owner of the dashboard notification must recreate the whole notification from scratch. They also generally have no visibility into what the exact settings of the original notification were (custom messages, recipients), so one must infer that info based on a notification they received in the past, or ask a Yext agent to retrieve that information for them.

summary: Notification by Last Login Date
description: Allow a client to enable automatic notifications triggered based on a user's last login date. For example, if a user hasn't logged into the platform in 90 days, a notification would be sent kindly reminding them to review Knowledge Graph for any updates. "Hey there, we see it's been a while since you've been in the Yext Platform. Take a few minutes to log in and review your data to ensure everything is up to date."
workaround: Manually checking login dates and pushing notifications, or resending the welcome email to inactive users.

summary: Upload Environment Variables From Local File via CLI Command
description: I was recently working on a Typescript function plugin for use by a webhook that requires 4 different variables that I am storing locally in a .env file. Each time I make code changes and run yext resources apply, I have to manually copy and paste each variable into the terminal even though the values haven't changed since the first time I uploaded them. This process tedious and prone to human error.    Firebase offers the firebase deploy command that automatically uploads the .env file in your directory. That way, you don't have to upload the variables each time.
workaround: Include the variable names in _resource.json and then copy and paste each variable's value from your .env file one at a time when prompted by the Yext CLI.

summary: Yext Function Scaffolder
description: Every time I write a function I have to pull up this page to remember what files I need to create and the boilerplate content they need: [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/guides/functions\-get\-started\-guide/02\-applying\-the\-config\-resource/](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/guides/functions\-get\-started\-guide/02\-applying\-the\-config\-resource/)    It would be nice if the yext cli exposed a command that would create these base files for you, maybe something like `yext function init`.
workaround: Manually create the files.

summary: Plugin Local Testing Tools
description: It would be nice if the `yext` cli had some tools to help implement deno functions locally. For example, if I have a function I want to use as a connector source it would be nice if I could run a command that would run it and handle the logic of passing in pageTokens and making consecutive calls if necessary. This would be similar to `serverless invoke` when working with AWS Lambda functions locally.    It's not too difficult to write a script to do it, but it would be nice if we didn't need to and to be confident that it's using the same logic as the platform.    A similar thing could be helpful when developing functions intended to be used as webhooks, and potentially other contexts.
workaround: Write a script yourself to call the function in a loop.

summary: Mass Messaging Users
description: We have many many users in the platform. It would be an amazing feature if I could message all of them at the same time regarding a company objective or policy, or to message subgroups of these users. For instance, our locations are organized into 23 geographical regions worldwide, and the users are in User Groups corresponding with their area. I would love to be able to message just one area, or sub\-area\-area, etc.
workaround: Export user's email addresses and send a message manually.

summary: Important request from St-Hubert- integration of Libro into to YEXT
description: We have a major online booking provider. After a client visit, he is invited to submit his comments on his visit. It would be important to centralize all comment sources under yext by integrating Libro into your platform. They already have the API it would be child's play. note that this is a request from the management of St\-Hubert and our network of 127 restaurants.
workaround: restauration

summary: Thryv | Optimize your listings
description: Support Ticket: [https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1442504](https://tags.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/1442504)    Currently those with a user role which is not full account access, do not get an Alert button allowing easy access back to the Optimize your listings page. The user would have to log out and log back in again. Can we either show the Alert button regardless of role access or provide a simple way to access the optimize your listings page?

summary: In Platform Client Information /Article

Until the approver opens the link within the email, they cannot determine which entity's item was changed and who made the modifications.    It would be more effective to provide the approver with this information at the moment they open the email.
workaround: N/A

summary: Saved Filter Licensing Scheduled Removal
description: For Saved Filter Licensing, have a setting that allows for the removal of a license to be delayed by X days/weeks/months to correspond with best practice for keeping Listings live after a location closes.    Reason this is needed: Saved Filter License assignment is typically done by using a connector \- one common criteria is when a location closes, remove it from the saved filter. Instead of removing the license immediately, have the license removed after a certain period after it falls out of the saved filter.
workaround: Manually schedule the license removal and do not set a location closing to fall out of the saved filter.

summary: Show Licences Expiration Date in Entity Detail
description: Show Licences Expiration Date in Entity Detail

summary: Alphabetically arrange labels
description: When we click on "Account Settings" in any Demo account, the labels getting open on left side like "Personal Settings", "User Management","Role Management".....so on.....I see they are not alphabetically arranged.....it will make easier to identify if we arranged this as alphabetically.  Same goes for all other labels in KG too.

summary: Make App Directory API Logs Available in Platform
description: I'd love if users who install apps in the App Directory were able to see the app logs as well! (Similar to how we can view logs for an app in the Developer Console). This would make troubleshooting App Directory app configuration much more straightforward and would allow users to identify the root causes of errors without needing to contact Yext Support.

summary: Implementing Zendesk/Slack Integration
description: There is a Zendesk/slack integration that allows people to view, add, and get updates about their tickets through Slack. This sort of notification system would be a lot easier for managing zendesk updates and notifications, as opposed to the current notification system, which just sends an email whenever a zendesk ticket is updated. Due to the high volume of emails that Yext employees tend to get, these sort of updates can easily get lost. I am not sure if this integration is already avaliable through Yext, but of the people I've talked to, most currently rely on the email notifications. If this is already available, it could be incorporated into onboarding so that people can use this integraiton as soon as they start working at Yext.
workaround: Currently we get email notifications whenever Zendesk tickets are updated, but I think slack notifications would be more effective

summary: API - Add approvals to API
description: Approvals are not currently in the API. We would like to identify reviews that haven't been approved yet for each store.
workaround: No

summary: API - Activity Log for Users
description: Currently we can export the activity log but it's not always specific on what user and what change was made. It would be great to add this so that we can understand each element on a store page that is updated by a User.

summary: Retrieve an existing Account Report using the Analytics API
description: Hi Team!    Our client is asking for a way to retrieve an existing account report using the analytics API. They are envisioning the following:    1. Configure a report using the Report Builder UI.    2. Save it to our Account Reports so it's assigned a permanent report ID (for example, **63381** in [https://www.yext.com/s/3498166/reports/63381](https://www.yext.com/s/3498166/reports/63381))    3. Use the report's ID to request it via the Analytics API.    4. Yext generates the report with the latest data and returns it as JSON in the API response.    As an analytics user, this would help them in two ways. If they already created a report in the Yext platform, it would save them the effort of recreating the same report configuration as JSON for the API. More significantly, it'd give them assurance that they are looking at the same data whether it's in a Yext dashboard or an external platform like Tableau. They wouldn't have to worry that they used different dates or missed a filter (both of which they done several times already with the API).    Thank you for your consideration!    Wilhelmina
workaround: Just getting analytics in report builder not via API

summary: Create A Parameter That Allows the API Response To Bring Back Blank Field Values
description: I've had a few clients request the ability to have their API responses bring back all available fields, regardless of if they have a value associated with it or not.    The idea being that they could choose to have a larger response but they'd be able to map the null/blank value to their system
workaround: No option for more restrictive databases/integrations.

summary: Listing Scan Contact Information Collection Improvements
description: Hi Team,    Could we add the option for listing scan accounts to expand contact info collection fields? Clients have expressed interest specifically in being able to separate the name field into separate First Name and Last Name in order to map these to separate fields using Yext's Salesforce Lead Generation feature.
workaround: NA

summary: Knowledge Tags Holiday Hours Improvement
description: Knowledge Tags currently treat primary hours and holiday hours as separate info. So when applying consumer facing info drawn from the KT's on a page, you'd need to map two different fields.    We've had a number of different accounts request that we create a \<span\> tag that draws in the primary hours but takes into account any holidayHours info. This way, if a day has holidayHours info in Yext, it will reflect on the primary hours tag rather than an entirely separate field.
workaround: Currently clients use a combination of two tags:    \<span data\-yext\-field="hours"\>\</span\>    \<span data\-yext\-field="holidayHoursFormatted"\>\</span\>    The info for the "hours" tag will not be affected by the holiday hours from the entity.

summary: API Integration with Go Parrot & Yext
description: Speaking with a potential client in syncing their menu information directly to Yext through their current third party solution housing their menu information (Go Parrot). Is it possible to explore the connectivity of this system to Yext for Menu information to be pulled into the Yext platform?
workaround: NA

summary: Allow Country To Be Mapped In User API Endpoint
description: When creating a user, the API does not currently allow countryCode mapping for the user getting created. In certain circumstances the user may not match the default/primary countryCode of the account.    The main goal is to have this info editable via the POST/PUT calls to the user endpoint    [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/accountsettings/users](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/accountsettings/users)
workaround: At the moment you can only manually edit the country info for a user directly in the UI

summary: Adding support for OAuth2 with OIDC
description: The idea is to add support for the OAuth2 protocol with OpenID Connect (OIDC) to the Yext service. Through this enhancement, we aim to improve usability and interoperability to meet current and future requirements. OAuth2 and OIDC are standard today and offer many advantages, such as the use of standardized OAuth2 libraries and SDKs in different languages and seamless integration with other services that also support OAuth2.
workaround: Currently, Yext does not support the OAuth2 protocol. Instead, we use the SAML protocol for authentication. Although SAML is a proven and widely used solution for authentication, it has some drawbacks. For example, the disadvantages include more complex and time\-consuming configuration and maintenance of SAML identity providers (IdPs), lack of support for some modern identity and access management features, and limited interoperability with other protocols and systems.    Because of these drawbacks and the advantages offered by OAuth2 with OIDC, an extension to OAuth2 with OIDC is desirable.

summary: Increase Webhook Execution Time
description: A single webhook execution can only run for 30 seconds which presents a limitation for larger volume of data updates. Our recommendation is to increase the length that webhooks can run so that the webhook can complete more data updates.    We have a custom webhook solution for Wells Fargo and noted that only three entities are updated at a time, compared to the intended 1000 entities. The webhook pulls data changes on certain fields and pushes it out to other entities. It also updates assets.
workaround: We are looking into rebuilding this using connectors triggered by webhooks which does not have have all of the same functionality.

summary: Entities Webhook Trigger Improvement
description: When you adjust a more universal field like "hours" for a location that has multiple language profiles, it will send multiple webhook calls with the same info.    Since we send all primary and alternate profile info already within 1 standard webhook, it would seem more streamlined to send 1 call in this scenario.
workaround: NA

summary: API Error Codes
description: Please share the API Error Codes. On the reference documents, there is only 200 and 400 codes. For example, almost all developer wants to know the error code of "Entity Not Found", however, it's not documented.
workaround: We have to spend a time to investigate error codes using test call.

summary: Add Publisher Type to API Listings Endpoint
description: Add in an additional field within the Listings endpoint to indicate whether or not a publisher has an API connection with Yext or is submission based.
workaround: Current workaround is to use the Listings dashboard as a reference.

summary: Postman API library
description: Launch of new endpoint naming conventions, can we build a library of all the new endpoints to be downloaded in to Postman.
workaround: Currently no workaround (I cannot find this via our documentation), we previously had this for our legacy endpoints.

summary: Admin API
description: When an entity is still within their 30 day window and a call is made to cancel the location, it errors. It would be helpful to update logic so that the location is canceled at the end of the 30 day window without needing a secondary call.
workaround: Placing a secondary call at the end of the 30 day window.

summary: Allow selecting List field length in stream
description: I have a list field in my account. I would like to know whether or not the field is empty or not for a given entity, but I don't want to actually fetch the data in the field.    I saw that new Markdown/Lexical fields use properties (like markdown, json , html) that are sometimes synthetic to access the data.    My proposal is allowing a similar thing to access the length of list fields.    My specific use case is this stream I'm using to fetch a tree of category entities for Trane: [https://streams.yext.com/v2/accounts/me/api/categoryTree](https://streams.yext.com/v2/accounts/me/api/categoryTree) ([Stream Definition](https://www.yext.com/adminconsole/resources?sourceAccountId=3895290&organizeByResourceType=true&type=streams/streams\-endpoint#streams/streams\-endpoint/categoryTree.json)). Each category returned here is linked to a list of products. I'd like to be able to access the number of products to display in my frontend. I don't want to actually fetch the linked entity ids because that would make the response size very large.    A potential conflict for this syntax would be if we wanted to allow it to be used for addressing subfields of array elements in the case of lists of structs. That would be in line with how it's done in Live API filtering today.
workaround: Create a separate field that stores the length of the list field and use webhooks to keep it up to date.

summary: API Authenticators
description: Right now, anyone with the API key (and JWT, if enabled) can access the Live API. For security purposes, it would be helpful for financial services clients to have a functionality that authenticates that the originator of an API request is internal to the client or Yext. Ideally, this restriction will return a valid API response only if the originator is from the client or Yext IP addresses.
workaround: NA

Please contact support!". This isn't an issue on our end, and is an issue on the client's end for trying to pull reports for an inactive account\- so should be a 400 error of some type. Wondering if we could update the error messaging so it's more accurate\- would help our big partners know why their calls are failing.

summary: SSO deep link to linked accounts

This would make a lot of use cases easier for us:    * We could support ETLs that process images data    * We could build custom interfaces for editing profile data more easily, this potentially lets us replace custom dashboards with pages sites    * Tools for bulk uploading images would be much simpler

summary: Adding Custom Fields to Analytics API

Their roadblock is that Entity IDs are not a meaningful identifier for their internal team, so they want to be able to include a custom field that contains Branch IDs in their Analytics API calls (which is not possible today).

summary: Add option for display co-ordinates to be highest confidence when sent through knowledge API

summary: API Notifications (Developer Console App)
description: For a client who set up its own API, in which Yext did not configure nor has control over, there should still be a way to set up Notification settings for things such as 1) API errors \- specifically blocking entity creation \- or entity update 2) Parse errors (similar to how ETL error spreadsheets are set up via Yext ETL product).
workaround: Client may try to configure notifications itself and view API calls/responses.

summary: Export full API Logs in the dev console
description: At the moment you can only export the API logs for up to 100 rows. For analysis purposes, it would be helpful to be able to export more rows to get a full overview. In the best case it would be possible to export a timeframe e.g. last 24 hours, last week, last two weeks, or alternatively last update cycle.
workaround: You can export 100 logs at the time and would need to click through all the pages and export 100 logs and add them to one excel file.

summary: Enable the "Specify Hours" field in the Knowledge Graph to be changed via API
description: **Ask:** Allow the "Specify Hours" checkbox subfield in the Hours field for Locations in the Knowledge Graph to be set via the Knowledge Graph API.    **Context:** It is not currently possible to "blank out" or "file null" hours for all days in the KG via API/ETL when a data source uses the absence of hours to indicate that a location is closed and the hours are removed.    **Challenge:** This means that a user needs to manually update the hours in the KG after ETL runs, which reduces the automated benefits of an ETL to update hours.
workaround: N/A

summary: Adds Labels: List API Endpoint
description: It would be great to be able to hit a Labels endpoint within the Knowledge API that returns all of an account's labels, similar to the current [Folder: List](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/knowledgeapis/knowledgegraph/folders/#operation/getLocationFolders) endpoint.
workaround: Currently, the best way to get all of an account's labels is to hit the Entities: List endpoint and pull each "labels" field, but this is a much longer process.

summary: Live API Support
description: Challenge: For current and net new customers that sign on with Live API, there is a lack of support once the agreement is executed (i.e. it is on the customer to go in and tweak code/test/launch). For smaller customers, there are limited resources and our platform is "built for the marketer".    The Idea: would love to see an internal resource aligned to help with setup and launch. When it's a current customer, their resources are the AE and CSM who aren't currently well equipped to easily navigate development. With such a crucial part of their business and CMS involved, it would be helpful to make this full proof. If anything, I'd personally be open to a full training with a developer and heading this up myself to help CBU avoid any future friction and more Live API wins. Another alternative is Premier Support being able to add value by doing this.    The Result: Time to value, less customer lift, trust, product knowledge for further discovery, etc.
workaround: Current workaround is emailing apisupport@yext.com

summary: Scheduled Review Invitations
description: In order to maximize the probability of a review email/SMS being responded to, it would be nice to be able to pass a date time to the [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/liveapis/reviewsubmission/reviewsubmission/#operation/createReview](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/liveapis/reviewsubmission/reviewsubmission/#operation/createReview) endpoint and schedule what time the Review Invitation is sent out instead of the invite being sent immediately.
workaround: Only call the createReview endpoint at the exact time when the review invite should be sent out.

summary: Ability to Assign User Access to Custom Entities via API
description: Hi Team \- It would be great if we could assign user access to custom entities via API. Right now, if a new location opens and you want to assign the right users access to their relevant entities, we can only automate out of the box entities. Anything else has to be done manually through a time consuming process. Allowing for automation on custom entities as well would be a huge time saver.
workaround: Manual time consuming process

summary: Share view of notifications on the platform with other users
description: Partners/resellers interact with notifications in two ways:    * Partner fulfilment team configures review notifications on behalf of the customer following DIFY or DIWM onboardings    * End\-customer (SMB) configures those notifications in the platform    DIFM is the main approach though, and accounts are accessed by multiple reps (fulfilment rep, his/her manager, sales rep) during the onboarding to make sure the account was correctly set up. Because the notification is only visible in the dashboard for the user that has set it up \- the rest of the team would have to rely on that person to show them that the notification was in fact created or ultimately the Yext team.    Similarly, on the second scenario a customer could forget to set up notifications, not remember that functionality existed, leave for later and forget etc and the only way for the partner fulfilment to know for sure if they had set them up would be to ask the customer or ultimately Yext team.    The current solution of asking the creator or Yext is not scalable when we're talking about resellers due to the volume of the accounts. Could we add functionality to share the notification view in the platform with other users? Ideally the format would be the same as we have for sharing notifications: Myself, All Users, Specific user roles, Specific users/email addresses.    Thank you for your consideration and look forward to hearing from you soon.
workaround: No workaround in the platform

summary: Modify SSO Troubleshooting Guide for Clarity
description: The [**Troubleshooting SSO Errors and Managing Users**](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/guides/troubleshooting\-sso/) guide should look more like [https://help.yext.com/hc/en\-us/articles/360029278352\-Not\-Synced\-Listing\-Troubleshooting\-Facebook](https://help.yext.com/hc/en\-us/articles/360029278352\-Not\-Synced\-Listing\-Troubleshooting\-Facebook), with a table of issues and the necessary steps to resolve them. Platform updates to SSO have been excellent and should be paired with equally\-excellent documentation that clearly directs users where to look / what to do for specific issues.
workaround: Currently there are a lot of great tools for troubleshooting SAML SSO, but if you don't know what you're doing, it can be quite difficult. Our [**Configure SAML SSO**](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/guides/configure\-saml\-sso/) guide is excellent, but we lack clear and actionable troubleshooting steps—specifically related to issues within the Yext platform (i.e. we have lots of IDP\-related guidance)

summary: Account-Wide Notification Management
description: Hi team,    Currently, there is no way of managing notifications across an account since they are tied to the users that created them. Can we create a compiled view of an account's total notifications (internal user + all other users), and possibly give access to this view to Account Manager or Full Access roles? Additionally, any way of bulk editing and exporting notifications would be very helpful.
workaround: Currently, there is no workaround for this. Managing notifications for accounts with hundreds/thousands of users is incredibly difficult and time\-consuming, and support needs to pull complicated queries through the ops database to get this information.

summary: Support Licence Count
description: Hi team,    Can we remove the licence count feature for basic / premier support as this is across all locations in an account? This causes a lot of client confusion. Example query below for context:    "I still don't have the possibility to add basic support licenses. Could you please precise the process to activate those licenses please ? Do we need to add those licenses to the entities in order to make them live on google and store locator ?"    There's some confusion around the support licences and when assigned licences for new locations the platform says "one licence to buy" / "you don't have enough licences" when talking about basic support.    Thanks,    Laura
workaround: This causes confusion with clients and they often reach out to CSMs asking for clarification and if they need to purchase more licences.

summary: Yext Push Notifications via Clients Slack
description: Hi Team,    I am curious \- is it possible to set up notifications for our customers where, for example, they will get a Slack notification/message from Yext saying they receieved a new review for X entity/location.    Same logic/question applies for analytics, attribute changes, etc.    Like a slackbot that can be configured for each client to get whatever notifications they want via slack in real\-time.
workaround: Unknown

summary: Permissions for Users to View All Tickets in Platform
description: Ability to provision users access to see *all* tickets submitted, as opposed to only the tickets that they have. It would be great to have the ability to make this native to a built\-in role (e.g. Account Manager).
workaround: Currently, there is no workaround.

summary: Ability to customise sub account set up for a partner portal
description: It would be great for Partners to be able to customise their sub account set up and apply this to their sub accounts for example the content fields displayed, addition of custom fields, which fields are collapsed/expanded etc. Ideally Partners would be able to use this to  a) set up these preference for each new sub account?  b) to amend existing sub accounts to meet these preferences?
workaround: Manual adjustment \- not feasible for partners with hundreds/thousands of accounts. Solution templates enable some of this functionality but not all.

summary: JSON Diff on App Update
description: When updating an app in the platform, the Authorization page includes a link to show a JSON diff in Admin Console. But that link doesn't show you the diff directly \- it just redirects you to Admin Console. I assume the expectation is that you'll click on Show Diff on the top\-right, authorize the account, and proceed to the next screen where you see the diff for each affected file, but that wasn't obvious to me at first.    It would be great if that link could redirect you to the diff itself as that's really what the user is after!
workaround: Figuring out you need to use the Show Diff button, authorizing the account (and manually inputting the account number), then going back to another screen to click "Continue" and viewing the diffs

summary: Login via Yext CLI
description: The Yext CLI doesn't provide an easy way to redirect to your account, even though you might often want to access your account immediately after applying resources. I often find this annoying when I'm working with Streams or custom fields, and I want to verify that the CaC changes I made are correct.    The idea would be to have a yext login that does that lets you redirect to the platform with ease.
workaround: Looking up the account ID, and manually typing in www.yext.com/s/++{account\-id}++

summary: Low License Count Notifications
description: Allow users to create an automated notification that alerts them when their remaining available licenses drop below a certain threshold. Allow the user to choose the threshold (\<50 licenses remaining, \<10, etc).
workaround: None

summary: export all login attempts of users
description: We have seen major interests where users would like to have the option to export all login attempts of users within an account.
workaround: NA

summary: Have permissions to add new users without having access to SAML Configuration
description: Currently, if we set up a user permission to be able to add new users they also get access to set up SAML Configuration. Can we please separate these two features? There are some individuals who should be able to add new users but should not have access to SAML configuration.
workaround: Only users with access to SAML configuration can add new users

summary: Add User Filter and Export to the User History View
description: Usually you are using User History to find event logs for a specific user, not all users, so it would be nice to filter the view. Similarly, add export functionality to enable offline analysis of User History Events.
workaround: NA

summary: Add Partner Support Center to White-label Brand Settings
description: Add Partner Support Center to White-label Brand Settings
workaround: User contacts Yext and Yext directs user to reseller contact.

summary: Basic Metadata Extraction for SAML Certs in UI
description: On the [https://www.yext.com/s/me/account/saml/](https://www.yext.com/s/me/account/saml/) page, we show the base64 encoding of the IDP certificate. Since the contents are opaque, you often copy/paste that encoding value into a decoding tool like [https://www.sslshopper.com/certificate\-decoder.html](https://www.sslshopper.com/certificate\-decoder.html) in order to see basic data. It would be helpful is the UI attempted to decode the certificate and show basic metadata. Useful fields to extract:    Common Name    Organization    Organization Unit    Locality    State    Country    Valid From    Valid To    Key Size    Serial Number
workaround: Copy/paste into an external tool.

summary: Bulk select and turn on early access features in Account Features
description: The Spring 23' Release was jam\-packed with new features but turning all of them on in Account Features took a long time. It would be great if there was an option to select all in bulk and turn on everything with a few clicks.
workaround: Go one by one and switch from off to on and then click save.

summary: Add Non-Assignable Licenses to Management API
description: Right now, the Licenses API only returns assignable licenses (e.g. Review Monitoring), however, it would be useful in to be able to check via API if an account has a non\-assignable license (e.g. Search, Live API, etc.) when building apps, integrations, and functions.
workaround: When app\-building we can hit certain servers to see if licenses exist, however, there is no equivalent workaround when making calls from Postman, a function, etc.

summary: Improved role concept for global accounts
description: While it's possible to manage permissions at an entity\-folder level, it's currently not possible to manage permissions for Sites (Sites UI, Site Attributes, Code Editor, Repository, Point\-in\-Time Backups, etc). This is problematic for global accounts that seek to ensure country users can only access sites relevant to their country.  With this idea, it would be possible to define permission rights to Sites, so that a user role can be granted access to one or several sites (as opposed to having access to all Sites, as is the case today).
workaround: No, because there is no Folder\-like scope structure for "Sites": you can either grant access for a role to all Pages or to none. Due to this, Global accounts that seek to prevent country users from accessing Sites that are not relevant to their country, have to prevent country users from accessing any site, and instead have a global user do the work that should be done by country users. This creates unnecessary dependencies between the Global and Country teams, and makes it harder to use the platform efficiently.

summary: Notify Button on Create User Page
description: Create a button that determines whether or not a welcome email goes out to a created user.
workaround: Right now if you create a SAML user within the Add a Single User section, it requires you to have the Password section set to **User will create their own password (an email will be sent)**. This would send a SAML user a welcome email even though these users might not need or want them.

summary: Support for "Does not contain" Logic in Filter
description: It would be helpful if we supported a filter that provides "does not contain" logic.    For example, in the Dev Console \> Logs table, I have many Knowledge API request logs that look like this:    * api.yext.com/v2/accounts/me/entityprofiles/**prefix_entityID**/en?v=20221010    * where certain entities begin with a prefix, and some do not    In my use case, I want to filter my request logs to only requests that **do NOT** have prefix in them. I'm trying to debug a subset of logs that only pertain to a certain group of entities.
workaround: One must individually filter to specific URLs.

summary: Allow custom roles to manage certain entity types only
description: Allow custom roles to manage certain entity types only.    E.g. Role 'A' should only be able to manage data for Entity Type 'FAQ' and 'News' only.
workaround: NA

summary: Add Notes Section to App Details in Developer Console
