But once the client fill these Brand Settings (checklist) by using the quick link ([https://yext.com/s/\[Business_id}/listingsLaunch?publisherId=715](https://yext.com/s/%5BBusiness_id%7D/listingsLaunch?publisherId=715) or559), it reset the ID(s).   I am aware this is a low priority request, but it will spare time to the involved teams to be able to keep these IDs without having to search for them again.   Thanks !
workaround: Connect to GBP or use or Facebook Bulk Tasks again to pull the IDs (when possible, all people don't have access to it)

summary: Ability to bulk Force Sync publisher listings
description: Ability to bulk Force Sync publisher listings in Network Profiles.    **Can you add Force Sync as one of the actions to 'More Options \-list' in Network Profiles / All listings \-page?**    For example, when we do some major changes to location(s) information that should update as soon as possible to all publishers, we must Force Sync all listings one by one. It would be much easier and faster if we could use bulk Force Sync.
workaround: Click Force Sync action for all publisher listings one by one.

summary: Google Search Term Impressions' as a default metric insight in Dashboards
description: Can you add 'Google Search Term Impressions' as a default insight in Dashboards section?    **Metrics:** Google Search Term Impressions (Location Listings)    **Dimensions**: Raw Search Term (Listings)    **Time range:** Comparing to Previous Period (for example Last 12 months)    This new metric provides very useful and interesting data for all clients, so it should be easily added to dashboards.
workaround: Build this metric manually for every client account in Report Builder and then add it as a New Custom Insight.

summary: Ability to use Yext via Smartphone
description: They want to use Yext via their smartphone especially for making Listing Posts, replying Review comments (and approving them), and update entities in Knowledge Graph.
workaround: No workaround. They have to use PC so far.

summary: Partner can select Enable or Disenable for each social post publisher
description: When partner's customer want to use social post, whether or not customer connect with a publisher, they can see homepage post(1st party), Google, and FB.    Almost all partner don't use homepage post using widget, so that partner want to delete homepage post (house mark) as default.    Also, in Japan almost all customer don't use Linkedin so they want to decide showing Linkedin logo or not in the future.
workaround: Enable or Disenable functionality for each social post in setting section for both each account level and partner level

summary: Grubhub integration
description: Grubhub menu/location integration like Doordash.
workaround: Manual update.

summary: Adding Angi as a Publisher
description: Would like to have Angi added as a Publisher
workaround: Currently updating Angi information independently of Yext.

summary: Ability to add label on Locations on the Publisher tab
description: Often times when I am QA\-ing locations I would like to add a label to the locations that are showing a certain error, so that I can make those changes on KG \> Entities \- as some errors can be resolved in bulk.
workaround: Have to click into each one and fix the issue or add a label into them individually.

summary: Allow higher menu prices on specific delivery app publishers
description: How can we enable publishing higher menu prices to DoorDash, UberEats, etc. to help combat the fees that these delivery apps charge.    For both Menu Listings and Yext for Food, we currently we can only push a single price per menu item to all publishers.    Could it be possible to create a field in the Knowledge Graph such as "Incremental Delivery App Price Increase" where clients could input "20%" increase value for example. Then, the Knowledge Graph can push out to the delivery apps:    ("Menu item price") \* ( 1 + "Incremental Delivery App Price Increase")    i.e. a hamburger might be $10 on the menu listing, it will be $12 on DoorDash and the other delivery apps
workaround: No current workaround that I am aware of

summary: Bulk social posts with Photos for each locations
description: The Spring release enables batch social posting for each store, but we want to post different photos for each store.
workaround: Need to work on one social posting for each store, one at a time.

summary: Posts Notifications
description: Is there a way to create a notification of any errors / issues posting a scheduled post?    The example: pre\-scheduling a bunch of posts to go out on July 4, noticing that they have not been posted, logging into Yext and seeing an error message. It would be nice to be notified of these errors ahead of time / in realtime to be able to adjust.
workaround: double checking the directories and yext dashboard to confirm posts were made.

summary: Notification for Live Listings
description: You can currently only set up Listings notifications for data, verification, publisher credential, and duplicate issues. Some clients would benefit from a notification to let them know when a listing goes live, especially if they are on self\-serve, to avoid having to go directly into the Yext platform periodically to check.
workaround: Going into Yelp and the Listings tab, filtering to the desired entities, and then checking if the listings have gone live for however long it take them to go live.

summary: Choose publisher field sync settings on folder level
description: Right now, the publisher field sync settings apply on an account level. However, because we have different teams/stakeholders in the same account who have differing syncing requirements, it would be great to be able to customize the field syncing setting for specific folders/entities.    For example, one folder can have sync "yes" for the Facebook cover photo and another folder can have sync "no"
workaround: No workaround

summary: Enhancements to the Listings>Publishers>Configuration Sub-Tab
description: Hi Team,    We receive a\-lot of feedback from clients that there is not enough transparency around the many fields in the KG and where they actually sync. The configuration tab is a great start but it does not provide a complete look at ALL the fields that a publisher accepts (ie looking that the Google tab there are a number of fields like attributes, COVID fields, hours, secondary hours types (including holiday), temporarily closed, telehealth link, FAQ, services, ECLs. that are not included. It would also be helpful to include information about where each of these fields get synced on the listings themselves and have a description about each (especially for the photo and URL fields).    Lastly, it has come to my attention that users who do not have access to the FULL account cannot access this configuration page. I have clients who manage a region of a health system who need to have that focused view on their region but also need visibility into what fields sync where. This also applies to clients who share an admin account (ie large hospitals where each organization lives under one account but they have access to only their own organization.)    Please let me know if you have any questions. Transparency and making our entity profiles less confusing will absolutely lead to more complete KG and we are just not there yet.    Thank you!    Wilhelmina
workaround: NA

summary: Further Integration with "Order with Google"
description: We're seeing more and more customer activity around "Order With Google", and there are requests to build out the integration, specifically around 3 fields:    1. The ability to designate one of the links (likely the corporate site) as "preferred by business"    2. The ability to update the photo that shows up on the "Order with Google" screen    3. More detailed analytics on Clicks for the different links (i.e Corporate Site vs Door Dash vs Uber Eats)
workaround: Currently there is no workaround

summary: In-Platform Banner: Update Holiday Hours Reminder
description: Hi team, submitting the following to the Ideas Board per Teddy Riker and Jessie Smith's guidance!    Historically, Yext CS Strategy \& Operations has distributed "update holiday hours" reminder communications to clients around the December holidays.    Perhaps we could create an in\-platform "update holiday hours reminder" banner / alert that could be displayed around major holidays (i.e. the Fourth of July, December holidays). This process would more proactively and seamlessly encourage day\-to\-day users of the platform to make more appropriate updates to their hours.    It could potentially link to resources to provide more context like the fantastic [HH blog post](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/blog/New\-Listings\-Findings\-Underscore\-the\-Importance\-of\-Setting\-Holiday\-Hours) that Jessie Smith published and this [++press release++](https://www.yext.com/about/news\-media/memorial\-day\-holiday\-hours?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=pr&utm_content=memorial\-day\-holiday\-hours\-may\-2022). Please let me know if you have any questions or additional recommendations for how we could streamline these reminders!    Thank you,    Alexa
workaround: Historically, Yext CS Strategy \& Operations has distributed "update holiday hours" reminder communications to clients around the December holidays.

summary: Calendar View Format for Scheduled Posts & Scheduled Listings Updates
description: It would be helpful if customers could view their scheduled listings updates in a calendar view/format.    * For scheduling holiday hours, they can pull open the calendar view to ensure they are planning out their scheduled updates ahead of time and seeing them in an easy\-to\-read format    * For posting \- they can view their posts in almost a content calendar format to make sure time\-specific posts are set up and scheduled
workaround: unknown

summary: Adding Mapbox/OpenStreetMap to Publishers List
description: There are dozens of major companies/services across the internet that incorporate some combination of Mapbox and/or OpenStreetMap into mapping functionality on their websites. Adding these open\-source maps as Publishers would immediately increase accuracy for business locations and addresses across many different services with only one or two additional Publishing sources.
workaround: Update both services manually on an as\-needed basis.

summary: Historical listings records
description: It would be helpful to be able to access historical listings statuses both as a one\-entity record but as well as a wholistic account view in the UI. This will allow users to see the progress made by purchasing Yext Listings and potentially Premier Support or other assisting products as the number of live listings increases over time. It would also assist in troubleshooting as we can easily see if multiple locations were live at one point, etc.
workaround: Periodically manually making "snapshots" of the listings statuses as export files.

summary: Undo Button
description: If you opt in and claim something that you shouldn't (wrong match), hit 'undo' and you'll opt out and resend the data that used to be on the listing
workaround: Escalate to support

summary: Option to Indicate Search Strategy in Identify Listings flow for Service Area Businesses
description: The [Identify Listing](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/tracks/listings/lis207\-listings\-sync\-process/03\-searching\-and\-matching/) flow in Yext allows users to identify existing listings for their entities. However, this process is only able to identify matches for listings that do not have Address hidden \- it does not work for Service Area Businesses.    It would be awesome if the Identify Listings scan gave users the option to use "Name + Store ID" or just "Name" as a search strategy so that matches could be identified for Service Area Businesses. Often SABs have a geomodifier in their name, so matching on Name would be sufficient in order to identify matches.
workaround: Reaching out to Yext Support to help identify matches for SABs

summary: Complete Field List and Sync Times in Publisher Details Page
description: Could Publisher Details pages be updated to include:    1. Information about integration type and expected update times    2. The full set of Fields that are being sent to the publisher    Currently, we have information about Dual Sync v. Submission publishers in [Hitchhikers](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/tracks/listings/lis107\-listings\-integrations/02\-dual\-sync\-vs.\-submission/), and update time questions are answered one off in the [Community](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/community/t/apple\-maps\-updating\-times/2407), but it would be really helpful to have this information directly on the Publisher Details page in Yext.    Additionally, including the full set of fields that can be synced to a publisher on the Publisher Detail page (like attributes, Services lists, and hours fields), would be really helpful in understanding how the Knowledge Graph is powering Listings across various publishers in the publisher network.
workaround: Reaching out to Yext Support, asking Community, searching in Hitchhikers documentation

summary: Entity Preview consistency through the platform
description: Hey,    We have clients customizing a lot the Entity Preview by using custom internal names and other information they want to be surfaced out in the KG/Entities view.  The problem is when they switch to the Listings (Publishers, All Listings,...) or Analytics views (Activity,...), the Entity Preview is back to default.    It is not really a really consistant user experience, and this is not the first time that I hear clients saying they are a bit lost with this change of display.    Having the same Entity Preview everywhere would be easier to "read".    Thanks if you have any input on that point.    Nicolas
workaround: None as far as I know

summary: Posting Special Offers with Expiration Dates that Revert/Delete
description: I was recommeneded to add an idea here that a prospect asked during a demo call:    It would be really cool if a customer could schedule a post such as a special offer or limited time offer with the same scheduling \& reverting logic as scheduling hours changes for Listings.    For example, my prospect works for a 100+ location property management company. They run special offers such as "1 Month Free rent when you refer a friend \- expires x/x/xxxx." and want it set up where they can post it to GMB and it will revert/be removed once the offer has expired.    If there is a workaround for this, please let me know!    Best,    Micah
workaround: Unknown

summary: New UI of Meinungsmeister needs to be optimized
description: If you go to the Publisher Overview, you will see the Reviews only Publisher Meinungsmeister. However, without specifying the synchronized entities ...    ![0](data:image/png;base64,)    Since the entities have to be linked manually, all entities have to be displayed as well. Currently there is no display.    Message from Yext Support:    The new UI no longer shows the list of sites that are "review only" publishers. Furthermore, we cannot know which opinion master listings we are currently matched to or which listings are live/unavailable/in progress.
workaround: none

summary: Respect Folder/Labels at the time the post goes live
description: The folder/label selected at the time a post is created/scheduled is static. Whatever folder/label the entity had at the time the post is created is what will be used at the time the post is posted.    We would like these folders/labels to be dynamic in that they are respected at the time the post goes LIVE. This allows us to make changes to the folder an entity is in (Active vs Inactive) or label (maybe they switched promotions or opted in to a promotion) between the time the post is created and when it actually posts. This would allow for scheduling posts further in advance, knowing we could still update labels before it goes live.
workaround: If a folder or label is changed between the time the post is scheduled and when it goes live, we have to delete the entire scheduled post and re\-do it.

summary: Include Website URL for Social Post CTA Option
description: When doing a social post, you can add a CTA with either a static custom URL or a URL based off a field in the Knowledge Graph. Website URL is not an option. Some customers will want a social post with a quick link to their store pages. Having Website URL as an option would be nice for these circumstances.
workaround: Create a custom field that has website URL as an embedded field for all locations in the account

summary: Google Q/A - Match Reviews features like Date filter + Q/A label + Clean Export
description: * Add a new date filter with the format (between date 1 and date2) like for Reviews but for Q/A    * Add label field to Q/A (like for reviews)    * Export XLSX for Q/A without special characters (like for reviews)
workaround: No workaround for any of these requests

summary: Schedule cancellation of service in advance
description: Our cancellation policy allows us to know in advance when a client's listing service will officially conclude and can be canceled. Thought it might be handy to be able to 'schedule' the cancellation.
workaround: No workaround currently available.

summary: Posting - image information
description: Under the 'posting' section can we add the suggested image ratio size to the UI? (not just the image size).
workaround: No work around.

summary: Account verification status
description: Hi team,    It would be amazing if there would be a way for CSMs to be able to check whether an account has Direct Verification or Bulk Verification status.    Can this be added to the account settings, or be checked off in a section of admin perhaps?
workaround: Currently there's no way for us to check this without asking support / PS to go into their GMB folder.

summary: Notification about conflicts / problems with Publisher
description: In the setting of notifications it is possible to create a notification about conflicts / problems with the publishers (all).    It would be extremely helpful if you can limit the publishers by selection in this setting. For example, for Facebook and GMB.
workaround: Currently no selection is possible.

summary: Listings Sync History
description: Currently, we don't have a way to view the history of listings' sync statuses. We can only view the current status per listing. We can not see when the listing status changed and what it was before that. This information would be useful when trying to understand listings that are not currently synced. By seeing when the listing went unavailable, we have more information to investigate the issue and a better understanding of the impact by seeing how long the listing has not been synced. This can be especially useful if we see a large number of listings not synced for a publisher. Additionally, by being able to easily view the number of listings that are synced or not over time, we can get a better understanding of our impact across publishers.    Having an audit history of listings directly in the platform will be very useful.
workaround: We have some reporting capabilities for listings but in order to get an in depth audit history of specific listings, a ticket currently needs to be opened with Yext

summary: Specify what photo isn't meeting GMB requirements under "Data Issue" within Listing Status Details
description: Currently, in the Listings UI, we get a common unavailable reason being Data Issue, copied below. However, we have to go through each photo one by one to catch one of the many images that don't meet this requirement. Since the API knows which photo is returning the error, it'd be great to be pointed to the image that is causing this data issue when clicking into "Verify Data" instead of being led back to the general Knowledge Graph, to make for a quick \& easy update.    Unavailable type data issue type:    "Google has notified us that one or more of your photos do not meet their requirements. Please ensure that your photos measure a minimum of 250px on the short edge and a maximum of 5000px on the long edge. Photos must also have a file size greater than 10240 bytes (i.e. 10 KB) and less than 5 MB. We have excluded sending photos to Google My Business for now so that the rest of your data can be synced with Google My Business. To resolve this error, please ensure all Google photos, gallery photos, and the business logo are within these sizing requirements."
workaround: In order to resolve for this, we have to go through each photo one by one \& check to see if it meets the minimum of 250px \& 5000px with a file size greater than 10240 bytes by clicking 'Verify Data' \& being sent back to the Knowledge Graph. [(sample video of the error here)](https://www.screencast.com/t/u11ldUSYNxN)

summary: Description text as short and long description
description: There is a problem that some publishers only allow a short description (Foursquare, tellows). The description text in the Knowledge Graph is then truncated in the listings. Is it possible to create another field for a short description next to the description? This will then only be transmitted to the publishers who have a limit of less than 750 characters.
workaround: None

summary: Photo captions sync with GMB
description: Photo captions should be synced to GMB more than once.    At the moment photo captions are synced to GMB only when they are added at the same time as photos are saved in Knowledge Graph. After that added captions to existing photos won't sync to GMB, which would be great when we or clients want to optimize GMB profiles with Yext. Also, it is confusing to consult clients that they should add caption ONLY at the time when they load new photo to KG, which is not easy to remember.
workaround: Delete photo from KG, then load same photo again and add caption before saving

summary: Facebook setup flow
description: We sometimes cannot sync Facebook pages through the flow since we're not given the option we need every time.    Please allow 3 options for every FB sync flow:    * Option to 1) sync to a different page from the ones shown in the screen before    * Option 2) create a new page    * Option 3) sync to specific page by providing URL

summary: Google Chat
description: Hello Team,    Are we planning to develop a functionality to answer to GMB chat messages via Yext?    Many thanks,    Olivia
workaround: None

summary: Automatic Geotagging of Images
description: Geotagging is the act of adding a location to your image using specific coordinates. Since [**Google My Business**](https://g.page/GilbertSEO) and ranking in local SERPs is dependent on your location, geotagging is vital to showing up in search. The first step to geotag is to find EXIF data. EXIF stands for Exchangeable Image File Format. This data captures the file format of every photo on your GMB, but it also adds more information, such as GPS location and camera properties.    The idea would be that Yext will automaticly add geotags to the image EXIF data directly after uploading the images in Yext. This could help as well for Local SEO.
workaround: Workaround to add geitag information manually:    * Go to [**tool.geoimgr.com**](https://tool.geoimgr.com/) in any browser.    * Search your business name and correct location. You want the latitude and longitude to match exactly for someone searching in the area.    * Upload your business image that you want to appear on your profile.    * Click "Write EXIF Tags" and wait for the tool to add location information.    * Click "Download" to save the new image now with geotags.

summary: Show more data per page
description: The default number of listings statuses shown per page is only 25, this is too low for a large account. Please consider making the default 100 statuses, and also add options to show 500 and 1000 per page.
workaround: The only way is manually change the view to 100 per page each time, to further filter the statuses (when possible), and then when there are thousands of listings, you have to click through many pages.

summary: Only accept images to be uploaded that meet file size and dimension requirements
description: Our Partners are adding photos to their listings which are beng flagged from Google My Business as not meeting their requirements. Subsequently no photos are submitted to Google My Business.    We only find out that the images are incorrect when we view Entry Summary. There is also no flag on which image doesnt meet the requirements.    Is it possible to    1 \- only be able to upload photos that meet the requirements / alert before publishing that it doesnt.    2 \- flag which images do not meet the requirements
workaround: None im aware

summary: Checking the statuses of social posts on Yext platform
description: It would be much easier if we can check on Yext platform whether a social post for GMB is rejected or not.   I understand that social posts are once uploaded to Google and then retroactively taken down by them, and we do not continually monitor social posts after successfully sent, but is there any way that we can monitor the status after the submission is completed?
workaround: The information is available on GMB but need to go to individual store pages \> Posts to check. Clients with many locations and who posts frequently would appreciate this feature.

summary: Add a completion % for fields to incentivise field users to add content to more fields
description: A business I work with suggested that we should 'gamify' the completion of fields in the Knolwegde Graph for the Listings product to incentivise field users to fill in more fields. This is something that Booking.com and some other data aggregators do and it helps make local users more motivated to fill in the fields.
workaround: None

summary: Google Place IDs in Bulk
description: Hey team,    With the new release and addition of the "Google Entity Relationship" field within the Knowledge Graph, it would be great if there was a tool that would allow us to extract Google Place IDs in Bulk! To provide some additional context, a lot of customers operate business outside of another store location/shopping center, so having this tool will allow customers to easily set up this field for their business.    Thanks!  Hannah
workaround: Use the Google Place ID Finder ([https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/places\-placeid\-finder](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/places\-placeid\-finder)) to extract the Place ID on an one\-off basis. However, for large clients with lots of relationships to set up, extracting the place ID one by one can be tedious and time consuming.

summary: Default option for Google My Business connection should be neutral
description: When connecting multiple GMB Listings to a Yext account from a Partner Portal perspective, when a matching GMB Listing is not shown for the locations you are connecting, the default option is to "Add a new GMB page". This unnecessary duplicate listing creation can cause Google to flag a business' account and have negative repercussions. This can be problematic when you are connecting multiple GMB listings in multiple categories that need to be connected separately, and the Yext UI does not immediately recognize connections so you may unknowingly create a duplicate listing. If you changed the default option on this drop\-down choice to Skip instead of Add new GMB listing, this would cause less problems and make it easier for users to manage multiple connections  ![Screen Shot 2021\-07\-23 at 12.27.01 PM](https://aws1.discourse\-cdn.com/turtlehead/optimized/2X/5/5fe28bf7a9b6efffdb026fdfa56a434542fb1858_2_690x498.jpeg)    [**Screen Shot 2021\-07\-23 at 12.27.01 PM1596×1152 126 KB**](https://aws1.discourse\-cdn.com/turtlehead/original/2X/5/5fe28bf7a9b6efffdb026fdfa56a434542fb1858.jpeg)    . Image attached for clarity.
workaround: NA

summary: Bring the 4 Listing "Overview" Boxes Back
description: Wanted to share some feedback for everyone to get others' input and to hopefully pass it along to the developers with regards to the changes made to the Listing page within Yext after the Winter release…    Every month I do an audit of my client's account to ensure everything is in working order, and before the Winter release, I would do that by logging into my admin account and visiting the top\-level Listing page. From there I would review the 4 boxes at the top of the page to see what clients and listings have errors, were not synced, had duplicates to suppress, and review suggestions that come in. It was super easy to see all 4 categories within the boxes and I even told clients to regularly check those boxes to see how things were going with their listings whenever they were interested.    Now with the new Winter release, the 4 boxes have been removed and replaced with a log, transparent view of all listings and their status. This is fine for showing transparency, and will probably be okay for my clients when they are logged into their accounts that only include 1\-4 entities, but for me it's awful. I can no longer get a pulse of things at a glance when auditing 86 entities, instead, I have to visit multiple pages and lists to get the details and it's taking me 3 times as long to complete my audit.    All in all, the winter release is great with the exception of removing the 4 "overview" boxes on the Listing page. Is there any way to return those boxes? If not then what's the best way to audit all my client's accounts in an efficient manner?
workaround: Having to visit more than one page to complete my audit

summary: New Listings UI in the Knowledge Graph
description: Hi Team,   In the old UI you would be able to quickly click on a publisher from the quick access tab on the left side of the screen. However in the new UI this has been removed, we know that this can still be done in the new UI by clicking on the publisher filter tab, but the filters can be inconvenient when clients just want to look something up real fast. Would be great of we could add a similar tab like this in the new UI! [https://www.screencast.com/t/vk2z3VOiz8sa](https://www.screencast.com/t/vk2z3VOiz8sa)
workaround: No current workaround on this.

summary: Listings Filtering Behavior
description: Listings Filtering Behavior
workaround: If the user wanted to see the full numbers again, un\-filtering by status to see the numbers for missing values. However, for larger accounts (as in this example), this can take some time to re\-load due to the amount of information.

summary: Listings Not Sync'd Scheduled Export/Notification
description: Hey team,    It would be great if we could either schedule an export or use notifications to send out all listings that are currently not sync'd on a set cadence. This information can be easily viewed/exported within the All Listings tab currently, but there's no way to schedule that export on a set cadence (i.e. weekly, monthly, daily, etc.). Let me know if any additional information is needed!
workaround: Current notification settings can be set up to Listings for invalid location data issues, location verification issues, and publisher credentials. However, there is no way to filter for status as not synced as part of these notifications. Therefore, soft unavailable data issues (i.e. too long business description) are included, which makes these exports appear to have more not synced listings that what is true. Additionally, there could be additional reasons that listings are not being synced that are not incapsulated in one of the above reports.

summary: Pulling Alerts from the Publishers Tab
description: Partners love the publisher alerts in the dashboard. However, many have their own dashboard that their end clients log into. Is there a way we can let these alerts be pulled through API or webhooks so that the partners can alert this in their own dashboard to the end client?
workaround: None at the moment \- plan to share this with the partners on using this for any changes on listing statuses.    [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/knowledgeapis/webhooks/listings/listings/](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/knowledgeapis/webhooks/listings/listings/)

summary: Edit scheduled Post after creation
description: **Describe your Idea**  It would be great if I can re\-edit a social post that is already being scheduled in the dashboard. Currently for scheduled social posts you cannot edit the text once it's being submitted.
workaround: Now I have to remove the social post first and re\-create and re\-schedule it again. Would be much simpler if I can just edit the scheduled post in the posting queue.

summary: Google User Photos | Indicate Removed Photos
description: For Photos that we would like to flag as inappropriate to Google, we currently navigate to the Listings \> Google User Photos tab to do so. If they don't exist anymore, a 404 error will appear upon hitting that button. I'd be great if we could have a button that allows us to "Mark Removed" themselves. Ideally, that would happen automatically without user intervention! It currently adds a bit more time go through all photos as the removed ones are mixed in, so this would definitely make the workflow a bit more seamless.
workaround: Customers can submit Support tickets for our teams to indicate they have been removed internally.

summary: Turning Off Ability to Create GMB Listings

Sometimes scanning can miss these matches or if they're not created a GMB listing is created. Some clients don't want Yext to be creating any listings on their behalf but there is currently no way to turn that functionality on and off like it's possible for Yelp.    This would help us ensure no duplicates are unnecessarily created in GMB when a client is explicit that we shouldn't be creating listings.
workaround: Matching to the correct listings, but sometimes I've seen that even when the listing is matched to, we end up creating a duplicate.

summary: Editing of already created Google Post
description: A Google Post that has already been created cannot be edites afterwards. For Example, because errors are noticed. Then you have to create a new Google Post and delete the faulty one. That is not comfortable.

summary: Listings Data Error Notifications Filters

workaround: At the moment, the only options is to Opt out from the Publisher site.

summary: Google Video Posting

summary: Add LinkedIn locations to Yext Listings
description: LinkedIn allows us to manually list up to [100 locations](https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.linkedin.com/help/linkedin/answer/71216/edit\-your\-linkedin\-page\-or\-showcase\-page?lang=en__;!!OLcMSNlyTjs!RdCNJ84p6EX2r8H5DezFBzjWQPKtVQV_LziQbDFhc2nwAn4WcXkxoCM3RHPjm\-Yc$).    Does Yext have a way to update our LinkedIn locations (e.g. through Listings or [linked accounts](https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.yext.com/s/3307270/account/linkedAccounts?isLandingPage=false__;!!OLcMSNlyTjs!RdCNJ84p6EX2r8H5DezFBzjWQPKtVQV_LziQbDFhc2nwAn4WcXkxoCM3RAJamVYo$))?    Listing businesses' locations should help the LinkedIn knowledge graph and improve my company's ability to appear for searches in out\-of\-footprint areas. For example, a LinkedIn user in New York City viewing their activity feed will be more likely to see an article shared or recommended on LinkedIn that is attributed to a company that has locations nearby.
workaround: Manual input on the LinkedIn website.

summary: Advanced Filtering for Social Posting

It would be great if we could use the same advanced filtering functionality we have on the Locations tab on the Listings \> Post tab to filter on the desired entities. The ability to save these advanced filters would be very useful also as it would keep the results dynamic.
workaround: Use advanced filtering in the Locations tab to identify the entities that meet a certain criteria, apply a label, and remember to actively manage the label for future social posts.

summary: New Notification Type
description: Add an automated email notification whenever a new Question is found.    Currently I need to manually check for Q\&A entries periodically.    My clients seldom have a new question appear, so I don't check on their behalf very often. But that means that a question can languish for weeks before it's answered, which is sub\-optimal. An email notification as soon as a new question is found would be very helpful. An option for that doesn't seem to be availanle in Notifications.
workaround: I manually check for new questions roughly monthly.

summary: Set up a Requested Edit functionality for softer issues like 'GMB Cover Photo needs to be 16x9 Ratio' and 'Facebook Name does not match' that show up as a Needs Attention Issues
description: There are 2 specific Listings Needs Attention issues that are so minor, they should be automated as a Requested Edit functionality because they should be categorized as data updates to the Knowledge Graph instead of Needs Attention Issues, since they do not disrupt the Listing connection from being Live. These two issues are:    * GMB Cover Photo needs to be 16x9 Ratio    <!\-\- \-\->    * Facebook Name does not match    To give more context, working in a Partner Portal and managing Yext for clients. This can give customers unnecessary concern about their Listings, when it is a simple change on the Knowledge Graph that could easily be pushed through as a Requested Edit.
workaround: The workaround now is to manually update these fields.

summary: Escalate unavailable listings to support without leaving the account or in bulk for SMB and Partner
description: Whenever there are some listings unavailable there is a high chance support can push them live. The possibility of escalting those without leaving the platform would make working in the platform more efficient.

summary: Entity Creation Date Column in KG
description: Having an entity created column would be really helpful especially to be able to filter and export. Some of my clients want to see how many locations/agents they added in a certain time frame
workaround: pulling full entity export in admin and doing a custom SQL query to pull the entities within a time frame

summary: Documentation for API Validation Rules

I see some mentions of validation rules in our docs currently, but some errors I've seen recently that I can't find written anywhere include:    "Value must not contain control characters. These are non\-printable characters that can be mishandled by publishers."  "Invalid field twitterHandle: Length may not exceed 15 characters;"    Including what fields are required for each entity type creation would also be a huge help!
workaround: Try submitting a request to see if validation fails

summary: Schedule updates at a specific time
description: We currently have the ability to schedule updates for a specific **day**, and the update will run at midnight of the location's time zone set. It would be great to be able to set the update at a specific **time** on that date as well.
workaround: One could either set the time zone of the location to be shifted by the number of hours needed (UTC+8 if they are actually stationed at UTC+0 but want the content to update at 8am), or to simply log in and manually update at the time desired.

summary: Filter by address parts (i.e. postal code, city, canton, region,...) not possible
description: An additional filter option of address parts (i.e. postal code, city, canton, region,...) would be very helpful.    Example: This way we could search for incomplete addresses and fix them fast.
workaround: Our workaround is to export all entities as CSV and filter them in Excel. However, this can only be done by experienced users.

summary: Show Publisher Sync settings at field level / in entity

For example \- there's no point adding in a Google Logo because the sync is off.    An idea could be to have this integrated through as a tooptip/hover help so clients can easily understand. Often this is inherited and new users don't know about sync settings and where to find them.    This can cause frustration.
workaround: Client needs to know that sync settings are available by field for certain publishers.    They also need to know to check them and where to find them.

summary: Add a notice that Google does not accept Holiday Hours for Additional Hours fields

Since Google does not accept those holiday hours, it would be great to know this ahead of time rather than updating those holiday hours and finding that they did not update on Google listings.
workaround: No workaround unless you temporarily mark that day as closed in the main associated Additional Hours field, which is not best practice.

summary: Olo Menu Connector
description: Instead of importing Olo Menus as an ECL, the connector will import customer Menu's as entity types so they can be synced to Menu Listings + be leveraged in Answers, Pages, and potentially product reviews!
workaround: Export ECL file and re\-import as entities

summary: Knowledge Graph Bulk Editing - Add to Existing vs Replace All
description: Hey team,    Many users accidently override existing info in Yext when bulk editing since there isn't a clear message around the action being done. For example, users accidently override Holiday Hours all the time by trying to add an additional one through bulk editing.
workaround: The user has to upload edits through a spreadsheet and make sure to enable the "add to existing" setting (which is missed all the time as well)

summary: Showing additional categories without permission to edit them
description: Currently users cannot see the additional categories if they don't have permission to edit them. We have restricted the possibility to edit the additional categories so that the Google Business Profile doesn't get suspended due to unnecessary edits. It would be beneficial to anyhow show what additional categories have been added but currently that is not possible.
workaround: NA

summary: Ability to schedule the edit of labels and folders
description: It would be nice, if the future sorting of entities in a specific folder and the change/edit/remove of labels would be schedulable in Content. Just as, for example, the change of opening hours or the address of the location can be planned for the future.    Background: We got 2 main types of entities (type A, type B). From time to time it happens, that some entities change their status from A to B or from B to A. In this case, the unique ID of the corresponding entity changes, as well as the label(s) change. And sometimes also the folder the entity is sorted in is changing too.
workaround: Set a reminder (in personal calendar), look up the corresponding entity in Yext, edit the label(s) and move the entity into the correct folder.

summary: Use Labels as filter option in Products and Services
description: Now you can only select entities or folders when assigning Products \& Services. Would be nice to select Labels.
workaround: Edit the Enhanced Content Lists from the entities view.

summary: Deselect Entity Type Availability in bulk on Field Creation
description: Could we add a function to deselect Entity Type Availability on Field Creation?  There is currently a "Select All" button but when you accidentally use that one would need to deselect all manually or refresh the page.
workaround: Manually deselect the page or do a page refresh (filled out data gets lost though)

summary: Saved Filters
description: While the dedicated Saved Filters screen is helpful (configuration \-\> saved filters), it would be much more helpful if there was a column for "Criteria". That way, a user can spot criteria that is common throughout the filters and adjust if needed.
workaround: Looking into each filter 1 at a time which is time consuming.

summary: Yext Content to provide clients both a Staging & Production environment
description: Most CMS/DXP providers allow users to add content in a Staging environment. After sign off, data can be pushed to a pre\-production and production environment.    This is often viewed as a core requirement for clients choosing a CMS/DXP provider.
workaround: Currently, we offer a Sandbox for clients to develop within, upon which a CLI operation to clone the account is required. This is viewed as considerably "clunky" compared to the out of the box environment setup and automated data transfer between environments that other CMS/DXP offer.

summary: Ability to filter for and bulk apply changes to stores with Scheduled Updates

summary: Change Navigation Behavior for Saving Computed Fields

Similar to other save actions in the platform, users should remain on the current screen to make further changes rather than being auto\-navigated to another screen.
workaround: N/A

summary: Expand Healthcare Professional Built-in Training Field Options
description: The current Education \& Training field within the built\-in Healthcare Professional entity type is limited to education and training options specific to physicians and doesn't provide flexibility for including the training/education received by physician assistants, nurse practitioners, etc. Recommend expanding the pre\-built options to include other education options, such as graduate or phd programs, in addition to the current medical school option.
workaround: Create a custom field

summary: Email Functionality for Knowledge Assistant
description: I had a discussion with a very strategic, large, complex prospect last week and they asked about how we could make it easy for non\-employed providers to get their data into Yext. Specifically, they want a way to make it easy for doctors (very busy people) to validate which locations they work at, after Yext has received a data feed of all locations that they're approved (credentialed) to work at. And then they added more detail saying they wanted providers to be able to do this via email specifically.    I recognize that they complexity of this question is an edge case for knowledge assistant, but I think its something that KA should be able to support in the future. AND I think that KA should work for email flows in addition to text messaging flows.    This relates to this [Hitchhikers Idea](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/3649728049977843871) but is slightly different. I'd be happy to discuss this in greater detail with Product (I will probably be reaching out to Sam Lee about this anyway because this is something we're going to need to figure out for this strategic deal).
workaround: NA

summary: Store multiple searchable addresses in one entity profile in Content (KG)
description: Many customers, especially those in healthcare, have entities (especially doctors) that work at multiple locations. Currently, you can only store one address in a doctor's profile in the KG. To associate the doctor with additional locations where s/he works, you have to create multiple KG entities, either for the doctor or for the secondary locations, and then link the secondary entities to the primary entity. This is not always a desirable option for our customers for a variety of reasons, including the fact that it requires more KG licenses and makes it harder to manage the data for all providers easily.    We need the ability to store multiple addresses that are searchable with a Search experience (ie, addresses that have geo\-coordinate logic included) within a single entity profile. We need to be able to designate the primary address, and then whether all other addresses are "equal" or whether there's meaningful ranking for between addresses.    This would make it so much easier for our healthcare customers to manage provider data, period. It would also make it easier to meet customer needs for search configurations. It would also make listings management easier (assuming we can power listings off the secondary addresses and keep all other info in the provider's KG profile consistent across listings). Let's make it possible for the KG to reflect reality: there is ONE doctor who works in multiple places. Let's make it so that there is ONE profile in KG for that doctor that lists all the places s/he works within it. Thanks team!
workaround: NA

summary: Schedule Temp Closed Updates
description: Be able to schedule updates to have an entity set to Temporarily Closed. We are currently unable to do so due to the fact that temp closed is part of the hours field, so an update to temp closed can't happen unless you're updating the hours as well. However there are cases when a client knows they will be temporarily closed ahead of time (i.e. planned construction), and being able to schedule these updates would be useful. Relevant Slack thread [here](https://yextops.slack.com/archives/CDA4M8ULS/p1686849041710069)
workaround: Marking the entity as temp closed on the exact date

summary: Bulk editing addresses and categories
description: It would be more efficient to allow users to bulk edit addresses.    Additionally, allow users to edit categories, including bulk editing. Currently a ticket has to be submitted and that is not efficient.

summary: Realtime scheduled update failure messages

workaround: User who creates a scheduled update will go through the full flow of setting the update with no error feedback, and then later when the scheduled date comes and something fails, a Yext support agent will reach out to me to let me know that it has failed and assist me in fixing the content that was intended to have already been displayed as this point in time.

summary: Adding multiple photos in the Assets tab
description: Hello,    The tab of Assets only allows one photo to be uploaded at a time, which slows down the process of uploading content specially for reseller portal accounts where each client has its own account. Can we please have the option to upload multiple photos at the same time like in the Gallery field in Knowledge Graph?    Thanks,    Natalia

So a user should be able to navigate away and back, and still see the same visual options applied.

However, the data isn't valuable as Saved Filter "stamping" is largely and internal optimization technique and the Saved Filter field cannot be interacted with or inspected directly. This can sometimes cause weird error out issues and me to lose my changes entirely which is problematic, especially if I made a large change

summary: Add Ability to remove Custom Field Types from Asset Landing Page

It seems that all these custom fields are added to our Asset landing page by default which makes the UI a bit confusing and creates and extensive list of empty Asset Types in the sidebar. Is there a way to remove Custom Field Types from showing up in the asset library? summary: Knowledge Graph filtering for exact string
description: When choosing `is equal to` in a filter in the Knowledge Graph, the filter doesn't necessarily apply character\-specific filters. This is often but not always desirable. For example, it was recently found in a client account that while some healthcare providers had a `Specialty` value of `Infusion, Arthritis & Rheumatology`, one had the same specialty but written with an Oxford comma: `Infusion, Arthritis, & Rheumatology`.` `Searching within the Knowledge Graph for **Specialty** `is equal to` "Infusion, Arthritis, \& Rheumatology" ignored the Oxford comma and returned all entities with the specialty written both ways.    It would be helpful in situations like this to have a filter that returns *only* what is searched for, ignoring nothing about the filter string. `Is equal to` implies a degree of prevision that the filter action does not currently provide.
workaround: NA

summary: Improve Saved Filters UI Function
description: We utilise Saved Filters to control website structure for Pages across a number of clients that are working on launching a lot of microsites (100+), currently, each microsite requires at least 8 filters. We are already at 300+ filters, which will only keep increasing, it is difficult to manage filters at this scale with limited filtering and editing functionality. It would be great to add a few improvements to the filters UI so we can manage them more effectively at scale.    List of improvements:    * Filter Saved Filters by creation date, filter values    * See Saved Filter values    * See more details in Used by (which domain/entity)    * Bulk edit or/and bulk export/import Saved Filters    * Clone Saved Filters
workaround: NA

summary: Enable Entity History for User Roles with Limited Access
description: Currently, Entity History is only accessible to users who have account\-level permission.  As a result, users without account\-level permission are not able to view any entity's history, even for entities within their access scope.    This is especially problematic for global accounts where users' access is restricted to their country's/region's entities, as those users are not able to view the history of their entities, whereas this was previously possible with the Activity Log.
workaround: There is no existing workaround that I am aware of.

summary: Undo the rejection of a suggestion
description: Many of our clients have requested whether can have a undo button when someone accidentally rejects a user suggestion for listing data or for new Knowledge Graph data.
workaround: NA

summary: CaC support for Field Permission Groups
description: Currently we are missing CaC support for Field Permission Groups. This can make syncing/cloning accounts that use these features more tedious, as we would need to manually recreate all the Field Permission Groups in the target account and configure custom fields ourselves instead of using more convenient tools like the Yext CLI.
workaround: Manually recreate the Field Permission Groups and configure the fields in the target account.

summary: Customize Entity Relationship Dropdown
description: Currently, when adding entities to an entity relationship field, the only information available in the dropdown is the name of the entity and the entity ID. However, for users with complex naming taxonomy, this can make it difficult to relate entities to each other (see image below): ![](https://a.mktgcdn.com/p/TEtWdMpH0WeP1rpNtdM1hRv52kGlLNjiaLrM4JvD0Ls/1106x740.png)    Similar to how an admin can customize what fields are available when adding a new entity, or what fields are visible in the Knowledge Graph UI, it would be helpful if an admin can also customize what fields are visible when linking entities, or make certain fields on the linked entity searchable.
workaround: N/A

summary: Change Photos in Gallery without having to Delete them and lose the text information
description: Create the ability to simply edit photos in the Photo Gallery section of the knowledge graph without losing the text data that is already added.
workaround: NA

summary: ECL managed by custom entities
description: Right now we are managing ECL one by one for each different list of services that a client has. For Big clients like la Poste, this is not manageable at scale or automatically.    It would be nice if we could manage ECL with custom entities are we are already using them to manage services appearing on the local pages. That would avoid doing this manually a second time and to scale it with ETL.    Is this something we are planning to add as this would be in the same logic as the rest of the KG
workaround: NA

summary: Add KG Notification for Majority Updates to Live, Saved Filters
description: Hi team,    I'd like to request a new notification for updates to entities that trigger 50% or more of those entities to fall out of a Saved Filter that's being used by a live experience. For this specific type of notification, it would be helpful to have some type of approval flow in the platform off of the notification itself for the customer to approve the changes. I'm thinking something like:    1. Updates to 50%+ entities are made causing entities to fall out of x filter    2. Notification is triggered    3. Customer navigates to approval page in platform to review updates and push changes live    Open to any and all ideas on how this should function, but wanted to throw that out there! Let me know if you have any questions.    Thanks,    Ash    This would ultimately eliminate the need for the workaround listed below.    Thanks,
workaround: Today, a ticket is generated to the internal Yext team to approve changes when 50%+ more of entities fall out of the filter. The internal Yext team member assigned to the ticket will liaise with the internal account team and customer to ensure the changes were intended before approving the changes. If we could implement the change above, we could push the change to the live experience once updates are approved since we'd have a notification in place via email and in\-platform that is customer\-facing.

summary: Implement Warning Message for Majority Updates to Live, Saved Filters
description: Hi team,    Today, when an update is made to a Saved Filter that is tied to a live Search or Pages experience, we get a pop\-up to confirm the changes with the following information:    1. What system the saved filter is used by    2. Before entity count + filter criteria    3. After entity count + filter criteria    I am requesting to add a warning message somewhere in the pop\-up in cases where 50% or more of the current entities would fall out of the filter. Sometimes, updates are made inaccurately and the user making the changes isn't fully aware of the implications, so adding this message would create another safety layer before the user decides to save the changes. If they do decide to take that step and save, we can be confident that the change was intentional and automatically push it live.    Let me know if I can clarify anything!    Thanks,    Ash
workaround: Today, a ticket is generated to the internal Yext team to approve changes when 50%+ more of entities fall out of the filter. The internal Yext team member assigned to the ticket will liaise with the internal account team and customer to ensure the changes were intended before approving the changes. If we could implement the change above, we could automatically push the change to the live experience since we'd have another layer of safety in place.

summary: Creating number fields with the Entities API
description: If you have a number field on your entity type and you include when creating the entity, you need to pass the field as a string and not a number. If you pass the field as number, the request will fail with a 400 error and the entity will not be created.    The end point should change to accept numeric data types.
workaround: You need to convert your number to a string before making a request to the API.

summary: Copy and Paste multiple entity IDs
description: It would be extremely helpful to be able to copy and paste multiple Entity ID's into a filtered search.    For example, if I copied multiple entity ID's from a spreadsheet or list separated by commas into the Entity ID filtered search, it would recognize that they were separate entities.
workaround: Input entity IDs one by one.

summary: Notifications for when Opening Soon date is approaching
description: My client has a number of locations opening in 2023. We add them to the platform as soon as the lease is signed. The open date often changes, so we're hoping to set up notifications to alert us when the open date is approaching.
workaround: None \- manually setting calendar reminders

summary: Reminder Notification for Scheduled Holiday Hours
description: For any previously scheduled holiday hours (maybe exclude federal holidays, or a reminder checkbox for every line item that's optional to check), an automated reminder to be sent to the user(s) a week or so prior that there is an upcoming scheduled holiday hour. To check for accuracy or make any changes. This way it can help create checks and balances in case the originally planned closure or open date was moved/changed/cancelled. Otherwise, we might forget if it's too far in the future
workaround: Add it to my personal calendar as a reminder to verify dates haven't changed.

summary: Allow Users to Filter Entities by the Last Updated Date Field
description: **Ask:** Add the option for users to filter in the Knowledge Graph by the "Last Updated" field. Users can add this field as a displayable column on the Entities screen, but cannot filter the entities shown by this field. Users should have all available data matching options when filtering (equal, before, after, etc.)    **Impact:** This makes it impossible to edit a select set of entities in the KG Bulk Edit UI. Entities must be exported, edited, and then reimported.
workaround: NA

summary: Add tooltip for Description field
description: It would be very useful if you could add tooltip for Description field indicating Google Business Profile's character limit (750) and restrictions against phone numbers URLs, and ALL CAPS. This would prevent some problems with publishers and easier for users to remember these restrictions when editing description.
workaround: N/A

summary: Add 'does not equal' as filter option
description: When filtering by fields in the KG, you can currently choose "contains", "does not contain", "equals" or "starts with" as criteria. I think "does not equal" would be really great! I was trying to filter out 10 entities by their IDs but when I tried "does not contain", it excluded entities that had very similar entity IDs.
workaround: n/a

summary: Embedded United States to actually show Country code and not Country in value
description: Hi team,    My customer is using the embedded field United States in the tracking tag for their \[count of entities\] hotels' tracking parameters. It seems though, that when exporting in values, the embedded United States translates into the Country NAME and not Code. It would be great to have the actual country CODE embedded and now the country name as it is now.    Thanks!
workaround: Current workaround is to manually replace the embedded field by the country code in the tracking tag.

summary: Move Embedded Fields button from the KG description field scrollbar
description: Move Embedded Fields button from the KG description field scrollbar
workaround: N/A

summary: Allow tooltip descriptions for standard fields as well as custom fields
description: Hi team,    Would it possible to enable adding a tooltip description to built in fields? Use case is to highlight in platform which fields are updated by the ETL, which currently we can only do for custom fields from what I can see
workaround: None currently

summary: Ability to bulk edit 200 keywords
description: Bulk editing over 50 Keywords is not currently available. When we try to bulk edit multiple locations that have exact same keywords, editing single or couple keywords is not available because over 50 keywords merge together. We can only delete all or / and add new ones.    It would be great if this merged list limit would be 200 instead of 50 keywords.
workaround: N/A

summary: Approve suggestions outside of Yext
description: Hi Team!    Currently in approval workflows, approvers get an email when content has been submitted. That being said, it would be great if they could approve directly in the email (or even a text) instead of having to log into Yext.    Best,    W
workaround: Users must log into Yext to approve.

summary: Editing and approving content in approval work flow at the same time
description: Hi Team,    In the approval workflow set up you can currently reject with comments but you can't edit and approve. Having this ability to just edit and launch the suggestion would make the process easier since there would be less back and forth. The just case for this request came from social posting but I can imagine it would be helpful in other areas as well.    Thank you,    W
workaround: Currently they would have to reject with comments and have the post go back to the content suggester to edit and resubmit for approval.

summary: Venmo as a payment option in Knowledge Graph
description: With Venmo rising as a payment option, some businesses may want to use it to support payments with their potential clients. The ask is to add Venmo as an option on the list of payment solutions on Knowledge Graph.
workaround: NA

summary: Set user permissions per entity type
description: When creating a custom role you should be able to set the entity type that a user will have access to. Currently you are only able to restrict certain fields, but if there is any overlap between two entity types having the same field a user will see both of them when they log in. This will always happen for builtin profile fields like the entity name.
workaround: NA

summary: Protected Fields & Entities
description: Now that entities are used to power directories and other pages site features (e.g. headers/footers and other configuration), we need the ability to protect certain entities and fields from deletion and/or direct editing.    The most obvious example is the entity that backs the root of a directory (it's url is index.html). If, due to a misconfiguration or data error, the directory manager deletes that entity from the account that page will start to 404 leading to a high\-profile outage for a customer.    More and more functionality in the future will be powered by entities and those entities will need better workflows for protecting their downstream usage from misconfiguration.    A potential solution would be marking certain entities / fields as "suggest only" so that any edits have to get approved prior to them going live.
workaround: NA

summary: More than one Knowledge Assistant user to get proactive texts
description: Hi Team,    Currently KA is set up so only one primary contact per entity can receive a pro\-active text. That being said we would like to explore if it is possible to have more than one uses get those proactive KA notifications similarly to how it is with email notifications.    Thank you for your help!    Wilhelmina
workaround: They non\-primary users can text into KA but its not as easy a process for users who want to be actively notified.

summary: 404 Checker for URL-type Platform Fields
description: The Yext Platform should intelligently flag \& report on URLs entered into URL based profile \& custom fields that ultimately return a 404 status code.    **Idea #1:** Add a visual UI indicator directly next to a field on an entity edit screen that indicates the URL entered in that field results in a 404 status code and suggests the user replace the field with a valid URL.    **Idea #2**: Create functionality in\-platform that allows an account administrator to see all fields \& entities across the entire Knowledge Graph that contain a URL that results in a 404 status code. This could allow a Yext user to quickly find and replace all locations of a broken link within the Knowledge Graph.    **Impact:** Malformed URLs that result in 404 codes can be extremely detrimental for SEO and user experience for critical fields like Website URL, Menu URL, Primary CTA, etc. Users should have a way to identify and fix these issues at scale.
workaround: Users must manually identify the 404ing URLs via individual inspection or submission to a 3rd party tool that either charges a fee to check the status or blocks the user from submitting more than 100 URLs at once.

summary: Create Error when Advanced Filter becomes Invalid
description: If a change is made to an account that will cause an Advanced Filter to become invalid (e.g. an Advanced Filter that uses "Subscription = Pages" and that account moves from Pages Licenses to Capacity), then an error should be automatically created.
workaround: No workaround at this time, other than catching it when someone notices.

summary: Searching by Hours in Knowledge Graph
description: Adding the ability to filter based on hours entered for the entity such as "entities opening at 10am" or "entities closing at 5pm". This can be useful for tracking your locations and from an internal standpoint this is helpful for Professional Services when testing out hour based logic such as custom open/closed logic or dayparting. Both of these are pretty common requests that we receive.
workaround: Wait for the right time of day or spoof your browser to try and get the results you want.

summary: Knowledge Nudge Via Email
description: Hi team,    Our client had a really great suggestion that we should be able to send a knowledge nudge via email vs just through text. This particular client needs a lot of field users to add in data to Yext!    Is this something we could consider?    Thank you!    W
workaround: Reaching out via email not through or setting up a text knowledge nudge.

summary: Left Panel Expansion
description: The left panel in the Knowledge Graph \> Entities will not expand horizontally. My entities are organized into several subdirectory folders and as I open these folder, names get cut off and I can't expand the view to see more.
workaround: Not that I can tell.

summary: Knowledge Graph - Enable Folders & Labels to Have Alternate Languages that Respect Users Display Language
description: Folders \& Languages can only be named in 1 language that is the default for all users regardless of the user's Display Language preference. This is a challenge for international accounts where the Folder has an English name by default but also needs a name in other languages for when field users log in that don't speak English.    **Ideal State:** Have Language Profiles for Folders that support a default language for the name and alternate language options that will display based on the user's Display Language configured in the Yext account.
workaround: NA

summary: Broken Link Checker for URL-based CTAs
description: Most Yext Pages and Search experiences are filled with CTAs, and with CTAs comes the chance to have dead links. Whether in the search front\-end or within any URL type fields in the KG, it would be great to have an ongoing broken link checker to assess pages that 404 or present another error and continue to check this over time.
workaround: Manual review or 3rd\-party tool.

summary: Peer-to-peer Suggestions/Approvals
description: Our Suggestion/Approval process is great if businesses work in a hierachy, but my client has a team of three where this doesn't really work.    They would like all three team members to be able to suggest content changes, but to **only** be able to approve each other's work. Currently this is not possible \- a user cannot have suggester and approver access, without it providing them with the ability to approve their own changes.    TLDR: a change approval flow which allows for a user to suggest changes that a teammate has to approve, but also to be able to approve the changes suggested by the teammates.
workaround: Current workaround is for each team member to have two logins (one to suggest, one to approve) but this doesn't really work for them as they use SSO

summary: Google Logo / Alternate Text - Not available in Templates
description: Hi,    When building an entity template in the KG, we often add logos to it.    When adding the Business Logo, it is possible to choose the picture and the secondary fields (description, alt text,...)    When adding Google Logo, we can ONLY choose the picture: the alternate text is missing.    I am aware this is a minor issue, but I guess it would also be a minor fix to implement.    Thanks for reading!!
workaround: Manually update the Google Logo Alternate Text after applying the template

summary: Coordinates for Maps Point (map maker)
description: Hello,    currently the coordinates cannot be adjusted by us alone. You always have to contact the support. It would be helpful if the coordinates can be adjusted independently in the Yext\-CA or by the Upload via csv.    Greetings    Sina
workaround: none

summary: Show an ECL's Internal ID in on the ECL Detail Page
description: On the ECL detail page, we'll show the Name, Title, and Internal ID of the ECL, but we don't show the "List ID" which is used in calls to associate an Entity with an ETL. This really hampers API and App debugging w.r.t. Entities and ECLs. It would be great to show all the ECL IDs on the detail page.
workaround: None.

summary: Add Phone Number as a Custom Field Type option
description: I'm creating custom fields and want to add a Support Phone number field but there is no phone number custom field type for this. I would love to have the ability to do this the same way as the built\-in phone number fields with the ability to select the country code!
workaround: Currently, I will either repurpose an existing built\-in phone number field or make a custom text field for the phone number.

summary: Apply Template Pre Entity Creation
description: It would be great to have the option to have data pre\-filled out on an entity/apply a template when I add new entities to the Knowledge Graph before it's created.
workaround: NA

summary: Add Column: Listings Live
description: On the Knowledge Graph Entities List page, can we add Listings Live as a Column option? This would allow us to see at a high level locations that are actively receiving service but have 0 listings live and need attention (i.e: no category, incorrect address, etc).
workaround: Manually checking one by one.

summary: A dropdown option to select all profile languages in Export tool
description: It would be very helpful to have a dropdown option in the Export Entities tool to select all profile languages at once.    Currently, we select every profile language manually.    Example: Fendi has at the moment 73 profile languages and when they request an Export of all profiles, we have to tick off 73 profiles one by one.
workaround: NA

summary: Save Entity Edits as Draft
description: In Entity Edit in Knowledge Graph, it would be great to have a feature where when you are editing a field. Your changes are saved as a draft before you hit Save. This way, if you accidentally close the tab, you reload the page etc. Your edits are still saved as a draft. This is especially helpful when writing out long Rich Text or Text fields and you forget to hit save and then you lose all of your edits.
workaround: Hit save multiple times when editing a text field in order to ensure that all your text writing does not get lost in case you close out the tab, reload the tab or forget to hit save.

summary: Entity template accessibility to user
description: Hi team,    One of my clients has a lot of local users that are relying on the Entity templates to create or modify Corners and Event entities (for their pages). In parallel, they would like to simplify the access of those users to limit human error, mainly on the Location entities. They want for example to remove the ability to change some fields like address for those users.    The issue here is that if a user don't have the rights to modify all the fields in the platform, they don't have the ability to use entity template anymore. Even if you only remove access to fileds that are not in the template itself, the feature completely disapear to the user.    What would be needed here is that the usage of the Entity templates is not bound to having access to all the fields but maybe at least only the field in the template. Or just to be an option to add or not in a Custom role without the constrain of needing to access all the fields.    Here is a ticket with all the details and test of tech ops about this [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS\-6706](https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS\-6706)
workaround: NA

summary: Re-Order Folders (non-alphabetically)
description: Folders are currently automatically alphabetically ordered in the KG \- it would be great to be able to re\-order them as required/wished by the customer and according to account preferences/for individual organisation. E.g., I needed to re\-name "Closed" to "X_Closed" to keep it on the bottom.
workaround: Re\-name folders: "X_Closed" instead of "Closed" or "A_" to have it on top, etc.

summary: Deletion Records or Deletion Warnings for Labels, Folders, and Advanced Filters
description: Ideally a warning that displays when clients try to delete labels, folders, or Advanced Filters if they are leveraging Pages, Search, or ETLs (Developer Console/Knowledge API). When these are deleted whether it's the client not knowing or internal teams not knowing, it can lead to pages coming down, publishing failures, ETL failures for creates or removals, or Verticals no longer existing.    I believe this feature may exist already for advanced filters that are leveraging pages but it would be great if this worked for other products as well and if these warnings could occur for labels and folders too.    In addition, the ability to see deletion records would be helpful for root cause analysis or educating teams afterwards so it doesn't continue to happen in the future.
workaround: None at the moment. Let the problem happen, alert the everyone that you think might be involved, rebuild what you need and reconfigure the product to use the rebuilt advanced filter, label, or folder. This can usually take a week or 2 to resolve whenever it happens depending on sprint capacity. Occasionally it can be a quick fix but just a warning could help in cutting down the frequency in which these errors occur.

summary: Grouping / subdividing labels
description: Hi team,    I was on a call with a client today and they would love to be able to better organise the labels into groups, or via subdividers.    For example all product labels are together.
workaround: No option and all labels are mixed into one group / in alphabetical order

summary: Make Location/Map Marker Available as a Custom Field for Any Entity Type
description: This would be impactful for Search and Pages if the field type was available on more than just Job.
workaround: NA

summary: Showing Scheduled Updates Coming Up in Platform
description: It would be helpful to keep a queue of upcoming scheduled updates in the platform. A client that I recently showed a product demo to went into the KG to make some scheduled updates. He wanted to confirm that he made the updates properly. It would be nice if we had a view to see upcoming updates on the schedule.
workaround: Looking at individual entities

summary: Notification for low confidence geocodes
description: Currently locations are geocoded manually and a pin is placed on the map with a specific confidence score.    Sometimes locations only get a low confidence score if the address data is not sufficient to be sure of the exact geo\-position of the location.    Low confidence scores cause locations not showing up on pages locators and sometimes unavailable listings.    Since there is no easy way to find out the confidence score of a pin it would be great to have the option of client facing and internal notifications for whenever a location could not be geolocated.
workaround: * Check individual locations in admin. This is usually only done when someone has noticed the location missing from a locator and can not be done in bulk.    * Using SQL to query for low confidence geocodes. This can only be done by Yext and since locations are added frequently this is not a suitable workaround for the long term

summary: Create Sub-Folders in Assets
description: Currently, the only way to categorize Assets is with Labels. I would like to have the ability to create sub\-folders in Assets similar to how we can create Sub\-Folders in Entities.
