If that is not an option, then sub\-labels would be nice to have . For example, I might label my assets by a county and then within that have sub\-labels of the cities in that county. Now that ATMs allow for Image Libraries, I think that Assets UI needs to be re\-examined!
workaround: Create a Label. Unfortunately, once you create more than 10 labels, a scroll\-bar appears making it impossilbe to see all labels in a screen.

summary: Character Limit Increase For Labels
description: Hi Team!    One of my clients requested expanding the character limit (I believe the limit is 20 character right now) for Labels\-\- they have many DMAs that are quite long and want the DMA naming conventions to match their CMS system exactly, if possible. They would like to use these DMA labels for things like: reputation management projects, A/B testing markets with Google's appointment CTA, etc. Let me know your thoughts here!
workaround: We looked at another option of pulling data by subfolders, instead of labels\-\- but this gets tricky, as the top\-level folders + subfolders would require manipulating data outside of Yext.

summary: Add field in entity, displaying which filters the entity is part of
description: Add field in entity to show which filters the entity is a part of.    When there are lots of filters in an account, sometimes it is easier to work back from the entity you are interested in for which filters they are within. As opposed to checking the filters and then working through the the desired entity.
workaround: Search through each filter for the required conditions and check if the entity meets those, OR select each filter and search for the entity name.

summary: Remove the drop down option to "Reject" a suggestion on the "Suggestions" screen when the suggested edit is not shown
description: Request from client\- When viewing all suggested changes under Knowledge Graph \> Suggestions, can we remove the "Reject" drop down option that shows up before the approver has clicked into the suggestion to review the change? Right now the approver has access to the "Reject" drop down button on the Suggestions landing page before they actually view the requested change. Once the approver selects "Review" they then can see the requested change and they have access to "Assign", "Reject", and "Approve". We would like to remove the "Reject" drop down from the Suggestions landing page and only show it once the approver has clicked "Review" and can see the change.
workaround: There is not an easy way to make this platform change for clients. In the meantime we are coaching approvers not to click "Reject" on the Suggestions landing page and to select "Review" and view the change before reject/approving.

summary: Prioritize Entity ID Matches In Knowledge Graph Search Results
description: When using the search functionality within Knowledge Graph, it would be great if exact matches on Entity ID were shown first always. Right now if I search for the Entity ID "123", the Knowledge Graph almost always returns a lot of other results first such as entities with 123 in the phone number or 123 in the address. Typically when searching within the Knowledge Graph an user is searching by Entity ID so showing that entity first would be amazing.
workaround: Add an Entity ID filter

summary: Add Multi-Hop Support for Saved Filters
description: I would love to be able to create a saved filter based on linked\-on\-linked entities (sorry, weird way of phrasing this). For example, entity A has linked Entity B on it. Entity B has linked Entity C on it. I would like to have a saved filter for entity type A based on fields from Entity type C.
workaround: NA

summary: KG Content Suggestion Approval Workflow
description: It'd be great if it were possible for Yext customers to set up multi\-level approval workflows for content suggestions in KG (and for other parts of the platform too, in due time). This was an expectation from one customer in enterprise HC and has come up in other conversations with customers and prospects since.
workaround: I think the enterprise HC customer ended up getting some very specific solution from the Yext Professional Services team; I'd have to consult the Yext account to confirm this. Otherwise, I don't think there are any workarounds for this today.

summary: Limit custom user roles from applying assets and embedding fields
description: I am making a custom user role for my client and they do not want users to be able to apply assets. They also don't want users to be able to embed fields (using the little + on the right side of a field), *especially* ones that the custom user is not allowed to view nor edit.    It's confusing to a custom user to see all these extra buttons, options, and fields, especially if they aren't visible to them in the first place.
workaround: None

summary: Saved Exports: Adding Saved Filter Name to Export Basic Info Screen
description: It would be awesome to be able to see the Saved Search Name/Advanced Filter Name associated with a scheduled/saved export, especially when you need to audit or update that filter to include/exclude new entities and weren't the person who set it up. Currently, the screen shows the count of entities that fall into the filter configured, but it would be great to see the Saved Search name, the filter criteria, or both on the Basic Info screen
workaround: Bouncing through existing saved searches to see if they match the entity count, which isn't a perfect science!

summary: Dynamic field with no spaces OR, automatically strip spaces from dynamic fields when adding them into a URL
description: Add the ability to include dynamic fields with no spaces OR, automatically strip spaces from dynamic fields when adding them into a URL.    Currently dynamic fields always take the value of the original field, as expected. This works in most cases, however when there are URL's it does not. If fo example I wanted to add a dynamic field into a URL based on the city (ie in the extended network) and the city included a space, then the URL would throw an error that it is invalid.    Therefore either add an option for dynamic fields to not include spaces OR, automatically strip spaces from dynamic fields when adding them into a URL.    Where multiple entities have been selected and attempted to have a dynamic field added to them, an error in one dynamic field spacing causes the whole edit to fail.
workaround: Download all entities as raw data, strip the spaces in excel and then re\-upload as hardcoded values. However these lose value over time as the dynamic field may be updated.

summary: Input GBP Location Group by Name in Knowledge Graph
description: When inputting the GBP location group for an entity in the knowledge graph, you must use the location group ID in a free\-text field "Google Account ID".    On the GBP Publisher Configuration screen, you can map location group IDs to the location group name. We should have the ability from within the Knowledge Graph to select a location group name from a list and it can auto\-fill the ID into the "Google Account ID" field based on the mapping. This would be a more user\-friendly interface because you can now select by name which would make it easier to ensure you are selecting the correct location group. It also would help prevent typos in the free\-text field.
workaround: Templates can be used for inputting the location group ID but the name it maps to is still only visible in the publisher configuration screen.

summary: Ability to see an Overview of which fields are being used
description: It would be from a internal and external (client) perspective to be able to have an overview of what fields are being used on the platform. At the moment the only way to check if a field is being used for Pages or not is to have the developer look into the code, and is not something that can be done from the user / TEM side.    I have seen several instances where internally there was some confusion on which field was mapped to what (due to pages clipboards not being updated) or clients deleting fields they thought were not being used.
workaround: Asking dev to look into code

summary: Select ALL in Suggestions View
description: In the Suggestion Section of the Knowledge Graph, you can only select up to 100 suggestions at a time. For companies with a lot entities, this can be time consuming when you need to reject 1000+ suggestions or more.
workaround: None

summary: Moving language profile and country selection when adding entities further up
description: Currently the option to select language profile and country is hidden at the bottom of the Add Entity UI \- sometimes the user has difficulty finding them as they're hidden by default despite being a key field (this is something account owner should have option of controlling), and when the realised it's set to the wrong language/country and try to change it, all of the pre\-entered data gets deleted resulting in frustration and more time spent re\-entering the data.    A quick workaround would be to push these key fields further up (just under name for e.g.) or grant account owners flexibility to control this, and making sure changes don't results in auto\-deletion of fields already populated (maybe a gentle pop\-up reminder for user to inspect data entered so far following a language change).
workaround: None

summary: Ability to add alternating hours
description: Currently in the knowledge graph there is no option to add alternating hours. Some of my clients are open every other Saturday, or the third Friday of the month. It would be great if we were able to put this information in the knowledge graph and have it update the main hours at the set alternate or create holiday hours for the days it is open.
workaround: There is no workaround that Yext provides. The way I end up doing this is by writing down all the dates they will be open this year, and then adding them to the holiday section of their knowledge graph. I then set a reminder to check back next year to fill it out again.

summary: Select All option within a template
description: Currently, when creating a template you have to manually select each field you want to fill out. It would be great if there was a "Select All" button at the top that would automatically select all the fields for you.
workaround: Manually selecting all the fields you want to fill out.

summary: Ability to assign a folder within a template
description: Currently there is no way to assign a folder within a template. When creating entities without this feature, I have to manually assign each one to a folder if I want them to be stored in one. When I onboard a client with 3 or more entities, I create a template and place each new entity into the client's folder. The ability to assign a folder within a template would simplify the whole process of manually assigning a folder to each entity.
workaround: Manually assign the folder to each new entity when onboarding, or bulk edit the entities after they've been onboarded and move them to the same folder.

summary: Move entities to folders by Drag & Drop
description: Hi,  I was asked by a client of mine if it is possible to move entities to folders via drag \& drop instead of clicking to the entity itself and choosing the "move to folder" option.  In general managing folders via drag \& drop would be great.  Thanks
workaround: Clicking to the "..." in the entity view and move it into the folder.

summary: Entity Preview on Add Single Entity
description: A client pointed out that it would be highly beneficial from a UX perspective, if one was able to look at an Entity Preview of an entity when selecting linked entities. This functionality would enable the user to see what the entity they are trying to link contains, before selecting it.
workaround: Separate tabs would have to be opened to check which entities have what in them.

summary: Holiday Hours | Date-to-Date Function or "Apply to All" Function like "Hours"
description: Hi Yext Team!    It would be really useful to have an "Apply to All" aspect like that in "Hours" for the "Holiday Hours" field. Or a Date\-Date range for when Holiday Hours occur for an extended period of time. It's very time consuming at times to apply the same hours to weeks in this field setting. We try to avoid changing the "Hours" field if possible so the scheduling field isn't a possibly for us in most cases.
workaround: We don't currently have a work around for setting holiday hours until a specific date. We tried scheduling however, that caused some issues with our hours field on GMB so we try to avoid changing that field if possible.

summary: Schedule Updates for Folders
description: Hey team! It would be great if we could we schedule updates to move locations to from one folder to another. Currently, the field is locked from scheduled updates.    Thanks!  Hannah
workaround: Set a reminder for yourself on the day the entities need to be moved from one folder to another and then manually update on that date.

summary: Numbering issue in Entities
description: If an entity has a numbered list, and has a break in the numbered list, for example a table or a header, we may need to amend the numbering of the list as it will automatically return to '1.' after the table or header.
workaround: Adding a space the number \- looks bad in production

summary: Display the coordinates (latitude and longitude) on the entity page
description: It would be great is to expose the coordinate fields on the interface, on the entity page, somewhere around the Address field, I guess.    I know we can get them through an export but having them directly in the interface would be more convenient for the users.
workaround: Get the coordinates from an export

summary: Ability to Navigate between Entities on Label in KG and Entities on Label in Listings
description: Users are often navigating between Entities on a Label in Listings and Entities on a Label in the Knowledge Graph (especially when launching entities or troubleshooting Not Synced Listings).    It would be really helpful if there was a way to quickly flip between Listings and the KG while keeping entities on the same label (or other Advanced Filter parameters).
workaround: Currently, users have to reapply a label in the Advanced Filter / reset the parameters of their Advanced Filters when navigating between the Knowledge Graph and the Listings tab.

summary: Add Tool Tip Section to Templates
description: Could a tool tip section be added to Templates in the configuration tab (also to be visible in the Template application flow)? For accounts with multiple brands and multiple templates per brand, it can become difficult to manage content across Templates and know intuitively which Templates should be applied to which entities (without relying on external documentation).
workaround: Writing detailed Template names and creating external documentation to assist with content management.

summary: Bulk Approve Content Updates in Knowledge Graph
description: Hey Hitchhiker Team \- currently there are various clients that have content requestors/ approvals set up for users. For example (business ID 3414063) submitted holiday hours updated on Juneteenth for 230+ locations.    This led to 230+ notifications for the content approver of the client. This is a very manual lift, especially when the content is exactly the same.    It would be great to have a way to bulk approve content updates, even if there has to be a threshold for the number of locations required to have this feature.    Thanks!
workaround: Only workaround (that Yext Support confirmed) is that client would have to reach out to support to upload content so it does not get rerouted for approvals with client, which is not ideal.

summary: Filter "Columns" In Entity List Based on Entity Type Filter
description: A robust Knowledge Graph may have 10s of Entity Types, each with 10+ Fields. That makes the "Columns" and "Add Filter" UIs very noisy. When filtering to a specific entity type (common when looking for anything, rarer to look across entity types) it would be awesome if those UIs could filter themselves to *just* the relevant fields for the Entity you are filtered to.
workaround: Scroll and guess.

summary: Two Way Relationship Type - improvements
description: Hey team, I wanted to point out some limitations when using the Entity Relationship field type when the relationship type is Two Way. This was a great addition to the Spring '22 release, however, it only works for simple use cases i.e. 'Linked Area Page' field on the Location entity type \<\-\> 'Linked Location' field on the Area Page entity type    Let's say we set up the Two Way relationship as shown in screenshot 1 to achieve the use case described above. The limitations are as follows:    1. If the 'Linked Area Page' field needs to be available on both the Location entity type and Area Page entity type (because we also need a One Way relationship to link Area Page entities to other Area Page entities) then what happens currently is when we link Area Page entity A on the 'Linked Area Page' field of Area Page entity B, then what happens on B is A shows in the 'Linked Location' field (which is incorrect).    **Potential solution:** Add "Field Settings" to appear when configuring Two Way Distinct Fields. Currently this setting only appears when selecting a Relationship Type of One Way (see screenshot 2). The "Field Settings" enables us to add validation to what entity types can be linked to a certain field, so in this example, we'd want the functionality to set the validation of 'Linked Area Page' field to only be linked to Area Page entities.    2. If the 'Linked Area Page' field should also have a Two Way Distinct Fields relationship with a field called 'Linked intent pages' then this isn't currently possible since we can only input the custom field once.  **Potential solution:** the platform should recognise that when adding a new Two Way Distinct Fields relationship then we are allowed to reuse an existing custom field as one of the field inputs.
workaround: Keep the current Entity Relationship fields the same and don't reconfigure the account to use Two Way Relationship Types.

summary: Allow customization of Add single entity interface
description: Currently the interface for adding a new entity cannot be modified or customized without affecting the order of the fields in the main entity page.    Also the most important section "additional settings" is at the bottom. If the user fills out all fields and then realizes that language/country are not correct, changing that selection will cause to eliminate all data previously added.    It would be ideal if users could customise the order of the fields and which fields can be shown only for this section.
workaround: The only workaround is to make sure to inform the client about that they should complete first "additional settings" at the bottom of the page and that the field order cannot be changed without affecting the single entity page.

summary: Have the Actions Nav. Track when scrolling down in Entities section
description: When viewing "Entities" and scrolling down, the filters will stay at the top, but to add data, or do more actions, you have to scroll up. It would be slightly easier to access these functions if they stayed at the top of the screen.
workaround: I'm scrolling up to the top of the page every time I need to take an action

summary: Apply More Than One Template at a Time
description: Currently, you cannot apply more than one template at a time. Sometimes, entity content lives in multiple templates for reasons such as one template changes often but other content in another template stays the same, etc. It would be helpful to have the ability to apply multiple templates at a time.
workaround: Apply templates one at a time.

summary: Pinned Filters in the Suggestions Tab
description: Hi Team,    Can we please mimic the 'pinned filters' option on the homepage to the 'Suggestions' sub\-tab? Users logging into the platform to approve suggestions have to adjust the filters to suggestions assigned to themselves every time they log in.    Thanks!
workaround: None \- users must adjust filters every time they log in to the platform. This client in particular cannot bookmark the specific hyperlink because of SSO limitations.

summary: Timestamp as a Field Type
description: We currently have Date as a field type and daily hours as a field type, but there is no field type for an individual timestamp.
workaround: Unless there is a better way, I think the current best strategy would be to store a timestamp value in a single\-line text field.

summary: Automatically suggest public and regional holidays in Knowledge Graph based on country and/or state fields
description: Hi all,    It would be great to have a set of public and regional holidays auto\-suggested when an entity has a country defined.    For instance, the 4th of July could be suggested for all locations in the United States and Territories. Conversely, October 3rd is a public holiday in Germany (Reunification Day), so this could be auto\-suggested as a holiday based on the country field.    To get even more detailed, we could even break this down to regional/state level. In Germany, for instance, Bavaria observes a lot of religious holidays (e.g. Pentecost, All Saints Day, etc.) while other states like Brandenburg or Berlin do not.    If a client has the state field populated, we could automatically suggest these days to speed up workflows.
workaround: Currently, all these dates must be manually entered.

summary: When editing holiday hours for multiple Entities, the dates are being grouped together
description: It seems like there was a recent change made in KG that has affected the way holiday hours are being displayed when bulk editing.    Previously, editing holiday hours for multiple entities meant you were able to see all the dates that were being edited.    It would look approximately like this:    ![Ein Bild, das Tisch enthält. Automatisch generierte Beschreibung](https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0?ui=2&ik=8dd9f4ca18&attid=0.3&permmsgid=msg\-f:1733250510798991054&th=180dbe280af02ece&view=fimg&fur=ip&sz=s0\-l75\-ft&attbid=ANGjdJ9rfxkKA2eAzXl5qg445av1SxoTcT6447H3eo2LAexihQ9A1QKP\-iT\-22a0olQc\-EmvUuO0KVxlQQjOXMjI8E0ArCA7cDcMcMPqNN3u4w7oLawwiWzoY8HMnD4&disp=emb)    Recently, it has come to our attention via our client RSG Group that these dates are now being grouped together when bulk editing. This makes it impossible to see which dates are being edited:    ![Ein Bild, das Text enthält. Automatisch generierte Beschreibung](https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0?ui=2&ik=8dd9f4ca18&attid=0.2&permmsgid=msg\-f:1733250510798991054&th=180dbe280af02ece&view=fimg&fur=ip&sz=s0\-l75\-ft&attbid=ANGjdJ86Tj5Z21QvCYUOB_KOeuJGQ8VG_8kuiQwWO24rRajenm6arSx1M0Y_i1hWxOjt5rMr4IFxdctAzLBPMI5Fu91FWvgnmzxsN7QpL9\-xUDfpYpOGqJjgmITld5A&disp=emb)
workaround: To the best of our knowledge, the only current workaround is to edit entities on an individual basis, since the holiday hours fields are still displayed individually there.    This is an untenable amount of work, especially for enterprise clients such as RSG.

summary: Clock Hours Based On Login
description: I have a client who has mentioned that they are seeing a 12 hour clock on some of their locations when adjusting business hours. They are based out of Finland and they have their language set to Finnish, but for some locations they have a 12 hour clock. The client has asked if we can adjust the clock from 12 to 24 hours depending on the primary language, rather than where the entity is located?
workaround: Current workaround is just to adjust the time manually by either simple math, or by using a calculator.

summary: Display Data Source & Destination Information Per KG Field Directly in the Platform
description: Create a new UI in the KG for entity fields (similar to tooltips) that show:    * where the data contained in that field comes from (ETL, Connector, manual change, API push, etc.)    * where the data is used (Pages, Answers, Listings, outbound API, etc.)    This field could be manually populated by users at first but then could be automatically populated by connectors, ETLs, the KG itself as the functionality becomes more sophisticated.    This would be a *massive* time saver for all Yext users as you could see in\-platform where the data is coming from and where it goes next outside of the KG. Helps with troubleshooting, enhancement opportunities, and all sorts of visibility.
workaround: Manually populated and maintained documents outside of the Knowledge Graph. These quickly become out of date and are often very hard to find.

summary: Allow Users to Suggest Scheduled Updates
description: My client noticed that users do not have the ability to *suggest* scheduled updates. While users who have the ability to "edit content with approval" can suggest edits to other fields, they are unable to suggest scheduled updates to any fields. In order to successfully schedule an update, the user would need to have edit access to the specific field. However, the client does not want these users to have total edit access.
workaround: There is no workaround for scheduling updates as this feature requires edit access. At the moment, scheduled updates are not something that can be approved.

summary: Sticky breadcrumbs in the Knowledge Graph
description: Hello,    I am piggybacking on this previous idea [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/8340538179900274488](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/8340538179900274488)    While the new sticky navigation bar on the left hand side is definitely a wonderful improvement, I wanted to enquire about the possibility of freezing/sticking the breadcrumbs pane at the top of the page.    My team and client (we are going to rollout about 1500 locations on Yext in the coming months) work with locations which have many fields in the Knowledge Graph and sometimes, when checking several locations, one may get lost and forget which locations they are looking at.    Having the breadcrumbs stick to the top of the page even when scrolling down could definitely help in being more effective, whithout having to scroll all the way up everytime.    Thanks for your consideration!    Giulia
workaround: Scrolling manually all the way up

summary: Automate alt image text generation with AI
description: Alt image text is super important for accessibility and image SEO. However, the generation of alt image text is a time consuming process and often deprioritised. With the roll out of Google's multisearch near me, and MUM in the future, images will become increasingly important, not only for image search but for simple local searches as well.    There are already openly available ML models out there to generate automatic image alt text. It would be amazing if Yext would be able to automatically generate Image alt text within the platform. We know that AI content isn't perfect yet, but with an approval process and human reviews this could support businesses and the overall accessibility of the web.    It could also give a huge advantage for Yext clients to appear for multisearch queries.
workaround: N/A

summary: Automatically Endorse Manually Uploaded Geodata
description: Hi team! If lat/long data is uploaded into the Yext platform, it would be great if that geodata is taken as source of truth and automatically endorsed. Sometimes, Yext's geocoding system will overwrite the lat/long that is uploaded, causing the lat/long data to be inconsistent from the expectation.
workaround: Have Yext manually endorse geodata that is uploaded to the platform

summary: Locale Profile Should be Able to Fallback to Language
description: Many global customers operate pages sites with dozens of locales but only a few unique languages. They tend to treat all locales the same from a content perspective but use the locales to create separate pages domains for each country in which they operate. This creates a difficult management problem in Knowledge Graph.    The simple solution would be adding the ability to fallback to the matching Language for a given locale rather than only fallback to the primary language profile of the account.    For example a if profile has en, en_DE, en_CA etc they generally will want all of those profiles to have the same content as the en profile.    This will significantly ease the management burden for global knowledge graphs.
workaround: Manual management of the locale profiles.

summary: Add ability to set default sort order for entities list view (sort by date modified)
description: I almost always need to view knowledge graph entities by date modified descending so that I can see the most\-recently modified entities. I do this to verify the ETL is running and to see which entities have been modified by us or via partners like Google via API. Currently, you have to re\-sort entities by date every time you refresh the list or view it for the first time. I would like the interface to remember which column I sort by every time I return.
workaround: Sort by date modified column each time the list is viewed.

summary: Custom Users per field instead of in groups
description: Instead of operating custom field permission groups, show custom fields within Storm the same way as non\-custom fields. Ie allow them to be view, edit, edit with approval per field instead of per field group.    Where there are accounts with multiple user roles and overlaps in their permissions to edit/view/edit with approval on custom fields, then we have to create a series of complex custom field groups and maintain these for each role.
workaround: Maintain large amounts of custom field groups and ensure that they are correct edit/view/edit with approval for each role.

summary: Being able to export available Single Option Select
description: When there is a single option select field, it would be great if it was possible to export all the options so that clients know what options are available to them, to make the bulk upload easier.
workaround: NA

summary: Option to Automatically Populate Holiday Hours When Scheduling Updates
description: When scheduling an update for Regular Business Hours, (ex: M\-F 9am\-5pm \-\-\> M\-F 9am\-6pm starting next Tuesday), we don't want the Regular Business Hours to change in the Knowledge Graph until the date when they take effect, so we would schedule Regular Business Hours to update next Tuesday. However, anything that is forward looking, like Google Maps or Pages, is technically going to show *incorrect hours* for the upcoming week (next Tuesday and beyond) until the scheduled update takes place.    **Proposed solution**: When scheduling updates to Regular Business Hours, there is an option to take the hours data in the scheduled update and copy it into 7 days of Holiday Hours, starting on the date when the scheduled update will take place. This would ensure that forward looking platforms (Google, Pages), display hours accurately before the date that Regular Business Hours change.
workaround: In addition to scheduling an update for Regular Business Hours, Holiday Hours need to be uploaded manually for the 7 days, starting on the date the scheduled update takes place.

summary: Update Field Name in Export Scheme Configuration
description: When using the Advanced Filter in the Knowledge Graph, the field for Subscriptions is called "Subscriptions." However, under Export Scheme Configuration, this same field is called "License Packs." Could the naming convention for this field be updated so that it is consistent across all tabs in the Knowledge Graph?
workaround: NA

summary: Customizable Linked Entities field to add some fields to display in entity edit screen
description: Linked entities field is only display entity type name, entity id, and entity name.    I would like to add more fields to display.    For example, if there is location and campaign entities and both are linked, so I can select specific campaign entities at specific location entity.    But it is only display entity type, id, and name in the field, It is difficult to know when those campaigns started and when they ended, etc.     If I want to sort them by campaign start date, it is not easy to determine by id and name alone.    So if it is easy to customize and add some field to display, I can add campaign start date field in the linked entities fields then I can easily sort out.
workaround: None.

summary: Multiple entities selection function at once when adding linked entities
description: When I want to add multiple linked entities to some entity, it required below steps…    1. Click +Add an Item    2. Search or select 1 entity.    3. return to #1 if I need to add more entities    I would like to have the ability to select multiple entities by Folder or Label, like the filter function at the top of the Overview screen in Pages, and I would like to be able to select multiple entities as well.        Current linked entities is really cumbersome to select multiple entities at once.
workaround: None.

summary: Switch to list mode after a field has been created
description: When creating a new field, we have the option to make it a list. However, when the field is already created, that checkbox is greyed out and we cannot switch to a list field.    It would be great to have the possibility to switch to a list field also after the field creation as sometimes you realize afterwards that it would be better setting it up this way.
workaround: At the moment you can only delete the field and create it from scratch.

summary: Embed full state name
description: Like some of the other embed requests, I propose that we add an embed for the full state/ region name.
workaround: Create a custom field with the full state/region or rreplace a target string in the backend before rendering the text.

summary: Add Ability to Edit API ID for Custom Field Value
description: When creating custom fields, we have the option to edit the field's API ID in addition to its name. However, the same does not go for values (or sub\-fields). If you were to select the field specification "Multi\-Option Select", you can input many potential options and whatever the name of each option is becomes the API ID.    For example, if you make a field for States and we list each state as an option, the API ID for "Alabama" would be "ALABAMA". The same pattern would go for the other states. However, if we want the Yext UI to display "Alabama", but we want to send the two\-letter abbreviation, "AL" in the API call, there is no easy way to configure these two items separately. Allowing the ability to separately configure API IDs of values/sub\-fields would allow greater flexibility for developers leveraging the Yext APIs.
workaround: In order to accomplish this for our Alabama example, you would have to name the field value, "AL", save the field, and then go back in and change the value to "Alabama", either in the Fields activity in the Configuration tab or in the Admin console. The API ID will not change with the edit. This process is not obvious to users and is more work than making the correct edits originally.

summary: Make KG Search bar bigger
description: Hi Team,    I had a call with a client where they mentioned that the search bar on the KG is too small and they would like it to be bigger so that it can be more prominent on the page.    Thank you!    Wilhelmina
workaround: NA

summary: Configuration and Edit History for Entity Templates
description: Hi Team \- It would be beneficial if Entity Templates had the concept of Configuration and/or Edit History. In collaborative accounts, it is possible that an user makes an unintended change to an Entity Template. Having a history of the changes made and by whom would be really helpful to see what needs to be rolled back. It would also help determine the user who made the changes in case any adjudication is needed with them.
workaround: None

summary: Group Multi-Language Profiles by Language for Bulk Editing
description: **ASK**: Have the ability to group language profiles which use the same overarching language and add functionality to be able to then edit multiple language profiles of the same language in bulk.    **CONTEXT:** For accounts which have the requirements to have several language profiles to store content for different locales, many of the language profiles actually contain the same content since the overarching language is the same.    E.g. fr\-fr, fr\-ch and fr\-ca all contain French language content. With this example, currently to make in platform updates to fields containing French language the user has to make the change within 3 language profiles which is very time consuming and increases the risk of human error.    It would therefore be great if we had the ability to:    * Group/order the list of language profiles in the entity which currently seems to be random. In particular, it would be good to group language profile by the language profile's overarching language.    * Bulk edit multiple language profiles, both for an inidivdual entity and across multiple entities. E.g. this would enable the user to update all relevant fields for in French for fr\-fr, fr\-ch and fr\-ca for all relevant entities.
workaround: Export content and update via spreadhseet to copy and paste across language profiles using the same language

summary: Service Area Business Entity Types
description: Could a new entity type be added for Service Area Businesses that would have Address Hidden = Yes by default?    In order to create a Service Area Business listing on Google, the Location entity type is used and the Address Hidden field must = Yes. An address is always required by Google in order to verify an SAB. For accounts with Direct Verification, the a valid address is required to fetch the verification options both in the Google UI and via API.    However, for Service Area Businesses where the address used in verification is a residential address, needing to manually check the Hide Address field (and making sure that this field is never changed) can be a big risk.    Creating a Service Area Business type where the only setting is Address = Hidden would ensure that sensitive address data is never accidentally published due to manual error.
workaround: Currently there is no way to make sure that the Hide Address field is never changed for a Location entity type that is a Service Area Business.

summary: Ability to add custom fields to permission group from the Custom Field Permission Group Screen
description: Currently we can only add a custom field to a permission group from the custom field view. It would be helpful to be able to add/remove custom fields from a permission group from the custom field permission group screen.
workaround: Can add the custom field permission group from the custom field screen

summary: Add the ability to provide custom tool tip information for built in fields
description: Currently we have no way to document in platform how a built in profile field is used in Pages \& Answers or any information about it's source (e.g. if it comes from a Connector or ETL)
workaround: manual documentation

summary: Copy Asset Data from Existing Entity
description: It would be awesome if we could copy existing asset data from an entity's profile. This would be similar functionality to being able to copy data from an existing entity to create a new template.
workaround: Today, if we add an asset to an existing entity, we need to go to the Assets UI and manually recreate the asset to add it to the asset library.

summary: Entity Templates - Copy Data from Multi Language Profiles
description: When creating a new entity template and selecting "copy data from an existing entity", you can only copy in the primary language profile of the entity selected. It would be great to be able to select an alternate language profile, so we could easily create templates for each language.
workaround: Manually create the alternate language profile template.

summary: Easily update entity previews from KG
description: Currently, if I want to update entity previews, I have to do it one by one per entity type by clicking into configuration \> entity type \> edit field \> preview    It would be great to be able to directly manage this from KG \> entities as I'm looking at the list just click into preview for a specific entity type that takes me directly to the preview page.
workaround: Currently, if I want to update entity previews, I have to do it one by one per entity type by clicking into configuration \> entity type \> edit field \> preview

summary: Ability to Set Default Export Format
description: When exporting from the Knowledge Graph, the default format is CSV. To export as an Excel (XLSX), you have to change the export format from CSV. It would be useful to be able to set the default export format on an account level if you typically want to export as an Excel. One reason for this is that when opening a CSV in Excel, leading zeros, which some clients have in their entity IDs, are stripped and can cause issues when uploading (e.g., changing entity IDs, incorrectly mapped fields).
workaround: The current workaround is to change the default export mode each time you export or use an export configuration from a saved export.

summary: Allow Embedded Fields from Linked Entities
description: It would be great if an user could embed fields from a linked entity, and would allow users to customize content a lot more. This would be especially useful for Pages / Answers to allow for rich tailored content.
workaround: Front End approaches which are prone to breaking

summary: Knowledge Graph Notifications
description: Two current clients have asked if it's possible to set up email notifications to go out automatically to corporate when a regional manager makes an update to the hours field in the KG. Right now, this is only possible if there is an approval workflow set up. The banks want to give regional managers the power to make the change w/o approval in situations like a robbery or inclement weather. However, they still want to be notified of those changes.
workaround: Have a regional manager set as both a suggester and approver so the change to the hours can happen asap while still utilizing the built\-in notifications in the Yext platform to alert other parties of the change.

summary: Possibility to configure "additional parameters"
description: Today, the creation form of an entity ( in our case, it's location), we can configure the creation form as we want by adding sections, field ...    However , the section " additional parameters" cannot be configured in the creation form. And it is placed at the end of the form and is collapsed.    In this section, you can choose the country, language and template of your entity which are the most relevant information.    In addition, when you change one of this parameter, the form is refreshed and all the information you put at first places are lost.    It will be nice if this section can be configured because it is the section that , in my opinion, has to be filled first
workaround: there is no actual workaround actually, except the fact that we ask our users to fill this section first. But it is strange, during formation to say that they have to go at the bottom of the form first in order to create your entity

summary: Audit History for Saved Filters
description: Saved Filters are typically used to control eligibility for products like Answer and Pages, and as such are quite sensitive to change. A modification to a Saved Filter could remove many live Pages or remove many entities from Answers search results, for example. It would be nice to expose Saved Filter modification history \- who and what change \- in order to stay on top of erroneous modifications and mistakes.
workaround: None

summary: Licenses Added' Date in Yext visible to clients
description: Hi Team,    Would it be possible to add a 'Date Licenses Added' field in Yext that is auto\-populated with the license assignment date? If multiple adds/removals of licenses, we could use the most recent license add. This information is stored in the [license history](https://www.yext.com/subscriptions/customers/3184141/licenseHistory) tab in Admin but is not visible to clients. It can also be queried for in SQL but this is a manual process.    Thanks!  Hannah
workaround: Manually find the license add date in Admin or by querying in SQL \& adding it into the KG.

summary: Better view for hours field in suggestions
description: When looking at a Suggest Edit for the Hours field, the rendering is confusing. There are three subfields, it looks like, but no labels on them and I'm not sure what the bottom two are.
workaround: Guess.

summary: Option to remove required fields from Location entity type
description: The Location entity type has many fields that may not be applicable to all businesses. In particular, there are many fields regarding additional hours (i.e. Brunch hours, Happy hours, etc.) URLs (i.e. Menu URL, Reservation URL) that often don't apply to non\-restaurants. It would be great to be able to remove them, especially considering there is already a built\-in Restaurant entity type. Fields like the Uber link and Fax numbers might not be relevant and it would be great to have the option to remove them.
workaround: Reconfigure the fields to "hide" them at the bottom of the entity, but they cannot be removed entirely.

summary: Add Pagination Controls to the Bottom of the Knowledge Graph > Suggestions Table
description: The Knowledge Graph \> Suggestions table has pagination controls at the top of the table, but none at the bottom. This means you have to scroll back up to the top if you want to go to the next page. Could we add some at the bottom like there is on the Knowledge Graph \> Entities table?
workaround: Scroll back to the top of the table and use the pagination controls.

summary: Improve Holiday Hours Filtering/Exporting
description: Ideally would love for the filtering in the dashboard to be a bit more fleshed out for holiday hours. The desired outcome would be the ability to target a specific day (12/15/22) for instance and be able to filter out any locations that have that value populated with holiday hours.    Preferably this would expand to exports to be able to target a specific day, week, month or year of holiday hours. Use cases for these would be a client attempting to target specific locations for holiday marketing campagins. Filtering in this manner would help cut down on any futher manipulation of the data.
workaround: At the moment since we export all holiday hours info at once, we can't get super granular. After all holiday hours are exported, the client manipulates the sheets to exclude certain info and filter any uncessary info.

summary: Better visual distinction in Suggestions View
description: Better visual distinction in Suggestions View
workaround: no workaround

summary: Embed Full Country Name in Fields
description: Hey team!    It would be great if we could add the full country name as an option to be embedded within other fields. In current state, the closest option is 'Country Code' The entire country name can be filtered on within the platform as well as is visible within the Address field of the Knowledge Graph in current state, so it would be great if we could extract that information and embed it within other fields.    Thanks!!
workaround: Create a custom field that contains the full country name and use that to embed the full country name within other fields, which involves manual lift and maintenance.

summary: Partner specific tooltips
description: It would be helpful to be able to add tooltips for our clients stating if there's something that should be considered when filling in the field, e.g. if a field is only relevant for certain types of business or if some country / partner specific instructions would be helpful.
workaround: Sharing instructions outside of Yext

summary: Multi-Language Profiles on Templates
description: Hi Team,    Can we explore the possibility of allowing multi\-language profiles to be added to Entity Templates? We currently have multi\-language profiles in Entity Edit but not on Entity Templates.    This creates a huge amount of overhead. It's very common to have an ETL automatically apply templates on entity creation, if we have a multi\-languages live on a site then we have to create a template per entity which can become very unmanageable \- especially when trying to make content updates to each template.    Even without an ETL, it's not possible to currently apply an Entity Template to an entity and have all language profiles created / updated at the same time. Currently, you have to go into Entity Edit, and manually apply one template per language profile.
workaround: Create a template per language instead of one template with multi\-language profiles.

summary: Ability to search for Yext IDs
description: I've had a couple of situations when I need to search for Yext IDs and I find it difficult to get the right results.    For example, I tried deleting an entity and I got this errror "This custom field may not be deleted. XX: This custom field is currently embedded in the following entity: **13164100**"    I type that ID in the search bar and get 16 results, and I cannot see this ID anywhere even if I open each entity, also do not have the option to filter by Yext ID. I have to export all those entities and make sure the Yext ID is there, but this is very time consuming, especially if I need to look for multiple IDs.    Not sure if there is an easy workaround I am not thinking about but I feel like this type of messages should either include the "Entity ID" instead of Yext ID, or we should be able to easily search for Yext IDs.
workaround: Export in excel, find the Yext ID to identify Entity ID / name.

summary: Search Feature for Templates
description: In the templates section, it would be ideal to have a search feature where you can search for a specific template or search for data within the templates. For accounts with 20+ templates, there isn't a way to search for templates with similar names to filter out the ones you want to edit/update. In addition, when content needs to be updated within a template, there isn't a way to search the templates to see if the content is populated. For example, if I need to remove certain words in a template due to legal issues, I need to click into 20+ templates and find/check each one manually.
workaround: Using the find feature (Ctr+F) to find a template, scrolling up and down the page to find the specific template you need, clicking into each template to find specific data content you want to edit/update.

summary: Detecting duplicates within Knowledge Graph
description: When manually adding entities in KG, whether by bulk upload or by single adds, it would be great to have an auto\-scan run as a final check to see if any entities in KG exist with very similar NAP. This will allow of easier and timelier detection of duplicate entries within the platform, and launching duplicate listings. Ideally, there will be some sort of warning to say "We found an entity in your account that may be a duplicate entry of the entity you are looking to add \- are you sure you want to add a new one?" or something along those lines.    For accounts that tend to have the same Name or Phone, I imagine something in the account setting that can be toggled upon implementation phase to ignore certain content overlap would minimize false positives. API and ETL entries would likely need to be omitted.
workaround: A user can export the entire platform content and check in a spreadsheet for potential duplicates manually. Alternatively, when they submit a license add request, support can do a scan using an internal tool to surface potential in\-account duplicates (along with cross\-account channel conflicts). However, this removes the ability for a user to completely self\-serve, and also results in additional back/forth with the support team on next step handling, e.g. which entity to keep, or to add both, etc.

summary: Save for Later enhancement
description: Hi Yext,    Our users would like to see a "save for later" feature added to the FA Website editor. The idea would be if they couldn't complete the work on custom content or a bio they can click "save for later" and the previous work would be available in the task when they return.    This enhancement would alleviate a level of frustration for our users.    Best,    Morgan Stanley
workaround: No existing workaround within Yext

summary: Show values of Embedded Fields in Entity Edit Screen
description: Hi \- it would be great if when you add an embedded field to another field within KG, you could somehow toggle to see the value of the field. For example, we tend to use embedded fields a lot to create URL structures (e.g. something like www.yext.com////. Then as a user if I am in KG entity edit, there is no easy way to see the values of that URL without navigating to those specific fields.    Below is my idea for how this could look too, if helpful for visualizing :).    An idea from a UI point of view could be to have a button "view values" when you click into a field to edit it when an embedded field is added to the field. ![0](data:image/png;base64,)
workaround: You have to ctrl + F for those specific values in their fields and construct the values together or export as values.

summary: Separate Permissions for Different Language Profiles
description: We have a customer who needs separate permissions for their Spanish language profiles. They have end\-users who log in to update the main language profile information, but they do not generally speak Spanish, so they have a special team to manage their Spanish content. However, they do not want the original end users to change the Spanish profiles.
workaround: The current work around is to create two entities and link them together, which presents a significant management challenge.

summary: Add a Mailing Address field or an "Address" Field Type in Custom Fields
description: It's important for my company to distinguish our mailing addresses from physical addresses. It would be great to have a Mailing Address field \- or have the ability to create a Custom Field that formats as an address.
workaround: We currently use a Custom Field with the field type set to "Multi\-Line Text" but it doesn't match the physical address formatting.

summary: Multiple Language Profiles for Text Assets
description: I think it would be useful for Assets (specifically text Assets) to be configurable for different language profiles. I'd like to be able to set a text field's value via Asset in one profile, and have that change propagate to all of that entity's language profiles in the correct language.
workaround: Unless I'm missing something (and please let me know if I am!), currently the platform only allows you to specify one language for an Asset. If a language is specified, that asset is only available for selection on that language profile. So the only workaround I see for this use case is to create a set of Assets (one for each language) for a single piece of content. Then I have to manually go to each language profile of an entity and select the proper asset for a given field.

summary: Video support in Rich Text field
description: Photo support in Rich Text fields is great, but it would be fantastic if we could also embed video from YouTube, Vimeo, Loom, etc directly from Entity Edit.
workaround: Best workaround right now is having a Video field and rendering that separately.

summary: Add On Features to Existing Services in GMB rather than replace
description: Clients have very robust services sections in GMB directly. When coming to Yext, no easy way to pull in that information into an ECL. Would love to have the option to add on to existing services in GMB rather than replace.
workaround: * leave it blank in Yext, work directly in GMB to enhance their content on the Services side. Not ideal as partner wants to work all in Yext    * [export](https://support.google.com/business/answer/3478406?hl=en) the business from Google, map it to the fields in the template given in Yext, and upload these into Yext.

summary: Autonumbering function
description: Autonumbering special functions
workaround: We are currently using multiple Rich text fields to power the Help Article body text on the client's site. They use multiple rich text fields split by 'content types' and within a numbered list they wish to use different content types and continue the numbered list within another rich text field. In their help articles, there is a table field within a numbered list, so we created 1. Rich text field with numbering on the top of the page 2.Table Section 3. Rich text field with numbering. Because we built it this way, the numbering from 1 Rich text field has to continue in 3 rich text fields, however the numbering starts from the beginning again as it is a new Rich text field. When manually entering the numbers onto the 3 Rich text field, the auto numbering gets triggered and starts from the beginning again. As a workaround, we are using data upload and markdown to accommodate this temporarily.

summary: Character count for Description field
description: As GMB accepts a description up to 750 characters, it would be helpful to have a character counter for Description field, because now there is an option to add more than 750 characters, in which case the description will not be synchronized with the GMB. The GMB listing also does not warn that the description is not synchronized because the character limit has been exceeded.
workaround: Checking the number of characters using the online character counter or word document.

summary: Visualise the Knowledge Graph into an interactive KG map
description: As we are encouraging customers to keep a more varied type of custom entities within the KG and with the Streams API that supports the connection between these entities and their fields it would be great to be able to visualise it. I.e. with a visual map where I can click into an entity type and see it's linked fields and what those fields are connected to. This way it would help highlight if there is data missing from the KG (i.e. linked product, or even just fields that are empty.)
workaround: Currently, to spot things like that, we'd use saved filters but for that, the user needs to know what they are looking for. an interactive map would highlight areas that need more data or more connections, and therefore improve the quality of the KG.

summary: Alerts to specific users when something is changed on the primary language profile
description: When using multiple language profiles in the KG, the local versions are usually translated/localized for the launch.  However, if someone makes any changes to the primary language profile, the local teams are unaware of the new/updated content and won't make the localized changes / check if necessary for their region.    =\> An alert should be sent to specific users, whenever content is changed on the primary language profile.    E.g.:    * US = Primary Language Profile    * DE = Local Profile    * FR = Local Profile    Change made to the US entity =\> Alerts to users defined by the DE\&FR profile with the changes that were made.
workaround: Manually creating a filter of all live entities in the entities view, and then sort by "last updated" [https://www.yext.com/s/919871/entities2#p0=fields\-with\-data\&p1=location.custom.919871.locale_page_status.1\&p2=\&p3=\&selectedSavedFilterId=119447](https://www.yext.com/s/919871/entities2#p0=fields\-with\-data&p1=location.custom.919871.locale_page_status.1&p2=&p3=&selectedSavedFilterId=119447)    You can then click into a particular entity, view Entity Summary on the right and see the edit history of a entity.    =\> Manual Checks \& setup required

summary: Allow image asset selection in edit UI
description: I would like to be able to replace an existing image value (via upload or asset selection) in a Knowledge Graph field directly in the edit UI. If I have a list of assets in an account, I can only populate an image field with one of them when adding a net\-new value to the field. Currently the image edit UI only allows you to crop/rotate the existing image. In order to select a new asset or upload a new image, I have to delete the current image first, then re\-open the "click to add" modal. The need to delete first seems like an unnecessary middle step. I think it would be convenient to be able to replace the image right there in the image edit UI.
workaround: I have to delete the existing image first, then re\-add in order to select a different image asset or upload a different image.

summary: Uploading 11:59pm hours in single open/close format
description: The platform currently does not accept 11:59pm hours when submitted via a single open/close format. You can upload it in a single day format (e.g. 9am\-11:59pm) but when 11:59pm is the value for an Hours \> \[Day\] \> End field, an error is returned. Adding this capability will help users upload these hours without the need to reformat which could lead to upload errors.
workaround: The current workaround is to reformat provided hours to single day format (e.g. 9am=11:59am). This method could lead to errors if not configured properly and it is not always easy for users to see that this is the way to upload the 11:59pm hours.

summary: Indicate under Field Specification when custom fields are not available for facets
description: Under KG \> Configuration \> Fields \- the Field Specification column could show whether a custom field is available in Facets, based on what the field type is.   For example: a Multi\-Line Text field will not be available for use in Facets, but a Single\-Line text field will be.   Use case scenario: When we select "Multi\-Line text" as the type for a field, facets will not come up for standard or prod\-prominent image card in the Answers experience. For the same cards, if we select "Single\-Line Text", facets will show up.   ![](https://a.mktgcdn.com/p/0luvQol5hrv9Xx8rIV_rKuGnsLcMcM6zcJCt27UrbGU/1544x356.png)
workaround: N/A

summary: Search When Applying Templates
description: Currently, when applying templates, there is no option to search for a specific template in the drop down option. Additionally, the templates are not organized alphabetically, so when going to apply a specific template, it is time consuming to identify the appropriate one for an account that has numerous templates. Sometimes these templates have very similar names, so the wrong template is applied. Could we please implement a search feature to allow users to search for a specific template to apply from the drop down? This will be time saving and reduce human error in applying the wrong template for templates with similar names.
workaround: None

summary: Rules for public holidays for easy managing of hours of operation
description: Most businesses have rules for their business hours on public holidays. For example a shoe store might be closed on every public holiday, a restaurant might serve food until noon.    As yext does has the information where a business is located in the knowledge graph, yext could use a list of every public holiday in that area. Then you could set rules for public holidays and yext would create special hours for those days automatically.
workaround: As of right now you need to create special hours for public holidays for each day. You can bulk edit those, but you need to research the dates of the public holidays for each region and year and upload them/bulk edit them/change them manually. That you have to do for every year and every location.

summary: Update the linked entity when an Asset is updated
description: When an asset is updated via the Knowledge API, it would be great if any Entity which is currently linked to that asset will be updated automatically also.
workaround: Currently, the assets operate like a "stamp" and you need to apply the new asset to the entities.

summary: Adjust Map Markers using Lat/Long
description: In the UI, users can only adjust map markers for display/pickup/routable/walking pins using their mouse. If I have a specific lat/long I want to use for these pins, it would be nice to be able to input that instead of dragging the pin around a map.
workaround: The current workaround is to add the lat/longs of map markers via file upload. However, map markers added via file are not endorsed and thus not delivered to many of our publishers unless an employee manually endorses them.

summary: Show the 'Yext ID' in the 'Core Information' section
description: We would like to request to show the 'Yext ID' in the Core Information section.    Clients often ask what the Yext ID is and where they can find it. It would be amazing to have this information in the Knowledge Graph.
workaround: Check Yext ID in the link    Export entities

summary: Blacklisted Terms Within a KG Field
description: We're looking for a way to auto\-reject or otherwise prevent end users from submitting fields for review that contain strings the client defines as always being invalid. We spotted an end user adding JS code to their bio field within an entity. Luckily, the code wasn't malicious but we don't want end users adding JS at all so we'd like to define strings like \<script\> to be automatically rejected. Other use cases of this feature could be auto\-rejecting inappropriate text or the use of a competitor's name in a given field.
workaround: Currently in this account, the end users' edits are being checked by an Approvals team, but we'd prefer to catch cases of obviously incorrect/invalid fields before they're sent to that team.

summary: Pull content in KG from Publisher Brand/Sync Settings
description: The ability to reference and pull content already stored in the publisher Brand/Sync Settings into the relevant fields in the Knowledge Graph entity edit UI.    Particularly for Facebook and Google Business Profile, we will often have multiple brand settings set up and upon Listings launches the Facebook Brand Page IDs and Google Location Group IDs would be required fields. Being able to select the relevant content, similar to how we can select in a dropdown the Linked Accounts, would improve efficiency and reduce required clicks in the platform.
workaround: Multiple clicks to apply a template OR navigating to the sync settings to manually copy content and re\-navigate back to the KG field to paste it in.

summary: Entity ID Field Validation Settings
description: For customers that have standards on Entity ID (e.g minimum 4 digits), it would be great to set up a field validation settings using the same logic as we currently have for postal codes and phone numbers. This would avoid accidental changes/updates during bulk uploads/entity creations/especially when ETLs exist.
workaround: N/A

summary: Allowing addition of multiple language profiles at point of entity creation via "Add Entity"
description: Instead of only being able to select one (primary) language profile when adding via "Add Entity", multiple language profiles should be allowed to be added at the point of creation (separated by Primary and Additional/Alternate Language Profiles). Ideally the account owner should also be able to set pre\-sets for this multi\-language profile selection as well. This will especially help with clients devolving the creation and updates of entities to local markets and stores \- where the users are infrequent users of the platform and hence might be annoyed by the current UI as it involves a lot of clicks (thereby also increasing risk of mistakes and language profile discrepencies).
workaround: None, other than manual add or bulk uploads which infrequent users aren't familiar with.

summary: Show Entity Type API Names on Entity Type List Screen
description: On the entity type list screen I'd like to see the API Name for each Entity Type.
workaround: Click into the Entity Type details

summary: A rule that can automatically reject hours or open/close User Suggestions
description: Users currently can suggest when a location's hours are or suggest if a location is open/closed. We'd like a rule in place that can automatically reject suggestions like these.
workaround: A current work around is manually rejecting any hours suggestions

summary: AI Generated Photos
description: Ability to create a consistent background for all people entities; generate new professional photo from a casual photo.
workaround: N/A

summary: Having a template with multiple languages
description: Possibility to add multiple languages to a template in order to avoid adding the languages after the boutique creation in the YEXT Back Office
workaround: NA

summary: Export Entity Templates
description: Hi all,    I also often get the question if it is possible to export entity templates. Also for recovery\-purposes it would be important to have the opportunity to export/save and possibly restore entity templates that were edited or deleted by mistake.    Thanks
workaround: Currently I don´t know a workaround. Clients are currently creating backups via screenshot which is not ideal.

summary: Reach Post Section (Listings) for specific Entity directly from Entity’s Content section
description: Hello,    it would be nice if you could reach the "Post" (Listings) section for a specific Entity directly from the entity's content section.    For example – this case occurs very often in our environment and steps 5 and 6 could be :    1. search entity A via search bar    2. open Entity A's content section    3. adding special opening hours for day X    4. adding a "catchphrase" for this special opening hours (visible on entity's page)    5. **opening post section**    6. **select Entity A (again)**    7. fill out Google Event data    8. schedule/release Google Event    Suggested shortcut:    Fill out Data in Entity A's content section and open "Post" (Listings) section for entity A right away from there. In best case with automatically Entity beeing pre\-selected for that post.
workaround: Open entity content section, fill out data, open post section, select entity, fill out post data.

summary: Scheduled content changes on certain days of the week to keep content fresh
description: We would like to update our promotional content on a bi\-weekly basis. On Tuesday for mid\-week deals then again on Friday for weekend deals. This would make a massive difference to content refreshing \& ensuring our Pages have the relevant content for the certain day.
workaround: Currently the only way to achieve this is to schedule updates indefinitely on the platform twice a week.

summary: Delimited Search Input for Entity search and filter
description: Currently, in the entity search bar and while using filters to search for multiple entities at once the user needs to add one entity id then click outside and then type the other entity id. This basically means to search for 100 entities a user must input 100 entities one by one in the search bar while having to click outside after inputting each entity.  We request the search input be changed such that when a string of values is given as input separated by a set delimiter the system automatically breaks it down into individual searchable objects. Kindly [refer to this deck](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1l9oZQU2UYFqHyfW2XsqXVL_vrBjh3SH36MhzTg3wHbE/edit#slide=id.p) for visual illustration of the ask.    Thanks!!  Ayan Mukherjee
workaround: Current Workaround :    1. Using the Yext IDs in the url and then assigning a label to the same.    2. Assign label via upload

summary: Opening hours can be set in a 2-week rhythm
description: Hello Yext,    there are industries (doctors/insurance companies) with the challenge that, for example, they are only open every 2 weeks on Saturday. Unfortunately, this cannot be set in the Yext platform.    This is only possible in a complicated way and with enormous effort via the special days. It would be helpful to be able to specify every 2 weeks when entering the opening hours.    Greetings    Sina
workaround: none

summary: Google Attributes - enable "Edit with Approval" currently only "Edit" or "View"
description: Currently, you can only "View" or "Edit" Google Attributes fields when creating a Role.    Requesting that the option to "Edit with approval" is enabled on Google Attributes    ![](https://drive.google.com/file/d/11s5yvjdd60I60\-pZL2JqdWDEWjlqPrsd/view?usp=drive_link)
workaround: No workaround

summary: Adding a panel to the "Entity Summary" on an Entity showing the number of duplicates that need to be suppressed. Similar to how the avg review rating shows.
description: See above
workaround: No current workaround other than notifications, but if an account manager does not receive notifications, this would be an easy way to see duplicates from the main entity page.

summary: Knowing which fields are updated via API
description: In the platform, it would be great to have a symbol or icon next to fields on the entity screens to indicate that they are powered by an API or ETL. This way it is clear internally as well as to clients whether updates need to be made directly in the platform or in the Client's source of truth.
workaround: Going to entity history, filtering down to the field and reviewing the author

summary: Protection for the images/files uploaded in Content
description: Today, uploaded images or files in Content are hosted on a public URL and accessible for everyone who knows the URL.    In terms of security, especially for customers/prospects who are willing to use Yext for their internal/workplace search, there is a huge problem if their confidential images or files are not protected.    Please consider a secure way to handle the uploaded images or files.
workaround: NA

summary: Ability to see source of last update to given field within the Entity UI
description: It would be amazing to have an easy way to see, for a given field in the Entity UI, from what source the last update came from (e.g. in\-platform user edit, ETL/API, publisher suggestion, scheduled update run, bulk upload).
workaround: Navigate to the entity history page, filter for the specific entity + field, and view first entry of recorded change. Alternatively reach out to Yext Support and have them look into it.

summary: Add a last updated date to the label configuration page
description: Since Yext employees and customers use labels for different purposes, support/ services often receives requests to clean up the labels in a client's account. It would be helpful to see the last date that a label had an entity added or removed on the label configuration page. This would help when performing a bulk clean\-up of labels.
workaround: Guess which labels are outdated based on the name and entity count.

summary: Include Last Updated Information on Entity Profile
description: Content freshness is an important consideration when assessing the quality of content and effectiveness of a digital experience. To make it easier to pull this information it would be helpful to include "Last Updated" information on the entity profile.    This information can be found in Entity History today but is not presented in a way/location that makes it easy to target where content updates are needed. It would also be helpful to add this as a filter so an admin can isolate stale entities/content (e.g., last updated is \>= 30 days, etc.).
workaround: Entity History or Logs API

summary: Setting a default pinned filter
description: Hi team,    I have a client who is asking to have a default pinned filter applied when they open the entity tab of the platform. This would make their process more efficient to be able to view their entities in this filtered view right away without having to actually apply any filters manually.    Best,    Wilhelmina
workaround: The current work around is applying the filters manually.

summary: Google My Business section name should be revised
description: We currently have these two field sections in the entity:  "Google My Business Fields"  "Google My Business Attributes"    Can we update these to say "Google Business Profile"?    This makes a lot of confusion to customer and partners. And also, **this gives a very poor impression to customer compared to our competitor.**
workaround: [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/5816725873617022059](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/5816725873617022059) says this section name can be revised via Entity Type settings. However, it's not make sense.

summary: Default Language Shown on Entity by User
description: Hello Team,    Currently the locale that you are shown when you click onto an entity depends on the primary profile. It would be great to have a functionality that allows the user to set the default locale that the entity should show when clicking into an entity. Then when that locale does not exist for a certain entity the primary profile should be used as a fallback
workaround: Manually selecting locale

summary: Add Address/State as Filter Option in Suggestions
description: It would be helpful for reviewers to filter pending suggestions based on their address/state. Many clients divide their reviews based on regions but the currently available filters in Suggestions do not include Address/Region/State.
workaround: We assign labels to entities and filter based on those labels

summary: Add embedded field where cursor is
description: When adding an embedded field via the "+" button, the field is added to the end of the text, not where the user's cursor is. This means that if you want to add an embedded field within an existing paragraph, you then have to cut + paste it from the end of the field into the place you'd like it. It would be convenient if the field is added where the cursor has selected in the text.
workaround: Cutting and pasting from the end of the text

summary: Add Primary and Secondary CTA as Built-in Field Types
description: All built\-in cards within the Search Theme reference a Primary (c_primaryCTA) and Secondary (c_secondaryCTA) field type for CTAs. Although these fields are widely used, they do not come pre\-configured in an account and an admin or implementor has to set them up manually.    Recommend adding these as built\-in fields that can be added to entity types as needed.
workaround: Add them manually.

summary: Ability to assign labels to a menu
description: It would be great to have the ability to assign entities to a menu using labels. Currently the only two options are by adding single entities or using a folder. It is suggested by Yext to divide your entities into folders according to region however we have three different price bands within our entities that do not align with the regional split. In order to assign entities to the menu we have had to use folders, splitting our entities into price bands rather than regionally. As entities can only be in one folder but can have multiple labels having increased functionality with labels would make them more effective.
workaround: NA

summary: Industry Specified Content Configurations
description: Capability to configure a Yext Content account specifically tailored to an industry so that *only* the fields that are relevant to the client are readily available in the UI. Currently, Content comes industry agnostic and it's evident in the number of field options any client has to fill out.
