summary: OOTB Integration with MindTouch for Knowledge Graph/Search
description: Hello!    I've had a prospect interested in an OOTB integration with [MindTouch](https://success.mindtouch.com/Integrations/API#5c50f9f05678bf5ef413bb60ce55deee) to be able to ingest knowledge articles created and stored within MindTouch into the Yext Knowledge Graph to be searchable in a support site search experience. Additional documentation on their integrations can be found [here](https://success.mindtouch.com/Integrations).    We anticipate that we could set up a custom ETL to ingest these knowledge center articles, but any updates on whether or not we have plans to build someone out\-of\-the\-box alongside this tool would be greatly appreciated.    Thanks!
workaround: Current workaround is to just scope and create a custom ETL, or to create a custom crawler/connector to pull in created knowledge center articles (assuming these pages are public\-facing)

summary: Hide or Grey out certain fields for certain users, folders, sites etc.
description: It would be useful if we could grey out or hide certain fields for specific users, sites, folders \& countries etc. I am aware in many instances we can use permission groups to achieve this, but it can be complex and time consuming. Also, In some cases, these fields should only be visible for certain entities, and in other cases, we might want users to be able to see the fields but not modify them.    For example, we are setting up a CF that is powering some descriptions for a client's city pages, we don't want all users users to see or modify these fields as there is a specific mapping put in place just for some pages. It would be great if we can easily hide that CF for the irrelevant users or make sure they can see that description is there but greyed out so they cannot modify it (as perhaps it is not up to a store manager to update it but for the regional manager).
workaround: Complex permission groups, even if the right users can see and modify relevant fields, their modifications might affect in some instances the mapping of some pages, API calls etc.

summary: Auto-Refresh Folder Structure
description: Hi Team. It would be great if we could have an option under folder management to automatically refresh the folder structure on a set cadence. Merrill (334533) creates a new folder for each agent ID. Their approval structure for advisor made edits is based upon folders, so, an unfoldered advisor will not show up in the suggestions queue until it is moved in a folder.    [https://www.yext.com/s/3343533/account/foldermgmt](https://www.yext.com/s/3343533/account/foldermgmt)
workaround: Currently, I have a calendar reminder to fresh the folder structure weekly.

summary: Spell Check functionality in text fields
description: Hey Team,    As I had a few clients ask this already, I was wondering if there is any spellcheck functionality on the roadmap for text fields on the KG?
workaround: NA

summary: Export Multi-Select Field Values
description: My client would like to update multi\-select fields in bulk across all their locations, but with varying field values. I tried to help them with a spreadsheet template, and I was hoping to populate the available field values to make sure they only provide acceptable answers for each field. For example, for the Hotel entity type, I would like the spreadsheet to contain the "Housekeeping" field column and the available field values (Housekeeping Available/Housekeeping Available Daily/Not Applicable)
workaround: The not\-so\-convenient solution at the moment is to make test entities, select each field value, and export that.

summary: Flagged or warn a user when Linked Entities are missing Multi-language Profiles
description: We power a number of pages using Linked Entities and members of the team are unaware when linked entities are missing alternative language profiles until they visit the page. It would be nice if there was some warning or flag on the alternative language profile or even an email I could subscribe to that warned about the missing alternative language profiles.
workaround: There is no work around besides adding manual notes is process documentation and educating KG admins to look for these potential issues

summary: Introduce a field with hours for one day only, not specified per day of week
description: We would like to roll\-out a field group to 400 franchisees where they can input holiday hours into a field which does not map to any publishers/pages. We can then export this field and check the values before pushing into the main holiday hours field.    To build this, we would require a custom field group with two fields. First field date and second field, hours for an individual date only. Currently we can build a custom field group containing a field for a specific date, but the hours field contains 7 days worth of hours and must be specified per day of week (Monday, Tuesday etc.).    The hours field would need to be for one day only, and not need to specify a day of the week.
workaround: In\-platform: Build a custom field type with two fields. First field date, second field free\-text. This doesn't work great for rolling out to large groups, as free\-text leaves alot of room for error.    Out\-of\-platform: send all franchisees an excel template

summary: Ingredients with allergen risk
description: Within menu items the following list of ingredients that risk allergic reaction should be possible to add against menu items when adding or importing from templates.    WHEAT, RYE, EGG, MILK, BARLEY, RYE, SOYA, MUSTARD, SESAME, CELERY, FISH    UK food regulations require all allergens to be highlighted. Some of the above ingredients that have known risk are unable to be added to the the YEXT platform.
workaround: It is currently not possible to mark that these ingredients are present in a menu item.

summary: Better visual diff for Hours field in Suggestions
description: When looking at the hours field in suggestions, it can be difficult to tell what has changed. It might be nice to highlight the days or even specific times that differ. In addition, the last subfield sometimes says \<various content\>. I'm not sure what this means of what the changes would be if I were to apply the suggestion.
workaround: Look carefully for hours, and guess on \<various content\>

summary: 提案の一括承認がしたい
description: 現在、エンティティの編集ユーザーと承認ユーザーを分けた運用を検討しています。    編集の提案が多くなることが想定されるため、一括承認ができると助かります。    現在、提案の一括拒否は可能ですが、一括承認が可能になる見通しはありますか。
workaround: NA

summary: Edit multiple suggestions at once OR create some kind of template for suggestions review
description: At first:    I did not find an option for multiple edit of suggestions. It's just possible to select multiple entities/suggestions and reject or review (and edit) them one after the other.    The Background:    Google keeps regularly giving suggestions for the same attributes (in 99 % they are identically).    The issue:    Most suggestions are very similar. For every single entity (in our case at the moment about 340) Google suggests i.e. the possibility of NFC\-payment, which does not exist in our locations. And there are some other things like "only cash", whats also wrong, because you can pay with VISA, credit card etc. but NOT ONLY cash. In our case all locations offer the same services and that is unlikely to change in the future.    The possible solutions:    a) The possibility to select multiple entities and bulk edit them at once not only open them each after another.    b) Implement some kind of template which can be edited and assigned for multiple entities at once.
workaround: Open each entity separately, review and edit the suggestions, accept the suggestions.

summary: Ability to specify table colors
description: Hey HH    I would like to be able to edit the coloring of the tables on the Rich text v2 field which I am using as part of the early release so that I can adjust the table to match with the branding.
workaround: No workaround.

summary: Allow HEIC Format for Image Fields
description: The HEIC format is standard for mobile (iphone) so allowing the image format would allow more flexibility with all Yext image formats
workaround: Convert to JPG or PNG

summary: Bulk Upload > Photos
description: When uploading photos to fields that require specific sizes, the system should flag if the photo needs an edit to the aspect ratio, during upload, or a resize due to being too small/large.
workaround: NA

summary: Allow Standard String Uploads for v2 RTFs
description: Hi Team,    It appears that v2 RTF fields can't have their data uploaded to the platform in standard fashion: they need to be formatted as JSON in the upload file. This became apparent when going through one of the [Hitchhikers modules](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/tracks/pages/pgs114\-customizing\-modules/assessment/); the challenge directs the user to upload data to the built\-in Answer field, but that option isn't present in the field mapping section of the upload configuration menu. You can select the Answer \> JSON field, but an error will be thrown if the data in your upload file isn't formatted in JSON. Not being able to bulk upload content for this new field type could hamper wider adoption.    Best,    Jon
workaround: You'd either have to edit the field for each entity in the platform UI, or just create a v1 RTF alternative

summary: More timezone options in event entity builder
description: When I create a new event entity, and let's assume that event in located in a smaller city, *and* I select the option that there is no specific location for the event, it's sometimes difficult to choose the appropriate timezone. For example, events held by our chapter leader in Banaglore; it's difficult to know which timezone option in the dropdown Yext has assigned for that region/timezone.
workaround: Look at google maps and see what other major cities around that area Yext potentially has included in their timezone dropdown menu

summary: Add the ability to translate built in fields
description: It should be possible to add translations for built in fields in the platform like paymentOptions and services.
workaround: Create a new custom field and copy over all the options from the desired built in field and allow translations. Then add the translations to the custom field as needed. This works, but is a pain when instead you could have just added translations to the already created built in field.

summary: Parking Details in the KG
description: Hi Team,    One of my clients asked where they can add parking details in the Knowledge Graph. It would be great if we had a field where they can indicate if they offer parking for a cost, if it's free, or if they don't have any parking on site.
workaround: I suggested they add it as an FAQ or in their business description.

summary: Add "Closed Date" Profile Field
description: Hi Team \- When the "Closed" field is set to Yes, it would be great if the date the field was set to Yes was displayed as part of that field and accessible via our Content APIs. This would allow solutions to build on this out of the box feature by using it for conditional logic on Pages / Search or to display the closed date on those products.
workaround: Right now work around is to create a Custom Field and have some sort of custom integration that writes the date whenever the field is set to closed.

summary: Synchronization and field assignments - differentiate main activity from secondary ones
description: ``` In synchronization and field assignments there is only one categories field. There is no possibility to differentiate main category from secondary ones. Please assess the option to have two independent fields, one for the management of the main category and another for the secondary activities. Thank you. ```
workaround: NA

summary: Being able to hide the labels / some labels from specific roles or users
description: Hi team,    We would like to have the possibility to manage who can see the labels. It could be by allowing specific users, or specific roles, and / or not allowing specific users or roles.
workaround: NA

summary: Bulk Edit on Complex List Fields - Error Warning to Flag fields being cleared
description: When bulk editing complex custom fields that are configured as a list, it can often be confusing to users what their edits are doing, especially when the entities being edited previously had varied content. We currently show the blank/\-/Check mark indicators on options in the list to indicate that content is not being applied to all entities, only applies to some, or applies to all entities in the UI, but that can be confusing for users who open the bulk edit screen, only edit the content and don't touch the application indicators, and then hit save and find that they've actually just inadvertently cleared the field out across all entities. Would it be possible to add a warning message before actually saving that edit to confirm with the user that they intend to clear out that field?
workaround: Reverting edits after the fact

summary: Bulk edit Templates
description: Hey Team,  It would be great to have a functionality where a user can edit Templates in Bulk \- as some companies have many locations in different countries meaning that there are a lot of templates. If any changes in those templates are needed one currently has to go into it manually. It would be great if we had the ability to do this in bulk (perhaps via Excel?)
workaround: Manually going into each template to make changes.

summary: Ability to remove KG fields with Config as Code
description: It would be necessary if you could use Config as Code to rename and remove certain Knowledge Graph fields.
workaround: N/A

summary: Add "Add or edit calendar lists with approval" permission for user roles
description: It is currently possible to set the permission that users with folder access level can go in an entity, go into an existing calendar and add/edit the events there via suggestion (that needs to get approved by the approver role then).    However, this suggestion/approval flow is not possible for the ++calendar itself++. Would it be possible to create the approval permission also for the calendars, not only the events?
workaround: 1) "approver" would need to create a calendar first themselves, communicate it to the "suggester group" and then they can add or edit events in this calendar that then again needs to be approved by the approver    2) second option would be only giving them access without approval flow \-\-\> because if you give them access to add/edit the calendar list itself, they automatically get permission to add/edit the events too (since calendar is the "higher" permission I believe)

summary: Label Based Knowledge Graph Alerts
description: At present we can create notifications to emails as seen: [Loom Message \- 7 September 2022](https://www.loom.com/share/9d6515f0fc4c4d3ca1246b84c1458f5c). Can we expand the options for notification triggers to include the assignment of labels to entities? This will allow Services and Support to better manage licenses and removals of closed entities.
workaround: Currently, there is no workaround

summary: Add Content Specs for Location Pages
description: * When updating pages content in the KG, photos differ in sizes/specs on the actual page ([++example++](https://locations.outback.com/texas/austin/11600\-research\-boulevard)).    * Once our team has designed a layout for a brand, and uploaded photos to the KG, we are currently able to see the file formats, max file size, and max file count    * Based on the page layout, would it be possible to dynamically or statically add the photo specs for those photos to the KG so that their team knows what size photos to upload if those photos ever change?    * Every template is different, so if we're building out pages and content matrices, why can't we add that into the KG during implementation?
workaround: Currently keeping notes in Google Doc for record keeping, but something in the platform would be more scalable and ensure nothing falls through the cracks (ie. if team members transition).

summary: Entity Update Webhook for Subset of Entities
description: In order to trigger an update for a subset of entities, one can either create a custom stream hook and add all of the relevant fields to be watched or use the generic entity update hook and add some filtering logic to the plugin itself to skip irrelevant entities. It would be useful to have a hook that can trigger for any change to a subset of entities to avoid some of this customization.
workaround: NA

summary: Convert Enhance Content List content into Custom Entity's content
description: Often times, businesses may be utilizing both Enhance Content Lists as well as Custom Entity Types that have overlapping content. A common use case is to be using Menu ECLs to push data into the Yext for Foods network, while using Menu Custom Entity Types to power Pages. There is usually much overlap between the content in the two product features in the platform. However, making any updates would require 2 separate content edits/uploads in 2 completely separate formats. When handling in bulk we will need two spreadsheets: entity uploader template format vs ECL spreadsheet template format. When handling via UI edits, we would need to update in 2 different places: the KG entity edit pages and the ECL edit page.    Being able to associate KG fields to ECL fields such that an update in 1 place in the platform is all that is needed, OR the ability to convert an ECL export file into a KG import file (or vice a versa) would make updates much easier.
workaround: In bulk: Create upload files in 2 separate formats even if the updated content is the same between the 2 products.  One\-off: make a change in KG entity edit UI and repeat the same change in ECL content edit UI.

summary: Alerts when removing or making edits to actively used custom fields with existing data
description: For clients who have been using the Yext platform for some time, sprawl of custom fields has been a big issue as it hampers adoption and accessibility. During clean ups, it would have been nice to have in the CF details if:    * We have a "currently used in X no. of entities for the following X\-Entity\-Types" overview for the specific CF, similar to what we did for the filter management view. This will enable us to make quick decisions if we can quickly update or delete a specific CF. It has happened before where we weren't aware the field was being *actively* used in a specific entity, and a change or deletion caused issue.    * A "Last entity update on" info so we know if this is a "moldy" unused field
workaround: Manual checking via export and then inspecting the exported data \- which is very time intensive. We have also started best practice of only adding CFs to specific entity types only if necessary.

summary: Stop repositioning map markers after it has been placed manually
description: After placing map markers manually, if the client has an API connexion and changes a field of an entity, the map marker is automatically recalculated and repositioned. My client would like to lock the map marker or to have a notification when the map marker is moved.
workaround: Contacting support to confront data 'lat in conf' and 'long in conf' identify data change for coordinates then endorse geodata

summary: MLP Profile Historical Export
description: Hi Team \- I'm suggesting we implement a historical export feature for secondary languages in the KG, similar to what we have for the Primary Language. Currently in the export screen, it appears this functionality exists but the export returns blank.
workaround: None

summary: Provide More Dynamic Fields for the FAQ Entity Type
description: It would be really helpful if the FAQ's could have more dynamic field options, specifically custom fields. We would like to be able to have a dynamic field for a unique phone number we store in the custom fields that is different from the main phone number.
workaround: Creating individual FAQ's for each location

summary: Have a way to link entities via transitive property
description: Ex: Entity A is linked to Entity B. You link entity B to entity c. Then entity a would be automatically connected to entity c. This would allow for easier bulk uploads of entities that preserve the existing relationship structure of entities without having to re\-do it entirely.
workaround: nothing

summary: Translation of all knowledge graph google my business fied from English to French
description: We need to Translate of all knowledge graph google my business fied from English to French before the next 170 restaurants network training in mid june.
workaround: We need to Translate of all knowledge graph google my business fied from English to French before the next 170 restaurants network training in mid june.

summary: Apply the modified item to the existing set of entities when editing campaigns
description: We have a client who has many campaigns running at the same time and each campaign is applied for a different set of entities.    When they need to modify a campaign which is applied to their 100 entities out of 120, if they click all the 120 entities and modify it without clicking on the checkbox ([https://www.screencast.com/t/PHUHEgdERr4Y](https://www.screencast.com/t/PHUHEgdERr4Y)), the campaign dissappears. If they click on the checkbox, it will be applied to all 120 entitties.    There is no way for them to apply the modification to the 100 entities which the campaign is currently applied to.    As they have many campaigns running at the same time and each is for different set of entities, it would be much easier for them to select all the entities instead of defining a group of entities which the campaign is applied to.
workaround: * Entity Export, modify the file and upload it, but each entity has different numbers of campaigns and the modification will not be easy.

summary: Create Functionality to Merge Templates
description: Some accounts have several templates and in some cases, they may have more than necessary. For example, an account may have a standard Listings template and a standard Pages template, but if every entity should be live on both Listings and Pages, there's no reason to have two templates. Having only one would save users time and prevent the case of forgetting one of the templates during an expansion.    Currently, to "merge" templates, you must pick one of the templates and manually add the field values from the other template. By creating the ability to merge templates, we would save users time and prevent any human error in the manual configuration. To make the functionality even more flexible, we could add the ability to select certain fields when merging in case the entirety of one template isn't relevant.
workaround: The two workarounds are to manually edit one of the templates or create a template by copying fields from an entity that has had both templates applied.

summary: Content Capture’s availability to more data fields
description: We have currently a limited set of fields in Knowledge Graph configurable by the Content Capture tool. We would like to use it to collect facts such as holiday hours and Google Business Profile attributes. At product release, Knowledge Graph gets expanded to hold more and more facts; nonetheless, it's challenging for the partners to collect data for these new fields. Should the Content Capture URL become generable for such new fields, it would serve as a way to promote them.
workaround: same as above. At product release, Knowledge Graph gets expanded to hold more and more facts; nonetheless, it's challenging for the partners to collect data for these new fields. Should the Content Capture URL become generable for such new fields, it would serve as a way to promote them.

summary: Folder Creation via API
description: Hey team,    It would be great if we could make PUT calls to create folders via API! In currently state, we can only make a GET call to retrieve folder information, but could we also add functionality to use PUT calls to create folders? For clients who are doing large expansions with complex folder structures, manually creating folders can be time consuming and lead to human error.    Thanks!  Hannah
workaround: N/A \- folders must be created manually

summary: Folder structure
description: On previous releases, users had the ability to drag and drop listings into different folders on the left side of the screen. That option got removed some time back and honestly, it was such a time saver to move listings around different folders in bulk. I would really love to bring that feature back as it's really helpful when you're managing over 7,000 listings and need good folder management.
workaround: Single select on property and using drop\-down menus to move them. It's too many steps and too many clicks. It just takes too long now.

summary: Keep selectioned entities when swichting to next site
description: Yext only shows max. 100 entities per page. When having more than 100 entities and a selection has to be made also for entities on the 2nd. page, this is not possible. When going to the 2nd page, the selection of entities is not active anymore.    It should be possible to keep the selection when swithing to the next page.
workaround: When I want to creata a new label I only select the available entities on the first page. Then I repeat the process for all desired entities on the next page.

summary: Shiftable Promo sections instead of saving promos on Assets. Like if i want to put on a new promo I dont have to delete and rearrange promos I can just move them around.
description: Shiftable Promo sections instead of saving promos on Assets. Like if i want to put on a new promo I dont have to delete and rearrange promos I can just move them around.
workaround: not movable need to delete and reupload

summary: Search by Country
description: At the moment it is not possible to search for entries related to a specific country in the search field in the "Knowledge Graph". So I can't just enter "Germany" there if I want to have a listing of locations from Germany.
workaround: Currently I have to create and save a filter for each individual country (over 40 in my case). Then when I search for a country, I first search for the associated filter and then activate it there.

summary: Promo management - To be able to set promos before the Live date like other social management e.g Hootsuite
description: If I have a promo to upload but that will go live in two weeks to come, must have a section that will able you set the time and date and the ad automatically goes live
workaround: I dont know

summary: Trigger a Crawler via API
description: Hi \- it would be great if a Crawler could be triggered via API. Even better, and aligned to a common customer use case, is for the trigger to allow an option for the crawl to crawl through specific page URLs specified in the trigger. The reason for this, is that it's common for a cache to be deleted on a website after a CMS has made a significant publishing update, and crawling an entire website may cause an unnecessary performance drain.
workaround: Manual run

summary: Export Crawler Run Results
description: Integrate an easy way for users to export the crawler run results (crawled + failed pages from the Pages tab) in a structured way (csv or Excel file with all the info currently shown in the UI).  This data can be used by our clients to to detect and resolve potential SEO issues like placing redirects for hyperlinks that currently lead to 404s.  Not the most important crawler use case but a solid bonus feature for the client.
workaround: Filter and then copy \& paste the data directly from the UI, individually for each results page. Takes way too much effort if there are too many rows + pages to go through.

summary: Crawler Notification When Crawler is Stuck/Failed
description: Multiple crawlers were stuck for several months without anyone noticing, and our client noticed that there were entities missing from the search experience.    We want to know if a crawler is stuck (taking longer than usual).    If we can get a notification when the next scheduled run did not run (previous run is stuck).    If we can have an option to either recieve by email or by slack, it it will be extremely helpful.    Another option is to force rerun on the next scheduled run.
workaround: Need to check manually after each scheduled run, and then cancel+rerun for stuck crawlers.

summary: Show Associated Connectors on Crawler
description: For a given crawler, I'd like to be able to view which connectors are associated with it. If there is no connector set up, there should be some CTA or hyperlink to set up to create a new connector
workaround: NA

summary: Set up alerts for Crawlers that notify users is a large number of pages failed to be crawled
description: Can we set up a report or notification for a subset of users to alert them if a crawler has a certain percentage or number of pages fail the crawl? This is particularly important for clients that user connectors to remove entities that are no longer being crawled.
workaround: User has to regularly log in to the platform and pull up each crawler to see what failed and are not alerted if a big change happens.

summary: Crawling for the internal web sites requiring authentication
description: Current web crawler function does not have login authentication capabilities, so our current web crawler cannot access pages which required login authentication.
workaround: N/A

summary: Be able to set an exact crawl start time
description: Be able to set an exact crawl to align with website content publishing cadence (i.e. the 1st of every month at 4PM EST) to prevent overloading traffic to the website
workaround: Hit run crawl on the day that the crawl should be initiated to start the general cadence

summary: Crawl JSON Sitemap to pull in more data
description: Be able to crawl the JSON sitemap to pull in data rather than HTML sitemap
workaround: None

summary: Add Version History to Connector Configuration
description: Hi Team,    It would be very helpful to have version history for Connectors, similar to what is currently available in the Search UI. This would allow for both reversion to prior Connector configurations and provide more visibility into who's updating a specific Connector.    Best,    Jon
workaround: For reverting to a previous Connector configuration, the only way I know of would be to undo the changes by memory if *you* were the one making the updates. As for gaining insight into who has updated a particular Connector, I don't currently know of a way to accomplish this.

summary: Built in "slugify" connector transform
description: When using embedded fields in a slug field, the system automatically slugifies the data for you: [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/pages/paths\-and\-slugs/?target=slugs](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/pages/paths\-and\-slugs/?target=slugs).    It would be nice if this slugification logic was available as a connector transform. Otherwise, users have to write a function to replicate that logic themselves. There are lots of "slugify" functions out there, but it would be nice to know that you're using the same logic that the field applies itself.
workaround: Write your own slugification function or use one from a common library.

summary: Connector to allow JSON format rather than Field Mapping
description: If the Data Source is a JSON file in the format that Entities are loaded from Yext' APIs, we should be able to just use the JSON format rather than mapping each Column to a Field.    Use Case:  Pull from API Connector with transformations (Custom Functions) that change multiple current field values that need to be reapplied to the same Entity.
workaround: Remap every single Field necessary.

summary: Duplicating Transforms
description: It would be great to have a functionality to duplicate transforms to avoid having to add transforms individually as this takes a lot of manual effort and requires a significant amount of time.
workaround: N/A \- only doing it manually

summary: Add descriptions on Connector transforms
description: It would be helpful to be able to leave descriptions/explanations on individual transforms in a connector so that someone who builds a connector can make things clear for other users who may need to make changes to the connector in the future.
workaround: NA

summary: Workday Connector
description: If you could add a Workday native source connector. That is our source of truth for a lot of our information that flows into Yext such as address.
workaround: NA

summary: Remove Empty Selectors
description: For Connectors with many selectors, latency starts to increase. When default selectors are added, there's often dozens of selectors added that provide a null response. Would be helpful to add an option to only add default selectors that contain data.    Alternatively after fields are mapped an option could be added to remove unused selectors.
workaround: Manually delete individually.

summary: Custom Data Source for Push Connectors
description: I would like to have a push connector, but instead of taking the request passed to it and going directly to the field selection/mapping stage, I'd like to use that data as input to a custom connector source function. If the source paginates, potentially that data could also be passed into each additional invocation.    This would add a lot of useful flexibility to connectors. For example you could make a connector that performs some generic logic, and then trigger it with data specifying the saved filter it should be run for, instead of needing to create separate connectors or updating the definition for each different set of entities you want to run on.    It would also make it easy to create pipelines that connect webhooks and connectors. For example you could have a webhook that listens for entity updates, and then passes those updates to a connector which can perform different actions depending on the input.
workaround: Create a bunch of different connectors.

summary: Connectors - View-Only Access
description: Connectors - View-Only Access
workaround: NA

summary: Filter Row by Date
description: For creating webinar or events entities, it would be super helpful to filter past events. 'Filter Row' Transform should have "is in the past" and "is in the future". Timezone should be handled.
workaround: Creating custom functions.

summary: Add Notes Section to Connector Configuration UI
description: To maintain connector, it would be super helpful to add comments/notes to each connector.
workaround: N/A

summary: Drupal Connector to accept parameters in order to filter out results
description: Current customer has items in drupal, and we have drupal connectors setup to import them.    However, we have items with the same entity ID but with different lang_codes, which causes an error in the connector.    Drupal connector needs an option to add parameters similar to API pull connectors.
workaround: Recreate connectors using the API pull connectors, but it is not realistic, as we need to recreate all the transformations in the new connectors, when all we need to do is add one parameter when pulling the json from drupal.

summary: Bulk Assign Linked Accounts in Knowledge Graph
description: Could functionality be added that would allow users to assign Linked Accounts to entities in bulk either via spreadsheet upload or data connector?    Currently, entities can be bulk edited to assigned Linked Accounts. However, this is not scalable for accounts that have hundreds of separate Linked Accounts to manage their individual GBP and Facebook listings.
workaround: NA

summary: New connector: Files in S3 bucket
description: A bucket in S3 full of files (optionally, matching a prefix) should be used as input to the knoweldge graph.    conceptually this is very similar to SFTP and used by many people (have encounted this a lot with most companies supplying data).
workaround: NA

summary: Ability to schedule updates via spreadsheet uploads
description: Hi Team,    We would like to upload a file that will then be processed at a later (scheduled) date. This would improve our resolution time for processes as menu updates , price changes etc and make sure that these info are updated in our non working hours/day that are needed for business operation.
workaround: Upload in advance (not always ideal for restaurant opening,events etc) or working outside our core hours/days

summary: Show Plugin Name and Function Name on Connector Detail Screen for a Function Source
description: Right now only "f(x) Function" is shown which isn't helpful to determine what the source actually is. Clicking into Edit and navigating that screen is not ideal.
workaround: Click into Edit and find the function source config.

summary: Add History Mode to Connectors
description: Hi Team,    I've seen somewhat related ideas on the board, but I think it would be very helpful to be able to choose between various history modes in the connectors UI. In the current state, when a connector runs it overrides every field it's mapped to, so manual changes to entities powered by connectors are never respected.    This is where common ETL functionality could be helpful. We could add different settings e.g., default mode (as with ETLs), where the last change made is the one that's respected. In this scenario, an update made in the platform on Monday wouldn't be overridden by the connector run on Tuesday, unless the source data had been updated on Tuesday as well. I think this would be a great step in improving the flexibility and general functionality of the connector framework.    Best,    Jon
workaround: Build custom ETL instead of connector, avoid mapping fields in connector that may need to be manually overridden.

summary: Connectors
description: Related to this post: [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/community/t/connectors\-with\-xml/4450](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/community/t/connectors\-with\-xml/4450)    I would like to create a connector that can pull in data from an XML feed, automatically.
workaround: The current workaround is manually downloading XML data from the data source, converting it to a .csv in Excel, and then uploading that .csv.

summary: Bullhorn
description: Bullhorn is one of the most popular ATS and CRM softwares in the staffing industry, and I am hoping we can build out a connector to be able to pull that data into Yext for review generation.
workaround: Currently we are exporting the data from Bullhorn and messaging clients using another application.

summary: Connector Transform to Convert Lat/Long Coordinates into an Address
description: For many public locations (state parks, trails, etc.) locations are managed by lat/long coordinates rather than a specific address. To easily structure this data for the pre\-built location entity template, one of the following would be helpful:    * A Transform within the Connector Framework for converting lat/long coordinates to an address    * The ability to store lat/long (for purposes of a locator) in place of the existing Address field
workaround: Custom function

summary: Folder Creation via Connectors
description: Currently, if there is not a folder match (whether it does not exist or is misnamed) when a Connector field is mapped to a Folder, the connector will fail. To apply more flexibility to the admin, particularly when the folder name is defined from the source, it would be helpful to enable:    * Allow a connector run to create folders OR initiate a folder creation prompt if a user attempts this action    * If the folder specified in the connector is similar to an existing folder (due to a typo or misaligned data), provide the user a "did you mean" prompt to select the appropriate folder in the KG
workaround: Setup folders prior to running connector.

summary: User scope options for individual connectors
description: Similar to this [idea](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/3920144025504131079?timestamp=1706715961995)for search, it would be great to have the option to restrict user access to a set of connectors. This is critical for global accounts with multiple regions accessing the same platform and the need to update their content only.
workaround: At the moment, we can only give access to all connectors or nothing.

summary: Map Connector Column Data to Multiple Fields
description: Fields returned from a Connector often need to populate multiple fields. For example, the image URL used for a primary photo might also need uploaded for an entity's photo gallery field.    Currently, the data from a column in a Connector can only be mapped to a single entity field, requiring an admin to duplicate selectors that need mapped to multiple fields. It would be helpful to add the option to select multiple fields that a particular column's data should populate.
workaround: Duplicate the selector

summary: Standard Plugin Runtime Environment Variables
description: I'd like to implement plugins that work in both sandbox and production without configuration from users. To make that easier it would be nice if some standard global environment variables were provided that gave information such as which environment (prod vs. sandbox) the function was running in.    This would be similar to AWS's default lambda environment variables: [https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration\-envvars.html#configuration\-envvars\-runtime](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration\-envvars.html#configuration\-envvars\-runtime).
workaround: Make the user pass in the environment as a configuration option for the plugin when they set it up.

summary: Umbraco Connector
description: Hi Team, I have data stored in Umbraco CMS. It would be great if we build a Search Connector into them so that we can quickly and easily power search experiences on site.
workaround: NA

summary: Create and update entities with field level errors
description: When a connector encounters a field level error during a run, the entity fails and is never created. Instead, I suggest these entities are created by omitting any data at the field level that has errors (as long as it's not a required field). This would make it easier to get important entities/content into the KG and help reduce the number of failed entities per run.
workaround: There isn't a great workaround. I usually unmap fields that have errors and rerun the connector so the failed entities are created. Then remap the field and rerun the connector.

summary: Pausing Connector Run
description: It would be helpful to be able to pause and later resume connector runs. This would be particularly helpful for connectors that hog API resources for an extended period of time.
workaround: Currently, you would need to cancel the connector run and re\-initiate it from the beginning.

summary: Allow Functions as a Source and Function Transforms to be applied from a Github URL
description: In the Data Connectors framework it would be great if we could import a function directly with a Github URL, making it faster and easier to add a function as a source or as a transform.    The thought is when a user selects a function as a source, instead of only picking from a dropdown of operations, the user can apply it directly from their Github, skipping the steps to apply the plug in files separately.
workaround: Currently, the plug in files are uploaded to the account via the CLI before selecting them as a source or transform.

summary: Force Run Button for Push Connectors
description: I've noticed that users will often want to trigger a run of a Push Connector before the next scheduled run, especially when working out issues on the third\-party side. As far as I know, there isn't a way to communicate the user's intent to force a run to the developer from inside the platform.    Would it be possible to expose a "Force Run" button in the Connector UI that sends a webhook to the developer indicating that the user wanted to force a run when clicked? Perhaps the developer could choose whether to expose the button or not (in CaC) so that the button won't be visible if the developer doesn't support it.
workaround: Users can reinstall the app that manages the connector to trigger a sync, but users don't always know to do that, and it's tedious.

summary: Allow function transforms to specify their options
description: Currently we have several built in transforms that specify their specific options. We should leverage our existing schemas from other areas of the system to allow function transforms to specify their options, allowing them to be easily re\-used across projects.
workaround: build custom code in the platform

summary: crawler to show all attempted pages
description: Crawler "skipped" pages when the pages returned a no results page, but in fact the server was returning no results due to the frequent access from the crawler.    If the crawler has the "skipped" pages shown as error or failed, I can recrawl the failed pages, but since they are "skipped", they are not shown in the UI.    [https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS\-7696](https://yexttest.atlassian.net/browse/TECHOPS\-7696)
workaround: no workaround currently, as the crawler stated above has a rate limit of 1 and a page depth of 1, while looking at specific pages

summary: Crawler Error Information in UI
description: Instead of just listing "0 pages successfully crawled", list out the exact error of *why* we couldn't complete the crawl. Would be very helpful for those customers trying to troubleshoot.
workaround: Maybe send an alert to the account users with the error?

summary: Crawler - Update UI to Condense Links
description: Crawler - Update UI to Condense Links
workaround: Scroll or Ctrl + F

summary: Connector Dry Run to allow Entity Search
description: Current Dry Run mode has all the Entities listed, but it is impossible to go through all the Entities, when there are more than 1000 Entities.    Need to debug why certain Entities didn't result in the expected outcome.
workaround: none

summary: Add URL to connector logs
description: In case of failed entities in the connector, the logs currently display only the EntityID/PageId, making it challenging to filter in connectors. It would be great to include the corresponding URL alongside the EntityID for easier debugging by selecting only those urls in the connector. While pagination is helpful, dealing with around \>500 crawled URLs and pinpointing the error at, say, the 750th, can be a cumbersome and frustrating task to extract the URL.
workaround: NA

summary: Transformer for Category Name to Yext Category ID
description: We currently have a transform for converting publisher category IDs into the category IDs utilized by Yext, however, for organizations pulling directly from a source system where only the category name is stored it would be helpful to have a native transform for taking a category name (e.g., Acupuncture) to the associated Yext category ID (e.g., 1235).
workaround: Custom function or multiple find \& replace transforms

summary: Distinguish between connector runs that completed with errors and runs that potentially failed
description: When using a function source for a connector sometimes a function invocation fails for some reason (plugin times out, hits memory limit, etc.).    When this happens the connector run table says "Completed with Errors". That's the same message you get if all of the plugin invocations ran but there was a data issue that caused some updates not to go through.    It would be nice to be able to tell these situations apart.
workaround: If you know how many entities you're expecting the connector to touch you can guess what happened based on the numbers.

summary: Modify 'and/or' options on Connectors' 'filter rows' transform
description: When using the 'filter rows' transform in a connector, users are required to include a combinator (and/or). I don't think it makes sense to have it there if you're only filtering based on 1 condition. Every time I have walked clients through this, they ask what they should select. I feel like the combinator should only need to be added when utilizing an additional rule.
workaround: Default to 'and' when including just 1 condition

summary: Reorder Connector Preview Columns
description: **ASK:** Add ability reorganise the columns in the connector preview table    **CONTEXT:** When adding transforms, it is sometimes helpful to compare 2 columns side by side, particularly if the transform has resulted in a new column being created. While the March Release included the ability to Ignore Columns via a transform, there are many instances where all columns need to remain visible and therefore, being able to reorder the columns would be much more powerful in these scenarios.
workaround: Remove and readd columns (though this is very tedious is transforms have already been added)

summary: Add helper text into the UI in the Data Connector screens
description: Add helper text (information icon button) to connector UI to give examples and guidance on what CSS/XPath selectors should look like and/or add a validation message if a selector logic is correct or invalid.
workaround: Guess and check by saving the selector path and seeing if any content returns in the entity preview.

summary: Details on Number of Records Included in Connector Setup
description: When setting up a connector, it's difficult to know how many entities/records will be pulled in prior to running the connector, which can be helpful in ensuring the connector is being setup correctly prior to running it. For crawler based connectors, there's currently the "Preview URL" field, but this number does not align with the actual number of entities that will be created (due to filtering in the first step and any additional transforms). It would be helpful to show either the number of crawled pages in scope for the connector or the results count from an API alongside the other connector details included in the top right.
workaround: Run connector to see number of entities included.

summary: Data Connectors Diff Screen
description: For scenarios where you already have data that's been manually added to the Knowledge Graph but now want to leverage a connector to automate future updates, deletion, additions, etc. to that data, it would be great if there was a diff screen to show you what your existing data looks like and what the data that comes in from the connector run looks like. There is a similar screen today when bulk uploading data by file.
workaround: Test in playground account, export and add into the file with existing data, see how the data differs

summary: Connector Preview to refresh without previous cache
description: Hi HH Team    Currently, when initially previewing a Connector view of data, you can view all results which meet the initial parameters, i.e. a list of URLs as part of a Crawled data set. This is great.    Then, let's say, you know that some of the data you want to narrow down may currently be on Page 5 of that preview. All good, as some Transformations will soon be applied to filter down the data set...    What we have noticed is that, once the transformations have been applied, the desired filtered data set may still render in the preview, but remained cached on say Page 5. Meaning the initial 4 pages of the refined filtered data set may actually be blank.    This could be confusing in terms of a Yext user thinking the transformations applied have not worked, but actually they have \- it's just the preview isn't auto\-refreshing to push all relevant remaining values to the top of the preview window set.
workaround: Either manually skip through preview tabs or run a Dry Run and view results prior dry run analysis

summary: Add a connector function to delete/remove base urls when a url slug is not merged
description: Can we add a function to connectors for cases where we are manually adding in a base url and then pulling in the url slug and merging the fields together, to remove the resulting merged fields that only have the base url.    Sample use case: Using the Drupal connectors we pull in the url slugs, but not the base url. So we manually add a base url field in and then merge this with the url slugs. In the resulting merged field, we do not want to display just the base url. If no url slug is provided we would want an empty url in the combined url field.    Would this be possible to develop/add to the connector? We have had this come up multiple times and have needed product/dev resources to add a plug\-in for this function.
workaround: Product or the dev team can help add a plug\-in for this function.

summary: Automating launch for new entities coming through a connector
description: As a connector (eg. Olo Restaurants Connector) brings a new entity to the Yext platform, the process should imitate the behavior of an ETL or API where in the launch is automatically requested for any new entities that are flowing into in the platform.
workaround: 1. Manually check if there are new entities in the connector history.    2. Request launch manually if there are any new entities.

summary: Allow for different country/address settings in connector
description: I am trying to pull in address data for location entities using a connector. However, I am working with US, Puerto Rico and Canada locations, which all have different field names/requirements. (i.e. 'state' is required for US entities, 'province' is required for CA, and nothing is accepted for PR). It would be great to have one connector for all 3 countries.
workaround: One connector per country

summary: Pagination Test Mode
description: It would be nice if you could test your pagination setup independently before fully running a connector. It's impossible to test pagination without running a full connector.
workaround: Run the full connector

summary: Connector Comprehensive Update to be able to look at Entity Folders
description: When there are multiple URL sources with different formats, but are all set to the same entity, we cannot use comprehensive mode to update the entities, as the entities not included in the connector will be deleted.    If the connector looks at the folder its in, or any other flag when running in comprehensive mode, creating multiple Entity Types with the same structure can be avoided.
workaround: Use different entity types (with the exact same field types) for each connector/source.

summary: Entity Assignment Transform in Connector Configuration
description: Allow metadata elements to determine the entity type during the "Map Fields" process of the Connector Configuration.    Different types of documents are often stored in a single location without the clear delineation needed to identify the appropriate entity and ultimately setup multiple entity\-specific connectors. Instead of selecting the entity type upfront, allow the metadata to inform the entity type during the field mapping process.    For example, a user would build a single connector to a location housing multiple types of documents (e.g., notes, contracts, reports). The metadata "document type" for each of those documents designates which type of document it is, and would be used determine whether the document is loaded in as a Notes, Report, or Contract entity upon mapping the fields and running the connector.
workaround: Build an individual connector by document type.

summary: Shopify Connector
description: Hi Team,    It would be amazing to pull in Collection data via the Shopify connector \- when customers go to add to cart in a search experience, we would like to pass Collection info via the Post Message such that we can capture analytics within our Google Analytics experience. Since we track our performance across our site using these collections, having this within the connector would allow us to compare search performance to the rest of our site, apples to apples.
workaround: N/A

summary: Allow passing .csv files through Knowledge API
description: To allow clients to send .csv or alternate file formats through the Knowledge API especially for Menu ECL updates. Clients within the Restaurant/Food Vertical tend to have menu data in spreadsheet format and will not always have the resources to change the data to JSON format for the Knowledge API integration.
workaround: Transforming .csv data into JSON format for Knowledge API

summary: Populate Suggestions via Connector
description: Currently Connectors can only be used to create or edit the actual/production content within an entity's profile. For organizations using Suggestions to review data prior to it going live (publishing via Listings, adding to a Page or Search experience, etc.), there is no way to pull in data via Connectors as suggestions that follow the set approval workflow. Similar to Comprehensive vs. Default mode, would be helpful to have a "suggestions" run option.
workaround: N/A

summary: Option to Load "First Party Reviews" via Connector
description: As of now we have only option to Load entities via connector, there is no option to Load the First Party Reviews Directly Via Connectors.
workaround: Right now we have only option to Load First Party Reviews via API's using custom scripts as follows:    1. [Management APIs \-\> Reviews \-\> Review Management \-\> Reviews: Create](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/reviews/reviewmanagement/#operation/createReview) and    2. [Content Delivery APIS \-\> Review Submission \-\> Review: Create](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/contentdeliveryapis/reviewsubmission/reviewsubmission#operation/createReview)

summary: Generic CaC Connector
description: I would like to be able to use connectors to power all of the configuration of an account, not just entities. It would be nice if my connector data source could return generic CaC objects. For example, some things I'd like to be able to create/customize automatically based on a client data source include:    * Custom fields    * Custom entity types    * Connector configurations    * Search configurations
workaround: Write and run code on my own system using the CaC API. Or use the CaC api from within a connector and don't return any entities.

summary: Create Users at Scale via Connectors
description: Hi Team \- It would be great if there was a way to use connectors to automate user creation. Some use cases would be to read entities to pull user information and create them, or pick up information from an SFTP/API and create them based on the information there.
workaround: Clients must set up their own API integration, or rely on an Expert Services built ETL that they can't access themselves.

summary: Asset connector
description: I need a simple way to add Assets into Yext.    If someone is able to provide a list, an API or something else, with a list of (Text / Image URLs) Assets, a nice option would be to set up a Connector to include the data into the Yext KG Asset list.
workaround: 1. Manually add Assets    2. API    3. Build custom ETL

summary: Ability to Create Custom Fields Within the Connector Flow
description: During the process of creating a new connector, I'll often by midway through the process before I realize that I need to create a new custom field to map a particular piece of data to.    It would be amazing to be able to **create a new custom field directly within the connector flow** instead of having to navigate away from the connector and into the KG Configuration.
workaround: Two workarounds:    1. Examine the API request ahead of time and ensure you have all possible custom fields created before starting to build your connector.    2. Save and exit the in\-progress connector, create the custom field, and navigate back to the connector to finish the setup.

summary: Option to exclude Connector Results Zip File attachment from Email Notification
description: The recently introduced notifications for data connector execution is a useful feature, but the mandatory inclusion of the execution details results in a zip file attachment are trapped by our corporate email filters which intercept all notifications.    Could you introduce a toggle option to include/exclude the attachments with the email notifications? This would not only enable it so that we can receive the body of the email summarizing the execution results and only therefore need to log into the portal to review if summaries are of concern, but it would also reduce the growth of Inbox data files.
workaround: I have found no way to exclude the attachments, we cause all emails to be blocked.

summary: Connectors
description: Hi! It would great to build out connectors specifically made to add/remove users. Many businesses (especially franchisee models) constantly add new users while removing old users. They often have a user management system where an ETL could or is built off of. It would be great to convert these users ETLs into connectors directly in the knowledge graph.    Ideally, the connector could:    Add new users (both SSO and non\-SSO)    Delete existing users    Add permissions to existing users    Remove permissions to existing users
workaround: Manual add in the platform, bulk upload in the platform, Users ETL

summary: クローラーの任意の日時でのスケジュール実行
description: クローラーは現在、作成時に実行され、その後は手動実行/日次/週次での実行となりますが、    クライアントによっては、任意の日時に実行したく。    下記の対応をご検討いただけますでしょうか。    ・クローラー作成時には実行しない。    ・任意の日時を指定してスケジュール実行できるようにする。    よろしくお願いいたします。
workaround: 手動実行

summary: KnowledgeGraph - Crawler - Transforms - LD+JSON Parser or Regex Criteria
description: Make working with structured data a little easier with the Crawler. Many sites have already taken the time to implement [LD+JSON](https://json\-ld.org/) script elements in their code. I can pull the contents of the LD+JSON element in with a XPath selector without issue, but I can't target the individual key value pairs inside.    Since this data is already structured in a standard format, I'd like to see one of two things:    1. A dedicated LD+JSON Parser     * This would allow one to target the LD+JSON script element and pull in the contents and interpret it as JSON with a transform. Then, maybe with another transform, I could indicate a target key or JMESPath expression and would have my target data returned.    2. Provide a regular expression criteria to extract an identified group match.     * The workaround I describe in my post linked below works, but it's a multi\-step process that requires one to first extract all of the data after a given key and then transform again to truncate everything after the target value.     * If instead there was a "regular expression extract" operation option as part of the extract configuration, I could extract using a regular expression with a group like the following.    Example [Regular Expression](https://regex101.com/r/yTjwYi/1) given the "mpn" key\-\-make sure to reference the group 1 result.    I can't seem to upload a screenshot here, so I'll link to my [transform example here](https://www.yext.com/s/2012431/addData/connectors/2878/configure?stage=transforms&transformIndex=10).
workaround: See [my post](https://hitchhikers.yext.com/community/t/knowledgegraph\-crawler\-connector\-extract\-from\-ld\-json\-element/4195) in the community with more details about the need and current workaround.

summary: Export list of crawled pages and/or connector output
description: Be able to export list of crawled pages to be able to review URL structure before having to feed into connector/entity. Be able to export the full results of the connector output to see what information is returning for all fields rather than having to send it into the Knowledge Graph before being able to export the information.
workaround: N/A

summary: When retrieving HTML contents from page in Crawler, I want the feature that allow me to switch from relative path to absolute path for the URL strings (href and src).
description: When retrieving HTML contents from page in Crawler, I want the feature that allow me to switch from relative path to absolute path for the URL strings (href and src).
workaround: After connector done, I download data from knowledge graph, and I change them manually in excel.

summary: Avoid Retrieving Specific Tags During Crawling.
description: My client is using a recommendation tool called Rtoaster.    This tool is randomly placed within the Body tag of the page. However, all instances of Rtoaster have class names starting with "rto\-," which allows them to be identified.    The crawler is currently retrieving everything under specific conditions, but my client has requested that the tags related to Rtoaster be excluded.
workaround: No workaround there.

summary: Navigate to Selector related to Transform
description: Small UX recommendation \- currently when you select an existing transform the user has to scroll to find any columns impacted by the transform. It would be helpful if when a transform is actively being edited, that the column/selector view on the right navigated to the impacted column(s).
workaround: Scroll or CTRL+F

summary: Connector to be able to use Yext ID as unique key
description: We need to update Entity IDs automatically created by the Directory Manager to use recognizable IDs.    Currently, if we run the Directory Manager for Japanese Addresses, the slugs and the entity IDs are created by replacing the Kanji (Chinese Characters) with their Chinese Pinyin Phonetics.    This is obviously different from how we read it in Japan, and we need to update both the slugs and the Entity IDs.    We can update the slugs via connectors, but we cannot update Entity IDs.
workaround: Export all Entities and Import them again after changing the Entity IDs

summary: Chat to Understand Search Backend Synonyms
description: When Search returns results, Chat doesn't understand Synonyms that is set in the Search Backend.    Any settings that we set in Search, is not passed on to Chat, so when Chat is trying to understand the Search Results, sometimes the Results are handled as "not having an answer" because the Synonyms are not handled by Chat.
workaround: Write down every single synonym in the instructions (not feasible because we have hundreds of synonyms)

summary: API Validator within Chat REST API Instruction
description: Most Chat instances leverage a series of APIs for returning results or fetching information. To make the configuring and testing process easier, it would be helpful to add a tool within a REST API instruction that validates whether a response is being returned from the API configured.    Similar functionality exists within the Connector framework for Pull Connectors.
workaround: Test in external tool (eg Postman) or see failure in Response Logs

summary: Clearer Indicators for Chat Response Statuses
description: In the current Chat response logs, it seems like every message is indicated with "Success" as the Reply Status long as a goal is predicted and none of the instructions fail. Many of these "successful" attempts are responses with no results. It would be helpful to include additional Reply Statuses to account for things that could represent a failure such as a no result.
workaround: N/A

summary: Ability to Configure Clickable Options for Select Instructions
description: For Select Instructions, when an end\-user is presented with options they have to indicate their selection by typing back the option they selected. If they misspell or re\-format an option, it can cause Chat to not pick up their selection.    To provide a UX more akin to other chat solution, recommend adding the ability to configure dynamic selection options that allow for users to click options rather than restating options.    This is particularly important for use cases that involve selecting scheduling options.
workaround: User responds with a text response to select an option

summary: More Clearly Surface Collected Fields within Response Logs
description: Currently, to see the responses provided by the user's (either via a Collect or Select step) an admin has to go through the detailed response logs and isolate the responses in the prompts. It would be helpful to more clearly surface all variables populated during a Chat exchange such as a table with all Field IDs and values, and which instruction populated the value.
workaround: Go through the prompts/responses in the response logs.

summary: Add Hitchhiker's Yext Chat to the Yext Platform
description: Hi Team \- It would be excellent if we embedded the Hitchhiker's Chat within the platform. I believe this would tremendously help customers self\-serve and find the information they need very quickly, while also showing off the strengths of Yext Chat. A couple features that would be helpful:    * Link users directly to places in the Platform that accomplish what they are looking for (e..g, if I search for "how do I add a Custom Field?", a link to the place to do it would be great)    * Allows an users to ask about status of their Services / Support tickets and provides information from previous responses    * Ability to toggle this off across the account for businesses who might not want this    * Understand whether an user has a Yext Services package to know when to hand off certain questions to Services vs. Support    Please let me know if you have any questions!
workaround: User must know that there is a Chat experience on Hitchhiker's and move away from the platform there

summary: Leveraging AI Chat for Pharma Industry: Virtual Pharma Rep Enhancement
description: Idea Summary:    Our AI assisted chat feature has proven to be a powerful tool for engaging with customers and providing them with prompt and accurate responses. I propose extending its application to the pharmaceutical industry by creating a virtual pharma representative. This virtual representative would be capable of interacting with healthcare professionals, such as doctors and pharmacists, in real\-time, answering their questions about medications, drug interactions, dosages, and more.    Benefits:    Accessible Expertise: The medical community frequently requires up\-to\-date, reliable information about medications. By harnessing our comprehensive Content tool, the virtual pharma representative can provide dependable responses, thus contributing to enhanced patient care.    24/7 Availability: In light of the changing dynamics brought about by the COVID\-19 pandemic, the availability of traditional pharmaceutical representatives for in\-person interactions has been curtailed. Our AI\-powered solution addresses this challenge by being accessible around the clock. This ensures healthcare professionals have immediate access to vital information, irrespective of their demanding schedules.    Adapting to a New Normal: The COVID\-19 pandemic has profoundly transformed the way industries operate, including pharmaceuticals. Face\-to\-face interactions have been limited, making our virtual pharma rep an ideal solution for remote and contactless engagement. This aligns seamlessly with the new normal of remote communication and underscores our adaptability as a company.    Consistency: The AI chat feature guarantees consistent messaging, reducing the risk of human error in conveying crucial drug\-related information. This consistency can be particularly crucial when dealing with matters as sensitive as patient health.    Scalability: Our technology's ability to manage multiple conversations concurrently positions the virtual pharma rep to cater to a multitude of healthcare professionals at once. This efficiency in engagement bolsters coverage and impact.    Data\-Driven Insights: The interactions between the virtual pharma representative and healthcare professionals could yield valuable insights into emerging trends, concerns, and knowledge gaps. These insights can be utilized to refine marketing and educational strategies.    Conclusion:    By introducing a virtual pharma representative fortified by our AI technology, we position Yext as an industry pioneer, facilitating the seamless dissemination of accurate and vital drug\-related information. I believe this enhancement aligns organically with our commitment to innovation and delivering tangible solutions that empower our clients and partners and can further our footprint in the Life Sciences vertical.    Thank you for considering this suggestion. I am enthusiastic about contributing to the realization of this idea and eagerly anticipate any discussions or feedback that could drive this proposal forward.

summary: Analytics - 0% Change from Previous Period
description: Hi team!    If there is a true 0% change from the previous period, the dashboard will show the change from the previous period as a "n/a", but if there is a change that's less than 1 but greater than 0 from the previous period, the change is listed is 0% and that is confusing to users. summary: Pre-built location listings overview with GMB and FB only

Therefore it would be better to be able to isolate only FB and Google within this table. Note that as this table is pre\-built, it cannot be built in the exact same format through report builder.    It would be ideal to have all of the other FB and Google information in this dashboard available in one small table, without needing to individually recreate in order to allow monthly or monthly when ready exports to work.

summary: Analytics - Report with a fix time range in a Dashboard
description: In the "Report Builder" you can actually choose a time range for your report. This is good an helps to find the best attributes et cetera for your individual report.    Now you add your new report to your dashboard. Then the previously chosen time range is obsolete, because the user of the dashboard can choose his own time range. In most cases it works fine and will display the correct values.    **Here is the special case / the idea / a wish:**    We create dashboards with several reports for other users to present current data (individual time range) and for example also total numbers, like "Total Answers Searches in 2021" (fix time range, a year). Therefore it would be great, if you could choose a "fixed" time range in the Report Builder, which is always fix, independet of the chosen time range in the dashboard. summary: Dashboards on a monthly when ready at/after a specific date
description: Currently the dashboards can be sent out in notifications either Monthly on a specific date or Monthly when data is ready (without a specific date). Would be great to add a feature to send out monthly when ready, on a specific date or later.    For example, currently if I want to send out dashboards on the 23rd of each month, I can set this to Monthly on 23rd. If a publisher does not yet have data, the section of the report will be blank. Then by the 23rd of the next month, the data is still blank as it is trying to read the current month (for which data has not been received yet). As such this section of the report will likely not load in any month.    Alternatively, I could set the report to monthly when ready, but then the data might send out at any point in the month. Ideally I could set a report to send out on the 23rd of the month if data is ready, or after this point whenever data becomes ready.

In many cases, an admin needs to isolate similar queries that may contain a search term. For example, if the admin wants to isolate all queries containing the term “form”. 

It would be helpful to  add “contains” logic to search term filtering so an admin can select a term and receive queries/search terms containing that string (e.g., “download forms”, “patient forms”, “forms”).
