It would be helpful to expose _distance_ from Search API in result set to make it easier for admin to assess why particular results are coming forward.
workaround: N/A

summary: Add Other Products to Entity Views Filters
description:
My new Assistance team will be responsible for operating within customer accounts on behalf of those customers. One of our biggest challenges is documentation and connecting that documentation to actual platform configuration. One of the biggest pain points of that is it’s very challenging to tell which fields publish to which experiences. This makes it very difficult to know what should be updated and what the impact of those updates is downstream. For the new beta feature Entity Views, it would be helpful to include views for fields utilized by other Yext products. This could include:

* Fields leveraged by a particular Search Experience(s) (Configuration field and/or Display Fields)
* Fields leveraged by a particular Page(s)
* Fields leveraged by Streams and/or a Content Delivery endpoint

Not to get too much into solutioning, but as I thought about this, it seems like we could do this one of two ways; either the system would be able to reference some combination of fields, pages/search/integration code, templates, and saved filters to determine the relationship/filter view OR you could allow admins to configure or set the filter/view attribute on the field level as a part of building the experience.  

Regardless, this would be massively helpful for the Assistance team who have their hands on almost 2000 of our Direct Enterprise customers. And, of course, I think this is valuable for the customers themselves, too. 

Thanks so much for taking this feedback! LMK if you have any questions!
workaround: Reference documentation, if it is available. Otherwise you have to reference code or use deductive reasoning.

summary: Count page views via CDN Request
description: As of today, when analytics are disabled we don't track page views. This is happening on PMI’s new locator as the client didn’t set up the CMP correctly and we are not tracking page views for 5 live locators. This has an impact on the billing as page views are the main metric for page usage and billing. I asked this question on [#discuss-pages|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C012CFFK4J0/p1711034471766569] and the suggestion is to measure pageviews by CDN requests.
workaround: No workaround as of today.

summary: Add Search Config/Display Fields, Stream Fields, etc. to Entity Views
description:
For the new beta feature Entity Views, it would be helpful to include views for fields utilized by other Yext products. This could include:

* Fields leveraged by a particular Search Experience(s) (Configuration field and/or Display Fields)
* Fields leveraged by a particular Page(s) 
* Fields leveraged by Streams and/or a Content Delivery endpoint

Additionally, it would be helpful to provide admins the ability to define their own filterable views, by selecting the view(s) to include a particular field in when configuring an Entity Type and/or Field.
workaround: N/A

summary: Integration with Predefined Google Services on Listings
description:
Allstate is asking if we have or plan to have an integration with Predefined Google Services on listings - [https://developers.google.com/my-business/content/services|https://developers.google.com/my-business/content/services|smart-link] 
We already use [Services ECLs|https://knowledge.telescope.yext.com/board/board-publisherfaqs?selected=df2e9375-ed57-4527-88e6-59fd4870b5a7-knowledge] for custom services defined in Yext, but they are also wondering if there can be an option to add the {{StructuredServiceItem}} in Google's API. I think these appear as suggested services to add to a Listing in GBP (see screenshot)
workaround: We already use Services ECLs for custom services defined in Yext

summary: Add Labels To Google User Photos
description: Wells Fargo escalates inappropriate Google User photos internally. They would like to be able to add labels to show when a Google User Photo has been checked or escalated, and they would like to assign a task for Google user photos to others.
workaround: Right now Wells Fargo has to keep track of their Google User Photo escalations in external systems, so it would make Yext stickier if the could do it all in our system.

summary: More Flexibility for Brand-Specific Responses and Templates
description: It would be helpful to provide more options to all elements of the Reviews process to be configured for specific brands or entity types or labels, such as different IRR assets for providers vs. location entities, different AI response instructions, and different domains for different entities review generation emails. More information found in the resource linked.
workaround: No workaround for AI responses (other than editing the response). Can include multiple templates that are irrelevant to some entities or scope a user's role to only particular entity types.

summary: SMS and Email Review Generation
description: Client would like the ability to send both an SMS and email review generation invite. This is currently supported by other competitors. Meanwhile, Yext only allows users to send one or the other.
workaround: None

summary: Unsubscribe / Opt-out Management
description:
In Yext, clients are able to download a list of users who unsubscribed from emails. However, Yext does not allow the option to add to the list of unsubscribes/opt-outs into Yext. For example, if a user had unsubscribed from a client’s normal email list (not Yext), they would not want to then send a review generation email to that same user from Yext. Meaning, a client’s existing unsubscribe list should apply to Yext. 

Similarly, if a review generation unsubscribe should only apply to the particular account. Meaning, if a user had unsubscribed from a review generation email for the NYP account, that should not influence their email subscription to another Yext account (i.e. NYU Langone).
workaround: None

summary: Review Generation Follow-Up
description:
Multiple Yext customers expect the ability to send follow-up communications (SMS text and/or email) to a customer (or patient) after they have an encounter with the brand (Yext customer). Right now, there is no way to set up logic such as "If the patient hasn't opened the text in 3 days, send a follow-up text" or "If the patient doesn't submit a review from the email within 1 week, send a follow-up email".



Provide the ability to establish rules so that when an action occurs, after a certain duration, the reviews system will send a follow-up request for a review. Press Ganey and Podium tout these features so feature parity would help mitigate churn risk and potential loss of net new opportunities.
workaround: I think Yext customers can re-submit (via API, flat file upload, or manual input) the customer/ patient's contact information in the Yext platform, and this will trigger another outreach (text or email) to the customer. However, this is completely manual and doesn't involve any logic about whether anything happened with regard to the original outreach.

summary: Linkage Between Sandbox and Production Accounts
description:
This idea is related to the Healthcare taxonomy but could apply to other accounts testing integrations, setting up their data model, etc. The request is to provide a better process and or linkage for “mirroring” configuration and content between a production and sandbox account, and a more straightforward process for pushing configuration and/or content changes from a sbx to prod account. 

Most organizations perform their reviews and edits of the taxonomy (pre- or post-purchase) within a sandbox account prior to shifting their customized taxonomy to their prod account. 

The current cloning process is known to be faulty when cloning large amounts of entities and/or entities with complex relationships, which presents many issues for customers using it to migrate our taxonomy. It would be helpful to provide more native functionality for linking sandbox and production accounts to better facilitate the migration process of configuration and entities, similar to the Yext-to-Yext Connectors.

Also for performing any testing, it’s not easy to spin up a sandbox that directly reflects the content and configuration within a customer’s production account.
workaround: Mix of Admin Console, CLI, and/or Yext to Yext Connectors.

summary: Healthgrades Integrations
description: Multiple customers and prospects have asked whether we have an integration with Healthgrades. We historically have explored a way to integrate with Healthgrades, but nothing has been released. This would be a differentiator.
workaround: For Reviews, customers can use Datashake; no workaround for Listings

summary: Improved Healthcare Professional Data Structure
description:
Healthcare clients have expressed frustration with Yext’s data structure because it does not allow for a seamless integration with how healthcare clients traditionally store and architect their data in most software platforms. Within the Healthcare industry, provider data is stored by the National Provider Identifier (NPI) with practicing locations stored within each NPI. Meanwhile, to align with non-healthcare publishers’ (Google, Bing, etc.) listings requirement (i.e. 1 address per listing), Yext stores data based on every location where a provider works/ provides care. 

Recommendation is to support a single entity profile for each healthcare professional, regardless of how many locations they practice at, utilizing Linked Entities (or another mechanism) to associate the various addresses where they practice.
workaround: Make multiple entities for each unique location a HC professional practices at. Some customers have opted for creating a "master profile" that leverages function hooks to maintain data between one summary level entity and all location-specific entities.

summary: Difficulty Finding Entity Relationship Fields for Taxonomy
description: Relationship fields created through the app are hidden at the bottom on the entities in the other fields section. This makes it hard to view and edit entity relationships. The taxonomy app should include a Section for all taxonomy related fields to make them easier to identify on the entity profile.
workaround: Admin manually changes Entity Type display settings/structure.

summary: "View All" on Entity Relationships
description: Currently Entity Relationships can only be viewed on the right hand side of an Entity’s profile at a rolled up level. It would be helpful to add a method to further expand the information included in this screen to show all linked entities in the standard Entities screen to allow for further editing, filtering, etc.
workaround: Utilize Content filters to isolate relevant entities.

summary: Report Builder | Listing Status with Publisher Logos
description: Many SMBs want to see a basic report showing their listing status with the publisher logos. However, this is not available in our dashboard. Currently, you can export listing status from the “All Listings” tab but it does not include logos and its not it a presentable format.
workaround: Currently no workaround.

summary: Report Builder | Listing Status with Publisher Logos
description: Many SMBs want to see a basic report showing their listing status with the publisher logos. However, this is not available in our dashboard. Currently, you can export listing status from the “All Listings” tab but it does not include logos and its not it a presentable format.
workaround: Currently no workaround

summary: Export Linked Entity Fields
description:
Currently Content exports can not include fields associated with Linked Entities (outside of Entity/Yext ID). Ideally, an admin could specific which linked entities fields are included in the export (similar to selecting fields on the base entity). At a minimum, including the name of the Linked Entity would be a major improvement. 

This issue has surfaced as a part of managing the Healthcare Taxonomy. From a review-ability standpoint, this makes it extremely difficult to manage changes to the taxonomy outside of Yext, which is how most health systems would like to perform the initial mapping of terms to doctors, since there’s no intuitive way to see the terms associated with a particular speciality in an export. 

Further, most customers want to review the terms prior to assigning providers and with the terms model there's no way to see those connections prior to running the plugin (which requires providers to be added). Additionally, with the speciality model, since the entities are one-way relationships, the export does not provide an easy way to tie back the entity IDs to the name of the entities (attempted with a series of vlookups and index:matches)
workaround: An admin can view the linkages within Yext or can build a tool utilizing our APIs/Streams to view all fields associated with entity relationships outside of the platform.

summary: Report Builder | Listing Status with Publisher Logos
description:
*Product Area*: Analytics

*Vertical*: Reseller

*Problem*: Many SMBs want to see a basic report showing their listing status with the publisher logos. However, this is not available in our dashboard. Currently, you can export listing status from the “All Listings” tab but it does not include logos and its not it a presentable format.

*Resolution*:

* Include publisher logos in report
* Include Status Type Metric/Dimension (Synced, Not Synced, Opt Out, Processing)

*End Result:*

* More engagement in analytics
* Quick to value for SMBs that initially launch
workaround: no workaround today

summary: Report Builder | Listing Status with Publisher Logos
description:
*Product Area*: Analytics 

*Vertical*: Reseller 

*Problem*: Many SMBs want to see a basic report showing their listing status with the publisher logos. However, this is not available in our dashboard. Currently, you can export listing status from the “All Listings” tab but it does not include logos and its not it a presentable format. 

*Resolution*: 

* Include publisher logos in report 
* Include Status Type Metric/Dimension  (Synced, Not Synced, Opt Out, Processing) 

*End Result:* 

* More engagement in analytics 
* Quick to value for SMBs that initially launch
workaround: Currently no workaround

summary: Filter Search Query Analytics
description: Our clients are not able to get Search Query analytics when Filter Search is used. Filter Search has become a standard way of implementing Find-a-Docs and our clients are not able to get the advertised value out of their Search spend when they have no insight into Search Queries, Unique Search Terms, Search Clusters, Click-Through Rates.
workaround: There is no work around, it is impossible to get Filter Search analytics.

summary: Multi-Day Event Listings
description: The client doesn’t like that we cannot support Multi-Day Events. The only way an Event Listing can be used for a multi-day event is if They purchase multiple event listings for every single day of the event for every single location which can end up being very costly. In addition to being costly, they are also highly time consuming. For this reason, they have decided not to use it on Multi-Day events despite their desire to do so.
workaround: The workaround is they can purchase multiple event listing licenses for every day and every location, which again costs more money sometimes 3X-5X depending on how long the event runs. Licenses can also be repurposed during the year. However it's hard to repurpose and set up a 3-5 day event and repurpose that much. It create a lot of manual work on the client's end.

summary: Reputation Score
description:
[Rep.com|http://rep.com/] are heavily using their "Rep Score" metric quite hard against us at the moment, at least, here in Europe. We all know the Rep score is only a loose value of which other factors in the marketing mix should also be considered, but brands seems so focussed on that score at the moment. 

Especially with franchise based businesses it seems, as it provides both a gamification metric across franchises, but also at an aggregate level across Regional managers. A car dealership prospect I'm working on has this right now; even including the Rep score in their Annual statement!

Anyway, after looking at the Rep website, [they list out|https://reputation.com/resources/articles/how-reputation-com-does-it-reputation-score/] the signals they crunch  in order to obtain such a score. Interestingly to us, they all seem like signals we also have in our analytics, see: [https://reputation.com/resources/articles/how-reputation-com-does-it-reputation-score/|https://reputation.com/resources/articles/how-reputation-com-does-it-reputation-score/|smart-link]
workaround: We try to steer clients to look at a "bigger picture" in terms of how reputation management directly impacts organic ranking factors. Share of Intelligent Search is useful here. However, most Rep.com clients are deeply wedded to the Rep Score and require a like for like equivalent.

summary: TrustPilot: Review Response for Multiple Brands in 1 Yext Account
description:
* CURO was previously able to respond to TrustPilot Reviews in Yext for more than one brand ([MyMoneyToGo.Com|http://mymoneytogo.com/] & Heights[Finance.com|http://finance.com/])

* Over a year ago, they consolidated both of these into [HeightsFinance.com|http://heightsfinance.com/]
* *Then* we merged CURO & HeightsFinance's Yext accounts (not knowing that this could affect how they respond to TP reviews) 
* Then they added CashMoney domain under their TrustPilot account
* Now they can’t respond to TrustPilot Reviews for both brands in their Yext account and are unhappy
workaround: We don't have one currently

summary: Trigger a Crawler via API
description: This is an idea raised on Hitchhikers some time ago: [https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/4723338243855712241?timestamp=1710515931286|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/ideas/4723338243855712241?timestamp=1710515931286|smart-link]  . Allianz are on the verge of a global expansion with Yext. In terms of content transfer, a Crawler is their preference. They would very much like to trigger a crawler via API if needed.
workaround: Log into the platform and perform a manual initiation of the Crawler

summary: Add Xing URL to Content and Publisher network
description:
Xing is a German social network, similar to LinkedIn. It’s quite popular in the DACH region and many German Publishers accept the Xing URL as a field to publish on Listings.

Ströer used to have their own Listings solution that did support this field. 

Can we please add the Xing URL field to Content and export it to the publishers that support the field?

This should at the very least be the following:

{{Acompio, Cylex, Dialo, Finderr, Hotfrog, MeineStadt, Ortsdienst, Stadtbranchenbuch, WhereTo and Wogibtswas}}
workaround: NA

summary: Allow Google Fields API to be queried with Google Category ID
description:
Ströer uses the [Google Fields API|https://hitchhikers.yext.com/docs/managementapis/content/categoriesgoogleattributes#operation/getGoogleKeywords] to fetch the available Google Attributes for a given Entity. This API takes the {{clientCategoryId}} as input and provides back the available Google Attributes. However, Ströer often uses Category Overrides for Google to ensure the right category is selected. This API does not take this into account and therefore it often returns the wrong attributes.

Now that we show advanced category overrides in the Entities API, a possible solution would be to allow the Google Fields API to accept the Google Category ID as a query param too. That way Ströer can make sure display the correct Google Attributes for every entity, regardless of whether they use category overrides or not.
workaround: NA

summary: Increase the limit on the Listings:List API
description:
Our Listings:List API has a maximum limit of 100 records per page. For many of our larger resellers this means that they would need to paginate through thousands of pages of results if they want to pull in all Listings Statuses.

This request for Yell returns about 1,200,00 records. With a limit of 100 per page, this means 12,000 API requests to get the full data set.

Localsearch has asked if we can increase the max limit to 1,000 which would decrease the number of requests required by a factor of 10.
workaround: None, except doing the additional API requests.

summary: Be Able to Set Click-Though URL for First Party POST
description:
Hi Team,

Background & Issues:

* This client has a contract with Yext Listings and Pages.
* They use Social Posts for each store via media such as GBP.
* They also want to display the content of these Social Posts on the store pages.
* They intend to utilize Widgets to display them by posting to the First Party.
* However, currently, only text and images can be posted for First Party Posts, and it's not possible to set click-through URLs.

Request:

* Due to the above arrangement, we would like the ability to set URLs for posts intended for First Party.
* Alternatively, if Widgets can display posts intended for GBP, that would also be a good idea.

Thank you,

Kenta
workaround:
By utilizing the Management API, it's possible to retrieve posts intended for GBP and display them on store pages.
However, this approach would incur development costs.
The request at hand is not specific to a particular client but applies to any client who has contracted Listings and Pages.

summary: Configurability of Displayed Fields on Entity Relationship Dropdown
description: Hi Content Team, a partner (Phase2) is helping a HC client (Sutter Health) move to the Yext KG as their source of truth. When linking location-based entities (i.e., HC professionals and HC facilities), the fields that are displayed are the Name and Address fields. Contrarily, all non-location-based entities display the Name and Entity ID. It would provide a ton of value, particularly when linking taxonomy entities, for a HC system to be able to configure which fields are actually displayed within a particular entity relationship field.
workaround: No existing workaround

summary: Increase the limit on the Listings:List API
description:
Our Listings:List API has a maximum limit of 100 records per page. For many of our larger resellers this means that they would need to paginate through thousands of pages of results if they want to pull in all Listings Statuses.



For example: [https://api.yext.com/v2/accounts/all/listings/listings?api_key=a0e4029f6861b0e11480d5ef1b5ac115&v=20250101&limit=100|https://api.yext.com/v2/accounts/all/listings/listings?api_key=a0e4029f6861b0e11480d5ef1b5ac115&v=20250101&limit=100]



This request for Yell returns about 550,000 records. With a limit of 100 per page, this means 5,500 API requests to get the full data set.

Yell has asked if we can increase the max limit to 1,000 which would decrease the number of requests required by a factor of 10.
workaround: None, except doing the additional API requests.

summary: Delivery Platform Reviews
description: REGO is asking if we include 3rd party delivery (Door Dash, etc) reviews. Would be great if we could get these added as additional publishers 🙂
workaround: n/a

summary: Entity Relationships Entity Validation Filter
description:
One of our partners, Phase2, reached out inquiring on entity relationships. While they are aware they can use the "Validation - Supported Destination Entity Types" feature to limit which entity types a one-way relationship field can point to, the client needs greater control over the actual drop-down list. For example, they have an entity that has a "type" field.  And they only want to show the entities in the drop-down for a specific value of that "type" field rather than showing all entities. This is not currently possible today.

[Here|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/CDA4M8ULS/p1710343718244079] is a link to the sme-content thread, and [here|https://yextops.slack.com/archives/C05P64MD42D/p1710281448794269] is a link to the original request from the partner on behalf of the client.
workaround: The only workaround we've been able to ideate would be to use an Account Connector to create new entity types which would be included as part of the validation.

summary: Review Generation Landing Page
description: They want easy ability for Yext to generate a single landing page to solicit reviews for Google and/or Facebook - where the customer can select the store, or the store is auto-determined based upon location. Podium is offering this as a standard option within their Review Gen / Solicitation program (not custom dev) putting Yext Reviews at risk. Other customers have mentioned this as well. This doesn’t violate our Yelp agreement as long as Yext is not specifically sending the Review Gen request. Adding this functionality could be a huge win for GEOs where we are generally unable to sell Review Generation (they seldom care about 1P, only 3P).
workaround: see above

summary: AI used for headshots/photos
description:
OptumHealth has terrible and inconsistent photos. Needs the ability to fix and make headshots consistent across all photos/headshots

From Ashley at Optum:  creating a consistent background for all Optum providers… and possibly generating a new professional photo from a casual photo.

Birdeye just made the following announcement:  [https://birdeye.com/press/birdeye-launches-ai-powered-social-platform-for-multi-location-brands/?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=organic&utm_campaign=press_release_social_&utm_term=press_release&utm_content=landing_page&utm_sfcamp=7015b000005MLxzAAG|https://birdeye.com/press/birdeye-launches-ai-powered-social-platform-for-multi-location-brands/?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=organic&utm_campaign=press_release_social_&utm_term=press_release&utm_content=landing_page&utm_sfcamp=7015b000005MLxzAAG|smart-link]
workaround: I don't know of any except employ a different tool.

summary: Spam Filters for Reviews
description: Some competitors have a ‘spam’ filter for reviews that are not ‘real’ based on the content type that slip into 3rd party platforms. For example, on a healthcare provider listing, a person may leave a review advertising some sort of service. Healthcare organizations don’t want those reviews to flow into analytics.
workaround: Flagging reviews in the platform.

summary: First Party Healthcare Reviews Analytics
description:
This is a follow on from *PFR-21 Healthcare First Party NPS/Surveys Tool with Analytics*. 

In order to best serve our customers IF we have a First-Party Survey/NPS tool, we need to also update our analytics in order to support holistic reputation management. This includes the following: 

* *Reporting:* Ratings out of 10 in Yext Platform
* *Reporting:* Add additional fields based on content in Review Generation
* *Reporting (API)*: Need to be able to send NPS scores out of 10 via API rather than only out of 5. This is critical as many organizations use a third-party BI tool to pull data points from multiple reputation platforms (or other non-reputation platforms)
* *Reporting (API):* Need to be able to send a Visit Type parameter through the Reviews/Analytics API
workaround: No workaround at this time.

summary: Healthcare First Party NPS/Surveys Tool with Analytics
description:
*Scenario:* Yext provides reviews for many Healthcare systems specifically for 3rd-party reviews. We are *unable* to compete with reputation management vendors that provide 1st-party Healthcare surveys or 1st-party NPS score. This is a critical component to reputation management for Healthcare organizations.

*Competition:* Yext is not able to compete with competitors that offer both 1st party and 3rd party options. These vendors include (with varying capabilities) Loyal Health, Podium, Press Ganey, among others. 

*Product Gaps for Yext:* 

* *Survey Functionality:* Survey tool to request for satisfaction score of 0-10 and prompt for a Google Review once the NPS score is input
* *Survey Requests*: Ability to sent a survey instead of a 1-5 review collection form. Then product should be able to ingest 1-10 scores and other survey responses. In short term, a 1-10 NPS score is the priority, other survey data can come later. 
* *NPS Analytics:* Parse reviews analytics by patient visit type which would be sent from an EMR/internal system via our Review Generation API. This ‘tag’ would remain on the review and be dimension-able within the platform. 

*Competitor Demos of Competitive Feature:* 

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fHa6I1ojC7U|https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fHa6I1ojC7U|smart-link]  - Podium
workaround:
Have review collection page request for 1-10 rating that is then halved in Yext to include as a 1-5 star review. Upon the patient leaving a review, the review is ingested into the platform on a 1-5 scale. 

This isn't a full scale solution because we can't view 1-10 within the platform for analytics and does not bring us to parity with our competition.

summary: Bulk Assign Linked Accounts in Knowledge Graph - Test from CSTA
description:
Could functionality be added that would allow users to assign Linked Accounts to entities in bulk either via spreadsheet upload or data connector?

Currently, entities can be bulk edited to assigned Linked Accounts. However, this is not scalable for accounts that have hundreds of separate Linked Accounts to manage their individual GBP and Facebook listings.
